gmw_00001938.name: ILT J114850.36+592456.2
gmw_00001938.overview: ILT J114850.36+592456.2 is an elliptical or S0 galaxy [arXiv:2106.13193, arXiv:astro-ph/0309620] hosting a low-luminosity active nucleus [arXiv:2106.13193, arXiv:astro-ph/0309620] with LINER characteristics [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. It features a compact radio structure [arXiv:2106.13193, arXiv:1003.2835] surrounded by an edge-on neutral hydrogen ring [arXiv:1003.2835] and a faint dust lane [arXiv:1003.2835, arXiv:0911.4531].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Chandra data for NGC 3894/1146+596, an elliptical/S0 young radio galaxy, finding extended soft thermal emission, a nuclear iron K line, and high-energy gamma-ray association.

quotes.quote: The radio source 1146+596 is hosted by an elliptical/S0 galaxy NGC 3894, with a low-luminosity active nucleus. The radio structure is compact, suggesting a very young age of the jets in the system. Recently, the source has been confirmed as a high-energy (HE, >0.1 GeV) ray emitter, in the most recent accumulation of the Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) data. Here we report on the analysis of the archival Chandra X-ray Observatory data for the central part of the galaxy, consisting of a single 40 ksec-long exposure.

Based on the simulations of the Chandra 's Point Spread Function (PSF), we have concluded that, while the soft thermal component is extended on the scale of the galaxy host, the hard X-ray emission within the narrow photon energy range 6.0--7.0 keV originates within the unresolved core (effectively the central kpc radius). The line is therefore indicative of the X-ray reflection from a cold neutral gas in the central regions of NGC 3894. We discuss the implications of our findings in the context of the X-ray Baldwin effect. NGC 3894 is the first young radio galaxy detected in HE rays with the iron K line.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3894 as a lobe-dominated gamma-ray CSO with a significant LAT signal, flat GeV spectrum, lowest luminosity among the three, and no variability.

quotes.quote: The ray emitting compact symmetric objects (CSOs) PKS 1718--649, NGC 3894, and TXS 0128+554 are lobe-dominated in the radio emission. In order to investigate their ray radiation properties, we analyze the 14-yr Fermi/LAT observation data of the three CSOs. They all show the low luminosity ( 10^ 41 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) and no significant variability in the ray band.

NGC 3894 . A significant ray signal with TS = 96.4 is detected around NGC 3894. The radio position of NGC 3894 is located at the 68 containment of the best-fit position of 4FGL J1149.0+5924, as displayed in Figure TSmap . Its spectrum at the GeV band is flatter than that of PKS 1718--649, i.e., _ =2.16 0.11 , which is slightly steeper than the derived value of _ =2.01 0.10 with the 10.8-yr observation data in . NGC 3894 has the lowest ray luminosity among the three CSOs, being (6.18 0.89) 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 . The confidence level of the flux variation is less than 2 , indicating no variability in its ray emission too.

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 3894 as a compact radio source with an edge-on H I ring, aligned dust lane, distortion by NGC 3895, double-peaked absorption, and a rich-galaxy environment.

quotes.quote: NGC 3894: The \ around NGC 3894 is distributed in what appears to be an edge-on ring-like (or possibly a disc-like) structure. Our deep optical image reveals a faint but extended dust lane along the direction of the \ ring. The \ ring seems distorted at the location of the nearby barred spiral galaxy NGC 3895, which lies 27 kpc east-north-east of NGC 3894 and does not contain any observable . The \ absorption against the unresolved radio source in NGC 3894 has a clear double-peaked profile see also dic86,gor89,pec98,gup06 . We argue that the bulk of the absorbing gas is likely part of the large-scale \ ring.
As shown in \ and emo06thesis , NGC 3894 is located in an environment of several nearby rich galaxies. The radio source is compact tay98 .

sources.summary: 1146+596 is treated as a nearby CSO with multiple radio outbursts and a gamma-ray counterpart whose luminosity is attributed to jet activity, not star formation.

quotes.quote: 1146+596 is characterized by a much larger LS 933 pc Taylor98,Perlman01 We note that 1146+596 is characterized by multiple radio outbursts, and the most compact radio component in the source, for which the kinematic age has been estimated as 60 yr, has a linear size of 3.5 pc Principe20 . , but located at a comparable distance of 47 Mpc, and recently associated with the ray source 4FGL J1149.0+5924 in the Fermi LAT 8-Year Point Source Catalog 4FGL; 4FGL .

The question follows if the observed ray emission in both sources is related to the jet activity and compact radio lobes in particular Stawarz08,Kino09 , or is instead due to the star formation activity within the hosts, analogous to the LAT-detected, star-forming and starburst galaxies Ackermann12,Hayashida13,Rojas16 . In order to address this question, we have collected all archival infrared data for both sources, including near-infrared 2MASS and MIR Spitzer spectra, as well as mm fluxes from WMAP, and estimated the integrated 8-1000 m luminosities, and next the star-formation rates assuming the Kennicutt98 scaling relation SFR M_ yr^ 1 = 1.7 L_ 8-1000 m 10^ 10 L_ , with the initial mass function factor = 0.79 Ackermann12 . The resulting values, given in Table tab:SFR , are consistent with the SFR estimates by Willett10 for 1718--649, based on either Ne lines or PAH features in the Spitzer high-resolution spectra (see the Table). In the case of 1146+596 we achieve a lower SFR estimate based on the integrated luminosity, which may be due in part to poor data quality (i.e. lack of data points over the infrared range) as well as due to the fact that some MIR emission can come from the dust heated by old stellar populations and not indeed by star formation itself.

Similarly, for 1146+596, based on the integrated 1-100 GeV photon flux of the high-energy ray counterpart provided in 4FGL, (2.17 0.35) 10^ 10 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , along with the best-fit photon index _ = 2.06 0.11 , we derive the luminosity L_ 0.1-100 GeV 6 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , which is again orders of magnitude larger than the expected emission for the ISM component alone. Hence, we conclude that the ray emission detected with Fermi LAT from 1718--649 and 1146+596, is undoubtedly related to the jet activity in the sources, and not the star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper places 1146+596 in a transitional merger phase, citing optical disturbance, thermal near-infrared colors, and straight unresolved radio jets.

quotes.quote: * 1146+596 (NGC 3894) (LINER, z=0.011 ): Disturbed morphology in the optical. This source is positioned in the thermal regime on the NIR color-color diagram (see Fig. fig:color_diagram_candidates ), confirming its transitionary phase. The above indicates that the system has possibly undergone, or is undergoing, a merger. The radio jet appears straight. A VLA jet is unresolved.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3894 as a compact radio galaxy with a regular elliptical host, a faint dust lane, and a large disc probably from older merger or accretion.

quotes.quote: The disc/ring around NGC 3894 has no apparent optical counterpart (Fig. fig:HIdiscs right ). The host galaxy has a much more regular elliptical morphology, although a very faint dust-lane crosses the host galaxy in roughly the same direction as the \ disc. Since we see the \ disc in NGC 3894 edge-one, there could be significant extinction within the \ disc. It is possible that the \ disc in NGC 3894 formed from a much older merger event, or though a steady accretion of \ gas from the inter-galactic medium over time-scales of several Gyr see for more details emo07,emo10 .

sources.summary: The paper links J1148+5924 to NGC 3894, whose bright host fills the masked area, creating possible polarization-degree errors from sky-background estimation and requiring a fixed aperture.

quotes.quote: J1148+5924: NGC 3894 has a very bright host. The galaxy fills the masked areas entirely. For this reason there is a possibility of deviation of the PD from its real values due to inaccurate estimate of the sky background. We measured this source with a fixed 6 arcsec aperture.

sources.summary: The paper inspects ILT J114850.36+592456.2 as a CSO counterpart with a possible convex spectrum and high-frequency turnover needing confirmation.

quotes.quote: The source ILT J114850.36+592456.2 also has a counterpart in the CSO sample presented by 2000ApJ...534...90P . From Fig. fig:cross_PS_1 , we see that this source potentially has a convex spectrum between 54--1400 MHz, with a possible spectral turnover at 4 GHz. However, since spectral variability can be significant at high frequencies, more high-frequency data-points after the turnover are needed in order to confirm this spectral turnover. A convex source with a spectral turnover above 1 GHz is suggestive of multiple epochs of AGN activity, where the low-frequency section of the spectrum is dominated by emission from aged electrons, while the higher-frequency peak comes from recent core activity and references therein Callingham_2017 .

sources.summary: NGC 3894 is described as the bright elliptical or S0 counterpart of 1146 + 596, with starlight-dominated optical continuum, active-nucleus indicators, peculiar gas kinematics, and LINER emission.

quotes.quote: 1146 + 596 has been included in the extragalactic radio-jets catalogue of Liu Zhang (2002); the optical counterpart of this source is NGC 3894, a bright galaxy at z = 0.01068 classified as an elliptical or an S0 (Nilson 1973). Although its optical continuum is dominated by starlight, this galaxy's radio and far-infrared continuum suggest the presence of an active nucleus (Condon Broderick 1988). Spectroscopic observations carried by Kim (1989) show the presence of a dust lane and ionized gas along the galaxy's major axis; the gas kinematics are rather peculiar, exhibiting non-circular motions. Analysis of our data shows that the nuclear emission-line region has LINER characteristics; such a low ionization spectrum could be associated with a low power AGN.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 1146+59 has a flat mid-infrared index, weak absorption features, and the smallest warm molecular gas mass among the CSOs.

quotes.quote: 1146+59 has the shallowest index and is flat between 5 and 8 .
We detect both features at a weak level in 1146+59; we use a spline-fit to characterize the local continuum and measure their optical depths and integrated fluxes.

The line luminosities can also be used to measure masses of the components (Table tbl-derived ); the mass of the warm gas varies over more than two orders of magnitude for the CSOs, with the smallest contribution coming from the gas in 1146+59 ( M_ H_2 =9 10^4 M _ ) to the much larger component in the galaxy 4C +12.50 ( M_ H_2 =4 10^7 M _ ).

sources.summary: J1148+5924 is a low-luminosity CSO 1 with edge-dimmed morphology in 5 and 8 GHz maps and evidence for a previous activity epoch.

quotes.quote: J1148+5924, CSO 1 [ 42 ] : The 2016MNRAS.459..820T 5 GHz and 8 GHz maps show this to be an edge-dimmed object, as is also seen in the MOJAVE ^ image. The morphology and low luminosity indicate this is a CSO 1 object. This object shows evidence of a previous epoch of activity.

gmw_00001939.name: host galaxy of GRB 060814
gmw_00001939.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 060814 is a high-redshift starburst system located at a redshift of approximately 1.92 [arXiv:1205.4036, arXiv:2305.13436]. It consists of two nearby galaxies [arXiv:1205.4036] and exhibits a star formation rate that places it about seven times above the main sequence [arXiv:2305.13436, arXiv:2007.11874].

sources.summary: The paper reports deep observations of the GRB 060814 host, finding low molecular-gas indicators, an offset from normal star-forming galaxies, and a position above the main sequence.

quotes.quote: Observations and Data Reduction ht We observed the hosts of GRB 060814 and 080207 with the IRAM 30m telescope (proposal 172-16; PI: M.J.M.), equipped with the Eight MIxer Receiver www.iram.es/IRAMES/mainWiki/EmirforAstronomers Carter2012A&A...538A..89C . We implemented the wobbler-switching mode and the Fourier Transform Spectrometers 200 (FTS-200) providing 195 kHz spectral resolution and 16 GHz bandwidth in each linear polarisation. The observations for both targets were executed between 2017-Feb-01 and 2017-May-22 and lasted in total 13.1 hr on-source for GRB 060814 and 17.8 hr for GRB 080207.

The \ for the GRB 060814 and 080207 host galaxies are found to be lower by > 0.8 and > 0.2 dex from our best-fit for the P04 method. At least in the case of the host galaxy of GRB 060814 the offset is in agreement with the distance from the best-fit estimated for (U)LIRGs ( 0.8 dex), high- z starburst ( 0.7), NGC 1674 ( 0.9 dex), and for the transitioning SFG mergers ( 0.4 dex).
We find that GRBs 060814 is about seven times above the MS, while GRB 080207 lies on the corresponding MS within the scatter.

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 060814 host system as two nearby galaxies at z≈1.92, identifies galaxy A with the afterglow, and rejects galaxy C as foreground.

quotes.quote: GRB 060814 A redshift of z=1.92 for the dark GRB 060814 ( _ OX <-0.1 ) based on these data was reported by 2011arXiv1112.1700S . The system hosting GRB 060814 consists of two galaxies A and B separated by 1 3 Figure 1 and 2012ApJ...752...62J at redshifts of z_ A =1.923 and z_ B =1.920 . The location of the NIR afterglow is consistent with galaxy A, and z_ A =1.923 is the redshift of GRB 060814. Galaxy C from Figure  fct is an unrelated foreground object.

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB060814 host SED, reporting SFR, stellar mass, a near-ideal reduced chi-squared, and consistency with previous flux upper limits.

quotes.quote: We present physical parameters of the best-fit SED template of the GRB060814 host galaxy in Table tab10 . A comparison between this work and previous works is summarised in Table tab12 . The physical parameters of the best-fit template are SFR_ SED =56 9M_ yr ^ 1 and M_ * =(2.68 0.76) 10^ 10 M_ . The reduced ^2 is 1.00 which is the closest to 1 in our four conditions. The flux upper limits reported in previous studies are consistent with the best-fit template (Fig. fig2 ).

gmw_00001940.name: Mrk 273
gmw_00001940.overview: Mrk 273 is a late-stage ultraluminous infrared galaxy merger [arXiv:1307.8440, arXiv:2206.02847] classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2402.10786, arXiv:1510.04453]. The system features a highly disturbed morphology with a 40 kpc tidal tail [arXiv:2402.10786, arXiv:1411.0361], multiple nuclei [arXiv:1307.8440, arXiv:2206.02847], and a collimated molecular outflow [arXiv:1307.8440, arXiv:1811.09281].

sources.summary: The paper presents adaptive-optics near-infrared observations of Mrk 273, resolving its inner kiloparsec, nuclei, AGN indicators, outflows, and star-forming clumps.

quotes.quote: There is X-ray, optical, and mid-infrared imaging and spectroscopic evidence that the late-stage ultraluminous infrared galaxy merger Mrk 273 hosts a powerful AGN. However, the exact location of the AGN and the nature of the nuclei have been difficult to determine due to dust obscuration and the limited wavelength coverage of available high-resolution data. Here we present near-infrared integral-field spectra and images of the nuclear region of Mrk 273 taken with OSIRIS and NIRC2 on the Keck II Telescope with laser guide star adaptive optics.

We observe three spatially-resolved components, and analyze the local molecular and ionized gas emission lines and their kinematics. We confirm the presence of the hard X-ray AGN in the southwest nucleus. In the north nucleus, we find a strongly-rotating gas disk whose kinematics indicate a central black hole of mass 1.04 0.1 10^ 9 M_ . The emission line shows an increase in velocity dispersion along the minor axis in both directions, and an increased flux with negative velocities in the southeast direction; this provides direct evidence for a collimated molecular outflow along the axis of rotation of the disk. The third spatially-distinct component appears to the southeast, 640 and 750 pc from the north and southwest nuclei, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 273 as an ongoing merger with a 40 kpc tidal tail, multiple radio components, an AGN-bearing northern component, disk H I absorption, and blended absorption regions.

quotes.quote: Mrk 273 This object is the host of an ongoing merger. The optical morphology shows a long tidal tail extending 40 kpc to the south (Iwasawa et al. 2011, and references therein). Low-resolution 8.44 GHz radio maps by show three radio components, a northern (N), south-western (SW), and a south-eastern (SE) region.

It is thought that the northern component is hosting a weak AGN, however it is also the site of very active star-formation. Using Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) observations, detected \ absorption against the N component supposedly coming from a disk (showing velocity gradient along the major axis), and estimated an \ gas mass of 2 10 ^ 9 M _ . Molecular CO gas of similar amount (10 ^ 9 M _ ) was also detected by . also detect extended gas and an infalling gas cloud towards the SE component, suggesting that the SE component is indeed an AGN. Our low-resolution observations cannot distinguish between the different absorbing regions, we detect the blended signal, coming from all the \ absorbing regions.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 273 as a double system whose northern nucleus likely hosts much of the molecular mass and an AGN, with compact OH outflow components and low covering factor.

quotes.quote: Mrk 273 : This is a double system with the northern nucleus containing most of the molecular mass and probably also an AGN that accounts for roughly 1/3-1/2 of the total luminosity rup13a . Mrk 273 is similar in OH79 to IRAS 05189 2524. In addition, both sources (and also IRAS 20551 4250) show an OH119 absorption trough of only 10 of the continuum, and OH79 correspondingly shows weak absorption and emission in comparison with other ULIRGs with similar luminosity such as IRAS 23365+3604, but extending to higher velocities. This indicates a relatively low covering factor of the continuum by the outflow, perhaps associated with the biconical shape of the outflowing gas rup13a . At systemic velocities, the high-lying OH84 and OH65 doublets show strong absorption, most likely associated with the very compact molecular core detected by .

Mrk 273 shows two well defined velocity components on the blue side of the OH profiles, both detected in OH84, suggesting a compact molecular outflow. indeed found the shortest radius in Mrk 273, 0.55 kpc, among the ULIRG outflows observed in CO. The 300 \ component, detected in both OH84 and OH65, appears to be extremely compact 130 pc and has high columns ( N_ H 8 10^ 22 ), yielding P _ out 2 10^ 35 dyn.

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 273 as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy whose northern nuclear disc has decoupled kinematics, dense warm central gas, compact core expansion, and a detected blue-shifted molecular outflow.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The observations were centred on RA (J2000) = 13 ^ h :44 ^ m : 42 1 , DEC (J2000) = 55 ^ :53 ^ ' : 13 5 .

quotes.quote: Aiming to characterise the properties of the molecular gas in the ultra-luminous infrared galaxy Mrk 273 and its outflow, we used the NOEMA interferometer to image the dense-gas molecular tracers HCN, HCO ^+ , HNC, HOC ^+ and HC _3 N at 86 GHz and 256 GHz with angular resolutions of 4 9 4 5 ( 3.7 3.4 kpc) and 0 61 0 55 ( 460 420 pc). We also modelled the flux of several H _2 O lines observed with Herschel using a radiative transfer code that includes excitation by collisions and far-infrared photons. The disc of the Mrk 273 north nucleus has two components with decoupled kinematics. The gas in the outer parts (R 1.5 kpc) rotates with a south-east to north-west direction, while in the inner disc (R 300 pc) follows a north-east to south-west rotation.

The central 300 pc, which hosts a compact starburst region, is filled with dense and warm gas, and contains a dynamical mass of (4-5) 10^9M_ , a luminosity of L'_ HCN =(3-4) 10^8 K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 , and a dust temperature of 55 K. At the very centre, a compact core with R 50 pc has a luminosity of L_ IR =4 10^ 11 L_ (30 of the total infrared luminosity), and a dust temperature of 95 K . The core is expanding at low velocities 50-100 km s ^ 1 , probably affected by the outflowing gas. We detect the blue-shifted component of the outflow, while the red-shifted counterpart remains undetected in our data.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 273 as a double-nucleus ULIRG merger whose southwest nucleus hosts an absorbed active nucleus, northern region hosts a powerful starburst, and tidal tail shadows soft X-rays.

quotes.quote: Analysis of data from the Chandra X-ray Observatory for the double nucleus ULIRG Mrk 273 reveals an absorbed hard X-ray source coincident with the southwest nucleus, implying that this unresolved near infrared source is where an active nucleus resides while the northern nuclear region contains a powerful starburst which dominates the far infrared luminosity. There is evidence of a slight image extension in the 6-7 keV band, where a Fe K line is present, towards the northern nucleus. A large-scale, diffuse emission nebula detected in soft X-rays contains a dark lane that spatially coincides with a high surface-brightness tidal tail extending 50 arcsec (40 kpc) to the south. The soft X-ray source is likely located behind the tidal tail which absorbs X-ray photons along the line of sight. The estimated column density of cold gas in the tidal tail responsible for shadowing the soft X-rays is N_ H 6 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 , consistent with the tidal tail having an edge-on orientation.

Mrk 273 (= UGC 8696, IRAS F13428+5608) is a nearby ( z=0.0378 ) ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) with a 8-1000 m luminosity of 1.6 10^ 12 L_ . The galaxy has a long tidal tail extending 40 kpc to the south, indicating that the system is an ongoing merger.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 273 as a Seyfert 2 ULIRG with optical and infrared nuclear structure, AGN-powered infrared emission, and variable absorbing columns in hard X-rays.

quotes.quote: Mrk 273 shows a single nucleus in UV and optical images, but the near-infrared reveals a double nucleus armus90, surace00, scoville00 . It is optically classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy khachikian74, vei99a . Continuum and emission-line diagnostics from Spitzer spectra indicate that roughly half the infrared luminosity of this source is powered by an AGN vei09a . teng09 reported a 1.8 detection of Mrk 273 by Suzaku PIN.

Their best-fit model used two partial covering absorbers to model minor spectral variability below 10 keV over six years. The model favored the scenario in which the covering fractions of the absorbers ( N_ H,1 1.6 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , N_ H,2 3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) was time-variable. koss13 reported a 2.4 detection in the 24--35 keV band.

sources.summary: The paper describes F13428+5608/Mrk273 as a nearby, well-resolved ULIRG and likely triple merger with extended ionized gas but a near-nuclear outflow limited to about 5 kpc.

quotes.quote: F13428+5608 (Mrk273) At a redshift of only z=0.037 , the Mrk273 system is one of the closest, and best resolved, of all ULIRGs. Recently, imaging and spectroscopic observations of Mrk273 have been presented by rz14 and spence16 . The deep HST [ OIII ] images presented in rz14 and Figure 1 reveal a spectacular structure of ionised gas in the form of arc-like filaments and clumps that extend 25 arcseconds ( 19 kpc) to the east of the nuclear region, in a direction that is close to perpendicular to prominent tidal tail to the south. The full radial extent of the warm ionized gas, as revealed by deep H imaging observations with the Gran Telescopio Canarias (GTC), is 45 kpc spence16 . Based on the overall morphology of the extended continuum structures, as well as the likely presence of three nuclei in the central regions, rz14 have suggested that this is object represents a triple merger.

The maximum radial extent measured for the outflow in the ground-based spectra depends on how we define an outflow: if we use the criterion of this paper (i.e. an outflow is any component with FWHM > 500 km s ^ 1 ), the maximum (spectroscopic) radial extent of the outflow is 5 kpc; however, even if we use the more conservative criterion that an outflow is associated with larger line widths FWHM > 1,000 km s ^ 1 , the maximum radial extent is only marginally smaller ( 4 kpc). Therefore we take 5 kpc as the maximum extent of the near-nuclear outflow in this case.

sources.summary: Mrk 273 is presented as a nearby ULIRG late-stage merger with high star formation, multiple AGN, fast central outflows, and extended nebulae possibly tied to outflow episodes.

quotes.quote: Mrk 273, one of the closest ULIRGs at z=0.0373 , represents a unique target for this study. It has a total star formation rate of 139 yr ^ 1 cic14 . It exhibits prominent tidal features which indicate it is a late-stage merger e.g kim02 . It has a complex nuclear structure hosting at least two to possibly three AGNs (see rz14 , hereafter rz14 ; liu19 for recent discussions).

Apart from the fast ionized outflows in the central region, a striking feature of Mrk 273 are two extended nebulae of ionized gas stretching >20 kpc in the NE and SW directions, revealed by ground- and space-based imaging studies and long-slit spectroscopy arm90 ; rz14,s16 . The nature of these extended nebulae is not completely clear. Given the presence of the multiple AGNs and fast central outflows in Mrk 273, it is of interest to determine the relation of the extended nebulae to the AGNs, such as whether they represent present or past episodes of outflows, or gas impacted by such outflows.

sources.summary: The paper presents low-frequency radio imaging of the ULIRG merger Mrk 273, finding 190 kpc arcs, a 45 kpc ridge, multiple nuclear components, absorption, and radio-AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We present such an analysis of the ULIRG Mrk 273. Using the International LOFAR telescope (ILT), we detected spectacular large-scale arcs in this system. This detection includes, for the first time, a giant 190 kpc arc in the north. We propose these arcs are fuelled by a low power radio AGN triggered by the merger.

Mrk 273 is a good object for this study since it is the second nearest ULIRG ( z = 0.0373, L _ IR = 10 ^ 12.1 L _ ; Kim1998a ) and a late stage merger, with a starburst and multiple AGNs. Previous studies have found evidence for three nuclei in the system, two in the near-IR Knapen1997 northern (N) and south-western (SW) and a third south-eastern (SE) in the radio Condon1991 and [ OIII ] ( RodriguezZaurin2014a , hereafter RodriguezZaurin2014a ). The presence of an AGN in these nuclei has been confirmed in the N component by Knapen1997 and Liu2019 (hereafter Liu2019 ), in the SW by Iwasawa2011 and Liu2019 , and a third AGN has been suggested in the SE component by RodriguezZaurin2014a .

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 273 as a ULIRG merger and OH megamaser galaxy with disturbed tidal structure, nuclear sources, and VLBI/WSRT evidence for circumnuclear OH and continuum kinematics.

quotes.quote: The galaxy Mrk 273 (UGC 08696, IRAS 13428 + 5608) has been classified as a ULIRG with an infrared luminosity of L _ FIR = 1.208 10^ 12 L . Its warm infrared color (ratio of the 60 m to 100 m IRAS flux of 1.02) suggests that the bulk of the infrared excess is associated with dust heated by nuclear star formation rather than by a dominant contribution of an accretion disk and references therein 1996ARAA..34..749S . In the optical Mrk 273 displays a morphologically disturbed structure with a thin tidal tail extending towards the south by 50 arcsec (1.3 arcsec 1 kpc) The optical redshift of z = 0.03778, q _ 0 = 0.5 \ and H _ 0 = 75 Mpc ^ 1 are used for all further estimates in this paper; therefore 1 mas corresponds to 0.7389 pc. and a fan-like plume. The infrared excess, the distinct morphology and the observed soft X-ray halo of 50 30 \ indicate a merger event within the last 10 ^ 8 yrs 1997ApJ...490L..29K .

New WSRT and EVN observations of the OH line and the continuum emission in the Megamaser galaxy Mrk 273 are presented in this paper. The OH emission structure at the northern nucleus and the continuum structure are used to understand the kinematics and the physical properties of the environment in the nuclear region. The hydroxyl line- and continuum emission in Mrk 273 was observed with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope [ WSRT ] and the European VLBI Network [ EVN ] .

sources.summary: The paper treats MKN 273 as a ULIRG whose inner-region spectrum needs Gaussian and additional unabsorbed power-law components because the central source is unevenly absorbed.

quotes.quote: The fit to the inner region of MKN 273 however is more complicated. To fit the line we have also added a gaussian. However we have found that an additional unabsorbed powerlaw is required. We have set the unabsorbed powerlaw to have the same slope as the absorbed powerlaw.

The additional powerlaw is motivated by examining representative color images of MKN 273. The central source is strongly absorbed along some lines of sight but not others. The final fit to MKN 273 gives us an acceptable reduced =1.2 .

sources.summary: The paper contrasts Mrk 273 with Mrk 231, describing a Seyfert 2 megamaser galaxy with stronger gain, an edge-on torus, and a massive compact core.

quotes.quote: Mrk 273 is classed as a Seyfert 2 and has an HI absorption column density of (17-18) 10^ 23 m ^ 2 (spin temperature/filling factor), almost three times greater than estimates for Mrk 231, assuming the same conditions ( Carilli98s ; Cole99 ). MERLIN observations show that the brightest maser peak in Mrk 273 is 67 mJy beam ^ 1 , slightly offset from the 12 mJy beam ^ 1 , 1.6-GHz continuum peak. The maser gain is much greater than in Mrk 231, suggesting amplification along a longer path length in Mrk 273, through an edge-on torus. The 1667:1665-MHz peak ratio in Mrk 273 is 5 . Klockner04 measure a lower ratio on larger scales (e.g. WSRT) and a higher ratio on smaller EVN scales, but even the most compact masers in Mrk 273 are still unsaturated.

Using the method outlined in Section efficiency gives 8 10^ 4 /4 for the pumping efficiency in Mrk 273, higher than in Mrk 231 by a factor of 4 , which may be due to a combination of a smaller beaming angle and better maser amplification (which can be achieved without saturation if clouds overlap along the line of sight). Klockner04 estimate an enclosed compact mass of (1.4 0.2) 10^9 M _ , larger than the value of (6 1) 10^8 M _ measured on MERLIN scales ( Yates00 ; Richards01 ) Note that the method is fully explained in Yates00 but an arithmetic slip leads to the wrong result; the correct value is given in Richards01 . which suggests that Mrk 273 also contains a massive compact core determining the maser kinematics on the smallest scales.

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 273 as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy whose nuclear region contains radio/NIR components, a double nucleus supporting a recent merger, and a likely unrelated southeast source.

quotes.quote: Mrk 273 (UGC 08696; 1342+561), at a distance of 151 Mpc We adopt H_0=75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 .

We have combined state-of-the-art radio, NIR and optical imaging observations, all at resolutions of around 150 mas, to study the nuclear region of the ULIRG Mrk 273. Our images have revealed three main radio components, two of which, N and SW, have bright NIR counterparts. Small-scale ( 150 mas) structure is also present in component N and, possibly, SE.

Mrk 273 has a double nucleus which is detected at both NIR and radio wavelengths and whose components (N and SW) are separated by 1 arcsec, supporting the hypothesis that this system formed in a recent merger. Condon et al. (1991) reached a similar conclusion on the basis of their radio data, but identified radio components N and SE as the double nucleus. The lack of a NIR counterpart to component SE, however, suggests that it is in fact a background source unrelated to Mrk 273.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 273 as the sample binary, with Seyfert and possibly obscured AGN nuclei, distorted CO kinematics, and symmetric double-peaked HI absorption.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, the CO velocity field of Mrk 273 is very distorted downes98, wilson08 , so it is unlikely that the gas is in well-ordered disk-like rotation. The double absorption in HI seen in Mrk 273 probably corresponds to the binary obscured nuclei in this object.

Mrk 273 This source is the only binary in our sample; its nuclear separation is 750 kpc scoville00 . X-ray observations by iwasawa11 identified its southwestern nucleus as the Seyfert 2 nucleus and suggested that the northern nucleus may host a heavily obscured AGN. The double-peaked HI absorption profile is very symmetric and is likely due to the two obscured nuclei. There appears to be a weak absorption feature in the YY spectrum at --500 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper highlights Mrk 273 as a Seyfert merger candidate, with CLASS and Chandra offsets aligned and prior evidence suggesting a dual AGN system.

quotes.quote: SDSS J134442.15+555313.8 (Mrk 273; UGC 8696; MCG +09-23-004) AGN class: Seyfert, Morphology: pec (NED) Brightest and nearest CLASS detection of 33.6 mJy (CRATES J134442.13+555313.5), offset by 559 mas from the SDSS position. There is a fainter 4.0 mJy detection slightly further away. The AGN is classified as a Seyfert 2 by . The Chandra detection is offset from the optical centre of the galaxy, and in the same direction as the two CLASS detections. It has been suggested U2013 that this galaxy hosts a dual AGN system.
The offset deviates from the fitted Rayleigh distribution with 5-sigma confidence.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 8696 as a highly disturbed post-merger Seyfert 2 with a long tidal tail, possible radio AGN, and two-component radio SED with low-frequency turnover.

quotes.quote: UGC 8696: classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy Koski78, Sanders88 with highly disturbed morphology with a 40 Kpc tidal tail, indicating a post-merger system. The presence of radio AGN from LOFAR imaging was proposed by Kukreti22 . Its radio SED is best modeled with a two-component model characterized by different nonthermal electron populations and turnovers (C2).

The turnover frequencies are 0.16 GHz and 7.51 GHz, respectively. Our radio SED fit model recovers a turnover at low frequency (160 MHz) because of the use of low-frequency data (54 MHz from LOLLS) by us. Clemens10 fits a two-component model with a single turnover at 1 GHz, which is not seen in our analysis.

sources.summary: The paper says UGC 08696 is a complex 8.44 GHz system with a double radio source and jet-like structure, and classifies it as a radio AGN because the spectral index steepens beyond 1.1.

quotes.quote: UGC 08696 At 8.44 GHz we see a complex system. There is a double radio source associated with the IR source at 8 m (map not shown here), which has jet-like structure and has two additional features, the brightest of which is located north-east of the radio centre. The map shows flattening in the centre, following the 8.44-GHz structure.
The values get steeper towards the SE of the centre. Based on the map and the histogram this is a radio-AGN, due to the steepening of > 1.1.

sources.summary: The paper describes MARK 273 as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy with double nucleus, dust lanes, radio counterpart, extended soft X-rays, and absorber-driven spectral variations.

quotes.quote: MARK 273 Also called UGC 8696, this galaxy is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy with a double nucleus that was optically classified as a LINER veilleux1995 , but later re-classified as a type 2 Seyfert from better S/N data kim1998 . Optical spectra are presented in Appendix multiimages , together with an HST image which shows dust lanes. VLBA observations showed a radio counterpart e.g., carilli2000 . Extended emission to the south is observed at soft X-rays, while it shows a point-like source at hard energies (Appendix multiimages ). It was classified as a Compton thick candidate teng2009 .

sources.summary: The paper presents imaging and spectroscopy of Mrk273, finding a 45 kpc ionized halo, nuclear outflows, Seyfert/shock ionization, and tidal debris consistent with a possible triple merger rather than outflowing halo gas.

quotes.quote: We present deep new GTC/OSIRIS narrow-band images and optical WHT/ISIS long-slit spectroscopy of the merging system Mrk273 that show a spectacular extended halo of warm ionised gas out to a radius of 45 kpc from the system nucleus. Outside of the immediate nuclear regions ( > 6 kpc), there is no evidence for kinematic disturbance in the ionised gas: in the extended regions covered by our spectroscopic slits the emission lines are relatively narrow (FWHM 350 km s^ 1 ) and velocity shifts small (| V| 250 km s^ 1 ). This is despite the presence of powerful near-nuclear outflows (FWHM > 1000 km s^ 1 ; | V| > 400 km s^ 1 ; < 6 kpc). Diagnostic ratio plots are fully consistent with Seyfert 2 photo-ionisation to the NE of the nuclear region, however to the SW the plots are more consistent with low-velocity radiative shock models. The kinematics of the ionised gas, combined with the fact that the main structures are aligned with low-surface-brightness tidal continuum features, are consistent with the idea that the ionised halo represents tidal debris left over from a possible triple-merger event, rather than a reservoir of outflowing gas.

sources.summary: The paper presents sensitive EVN and MERLIN radio imaging of the ULIRG Mrk 273, interpreting its compact nuclear components as starburst-related emission rather than a clear AGN.

quotes.quote: We report on 1.6 and 5.0 GHz observations of the ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) Mrk 273, using the European VLBI Network (EVN) and the Multi-Element Radio-Linked Interferometer Network (MERLIN). We also make use of published 1.4 GHz VLBA observations of Mrk 273 by Carilli Taylor (2000) CT00 . Our 5 GHz images have a maximum resolution of 5--10 mas, which corresponds to linear resolutions of 3.5--7 pc at the distance of Mrk 273, and are the most sensitive high-resolution radio observations yet made of this ULIRG.

Mrk 273 is a ULIRG at z=0.0378 ( D 150 Mpc for H_0=70 kms ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ) classified as a Seyfert 2 and/or LINER merging system showing a disturbed morphology on the kpc scale. The presence of a possible AGN in the nuclear environment of Mrk 273 is still elusive: its identification with component N1 is highly unlikely because of its radio spectrum; we also set up a stringent upper limit on the radio emission of an AGN nucleus in component SE.

sources.summary: The paper reports UGC 08696 as an FeK-detected LINER with Suzaku-based Compton-thick classification, but excludes it from confirmed status because its spectrum may vary.

quotes.quote: All together we have positive detection for NGC 0315, NGC 0833, NGC 0835, NGC 1052, UGC 05101, NGC 3690B, NGC 4486, NGC 4579, MRK 0266NE, UGC 08696, NGC 6240, NGC 7130 and NGC 7285. Moreover, recently Teng_2008 have classified UGC 08696 as a Compton-thick source, using Suzaku data above 10 keV. However, we have decided not to use it as Compton-thick source because they have reported changes in the spectral shape that might be due to large changes in the absorbing column density.

In fact, their seven objects in common with our sample (NGC 1052, NGC 3507, NGC 4736, UGC05101, UGC 08696, NGC 6240 and NGC 7130) with deficit of NeV 14  m show a high NH column density or have been classified as Compton-thick sources.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 273 as a local ULIRG with an extreme compact northern starburst and line-intensity ratios consistent with prior molecular observations.

quotes.quote: Mrk 231 Mrk 273 are local ULIRGs. It is suggested that 1/3 the bolometric luminosity of Mrk 231 is contributed by AGN, and 2/3 by starburst in the molecular ring or disk downes98,farrah03 . With respect to Mrk 273, radio observations have revealed an extreme compact starburst toward its northern region downes98,carilli00 . The HCN/HCO ^+ intensity ratios of Mrk 231 and Mrk 273 are larger than unity, whereas HCN/HCO ^+ < 1 in other galaxies. Our results are consistent with gc08 , and the intensity ratios of HCN/HCO ^+ and C _2 H/HCN are all consistent with costagliola11 . At > 1 level, we might tentatively detect HNCO(4-3) in Mrk 231.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 273 as a ULIRG with CO and dense-gas observations, Herschel OH outflow detection, and the first reported H2CO measurement in the galaxy.

quotes.quote: Mrk 273: Imaged in CO J= 3-2 by Wilson2008 . Based on these Wilson2008 measurements Iono2009 derive a deconvolved CO J= 3-2 source size of 0.4^ 0.3^ . GraciaCarpio2008 measured moderately intense HCN and HCO ^+ J= 1-0 and 3-2 , along with CO J= 1-0 emission from this ULIRG. Furthermore, Herschel has detected OH emission due to outflows from this galaxy Fischer2010 .

sources.summary: UGC 08696, identified as Mrk 273, is treated as a late-stage ULIRG merger with a molecular outflow from a likely obscured northern AGN and shocked gas tracing that outflow.

quotes.quote: UGC 08696 UGC 08696, Mrk 273, is another ULIRG in the late-stage merging phases analyzed in detail in our previous work . We have found evidence for a molecular outflow originating from a plausible obscured AGN nucleus in the north (instead of from the X-ray bright AGN nucleus in the southwest). The multiphase and multi-scale nature of its outflow has been detailed in other studies of the warm and cold ionized and molecular gases . Here, we can see that the outflow is traced by shocked gas in the northern nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 273 as one of three ULIRGs with an anomalously high Br/Br line ratio, above case B and not explainable by dust extinction.

quotes.quote: For the remaining three galaxies for which the spectra are shown in Figure fig:anospe , a change in the wavelength range does not affect the line fluxes by more than 5 . We conclude that the three galaxies (shown as blue circles in Figure fig:brflux ), IRAS 10494 + 4424, IRAS 10565 + 2448, and Mrk 273, show Br /Br line ratios of 0.96 0.12, 0.883 0.085, and 1.086 0.053, respectively, all significantly higher than the case B ratio (0.565). These line-ratio anomalies are not explainable with case B theory and dust extinction, which could reduce but not increase the Br /Br line ratio. We show the spectra around the Br and Br lines for these three galaxies in Figure fig:anobr .

sources.summary: The paper reports BeppoSAX observations of MKN 273, finding a lower 2-10 keV flux than ASCA, a marginal iron line, weak PDS detection, and either heavy absorption or fading.

quotes.quote: MKN 273: MKN 273 was observed with BeppoSAX in 1998 and was detected by the MECS (1.65-10 keV) at a 2-10 keV flux of 3.5 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , significantly lower than the flux measured by ASCA 2 years before (F _ 2-10 =7 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , Iwasawa 1998). Fitting the data with a powerlaw and an iron line we obtain a spectral index ( =1.1_ 0.6 ^ +0.9 , poorly constrained due to the weakness of the continuum, and a marginal detection of the iron line, at a 3 level (best fit equivalent width EW=1.2 _ 1 ^ +2 keV). The detection in the PDS is also weak. We conclude that either the AGN at the centre of this source is absorbed by a column density N_H > 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 or, alternatively, that the central source has faded and only the reflected component is now visible (this would also explain the flux decrease between the ASCA and SAX observations).

sources.summary: Mrk 273 is discussed as a ULIRG with possible compact radio supernova activity, extended OH maser emission in a circumnuclear disk, and X-ray/Fe-line AGN indicators.

quotes.quote: Subsequent VLBI imaging dramatically revealed a cluster of luminous RSN in the nearest ULIRG, Arp 220 smi1 -- clear evidence for a high star formation rate, estimated at that time as 100 M_ , yr ^ 1 by smi0 , which would be enough to power the infrared luminosity by star formation without resorting to an obscured AGN. momj,neff,bon have reported possible "supernova factories" in IRAS 17208-0014, Mrk 299 and Mrk 273. respectively.
On the other hand Mrk 231 lon2003,klo , Mrk 273 klo2 and 14070+0525 pil show more extended emission in a circumnuclear disk with conditions more like the classical maser model.

5 galaxies in the Chandra samples which are classed as AGN (including IRAS 05189-2524, Mrk 231, Mrk 273) show order of magnitude greater X-ray luminosities than the Starburst ULIRGs (UGC 5101, IRAS 17208-0014, IRAS 20551-4250, IRAS 23128-5919) with NGC 6240 being an intermediate case Mrk 231, IRAS 17208-0014 and IRAS 23128-5919 are included in both the pta03 and fra03 samples . As already noted UGC 5101 and Mrk231 are believed to harbor obscured AGN based on their Spitzer spectra. Mrk 273, NGC 6240 and UGC 5101 exhibit Fe K-line emission, but sufficiently weak that the authors argue against X-rays reflected from a Compton-thick ( N_H > 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) absorber as the origin of the observed X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper notes that Mrk 273 has high velocity dispersion, two nuclei separated by 0.8 kpc, and insufficient total mass to be classified as a giant elliptical.

quotes.quote: However, one source with high velocity dispersion, Mrk 273, has been observed in our sample (see also hinz ). Even for Mrk 273, the total mass of the system (which also depends on its effective radius) is insufficient to classify it as a giant elliptical (of stellar mass >5 10^ 11 ).
Mrk 273: This system has two nuclei separated by 0.8 kpc ( sco00 ). find a stellar velocity dispersion of 232 43 \ for the north component of this source.

sources.summary: Mrk 273 has ambiguous Gaussian fitting, is not well described by two components, and shows an irregular non-Gaussian [C II] line profile.

quotes.quote: The difference can be very large in some cases: F05189-2524 and Mrk 273 are best fitted either with a second narrow Gaussian or with a broad component, while 07251-0248, 13120-5453, F14378-3651 and F22491-1808 can be fitted either with or without a broad component.
Another complication is that NGC 6240, Mrk 273 and UGC 5101 are not well described by a combination of 2 Gaussians.
The [ CII ] line profile of Mrk 273 is irregular with a non-Gaussian shape.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 273 as a ULIRG whose steep 3-24 micron spectrum implies a large AGN contribution, estimated above earlier literature values.

quotes.quote: Mrk 273 : Mrk 273 is a ULIRG. The steep 3--24 \ spectrum implies a large AGN contribution that CIGALE estimates at 66 of the IR luminosity. This is higher than the value given by ros15 by around a factor of 2.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 273 as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy with two nuclei, extended X-ray emission, and a nearby X-ray companion separable in ROSAT and ASCA data.

quotes.quote: Mrk 273 and its companion, Mrk 273X, separated by about 2 , are clearly resolved by the HRI and PSPC (Figure fig:m273x4 ).
Mrk 273 Mrk 273 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy and contains two nuclei.
The HRI data suggest that the X-ray emission from Mrk 273 is extended, not confined to a point source, with R_ kpc = 18 .

sources.summary: Mrk 273 is used as a composite spectrum example containing direct and scattered AGN components plus soft starburst emission, with low-abundance models altering its components.

quotes.quote: The observed distribution in spectral types can be produced by varying contributions of an AGN and a starburst, including some examples that contain both the direct and scattered AGN components in addition to the soft X-ray starburst (e.g., Mrk 273).
Varying degrees of absorption, scattering, and relative strength of the thermal component (e.g., Mrk 273), account for most variations of the observed spectra.
The effects are strongest in Mrk 273, Mrk 463, and NGC 6221, in which the thermal fraction increases significantly and the scattered fraction also decreases measurably.

gmw_00001941.name: MACS0647-JD
gmw_00001941.overview: MACS0647-JD is a strongly lensed, star-forming galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift of approximately z = 10.17 [arXiv:2404.16200, arXiv:2306.00647]. High-resolution imaging reveals that the system is a galaxy merger candidate consisting of multiple stellar components [arXiv:2404.16200, arXiv:2306.00647] with a large neutral gas reservoir [arXiv:2306.00647].

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST MIRI observations of the z=10.165 lensed galaxy merger MACS0647--JD, detecting rest-frame optical lines and deriving metallicity, star-formation rate, and ionizing-photon production efficiency.

quotes.quote: Here we present MIRI MRS IFU observations of the lensed galaxy merger MACS0647--JD at z = 10.165 . This paper presents new MIRI Medium Resolution Spectrograph (MRS, Wells2015,Argyriou2023 ) and Mid-Infrared Imager Module (MIRIM, Bouchet2015,Dicken+24 ) observations of MACS0647--JD.

The strongly lensed galaxy MACS0647 JD was discovered in HST imaging Coe2013 . Three lensed images JD1, JD2, and JD3 are observed with JWST NIRCam F200W AB mag 25.0, 25.4, and 26.2, respectively, for a total AB mag 24.2, making it the brightest z > 10 galaxy known Hsiao2023a . Hsiao2023a reported that NIRCam imaging resolved MACS0647 JD as having two small stellar complexes A (JDA) and B (JDB) in a possible galaxy merger.

SFR and Given this nearly unprecedented measurement of \ at z > 10 see also Zavala2024 , we can directly measure the recent star formation rate (SFR) and ionizing photon production efficiency \ of MACS0647--JD. In this letter, we report MIRI spectroscopic observation of the triply-lensed galaxy merger MACS0647 JD at z=10.165 .

sources.summary: The paper supports MACS0647-JD as a multiply lensed very high-redshift galaxy, deriving a geometric redshift near z=10.8 and ruling out low-redshift solutions.

quotes.quote: A multiply-lensed galaxy, MACS0647-JD, with a probable photometric redshift of z 10.7^ +0.6 _ 0.4 is claimed to constitute one of the very earliest known galaxies, formed well before reionization was completed. However, spectral evidence that MACS0647-JD lies at high redshift has proven infeasible and so here we seek an independent lensing based ``geometric redshift'' derived from the angles between the three lensed images of MACS0647-JD, using our free-form mass model (WSLAP+) for the lensing cluster MACSJ0647.7+7015 (at z=0.591 ). Our lens model uses the 9 sets of multiple images, including those of MACS0647-JD, identified by the CLASH survey towards this cluster.

We convincingly exclude the low redshift regime of z<3 , for which convoluted critical curves are generated by our method, as the solution bends to accommodate the wide angles of MACS0647-JD for this low redshift. Instead, a best fit to all sets of lensed galaxy positions and redshifts provides a geometric redshift of z 10.8^ +0.3 _ 0.4 for MACS0647-JD, strongly supporting the higher photometric redshift solution.

After correcting for its magnification the luminosity of MACS0647-JD remains relatively high at M_ UV =-19.4 , which is within a factor of a few in flux of some surprisingly luminous z 10 - 11 candidates discovered recently in Hubble blank field surveys.

sources.summary: The paper describes MACS0647-JD as a triply imaged z~10.7 galaxy whose JWST imaging supports its high redshift and resolves multiple clumps.

quotes.quote: One of these candidates is MACS0647-JD 2013ApJ...762...32C , a record-breaking, triply imaged galaxy at z 10.7 , which was first detected in HST imaging of the MACSJ 0647.7+7015 (hereafter MACS0647; z=0.591 , 2007ApJ...661L..33E ) galaxy cluster under the Cluster Lensing and Supernova survey with Hubble (CLASH) https://www.stsci.edu/ postman/CLASH/ program 2012ApJS..199...25P . To study MACS0647-JD in more detail, the JWST general observer (GO) program 1433 (PI: Dan Coe) targeted the cluster and obtained JWST /NIRCam imaging in six different filters. The JWST imaging supported the high-redshift nature at z_ phot =10.6 with very high-confidence, and revealed that MACS0647-JD is actually made of two components with distance 400 pc, and a possible third clump about 3 kpc away 2022arXiv221014123H .

In 2013ApJ...762...32C seven new multiple-image systems were identified in the CLASH observations, including MACS0647-JD at z 10.6 . Based on the previous and newly identified systems 2013ApJ...762...32C refined the previous LTM lens model and presented two additional lens models using Lenstool parameteric; 1993A&A...273..367K,2007NJPh....9..447J and LensPerfect non-parametric; 2008ApJ...681..814C .

sources.summary: The paper uses MACS0647-JD as a z=10.8 lensed galaxy with magnification 8, F160W magnitude 28.2, UV luminosity, and estimated stellar and halo masses to constrain fuzzy dark matter.

quotes.quote: report the observation of another galaxy at redshift 10.8 with a magnification of 8 (MACS0647-JD). estimate intrinsic (unlensed) magnitude of MACS0647-JD to be 28.2 in F160W band and calculate the rest frame UV luminosity to be L_ UV 2.8 10^ 28 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 . They conclude that the stellar mass of galaxy is most likely 10^8 - 10^9 M_ and expect the dark halo mass to be e10 M_ . We do not use the mass of the galaxy for constraining the axion mass.

sources.summary: The paper presents MACS0647-JD as a triply lensed merger candidate whose NIRSpec spectrum indicates a z=10.17 redshift from break and emission lines.

quotes.quote: In the bottom panel of Figure fig_spec1 , we present the spectrum of MACS0647-JD, which was firstly photometrically reported in 2013ApJ...762...32C . This galaxy is a triply-lensed galaxy, and a recent study using NIRCam images suggests that MACS0647-JD is a merger. We show the NIRSpec spectrum of JD1 (MSA ID: 3593) in the observation ID of 23. The spectrum shows the Lyman break and the Ciii ] 1909, [ Ne iii ] 3869, and H emission lines, suggesting the spectroscopic redshift of z_ spec =10.17 .

sources.summary: The paper studies gravitationally lensed MACS0647-JD at z=10.170, finding a star-forming system with multiple components, an extreme DLA column, young stellar population, and large neutral gas reservoir.

quotes.quote: The third source, MACS0647-JD, is gravitationally-lensed by the foreground galaxy cluster MACS J0647+7015 and was discovered as part of the Cluster Lensing And Supernova survey with Hubble (CLASH) . JWST/NIRCam and NIRSpec prism observations (observation program GO 1433, PI: D. Coe) show a system with multiple intrinsic components (A and B) which are then multiply-imaged , and the galaxy spectrum covering mainly component A shows a star-forming system at z=10.17 based on the identification of seven emission lines .

The reduced and photometrically-calibrated 1D spectra of the three galaxies at z=9-11 are shown in Fig. 1. We determine the redshift of CEERS-43833 to be z=8.7622 0.0002 from the multitude of nebular emission lines detected in the spectrum (see Supplementary Materials, Fig. S2), consistent with the first analysis of this spectrum presented in . Similarly, we determine z=11.409 0.001 for CEERS-16943 based on the detection of [ ] 3727 , [ Ne iii ] 3870 , and He i 3889 . This is the most distant detection of nebular emission lines to date. For MACS0647-JD we derive z=10.170 0.003 , consistent with the previous spectroscopic analysis of this galaxy .

We derive best-fit models with (N_ H i / cm^ 2 )= 22.10 0.20 for CEERS-43833, (N_ H i / cm^ 2 )= 22.20 0.40 for CEERS-16943, and (N_ H i / cm^ 2 )= 22.40 0.15 for MACS0647-JD. The uncertainties represent the 16th and 84th percentiles of the posterior distributions and includes the systematic uncertainty of 0.1 dex due to variations in the intrinsic galaxy continuum emission templates.

gmw_00001942.name: NGC 5850
gmw_00001942.overview: NGC 5850 is a double-barred early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1202.0441, arXiv:1305.1802] hosting a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1202.0441, arXiv:1305.1802] and an inner polar gaseous disk [arXiv:1202.0441, arXiv:1401.3366]. Its distorted spiral arms and shifted neutral hydrogen suggest a recent tidal encounter with the nearby galaxy NGC 5846 [arXiv:1202.0441, arXiv:1108.5229].

sources.summary: The paper presents VIMOS spectroscopy of NGC 5850, describing it as a double-barred early-type spiral with a polar gaseous disk, distorted spiral arm, LINER-like nucleus, and bar-related fuelling context.

quotes.quote: NGC 5850 is a prototype double-barred early-type spiral SB() b galaxy. It is located at a distance of 34.2 Mpc and hosts a polar gaseous disk . Its two spiral arms form ring-like structure, almost resembling the morphology of the center, where the inner bar is surrounded by a (pseudo-) ring. The north-western spiral arm is distorted probably due to a recent ( < 200 Myr) high velocity encounter with NGC 5846 , which is 10' to the north-west of NGC 5850.

The low X-ray luminosity L_X(0.5-3 \ keV )=10^ 40.36 \ erg \ s^ 1 suggests NGC 5850 to be in a state of low activity. The high luminosity of [ NII ] lines at the center classifies it as a Low Ionisation Nuclear Emission Region (LINER) . Proposed by and simulated by , nested bars can be a mechanism to fuel AGN. Its morphology and proximity make NGC 5850 an ideal object to study fuelling and feedback processes.

sources.summary: NGC 5850 is highlighted for abundant central molecular gas, an off-centered molecular peak, and differing H and [OIII] gas distributions within the double-barred galaxy sample.

quotes.quote: The sample is composed of four double-barred galaxies: NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 4725, and NGC 5850. These galaxies were selected from the catalogue of Erwin2004 , paying special attention to the length and size of the inner bars. The main requirement for building the sample was that the inner bars were big enough to be well mapped within the \ field-of-view (hereafter FoV), but they were also small enough to allow the mapping of the transition regions between the two bars.

Particularly interesting is the case of NGC 5850, for which a very large amount of molecular gas is found in the central region, possibly driven there through the main bar PetitpasandWilson2004 . Moreover, high resolution interferometric maps of NGC 5850 obtained by Leonetal2000 reveal that most of this central molecular gas is concentrated in a single peak, which is slightly off-centred towards the North.

H vs. [ OIII ] 5007 gas distributions in NGC 5850 NGC 5850 is the only galaxy for which the gas distributions derived from the [ OIII ] 5007 and H emission lines are different. The corresponding maps are shown in Figure fig:gas5850 . Whereas the [ OIII ] 5007 distribution matches well with the stellar intensity distribution, the H map shows the maximum off-centred with respect to the stellar isophotes.

sources.summary: The paper presents VIMOS IFU observations of the LINER galaxy NGC 5850, describing its double-barred spiral structure, kinematically decoupled core, extended LINER-like gas, and low-level star formation.

quotes.quote: Here, we present the results of the analysis of the VIMOS IFU observations of the central 3  kpc of the LINER galaxy NGC 5850. We examine present ionization mechanisms and look for signs of AGN feeding and feedback. NGC 5850 is a nearby z=0.0085 prototype double-barred early-type spiral galaxy (e.g. ), with a position angle of 140  degrees and inclined by i 37 degrees . The large scale (primary) and the inner (secondary) bar differ in their P.A. by 67 degrees, with P.A._ primary 116.3 degrees and P.A._ secondary 49.3 degrees .

The LOSV map of the gas shows a large kinematically decoupled core (KDC). The spiral structure within the KDC is strongly twisted. This might have its origin in the lopsidedness gas distribution on galactic scale and within the KDC . In the center we find the s-shaped signature introduced by the gaseous secondary bar.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5850 as a double-barred LINER galaxy whose central kiloparsecs show extended LINER-like emission, complex gas kinematics, and likely distributed stellar ionization.

quotes.quote: Using optical spectroscopy, we examine the possible ionization mechanisms responsible for the extended LINER-like emission in the central 4 kpc of NGC 5850. We performed integral field spectroscopic observations using VIMOS at the VLT, which provides spatially-resolved spectra for the gas emission and the stellar continuum. We subtract the underlying stellar continuum from the galaxy spectra and fit the emission lines. With these methods, we derive and analyze emission line and kinematic maps. Emission line ratio maps are examined by means of diagnostic diagrams. The central few kpc of NGC 5850 are dominated by extended LINER-like emission.

The galaxy NGC 5850 is a prototype double-barred early-type spiral galaxy (SBb(sr) I-II) Buta_5850, Prieto, Friedli_5850 . It is located at a distance of 34.2 Mpc Wozniak95 . Its two spiral arms form a ring-like structure, almost resembling the morphology of the center, where the inner bar (projected half-length of 6 2 ) is surrounded by a (pseudo-) ring.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5850 as a benchmark double-barred galaxy, confirming its inner bar photometrically and spectroscopically, finding inner-disc/hollow signatures, and dating inner-bar assembly to over 4.5 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: NGC 1291 and NGC 5850 are undoubtedly double-barred hosts. NGC 1291 is a closeby (distance of 8.6 Mpc) and almost face-on ( i 11 ^ ) galaxy, whose inner bar is perfectly observed in direct images without the need for any further analysis (e.g. ellipse fitting or unsharp masking). NGC 5850 (distance 23.1 Mpc; i 39 ^ ) belongs to the sample of double-barred galaxies analysed by deLorenzoCaceresetal2008, deLorenzoCaceresetal2013 , who confirmed the presence of the inner bar not only photometrically but also spectroscopically through the kinematic diagnostics of the hollows (see Sect. sec:kin ). Butaetal2015 classified these two galaxias as (R)SAB(l,bl,nb)0 ^ + and (R ' )SB(,bl,nr,nb) a b, respectively. NGC 1291 and NGC 5850 are used throughout this paper as benchmarks to study the properties of double-barred galaxies with the superb spatial resolution provided by MUSE.

Our analysis confirms the presence of hollows appearing in the stellar velocity dispersion distribution at the ends of the inner bars. Both galaxies host inner discs matching in size with the inner bars, suggestive of a dynamical formation for the inner bars from small-scale discs. The analysis of the star formation histories for the structural components shaping the galaxies provides constraints on the epoch of dynamical assembly of the inner bars, which took place > 6.5 Gyr ago for NGC 1291 and > 4.5 Gyr ago for NGC 5850.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5850 as kinematically peculiar, with ionized gas deviating strongly from the stellar dynamical axis and evidence for a polar gaseous disk after collision with NGC 5846.

quotes.quote: The dynamical axis of the stars in the circumnuclear region of NGC 5850 is close to the line of nodes, while in the ionized gas, this axis deviates from it by more than 50-60^ (Fig. 5c) and is almost aligned with the position angle of the central isophotes. This behavior is typical of a disk inclined to the galactic plane (Moiseev and Mustsevo, 2000). In addition, if we, nevertheless, assume the gas motions to take place in the galactic plane, then it will turn out that they correspond to a radial outflow from the nucleus Here, we use the suggestion made by Higdon et al. (1998) that the disk orientation of the galaxy for which its western half is closest to the observer is most probable. with velocities 50-70 . Such features are characteristic of Seyfert galaxies, but the optical spectrum of the galaxy contains no emission lines indicative of an active nucleus, nor are staburst observed here (Higdon et al., 1998). A more reasonable assumption is that the gas moves at < 6-7'' in a plane polar to the galactic disk.

The hypothesis of a polar disk is also supported by the fact that, according to Higdon et al., (1998), NGC 5850 has undergone a recent collision with the nearby galaxy NGC 5846. Through their interaction, part of the gas could be transported to polar orbits.

sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 5850 contains ionized gas rotating in a polar plane, likely related to a recent collision with NGC 5846 that transported gas onto polar orbits.

quotes.quote: Since the inner isophotes have a sharp turn at <10'' , Friedli et al. ( ) have assumed the existence of a secondary bar in this galaxy. The MPFS data show that of the stellar rotation coincides with PA_0 , while in the ionized gas , in the circumnuclear region, differs by more than 50-60^ from the line of nodes position angle (Fig. 5850m ). Note that the ionized gas almost aligns with the PA of the inner isophotes. This radial behavior of is typical for a disk inclined to the galactic plane.

A more reasonable assumption is that the gas, at < 6-7'' , rotates in a polar plane with respect to the global galactic disk. In this case, the polar gaseous disk lies roughly in the smallest principal-plane cross-section of the outer bar, orthogonal to the major axis of the bar, as in NGC 3368 (Sect. sec_3368 ). The hypothesis of a polar disk is supported by the fact that NGC 5850 has undergone a recent collision with the nearby galaxy NGC 5846 (Higdon et al. 1998). Through their interaction, part of the gas could be transported to polar orbits.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5850 as a barred spiral with inner ionized-gas polar kinematics, outer gas aligned with the stars, and evidence for a strongly inclined inner disk.

quotes.quote: An inner ionized-gas polar disk was found in a barred spiral, SB()b, galaxy NGC 5850 by moiseev04 ; the stellar and gaseous rotations were compared over the 16 16 field of view of the 6-m telescope IFU MPFS. Now we have calculated larger stellar and gaseous velocity fields by using the archival SAURON data (Fig. n5850 ). One can immediately see from Fig. n5850 that the sense of the gas rotation changes at the radius of 7 -10 \ (1.3--1.8 kpc); the more outer ionized gas rotates together with the stars. And the same orientation of the rotation plane is demonstrated by all the 2 10^9 solar masses of neutral hydrogen measured in NGC 5850 by higdon .

Bremer2013 claimed that the angle between the inner and outer disks planes is only 24 , however the equation (1) from mois_rev gives also the second solution -- 54 , that corresponds to the case of strongly inclined inner gaseous disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5850 as a shared comparison object: its inner-bar/disc measurement follows the outer-bar relation, but its short, older inner bar may be non-assembled.

quotes.quote: For the one galaxy in common with that work, NGC 5850, we have overplotted the a_ IB /R_ e ID measurement in the right panel of Fig. fig:barlengthvsell . It shows a good agreement with the bar length vs. ellipticity correlation of outer bars.
In particular, the inner bar in NGC 5850 belongs to the short-length group. This structure was formed > 4.5 Gyr ago as demonstrated in deLorenzoCaceresetal2019b .
Within our proposed scenario, NGC 5850 would host a non-assembled inner bar.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5850 as a clear evolved-bar case, using radial velocity-dispersion profiles and elevated bar-disk dispersion differences as evidence for vertical bar growth.

quotes.quote: Clear instances of evolved bars are the ones in NGC 1302, NGC 1317 and NGC 5850. Their _z radial profiles resemble Fig(s). 5(b) and 6(d). These galaxies have _ z, bar equal to 100, 145 and 60 Km/s. Although the latter value is clearly much lower, this is a result from the fact that NGC 5850 is of a later morphological class than the former galaxies. In fact, _z 30 Km/s in the three cases, an evidence that these bars have vertically grown up from their parent disks.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5850 inner-ring structure, considers interaction with NGC 5846, and compares alternative corotation estimates including earlier hydrodynamical work.

quotes.quote: NGC 5850 : the inner ring of this SBb ( T=3 ) galaxy is reproduced with a model giving R 1.4 or R_ CR 105 12 (see Fig. modsel1 ). The outer structure of this galaxy is not regular, possibly due to interaction with NGC 5846 higdon98 . A hydrodynamical model for this galaxy has been published by aguerri2001 , giving R_ CR 90 . We found that an alternative model, giving R_ CR 82 6 or R 1.1 is also possible when matching the size of the inner ring, but then the shape is not correct.

sources.summary: The paper presents integral-field kinematics for NGC 5850, finding inner-bar velocity-dispersion hollows explained as contrast between the hot bulge and the colder, more ordered inner bar.

quotes.quote: We present \ integral-field stellar velocity and velocity dispersion maps for four double-barred early-type galaxies: NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 4725 and NGC 5850. The presence of the inner bar does not produce major changes in the line-of-sight velocity, but it appears to have an important effect in the stellar velocity dispersion maps: we find two hollows of amplitudes between 10 and 40 km s ^ 1 on either side of the center, at the ends of the inner bars. We have performed numerical simulations to explain these features. Ruling out other possibilities, we conclude that the hollows are an effect of the contrast between two kinematically different components: the high velocity dispersion of the bulge and the more ordered motion (low velocity dispersion) of the inner bar.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5850 as a disturbed H I-rich barred spiral whose shifted gas and distorted arms likely record a recent tidal encounter that perturbed NGC 5846.

quotes.quote: These may be residual gas `sloshing' from a previous galaxy merger or from a high velocity galaxy encounter with the large, HI rich, barred spiral galaxy NGC 5850, located 10 3 ( 71.5 kpc in projection) to the east (see Fig. fig:n5846dss ). The large shift of HI gas to the west and northwest in NGC 5850, in the direction of NGC 5846, and NGC 5850's distorted stellar arms and spurs, also to the west, suggest a recent ( 200 Myr) tidal interaction between the two galaxies (Higdon 1998).

This is consistent with the identification of the perturber as the disturbed spiral galaxy NGC 5850, whose relative radial velocity with respect to NGC 5846 is large ( 770 ). In this scenario, NGC 5850 would have approached in front of NGC 5846 from the west, measured relative to the elliptical galaxy, and is now receding behind NGC 5846 to the east.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5850 as a barred galaxy whose inner ionized gas likely rotates in a polar plane orthogonal to the bar's major axis.

quotes.quote: In the barred galaxy NGC 5850 the direction of rotation measured from the stellar component coincides with the line of nodes of the disk whereas in the ionized gas it differs by more than 50^ and coincides with the position angle of the central isophotes. Such a behavior is typical for a disk inclined to the galactic plane. A more reasonable assumption is that the gas, at <1 kpc, rotates in a polar plane with respect to the global galactic disk . In this case, the polar gaseous disk lies orthogonal to the major axis of the bar.

sources.summary: The paper uses HIPEQ1507+01 as a fiducial optically selected galaxy, describing it as optically luminous, relatively H I poor for its light, early type, red, and dynamically massive.

quotes.quote: As a fiducial comparison one can pick say HIPEQ1507+01 (NGC5850) which is typical of the kind of galaxy that turned up in optically selected catalogues and which were afterwards examined at 21-cm (e.g. Huchtmeier 1988). It is optically very luminous, 1.7 ^ m brighter than L _ * , has a low M _ H I /L _ B = 0.15 but nevertheless contains a significant amount of (log M _ H I = 9.8) simply because of its high luminosity. It is early type (T=3), red [ ( g- ) = 0.63 ] and dynamically very massive ( log M _ dyn = 11.7 M _ ) where M_ dyn =R_ 50 (g) (W_ 20 / sin(i))^ 2 / G .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5850 as an example of a barred galaxy with star formation near bar ends and an active inner ring, while its bar otherwise lacks star formation except for a central peak.

quotes.quote: Often there are regions of SF near the ends of the bar, and these can form parts of inner rings, as in NGC 5850 (Fig. Fig_classB , lower panel), or highlight the start of grand-design spiral arms, as in NGC 1300 (Fig. Fig_classB , upper panel).
Many bars are devoid of SF, as in the case of NGC 5850 (Fig. Fig_classB , lower panel), showing only a central SF peak.
An example of a galaxy with an active inner ring (NGC 5850) is shown in Fig. Fig_classB .

sources.summary: NGC 5850 is presented as a massive barred galaxy with low SFE and SFR, a closed inner ring, and perturbation from NGC 5846.

quotes.quote: In particular, the two galaxies in our sample with the lowest SFE and _ SFR (NGC 4548 and NGC 5850, SFE 0.25 Gyr ^ 1 , _ SFR 10^ 2.25 M_ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 , M_ 10^ 10.7 M_ ) are also those hosting massive bulges and signs of past interactions with nearby companions.
Of these, the majority have inner pseudo-rings (i.e. made of tightly wrapped spiral arms) and only NGC 5850 harbours a closed inner ring.
NGC 5850 is perturbed, as shown by 1998AJ....115...80H , due to the encounter with the nearby massive elliptical NGC 5846.

gmw_00001943.name: Orion dwarf galaxy
gmw_00001943.overview: The Orion dwarf galaxy is a nearby dwarf system located at an estimated distance of 5.4 Mpc [arXiv:1206.0314]. It features an extended, gas-dominated baryonic disk [arXiv:1206.0314, arXiv:1405.6314] and a pure exponential stellar disk with no stellar bulge [arXiv:1206.0314, arXiv:1405.6314].

sources.summary: The paper models the Orion dwarf galaxy rotation curve and mass distribution, finding a cored dark-matter profile, a useful nearby test case, and possible tension with canonical MOND under the adopted assumptions.

quotes.quote: The present investigation examines the DM content of the Orion dwarf galaxy. This nearby system harbors an extended \ disk, and thus provides us with an important test of the above paradigm. As we show below, the Orion dwarf is one of the few known galaxies whose kinematics unambiguously point towards a cored profile. This system is thus critically important for investigating the nature of the DM particle and of the evolution of DM halos.

For the Orion dwarf, 21-cm \ surface brightness and kinematics have recently been published Cannon : their analysis provides a high quality, high resolution RC, that, in addition, can be easily corrected for asymmetric drift and tested for non-circular motions. This galaxy is a very useful laboratory in that a simple inspection of the RC ensures us that it shows a large mass discrepancy at all radii. Moreover, the baryonic components are efficiently modeled (i.e., no stellar bulge is evident and the stellar disk shows a well-behaved exponential profile, see vaduvescu ). The distance to the galaxy, which is critical for an unambiguous test of MOND sanders02 , is estimated to be 5.4 1.0 Mpc vaduvescu .

sources.summary: The paper treats Orion as a small dwarf galaxy with an HI-dominated baryonic disk and a precise rotation curve that R^n gravity fits without a dark halo.

quotes.quote: Orion is a dwarf galaxy of luminous mass 1 100 the Milky Way stellar disk mass with a baryonic distribution dominated by a HI disk, whose surface density is accurately measured and, noticeably, found to have some distinct feature. The stellar disk, on the other side, is a pure exponential disk. The available rotation curve is extended and it is of very high resolution. Noticeably, this is one of the smallest galaxies for which we have a very accurate profile of the gravitating mass.

The resulting disk masses are (3.7 0.8) 10^ 8 M_ and (3.4 0.8) 10^ 10 M_ respectively for Orion and NGC3198. The other parameters are: Orion r_c=(0.013 0.002) \ kpc and gas fraction= (55 20) , NGC3198 r_c=(0.4 0.05) \ kpc and gas fraction= (29 10) .

gmw_00001944.name: NGC 1344
gmw_00001944.overview: NGC 1344 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1002.0845, arXiv:1712.05833] classified as either an elliptical [arXiv:1606.06815, arXiv:1712.05833] or S0 galaxy [arXiv:0710.5529, arXiv:2201.06704] located in the outskirts of the Fornax cluster [arXiv:2201.06704, arXiv:astro-ph/0606385]. It features concentric outer shells [arXiv:0710.5529, arXiv:1712.05833] and central patchy dust [arXiv:0710.5529], which suggest a relatively recent merger or dynamical interaction [arXiv:astro-ph/0703588, arXiv:0710.5529].

sources.summary: NGC 1344 is described as a type I shell galaxy whose inner shell colors and low shell count suggest dust effects and a relatively recent minor merger.

quotes.quote: NGC 1344 The shells in this galaxy are supposed to be the result of phase wrapping, since NGC 1344 is a type I shell galaxy. The colour of one outer shell of this type I shell galaxy was determined by carter82 . This shell appears to be somewhat bluer than the main body of the galaxy. In our GALPHOT residual data the innermost shell is visible at about 27'' North from the centre. For the first time, we determined positions and the colours of some inner shells for this galaxy.

The V-I colours of the Northern shells are redder than the galaxy while they are similar or slightly bluer than the galaxy on the South side. The redder colours on the North side are probably due to dust, since these shells are much nearer towards the centre where dust is more present. The blue colour of one of the Southern shells is at least consistent with the earlier finding of Carter et al. The low number of shells and blue colour of some of these may be evidence for a relatively recent merger event.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1344 as an S0 Fornax galaxy with young central stellar-population signatures, central patchy dust, outer shells, and evidence for a recent interaction or merger.

quotes.quote: When compared to simple stellar population models, three galaxies (NGC 1316, NGC 1344 and NGC 1375) appear to have a significant contribution from young stars resulting in luminosity weighted ages between 1 and 3 Gyrs (see Figure fig:fornax_pops ).

Three galaxies (NGC 1316, NGC 1344, and NGC 1380 -- see details below) have clear central dust features. The other galaxies in our sample have no visible dust feature within the investigated aperture. A few additional object specific remarks seem warranted: NGC 1316 - Fornax A, S0(3)pec. This galaxy has large scale, irregular, central dust features (based on inspection of HST-ACS images). NGC 1344 - this S0 galaxy contains morphological shells in its outer regions mal80 as well as optical spectral signatures of a younger central population (e.g. Kuntschner et al. 2002).
