Inspection of HST-ACS images reveals patchy dust within the central 5 arcsec. In combination, these characteristics suggest (1 -- 2 Gyr) dynamical interaction or merger event that triggered a central star formation episode.

sources.summary: NGC 1344 is included in the seven-galaxy elliptical sample traced to large radii, with two-parameter MOND fits and low dark-model mass-to-light ratios discussed.

quotes.quote: Thus we have 7 elliptical galaxies in our sample: NGC 821, NGC 1344, NGC 3379, NGC 4374, NGC 4494, NGC 4697, and NGC 5846.

There are 5 galaxies in our sample suitable for the analysis (NGC 3379, NGC 4374, NGC 5846, NGC 1344 and NGC 4494). NGC 4697 and NGC 821 are not included because of the lack of stellar data in the inner region close to the center. We fit the data by reduced square and Figure fig:two shows the result of the 5 galaxies.
In Table tab:masstolight , one notices that the mass-to-light ratios of NGC 3379, NGC 4374, NGC 1344 and NGC 4494 are too small for common dark matter model.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1344 as a shell-bearing S0-like Fornax-outskirts galaxy whose GC residual map is asymmetric, with multiple large eastern structures and one western structure.

quotes.quote: NGC1344, located in the N, very low-density outskirt of the Fornax cluster and sometimes classified as a S0 galaxy, is known for low surface brightness features resembling thin shells malin1980 , that are considered the remnants of relatively recent galactic mergers. studied, among other shell galaxies, the NGC1344 GCS using large field-of-view, ground based images and found that the overall NGC1344 GC population follows closely the photometric geometry of the host, but a significant fraction of the red GC subpopulation is also present at large galactocentric radii where, typically, blue GCs are more likely observed. The residual map generated by all GCs observed in NGC1344 (Figure fig:ngc1344 ) features an excess of GCs in the E side of the galaxy relative to W side (upper left panel); this asymmetry is also evident in the residual maps for all GCs, where three large structures (F1, F2 and F4) are detected in the E half of the galaxy, while only one structure (F3) is located in the W section. F1 and F2, both visible in the red and blue GC residual maps, have elongated shapes and are located along radial directions spanning a large range of galactocentric distances. The roughly circular, large structure F4 lies outside of the D _ 25 isophote and is not detected in the red map.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1344 as a shell early-type galaxy near Fornax with marginal red globular clusters, significant rotation, and a single-blue-peaked cluster distribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 1344 - located at the outskirts of the Fornax Cluster, 4.9^ from the central cluster galaxy NGC 1399. At this distance the density is comparable to the field galaxy density ( ). Sometimes classified as S0; shows rotation within 2 effective radii ( )

The colour distributions of NGC 474 and NGC 1344 (Figure VIplot ) are similar, and appear either to be unimodal, or to have a very low red (metal-rich) component. Although KMM returns a combination of a blue and red peak as a good description of the distribution, there are very few GCs in the red peak.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1344 as a Z 0.01, older-than-5-Gyr stellar system with an age-driven radial SBF-color gradient and possible merger-related morphology.

quotes.quote: NGC 1344 -- Models to data comparison seems to point out a Z 0.01 , t > 5 Gyr stellar system. Also, for this galaxy all the models predict that the observed trend of SBF and color could be explained by an age gradient along the radius, with the inner regions being older than the outer ones. In fact, for this galaxy we find m _I / (V-I)_0 =2.8 0.3 , and all models predict M _I / (V-I)_0 3.5 in this metallicity regime. As in the case of NGC 1316, this galaxy shows indications of a recent merger activity carter82 which could possibly be related to the observed age gradient.

sources.summary: The paper reports bad axis-2 fits for NGC 1344, an acceptable axis-1 fit at F_d about 6, and a dark-halo mass consistent with its baryon-fraction estimate.

quotes.quote: NGC 1344 Fits to data from axis 2 are bad mainly because of the flat plateau in velocity dispersion in the inner region. Axis 1 gives an acceptable fit for F_ d 6, with a significant improvement over the stars-only model. According to teodorescu2005 , there is a 3.8 10^ 11 M _ dark matter halo out to 3.5 R_ e . A rough estimate of the baryon fraction -- assuming 90 of the stellar mass is contained within 3.5 R_ e grah05 - gives a baryon fraction within this region of 0.29, in agreement with our estimates ( f_B 0.3 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 1344 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This galaxy, also known as NGC 1340, is an E5 elliptical belonging to the Fornax cluster. It is characterized by the presence of internal and external concentric shells 1980Natur.285..643M , as consequences of a recent merger activity. The smoothed velocity field does not deviate from point symmetry, however it shows that the galaxy in the outskirts rolls around the photometric major axis. The velocity dispersion field shows a gently decreasing profile.

gmw_00001945.name: NGC 4339
gmw_00001945.overview: NGC 4339 is a low-luminosity early-type galaxy in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0702605, arXiv:1712.05833] that lacks gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0702605]. It features a disk-dominated outer structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0702605], a circumnuclear young stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0702605], and a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1803.08055].

sources.summary: The paper treats VCC 0648/NGC 4339 as a major SBF-distance outlier, arguing the deeper NGVS data make its larger distance and group membership more reliable than Ton01.

quotes.quote: Our distance modulus for VCC 0648/NGC 4339 is 0.65 0.20 mag larger ( 1.9 times more distant) than Ton01, or more than 3 . The Ton01 quality flags for the galaxy are below average. Figure ngvs_t01 compares the NGVS i band and Ton01 I band (J. Tonry, priv. comm.) images of the galaxy-subtracted frame used for the respective SBF measurements. In the left panel we show the NGVS image, which reveals a large number of sources, not visible in the image used by Ton01 (right panel), many of which are likely GCs in VCC 0648. The main difference between the images is the seeing: the older data have a PSF FWHM of 1 25, while our NGVS image has FWHM 0 55.

The poorer seeing of the older data apparently caused, in this particular case, a poor characterization of the GCLF because the data do not reach the turnover magnitude. As pointed out in Ton01 and other works, this can lead to an underestimated P_r correction, resulting in an overestimated fluctuation amplitude and an artificially low distance modulus. In the case of the much deeper NGVS image, we see the turnover in the GCLF. We conclude our distance is much more reliable and, as discussed above, this galaxy is a newly recognized member of the \ group.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4339 as a low-luminosity Virgo early-type galaxy with disk-dominated outer light, broken index-profile slopes, no gas, and signs of a circumnuclear young metal-rich stellar disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4339, Fig. 7. This early-type galaxy is a Virgo member. It has a rather low luminosity for an elliptical and is classified as either E0 or S0 by different authors. We analysed its surface brightness profile obtained by tracing the flux integrated across the slit and have seen that the surface brightness profile beyond 20 is well-fitted by an exponential, so beyond 20 the stellar disk dominates. But within this radius we see a de Vaucouleurs' bulge with r_ e = 26 , so this effective radius is used to parametrise the index profiles in Fig. 7.

Nevertheless even these short profiles revealed the breaks of slopes, this time around (/r_ e ) 0.7 - -0.6 . The emission is absent in the galaxy, so the break in the H profile at (/r_ e ) 0.7 reflects the real change of the mean stellar age.
Though less prominent than NGC 2810, NGC 4339 has also shown some signs of the circumnuclear young metal-rich stellar disk that may be a product of the secondary nuclear star formation burst.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4339 with Gemini/NIFS and SAURON kinematics, finding a roughly 4.3e7 solar-mass black hole that is a large positive outlier from the relation.

quotes.quote: We present mass estimates of supermassive black holes in six nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies (NGC 4339, NGC 4434, NGC 4474, NGC 4551, NGC 4578 and NGC 4762) with effective stellar velocity dispersion around 100 km/s. We use near-infrared laser-guide adaptive optics observations with the GEMINI/NIFS to derive stellar kinematics in the galactic nuclei, and SAURON observations from the ATLAS ^ 3D Survey for large-scale kinematics. We build axisymmetric Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild dynamical models.

Both modelling approaches recover consistent orbital anisotropies and black hole masses within 1-2 confidence level, except for one galaxy for which the difference is just above the 3 level. Two black holes (NGC 4339 and NGC 4434) are amongst the largest outliers from the current black hole mass - velocity dispersion relation, with masses of (4.3^ +4.8 _ 2.3 ) 10^7 and (7.0^ +2.0 _ 2.8 ) 10^7 M _ , respectively ( 3 confidence level).

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4339 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This is a member of the Virgo cluster, classified as intermediate type between E0 and S0. It is regular FR, showing no misalignment between the photometric and kinematic major axis 2011MNRAS.413..813C . The PN motion agrees with the stellar kinematics showing a rotation of >20 and a dispersion of 30 .

gmw_00001946.name: NGC 4564
gmw_00001946.overview: NGC 4564 is an early-type galaxy in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1712.05833] that has been classified as both an elliptical [arXiv:1110.1275, arXiv:1712.05833] and a lenticular (S0) galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607378, arXiv:1110.1275]. It features a stellar disk [arXiv:1311.1530, arXiv:1712.05833], rotation along its photometric major axis [arXiv:1110.0833, arXiv:1712.05833], and a central nuclear X-ray source that is likely extended [arXiv:astro-ph/0511293].

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4564 among three additional early-type galaxies selected for high Na/Fe, with shorter minor-axis spectra that still support a bottom-heavy central IMF trend.

quotes.quote: In addition to these galaxies we observed three others, with the slit oriented along the minor axis: NGC 3414, NGC 4552, and NGC 4564. These galaxies were selected because of their relatively high [ Na/Fe ] values in data we had obtained previously. These earlier, as yet largely unpublished, data did not cover the Na D doublet. Exposure times were shorter for these objects (although still substantially longer than in van Dokkum Conroy 2012), and we cannot measure their IMF gradients to the same distance as for the primary galaxies.

For all six galaxies, the central M/L ratio is much higher for a varying IMF than for a Kroupa (2001) IMF. That is, all galaxies prefer a bottom-heavy IMF that is steeper than that of the Milky Way at low masses. The IMF parameter falls off steeply, from =2-3 at R=0 to 1 at 0.3 R_ e .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4564 strongly prefers dark matter with a compact radius, but concludes its peaky dispersion and S0-like rotation require nonspherical modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 4564 Models fitted to this E6 galaxy strongly prefer the presence of dark matter, but with radius of only a few R_ e . Perhaps this surprising conclusion is due to the centrally peaky stellar _ los profile. The mass and orbital distributions may depart significantly from a S ersic sphere. coccato2009 indicate that there is a rotation curve resembling ``an S0 galaxy rather than ... an elliptical galaxy''. The unphysically low values of M/L and of the baryon fraction confirm the fit for this galaxy must invoke non-spherical models. We omit NGC 4564 from the analysis.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4564 as an elliptical whose tracer-density profile fit is imperfect, whose tracer rotation is significant, and whose PNe system appears especially tangentially biased.

quotes.quote: In most cases, a single power-law is a good approximation (with probability 1 ). However, in NGC 4564 and NGC 4486 the probability is only 0.3 . Inspection of these profiles shows that the disagreement is driven by the outer 10 of tracers and hence the approximate single power-law is a good representation of 90 of the sample Note that an increase (or decrease) of the tracer density power-law index by 1 dex leads to an increase (or decrease) in the mass estimate of 30 .

In most cases, this fraction is small ( < 20 ) so it is safe to ignore rotation in our analysis and assume spherical symmetry. However, in a few cases (NGC 4564, NGC 4649 (GC) and NGC 5128 (PNe)), the rotation is quite significant and our assumptions may not be valid. This is most apparent for NGC 4564 where V_ rot / 1 .
Note that the most tangentially biased PNe system belongs to NGC 4564 which, as noted in the previous section, has evidence for substantial rotation.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4564 has probably extended nuclear X-ray emission, typical AGN-like colors within the sample, and weak or marginal radio-core emission.

quotes.quote: We focus in particular on the new results for NGC 3377, NGC 4486B, NGC 4564 and NGC 5845. The nuclear source of NGC 4564 (Figure 1, top right) has a FWHM 2 2 160 pc in the 0.3 - 8 keV band; therefore, it is probably extended. In five of the six nuclear sources, the X-ray colors are consistent with typical AGN spectra, a power-law spectrum of index 1.5 - 2 (Figure 3).

In NGC 4564, the upper limit is 0.52 mJy at 8.4 GHz (Wrobel Herrnstein 2000); however, the core is marginally detected at the level of 2 mJy at 1.4 GHz, in the NRAO/VLA Sky Survey. In other cases, (NGC 3377, NGC 4564, NGC 5845) the nuclear emission is extended on scales of a few hundred pc but its morphology remains unclear.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC4564, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC4564 (Fig. fig:NGC4564 ) is an early-type galaxy with high average ellipticity. The presence of a disk is suggested by the exponential decline in the surface brightness profile between 30 and 75 , combined with the increase in ellipticity and diskiness of the isophotes in the same region. However, like other elongated early-types in our sample, it is not perfectly fit by a simple bulge plus exponential disk model: such model does not fully account for the ellipticity gradient, and a bar-like structure can be seen in the residuals in the inner regions.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4564 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: NGC 4564 is an E4 galaxy in the Virgo cluster. It shows a prominent stellar disk along the apparent major axis, so that it has also been referred as an S0 1994A&AS..105..481M . We agree with the stellar kinematic from 2016MNRAS.457..147F and we extend it beyond 6 , where the galaxy is still clearly rotating along its photometric major axis ( V_ rot 95 ). The velocity dispersion profile is decreasing, from the central peak of 180 2004MNRAS.352..721E to 70 at 6 .

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 4564 was reclassified from elliptical to S0, requiring a bulge-plus-disk decomposition that raised its bulge Sersic index.

quotes.quote: The second galaxy in question is NGC 4564. Subsequent to the analysis in Trujillo et al. \ (2004), NGC 4564 is now recognized as an S0 galaxy that was mis-classified as an E galaxy. An R^ 1/n bulge plus exponential-disk decomposition has therefore been performed. This resulted in its bulge S ersic index n increasing from 2.1 to 3.2.

sources.summary: The paper discusses the disputed elliptical versus S0 classification of NGC4564, citing disk evidence and bulge-plus-disk decompositions from multiple studies.

quotes.quote: NGC4564 has been classified as elliptical in RC3 while tex:TE04 classified it as an S0 galaxy. The existence of the disk is also verified by GD07 and KF09 with a bulge S e rsic index value of 3.15 and 4.69 respectively. Also H09 found n_ bulge =3.6 plus an exponential disk.

gmw_00001947.name: NGC 4742
gmw_00001947.overview: NGC 4742 is a faint, early-type field galaxy classified as an E4 elliptical [arXiv:1712.05833]. It exhibits stellar rotation that is misaligned with its photometric position angle, and its central kinematics have been mapped using major-axis slit and planetary nebula data [arXiv:1712.05833, arXiv:2303.06132].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4742 as an outlier in black-hole fundamental-plane tests and notes that excluding it changes fitted scatter estimates.

quotes.quote: Barway Kembhavi data This section examines BK07's claim that the total root mean square (.m.s.) scatter in the M_ bh direction, when using _ e and R_ e as predictor quantities of M_ bh , is 0.25 dex (and 0.19 dex when excluding the outlier NGC 4742).
BK07 performed an additional analysis, excluding NGC 4742 whose SMBH mass derivation has not yet appeared in a refereed paper (see Tremaine et al. \ 2002) and may therefore potentially be erroneous.

The optimal relation after excluding NGC 4742 is given by the expression (M_ bh /M_ ) & = & (8.21 0.07) + (3.23 0.26) [ R_ e /3 kpc ] & - & (1.01 0.13) [ _ e,B 21.0 ] with a total scatter of 0.25 dex. The low value of 0.19 dex reported by BK07 appears to have arisen by dividing their scatter in the R_ e direction (0.061) by the coefficient in front of the M_ bh term in their eq.3 (which is their fitted plane). However, this approach overlooks the three-dimensional nature of the plane and consequently results in an over-estimation of the plane's ability to predict black hole masses. Computing the .m.s. \ offset between the black hole masses listed in Table 1 of BK07 (excluding NGC 4742) and the values predicted from their plane (their eq.3), which can be re-written as (M_ bh /M_ ) = 8.28 + 3.13 [ R_ e /3 kpc ] 0.97 [ _ e,B 21 ] , one obtains a total scatter in the M_ bh direction of 0.27 dex, not 0.19 dex, and greater than the value of 0.25 dex obtained above using Eq.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4742 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This is a field E4 galaxy. It is a faint object, so the number of PN detections is relatively low. We detect a rotation of at least 74 , in agreement with the stellar kinematics of 1983ApJ...266...41D . The direction of rotation is along a direction misaligned with respect to the photometric position angle.

sources.summary: The paper uses major-axis slit data for NGC4742, constructing a central 2D velocity field and velocity-dispersion field by interpolating between slit and planetary-nebula measurements.

quotes.quote: For NGC4742, only major axis slit data from are available from . We build a 2D velocity field in the center using Eq. eq:harmonic_expansion , where V_ rot (a) is given by the measured major axis velocities from the slit, the three parameters V_ s3 (a) , V_ c3 (a) , and PA_ kin (a) are given between a linear interpolation between 0 at a=0 and the measured values for the PN velocity field. The 2D velocity dispersion field is instead build using the major-axis slit data and assuming azimuthally constant .

gmw_00001948.name: UGC 463
gmw_00001948.overview: UGC 463 is a well-isolated, high-surface-brightness spiral galaxy [arXiv:1108.2946] featuring bright, logarithmic spiral arms with a pitch angle of approximately 20 degrees [arXiv:1511.06365]. Its mass budget is dominated by dark matter beyond one scale length, making its baryonic disk submaximal [arXiv:1108.2946].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 463 as a high-surface-brightness spiral whose mass budget is dark-matter dominated beyond one scale length and inconsistent with a maximal disk.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed and unique mass budget for the high-surface-brightness galaxy UGC 463, showing it is dominated by dark matter (DM) at radii beyond one scale length ( h_R ) and has a baryonic-to-DM mass ratio of approximately 1:3 within 4.2 h_R . Assuming a constant scale height ( h_z , calculated via an empirical oblateness relation), we calculate dynamical disk mass surface densities from stellar kinematics, which provide vertical velocity dispersions after correcting for the shape of the stellar velocity ellipsoid (measured to have / =1.04 0.22 and / =0.48 0.09 ). We isolate the stellar mass surface density by accounting for all gas mass components and find an average K band mass-to-light ratio of 0.22 0.09(ran) ^ +0.16 _ 0.15 (sys) / ; 2009MNRAS.400.1181Z \ and 2003ApJS..149..289B \ predict, respectively, 0.56 and 3.6 times our dynamical value based on stellar-population-synthesis modeling. The baryonic matter is submaximal by a factor of 3 in mass and the baryonic-to-total circular-speed ratio is 0.61^ +0.07 _ 0.09 (ran) ^ +0.12 _ 0.18 (sys) at 2.2 h_R ; however, the disk is globally stable with a multi-component stability that decreases asymptotically with radius to Q 2 . We directly calculate the circular speed of the DM halo by subtracting the baryonic contribution to the total circular speed; the result is equally well described by either a Navarro-Frenk-White halo or a pseudo-isothermal sphere.

In this paper, we focus on providing a detailed, initial example of these calculations using UGC 463, located at equatorial (J2000.0) coordinates (RA,DEC) = (00 ^ h 43 ^ m 32 5,+14 ^ d 20 ^ m 34 ^ s ). We continue our series by summarizing the baryonic mass fractions in 29 additional galaxies in Bershady et al. (2011; hereafter ), submitted . Here we summarize some salient properties of UGC 463: It is a well isolated galaxy with a moderately-high extrapolated central disk surface brightness 2010ApJ...716..198B , which is a factor of 2 above the mean derived by 1970ApJ...160..811F .

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 463 as an easy spiral-pitch test case whose bright logarithmic arms let Spirality recover a pitch near 20 degrees.

quotes.quote: Simple Galaxy: UGC 463 Figure fig:SimpleGalaxy shows Spirality's measurement of galaxy UGC 463.

Spirality's measurement gives a best-fit pitch of 19.85 1.57 . For comparison, 2DFFT's measurement of the image's 3-arm component is 22.38 3.21 . Visual inspection, done by overlaying transparencies marked with spirals of known pitch onto the spiral arms, suggests a pitch of 20 5 With an error bar of only 1.57 , Spirality is particularly confident in this measurement. There are several reasons that combine to make this galaxy easy to measure. First, the absence of foreground stars in the inner part of the image allows Spirality to fit the entire galaxy, not just the 2-arm symmetric component, to the pitch angle templates. Because the spiral arms extend from the central region to the outer edge of the galaxy, Spirality is able to track long segments of the arms. Because the spiral arms are bright, Spirality has no difficulty distinguishing the arms from the space in between. Finally, like many (though certainly not all) spiral galaxies, UGC 463 has arms that are reasonably logarithmic, i.e.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 463 as a PPak demonstration target, noting that reconstructed data reveal its basic spiral structures and provide many spatially resolved spectra across the galaxy.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:UGC463 , gives an example of the galaxy UGC 463, that was observed for the Disk Mass project. Despite the rather crude sampling of the fibers, the basic morphological structures of the galaxy seen in the POSS-II image (spiral arms, stars clumps...), are clearly visible in the PPAK reconstructed image. Apart from the ability to create mono- and polychromatic images from the resulting data, one exposure with PPak yields 331 spatially resolved spectra of the target. The high number of fibers at the outer and fainter parts of the galaxy, offers the observer the option to adaptively bin spaxels as to increase the signal-to-noise further.

gmw_00001949.name: MMJ065837-5557.0
gmw_00001949.overview: MMJ065837-5557.0, also known as the Cosmic Seagull, is a dusty, star-forming disk galaxy at a redshift of approximately 2.7 [arXiv:0803.3462, arXiv:1808.02828]. It is strongly gravitationally lensed by the foreground Bullet Cluster, which amplifies its emission by a factor of up to 50 [arXiv:0803.3462, arXiv:1808.02828].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MMJ065837-5557.0 as a lensed z~2.7 millimeter galaxy behind the Bullet Cluster, amplified by about 20 and intrinsically consistent with a luminous infrared galaxy.

quotes.quote: This source, MMJ065837-5557.0, lies close to a maximum in the density of underlying mass-distribution, towards the larger of the two interacting clusters as traced by the weak-lensing analysis of clowe2006 . Using optical--IR colours we argue that MMJ065837-5557.0 lies at a redshift of z = 2.7 0.2 . A lensing-derived mass-model for the Bullet Cluster shows a critical-line (caustic) of magnification within a few arcsecs of the AzTEC source, sufficient to amplify the intrinsic millimeter-wavelength flux of the AzTEC galaxy by a factor of 20 . After subtraction of the foreground cluster emission at due to the Sunyaev-Zel'dovich effect, and correcting for the magnification, the rest-frame FIR luminosity of MMJ065837-5557.0 is 10^ 12 L_ , characteristic of a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG).

The photometric-colors, and the small positional-offset of the AzTEC source and the optical--IR counterparts from the critical magnification-line derived from cluster mass-models strongly support the suggestion that MMJ065837-5557.0 is a moderately-luminous high- z galaxy that has been lensed and amplified by the massive foreground Bullet Cluster.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO and continuum measurements of SMM J0658, finding the Cosmic Seagull is a dusty, star-forming disk with high magnification and no decreasing rotation curve.

quotes.quote: We present Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array measurements of the `Cosmic Seagull', a strongly magnified galaxy at z=2.7779 behind the Bullet Cluster. We report CO(3-2) and continuum 344 m (rest-frame) data at one of the highest differential magnifications ever recorded at submillimeter wavelengths ( up to 50 ), facilitating a characterization of the kinematics of a rotational curve in great detail (at 620 pc resolution in the source plane). We find no evidence for a decreasing rotation curve, from which we derive a dynamical mass of (6.3 0.7) 10^ 10 M_ within =2.6 0.1 kpc.

Here we present the physical properties of galaxy SMM J0658 at z=2.7793 2012A&A...543A..62J , hereafter called the `Cosmic Seagull' (figure co32 ). This galaxy lies close to a lensing critical line, resulting in one of the largest magnifications reported to date ( up to 50 ), which facilitates precise measurements of the kinematics at the outskirts of this dusty, star-forming galaxy.

gmw_00001950.name: SXDF-ALMA3
gmw_00001950.overview: SXDF-ALMA3 is a galaxy identified as one of the brightest detections in an ALMA 1.1 mm survey [arXiv:1601.00195]. It is extremely faint in ultra-deep near-infrared images, representing a population of dust-obscured submillimeter galaxies [arXiv:1601.00195].

sources.summary: SXDF-ALMA3 is highlighted as one of the brightest ALMA detections, extremely faint in deep near-infrared images and evidence for a dust-obscured submillimeter population.

quotes.quote: The multiwavelength properties of the 5 brightest ALMA sources have been studied by exploiting the rich data in this field (Yamaguchi et al. in prep.), revealing that 4 of the ALMA sources are situated on the main sequence of star-forming galaxies with a significant stellar mass ( M_ star (4 - 10) 10^ 10 M_ ) at their epochs ( z_ photo 1.3 - 2.5 ). New populations of galaxies unveiled by the ALMA 1.1 mm survey: Then does ALMA only detect dust emission from ``already known galaxies'' that were selected using rest-frame UV/optical deep surveys? The answer is no; one of the 5 brightest ALMA detections, SXDF-ALMA3, is extremely faint even in the ultra-deep NIR images from CANDELS and HUGS, as shown in Figure fig2 . Further contributions to the star formation history may come from these faint submm galaxies that do not appear to be fully overlapped with UV/optical-selected galaxies (e.g., ).

gmw_00001951.name: AGESM33-31
gmw_00001951.overview: AGESM33-31 is a large neutral hydrogen cloud located near the galaxy M33 [arXiv:1605.01628]. It exhibits a ring-like shape and may represent either an extension of the Magellanic Stream or the disrupted remnant of a dark galaxy [arXiv:1605.01628].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGESM33-31 as an unusual H I cloud near M33, considering whether it is Magellanic Stream material or a disrupted dark-galaxy remnant.

quotes.quote: We find one particularly interesting cloud: AGESM33-31, it is shown on the left hand side of figure fig3 . As can be seen from the figure this cloud appears to be as large as M33 and has a ring-like shape. If at the same distance as M33 it has a diameter of 18 kpc and a HI mass of 1.22 x 10 ^ 7 M _ .

The first is that it is part of the Magellanic Stream. It has been suggested that Wright's cloud is part of the stream and, as AGESM33-31 has a similar velocity, it could simply be a further extension. However, the Magellanic Stream at this point of the sky is extremely sparse and made up largely of very small clouds so AGESM33-31 appears incongruent. Secondly, AGESM33-31 could be the remnants of a dark galaxy that has been disrupted through an event such as an interaction.

gmw_00001952.name: BCG
gmw_00001952.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy, BCG, serves as the reference center for gravitational lensing analyses of the Abell 383 cluster [arXiv:1103.5618]. Its halo contains a radial counterimage, and its light is modeled and subtracted to reveal other lensed galaxies [arXiv:1103.5618].

sources.summary: The Abell 383 brightest cluster galaxy anchors the lensing analysis center, is modelled and subtracted to reveal lensed images, and contains a radial counterimage in its halo.

quotes.quote: The reference center of our analysis is fixed on the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG): RA = 02:48:03.41 Dec = -03:31:44.91 (J2000.0). To better reveal these lensed galaxies, we have carefully modeled and subtracted the light of several cluster galaxies including the BCG. Explicitly, for its subtraction, the BCG has been modeled using the CHEF basis Jimenez&Benitez2011 . The flexibility of the CHEFs scale parameter allows us to accurately represent the BCG while keeping significant substructure and arcs unchanged.

gmw_00001953.name: System 5
gmw_00001953.overview: System 5 is a multiply lensed Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6.027 [arXiv:1102.5092, arXiv:1103.5618]. It exhibits a modest star formation rate [arXiv:1102.5092, arXiv:1603.02277], extended Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1102.5092], and a low metallicity [arXiv:1603.02277].

sources.summary: The paper reports ID 12065 as a multiply imaged z=6.027 lensed galaxy with a Balmer break, modest star formation, compact size, extended Lyman-alpha emission, and an old stellar population.

quotes.quote: In this letter, we report the discovery of a high-redshift i dropout in Abell 383 (ID 12065), which we confirmed to be at z=6.027 based on Keck spectroscopy. We summarize the observations performed in Section 2 and present results of our photometric and spectroscopic analysis in Section 3.

We report the discovery of a unique z=6.027 galaxy, multiply imaged by the cluster Abell 383 and detected in new Hubble Space Telescope ACS and WFC3 imaging, as well as in Warm Spitzer observations. This galaxy was selected as a pair of i dropouts; its suspected high redshift was confirmed by the measurement of a strong Lyman- line in both images using Keck/DEIMOS. Combining Hubble and Spitzer photometry after correcting for contamination by line emission (estimated to be a small effect), we identify a strong Balmer break of 1.5 magnitudes.

The UV slope is consistent with 2.0 , and from the rest-frame UV continuum we measure a current star formation rate of 2.4 1.1 M _ /yr. The unlensed half-light radius is measured to be 300 pc, from which we deduce a star-forming surface density of 10 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 . The Lyman- emission is found to be extended over 3 along the slit, corresponding to 5 kpc in the source plane.

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA detection of [CII] in the lensed z>6 galaxy A383-5.1, deriving a faint line luminosity, redshift, and low metallicity.

quotes.quote: The galaxy A383-5.1 (star formation rate [ SFR ] of 3.2 M _ yr ^ 1 and magnification of = 11.4 1.9 ) shows a line detection with L_ CII = 8.9 10^ 6 L _ , making it the lowest L_ CII detection at z>6 .

In case of A383-5.1 we detect the redshifted line. The spectrum is shown in Fig.  fig:a383result together with an image of the integrated line overlaid on a near-infrared HST F140W image from the CLASH survey https://archive.stsci.edu/prepds/clash/ . We fit a Gaussian line profile to the extracted spectrum and use this to derive the line properties. The resulting fit parameters are: central frequency = 270.4448 0.0061 GHz, peak flux of S_ peak = 0.96 0.19 mJy, and FWHM line width of V = 100 23 km s ^ 1 . This corresponds to an integrated line intensity I_ CII = 0.102 0.032 Jy km s ^ 1 (including the flux uncertainty added in quadrature). We derive a redshift of z_ CII = 6.0274 0.0002 , which is in agreement with the optical/UV redshift of 6.029 0.002 richard11 and the Ciii ] redshift of 6.0265 0.00013 stark15 .
Using combined stellar population synthesis modeling and photoionization modeling of the gas-component, stark15 finds that the metallicity is (Z/Z_ ) = -1.33 for A383-5.1.

sources.summary: System 5 is identified as a two-image multiply lensed Lyman-break galaxy at z=6.027, with CLASH photometric redshifts near six and leverage on the lensing-distance range.

quotes.quote: System 5: Two images of a multiply-lensed Lyman-break, high redshift galaxy at z_ s =6.027 , reported recently by Richard2011 based on CLASH imaging and Keck spectra. We also identify these two images and measure photometric redshifts of z 6.01 and 5.95 . The high redshift of this system expands substantially the lensing-distance range thus enabling us to constrain the profile with better accuracy, as discussed in magsec .

gmw_00001954.name: System 6
gmw_00001954.overview: System 6 is a high-redshift galaxy system consisting of four blue multiply imaged components [arXiv:1103.5618]. These gravitationally lensed images exhibit similar internal details, including a brighter white blob, and have a typical photometric redshift of approximately 2.4 [arXiv:1103.5618].

sources.summary: System 6 is described as four blue multiply imaged components with internal structure; the model reproduces them well and the authors provide SED and photo-z checks.

quotes.quote: System 6: This system consists of 4 blue images with similar internal details including a brighter white blob, at a typical photometric redshift of z_ s 2.4 for this system (see Table systems , Figure dist_sys5 ). Our model reproduces these images very well (Figure rep_sys5 ), though it slightly favors a lower redshift of z_s 2 but due to the distances involved this is in practice only a 1 difference in the redshift distance ratio. These images were matched up for the first time in this work enabled by the deep, high-resolution HST data. Due to the variance in the SEDs and therefore photometric redshifts of the images of this system, we supply also the photo- z distributions and SEDs in Figure dist_sys5 , so that the reader could more easily assess the plausibility of this system.

gmw_00001955.name: System 9
gmw_00001955.overview: System 9 is a faint, wide, greenish-looking galaxy arc located southeast of a brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1103.5618]. Gravitational lensing reproduces this system as a double image with predicted counter-images at a redshift of approximately 3.8 [arXiv:1103.5618].

sources.summary: System 9 is a faint green arc southeast of the BCG; the model reproduces it as a double image and predicts additional counter-images with z~3.8.

quotes.quote: System 9: A faint, wide greenish-looking arc 17 south east of the BCG (see Figure curves383 ). Our model accurately reproduces this arc as a double image. In addition, two other small counter-images are predicted, for which we identify the best-matching candidates in the data. These images were matched up for the first time in this work, and except for image 9.2, show similar photometric redshifts of 3.8 (see also Figure dist_sys8 ), in agreement with our model prediction. In addition, it should be noted that photo- z analyses of some segments of the arc designated as 9.2 imply indeed a redshift of 3.8 , similar to the other three images of this system.

gmw_00001956.name: NGC3665
gmw_00001956.overview: NGC 3665 is an isolated, fast-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:1703.05247, arXiv:1609.03559] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1703.05247, arXiv:1609.03559] and a central molecular gas disc with an unresolved central hole [arXiv:1703.05247, arXiv:1609.03559]. The galaxy features a double-jet radio morphology [arXiv:2209.14638] with large-scale jets extending 55 kpc [arXiv:2202.08593].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3665 as a nearby fast-rotator early-type galaxy with a molecular gas disc, an AGN, a central CO hole, and a measured SMBH mass.

quotes.quote: As a part of the mm-Wave Interferometric Survey of Dark Object Masses (WISDOM) project, we present an estimate of the mass of the supermassive black hole (SMBH) in the nearby fast-rotator early-type galaxy NGC 3665. We obtained Combined Array for Research in Millimeter Astronomy (CARMA) B and C array observations of the ^ 12 CO (J=2-1) emission line with a combined angular resolution of 0 59 . We analysed and modelled the three-dimensional molecular gas kinematics, obtaining a best-fit SMBH mass M_ BH =5.75^ +1.49 _ 1.18 10^ 8 M_ , a mass-to-light ratio at H band (M/L)_ H =1.45 0.04 (M/L)_ , H , and other parameters describing the geometry of the molecular gas disc (statistical errors, all at 3 confidence).

As NGC 3665 harbours an AGN, the molecular gas could be affected by its presence and/or that of the SMBH itself. With the highest angular resolution achieved so far, our CO observations have revealed that the molecular gas disc in NGC 3665 has an unresolved central hole, the position of which coincides with that of the radio core (detected with VLBI observations; 2009A&A...505..509L ) and the peak of the stellar surface brightness (identified from HST observations). The absence of molecular gas within a radius of 0 4 or 65 pc suggests that some mechanism may be dissociating the molecular gas or preventing it from forming or accumulating in the very centre of the galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC3665 is an isolated fast-rotating early-type galaxy with an AGN, radio jets, central molecular gas, and an IMF normalization between Kroupa and Salpeter.

quotes.quote: NGC3665 is an isolated fast-rotating ETG with a strong AGN, which powers large radio jets 1986A&AS...64..135P,2016MNRAS.458.2221N . The molecular gas and dust in this source is concentrated in a central disc perpendicular to the radio jet. As discussed in Onishi et al., in prep the best fitting surface brightness profile for this object is an exponential disc with a central hole, which we adopt here. The dynamically inferred M/L, on the other hand, shows an M/L dip at around 350pc. Overall the IMF mismatch parameter seems to lie midway between Kroupa and Salpeter in this object.

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 3665 as a candidate restarted radio source, with opposite large-scale FR I jets and brighter nuclear knots indicating possible renewed activity.

quotes.quote: From the point of view of the radio morphology, among the 46 extended sources, there are eight galaxies (NGC 2789, NGC 3665, NGC 3842, UGC 10097, IC 5180, NGC7436B, UGC 12482, and MCG+05-10-007; see brienza21 for a detailed study of this source) in which a bright, small-scale structure is accompanied by large-scale diffuse emission. These gETGs can be considered as possible restarted sources with the structures observed on different scales (and of different surface brightnesses) being produced by subsequent phases of activity.

NGC 3665 (FRI). This galaxy presents two opposite large-scale jets (55 kpc in total extent), for an overall FR I morphology. However, close to the nucleus ( 3 kpc) two bright knots of higher surface brightness are seen, possibly indicating a restarted source.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3665 as the only radio-dim sample galaxy with a clear double-jet morphology, lying at the boundary between radio-dim and radio-bright populations.

quotes.quote: Six radio-dim sources show some extension in the VLASS data, although the emerging structures are all rather compact and weak; moreover, only one of those, NGC 3665, display a clear double-jet morphology. Interestingly, NGC 3665 is characterized by the Eddington-normalized monochromatic radio luminosity L_ /L_ Edd = -8.51 0.37 , exactly at the borderline between radio-dim and radio-bright objects (see Section sec:bimodality above).

All in all, we conclude that the overwhelming majority of radio-dim sources in our sample of early-type galaxies is either undetected (11/36) or unresolved (19/36) at GHz frequencies in the VLASS survey with arcsec-scale resolution. Only several (6/36) display some elongated features on the VLASS maps, and only one of those is characterized by a clear morphology consisting of a prominent radio core with two-sided symmetric jets; this one outlier, NGC 3665, is in fact located at the borderline between radio-dim and radio-bright populations.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3665 data outside 15 because its rotation curve, ellipticity discontinuity, and isophotal twist complicate the inner region.

quotes.quote: NGC 3665. The rotation curve is flat from inside of 5 to close to 30 , but there is a discontinuity at 10 where the ellipticity suddenly drops from 0.35 to almost zero. The ellipticity then slowly rises to approximately 0.2 at 15 outside of which it is constant. There is also a 10 isophotal twist in the same range. Only data outside of 15 is used.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1122+39 as a core plus twin-jet radio source whose VLBA image is pointlike and preserves the arcsecond core flux on parsec scales.

quotes.quote: 1122+39 (NGC 3665) -- At arcsecond resolution, this appears to be a core plus twin-jet radio source ( ). In our VLBA image, it shows a pointlike structure with a total flux density S_5 = 8.8 mJy. No arcsecond core flux is missing on the parcsec-scale ( S_ VLBA /S_ 100 )

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC3665 has broader CO lines, a low-temperature best-fit model, and a double-peaked line profile that makes predicted SLEDs more uncertain.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies (NGC3665, NGC4526 and NGC5866) have broader lines (see Fig. fig:0 ) and for those the backend configuration used two overlapping sub-bands, each 1 GHz wide and covering a total bandwidth of 1.9 GHz with 977 kHz-wide channels.

The kinetic temperatures obtained for the best-fit models are either low ( T_ K =10 20 K for IC1024, NGC1222, NGC1266, NGC2764, NGC3032, NGC3665, NGC4150, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC4694, NGC4710 and NGC5866), intermediate ( 30 70 K for IC0676, NGC3607, PGC058114 and UGC09519) or high ( 120 K for NGC6014 and 150 K for NGC7465).

We know that for galaxies such as NGC3032, NGC3607, NGC3665, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC5866 and NGC6014, a double-peaked line profile is more realistic, so for these sources the SLEDs shown in Figure fig:9 are more uncertain. figure 1 figure 1 figure 1 figure 1 figure 1 Correlations with global parameters In Paper XI, ratios of molecular lines discussed in this paper were shown to correlate with some global physical parameters of the sample galaxies (e.g.

sources.summary: B2 1122+39 requires a young stellar component rather than a power law for its UV excess, and its MIR/FIR data are fit with dust.

quotes.quote: B2 1122+39. A young stellar component is needed to fit the UV excess. No plausible power-law component would be able to do this. MIR and FIR data are available, which can be fitted by a mixture of dust with temperatures of 30 K ( M_d = 1.7 10^6 M_ ) and 200 K ( M_d = 25 M_ ).

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC 3665 as a literature-supported FR I LERG, deriving its jet inclination from sidedness ratio and using HST imaging for a unique jet-disc misalignment.

quotes.quote: To improve the statistics of jet--disc orientations, we have also extended our analysis to two other FR I LERGs for which CO and jet inclination data of comparable quality are available in the literature, NGC 383 and NGC 3665.
For NGC 3665, we take the sidedness ratio from Laing99 , which was derived in an identical way to those in this paper (albeit at 1.4 GHz), and then use Equation eq:jet_inclination to derive _ jet .

For NGC 3557, IC 4296, NGC 383 and NGC 3665 the available archival Hubble Space Telescope (HST) optical imaging allows us to identify the side of the CO disc pointing towards the observer unequivocally (Paper I; Onishi17,North19 ) and we can derive unique values of _ dj .

sources.summary: The paper reports X-ray emission centered on NGC 3665, elongated along the galaxy major axis, but notes limited counts prevent detailed two-dimensional structure analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3665, z=0.0069 The bright part of the emission extends to 3^ in radius, centered on NGC 3665 and has a similar elongation as the galaxy's major axis. There is also a very bright background AGN east of the galaxy. Due to lack of counts, the spectral analysis was done on 4 zones that are symmetric around the center, which precludes revealing any 2d structure.

gmw_00001957.name: IC0719
gmw_00001957.overview: IC 0719 is a gas-rich lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1304.3339, arXiv:1609.03559] hosting two large-scale, counter-rotating stellar disks [arXiv:1804.03569, arXiv:2011.12023]. The younger secondary disk is linked to an accreted gas component [arXiv:1804.03569, arXiv:2005.02355] that fuels ongoing, ring-like star formation [arXiv:1304.3339].

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 719 with MUSE, separating two counter-rotating stellar disks and arguing that the younger secondary disk formed from accreted gas rather than a binary merger.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we study the S0 galaxy IC 719 known to host two large-scale counter-rotating stellar disks in order to investigate their formation mechanism. We exploit the large field of view and wavelength coverage of the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) spectrograph to derive two-dimensional (2D) maps of the various properties of the counter-rotating stellar disks, such as age, metallicity, kinematics, spatial distribution, the kinematical and chemical properties of the ionized gas, and the dust map. Due to the large wavelength range, and in particular to the presence of the Calcium Triplet 8498, 8542, 8662 \ (CaT hereafter), the spectroscopic analysis allows us to separate the two stellar components in great detail. This permits precise measurement of both the velocity and velocity dispersion of the two components as well as their spatial distribution.

The main stellar disk of the galaxy is kinematically hotter, older, thicker and with larger scale-length than the secondary disk. There is no doubt that the latter is strongly linked to the ionized gas component: they have the same kinematics and similar vertical and radial spatial distribution. This result is in favor of a gas accretion scenario over a binary merger scenario to explain the origin of counter-rotation in IC 719. One source of gas that may have contributed to the accretion process is the cloud that surrounds IC 719.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 0719 as a galaxy with counter-rotating large-scale stellar disks, distinct stellar populations, ionized gas, and double-peaked major-axis LOSVDs.

quotes.quote: The second case we study is IC 0719, a spectacular case displaying multiple kinematic components pizzella18 . The galaxy is made of stars in two counter-rotating large scale discs with distinct stellar populations. In addition it has an ionised-gas component showing the same sense of rotation of the secondary, lower-mass, younger stellar disc.

We used MUSE observations around the 4800 5300 \ region to extract three apertures along the major axis of the galaxy. As for the case of NGC 4371, the S/N of the spectra is well above 100 per pixel. We impose a velocity sampling of 50 . Figure fig:ic0719 shows an almost perfect Gaussian LOSVD profile at the centre of the galaxy, while LOSVDs along the major axis display clear double-peaked shapes.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 719 as a gas-rich lenticular with counterrotating gas and stellar components, ring-like star formation, subsolar oxygen abundance, and ongoing disk growth from smooth external gas accretion.

quotes.quote: We have obtained and analyzed long-slit spectral data for the lenticular galaxy IC 719. In this gas-rich S0 galaxy, its large-scale gaseous disk counterrotates the global stellar disk. Moreover in the IC 719 disk we have detected a secondary stellar component corotating the ionized gas. By using emission-line intensity ratios, we have proved the gas excitation by young stars and so are claiming current star formation, most intense in a ring-like zone at the radius of 10 (1.4 kpc). The oxygen abundance of the gas in the starforming ring is about half of the solar abundance.

Since the stellar disk remains dynamically cool, we conclude that smooth prolonged accretion of the external gas from a neighboring galaxy provides current building of the thin large-scale stellar disk. galaxies: elliptical and lenticular --- galaxies: ISM --- galaxies: kinematics and dynamics --- galaxies: evolution --- galaxies: individual: IC 719.

sources.summary: The paper argues IC 0719 is a gas-accretion counterrotator, with an extended warped HI disk, a tail toward IC 0718, and retrograde gas settling into a secondary stellar disk.

quotes.quote: IC 0719, the most instructive system in this study, shows an extended, strongly warped disc of 43 kpc diameter, with a faint tail extending towards its neighbor IC 0718. The gas has likely been accreted from this external source during an encounter whose geometry directed the gas into misaligned retrograde orbits (with respect to the primary stellar body of IC 0719). In the interior, where dynamical time-scales are shorter, the \ has settled into the equatorial plane forming the retrograde secondary stellar disc.

IC 0719 has an irregular \ distribution roughly 5 \ (43 kpc) in diameter in the east-west direction and 4 \ north-south (Figure both ). A tail of faint \ emission at column density 2 19 \ extends at least 4 \ (34 kpc) from the centre of IC 0719 in the direction of IC 0718. The sense of rotation of the \ matches that of the smaller, secondary stellar disc but is retrograde with respect to the bulk of the stars katkov13,pizzella .

sources.summary: The paper validates its mock counter-rotating galaxy model with IC 719, using its measured photometric, kinematic, and stellar population decomposition to reproduce two counter-rotating disks and their velocity/surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: To test the reliability of our approach, we built a kinematic model of IC 719 for which the photometric, kinematic, and stellar population parameters needed to construct the mock data are available in pizzellaEvidenceFormationYoung2018 . The decomposition of IC 719 provides very detailed information on the two counter-rotating components that allows us to drop some assumptions previously made to generate our model. For example, we do not use the same stellar population model and equal kinematics for the two counter-rotating components.

The velocity fields are generated adopting two rotation curves that best reproduce those extracted along the major axis of IC 719, while the velocity dispersion fields are assumed to be constant with values of 60 km s ^ 1 and 40 km s ^ 1 for _ pro and _ cr , respectively. The surface brightness distribution of both disks follows an exponential law, although with different scale length h_ pro =1.5 kpc and h_ cr =1.0 kpc. The counter-rotating components equally contribute to the galaxy surface brightness at about 1.0 kpc.

sources.summary: IC0719 is a low-mass lenticular with star formation from recent molecular-gas accretion, counter-rotating gas, and a strong outward IMF-normalization gradient.

quotes.quote: IC0719 is a relatively low mass lenticular galaxy (M _ * = 4.3 10^ 10 M _ ; 2013MNRAS.432.1709C ), with strong star formation all across its disc triggered by a recent accretion of molecular gas 2011MNRAS.417..882D . The gas exactly counter-rotates when compared with the stars, and some minor flux asymmetry is still present. Despite this, the gas appears kinematically relaxed, and sits in the galaxy mid-plane, and thus is is well suited to our modelling approach. This object is quite young (SSP equivalent age of 3.4Gyr within 1 Re, 2015MNRAS.448.3484M ), and has a large velocity dispersion gradient, making it an interesting case study for IMF variation studies. A strong dynamical M/L gradient seems to be needed to explain the observed rotation of the gas, as shown in the right panel in Figure imffig .

gmw_00001958.name: BCG
gmw_00001958.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy in RXCJ0352.9+1941 hosts a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2404.19549]. This central galaxy exhibits a strong radio core and diffuse jet-like emissions that may indicate an X-shaped radio galaxy morphology [arXiv:2404.19549].

sources.summary: The RXCJ0352.9+1941 brightest cluster galaxy hosts a radio-loud AGN whose jets, core, and diffuse emission are linked to X-ray cavities and possible X-shaped morphology.

quotes.quote: As reported in 2016MNRAS.461..560G , RXCJ0352.9+1941 cluster hosts a brightest cluster galaxy (BCG). A radio-loud AGN associated with the central BCG was detected by the LoFAR telescope at 144 MHz 2020MNRAS.496.2613B . Additionally, we detect extended radio jet-like diffuse emissions at GMRT 1.4 GHz. The 1.4 GHz contours reveal a strong core with a flux density of 13.7 mJy that co-insides with the central BCG. The radio map also shows the AGN outflow of non-thermal particles in the form of diffuse radio jet-like emission.

Another viable proposition is that RXCJ0352.9+1941 hosts an X-shaped radio galaxy 2019AJ....157..195L,2019A&A...631A.173B,2020ApJ...889...91G . The morphological features evident in the present case such as the absence of hot-spot in the secondary jet (`sj'), significant long length of `sj' relative to primary lobes, and its orientation along the major axis of the BCG all collectively point towards the spin-flip scenario of its formation 2012RAA....12..127G .

gmw_00001959.name: SHBL J001355.9 185406
gmw_00001959.overview: SHBL J001355.9 185406 is a high-energy peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1304.4023] that exhibits a flat-spectrum radio core down to 156 MHz [arXiv:1402.1320]. Very high-energy observations reveal a compact core-jet structure [arXiv:2310.10206] without any surrounding diffuse arcminute-scale radio emission [arXiv:1402.1320].

sources.summary: The paper observes SHBL J001355.9 185406 at low radio frequencies, finding no diffuse arcminute-scale halo and showing its core remains flat-spectrum down to 156 MHz.

quotes.quote: While analyzing the NVSS survey maps of the TeV detected BL Lacs, we have however discovered that the high frequency-peaked object SHBL J001355.9 185406 is associated uniquely with the one-sided, arcmin-scale, and edge-brightened jet/lobe-like feature extending to the south-west from the blazar core. In order to investigate in detail the large-scale morphology of SHBL J001355.9 185406, we have performed low-frequency and high-resolution observations of the source at 156, 259 and 629 MHz using the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope. Our analysis indicates that no diffuse arcmin-scale emission is present around the unresolved blazar core, and that the arcmin-scale structure seen on the NVSS map breaks into three distinct features unrelated to the blazar, but instead associated with background AGN. The upper limits for the extended radio halo around the TeV-emitting BL Lac object SHBL J001355.9 185406 read as < 10 1 at 156-629 MHz.

The another interesting result of our study is that the radio spectral index of the SHBL J001356 185406 core remains flat down to 156 MHz, with no dramatic curvature. Flat spectra (indices 0 ) of relativistic jets observed at small viewing angles are widely believed to arise due to a superposition of self-absorbed spectra of different parts of an outflow with conserved magnetic flux and maintained power-law spectrum of the emitting electrons 1979ApJ...232...34Blandford . The fact that in the case of the SHBL J001356 185406 core we do not see any spectral turnover down to 156 MHz indicates therefore that a self-similar character of the jet in the source holds up to relatively large distances from the jet base.

sources.summary: The source is identified as an HBL/BL Lac object detected by ROSAT, selected for VHE observations, detected by H.E.S.S., and modeled with a particle-dominated jet.

quotes.quote: First detected in X-rays with ROSAT 1RXS J001356.6--185408, RASS , was later identified as a BL Lac object by RBS . With a radio flux of 29.6 mJy at 1.4 GHz NVSS and an X-ray flux, between 0.1--2.4 keV, of 1.26 10^ 11 \ RASS , it fulfilled the selection criteria of the sedentary survey of extreme high-energy peaked BL Lacs SHBL, 2005A&A...434..385G , making this source a member of the HBL subclass. Its relative proximity z=0.095, 2009MNRAS.399..683J and its X-ray and radio fluxes are criteria that make an interesting target for very high-energy (VHE, E>100 GeV ) observations REF::TEVCANDITATE . Consequently, the source was observed with the High Energy Stereoscopic System ( ) and indeed reported as a TeV \ emitter in November 2010 2010ATel.3007....1H .

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0011 191 has a compact VLBA core-jet structure, a nearly stationary jet component, and a faint compact 2.3 GHz TANAMI core.

quotes.quote: 0011 191: 8.4 GHz observations of piner14 with the VLBA reveal a compact core-jet structure with an opening angle of 26.2 . The jet component is close to stationary at 1 mas piner18 . Our 2.3 GHz images reveal a faint, compact core structure with the flux density of 140 mJy.

gmw_00001960.name: IRAS 14348-1447
gmw_00001960.overview: IRAS 14348-1447 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy system [arXiv:1805.03667] consisting of a pre-merger pair of colliding galaxies [arXiv:1805.03667, arXiv:2402.10786]. The system features LINER-type nuclei [arXiv:1612.08181, arXiv:1805.03667] and hosts a likely Compton-thick active galactic nucleus in its southwest component [arXiv:1708.09503], which drives a high-velocity molecular gas outflow [arXiv:1612.08181, arXiv:1708.09503].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 14348 1447 as a massive LINER-type merger with a likely buried AGN, OH high-velocity wings, and an expanding southwest nucleus driving the outflow.

quotes.quote: IRAS 14348 1447 : No evidence for broad-line emission or very high ionization lines in the optical and near-IR is found in this LINER-type ULIRG vei97 , suggesting either a weak AGN contribution to the luminosity or an extremely buried AGN. In the far-IR, the strong absorption found in OH65 (GA15, Fig. fitsoh65 ) strengthens the latter possibility. It is a very massive merger, with projected nuclear separation of 5 kpc, and with the two CO 1-0 components associated with the stellar nuclei eva00 .

A very high velocity wing, in excess of 1000 km/s, is observed in OH119. An interesting characteristic of this source is the blueshift of 100 \ observed in the OH65 absorption feature (Figs. ciioh65prof and fitsoh65 ). Although the northeast (NE) nucleus has a redshift that coincides with that of the OH65 feature, the close correspondence between the OH65 and the OH79 absorptions strongly suggests that OH65 is also produced toward the SW nucleus, i.e., the nucleus is expanding. The associated momentum flux, 7 10^ 35 dyn, is of the same order as the momentum rate that can be supplied by the dominant energy source, whether starburst or AGN, and can be explained through a momentum-driven approach if it is rotationally supported.

sources.summary: The paper studies 14348 1447 as a low-redshift close-binary, pre-merger ULIRG with LINER nuclei; mid-infrared imaging locates its AGN in the SW nucleus, which dominates CO emission.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we study three low- z ( d 350 Mpc) ULIRGs (six individual nuclei) with L_ IR L_ =12.0-12.3 (see Table tbl_sample ) based on their mid- and far-IR spectral energy distribution modeling (Section ss:ir_lum ) . These three ULIRG systems seem to be in a similar dynamical state. They were classified as type III by Veilleux2002 , which corresponds to a pre-merger stage characterized by two identifiable nuclei with well defined tidal tails and bridges. They also belong to the subclass of ``close binary'' (i.e., ``b'') as the projected separation of their nuclei is smaller than 10 kpc. Their nuclei are classified as low ionization nuclear emission-line regions (LINER; 12112+0305 and 14348 1447; e.g., Colina2000, Evans2002 ) or H ii ( 22491 1808; e.g., Veilleux1999 ) and in all systems a weak AGN contribution ( 10-15 ) is detected in their mid-IR \ spectra Veilleux2009 .
For 14348 1447, high-angular resolution mid-IR imaging suggests that the AGN is located in the SW nucleus AlonsoHerrero2016 .
In 14348, the CO(2--1) emission is also dominated by one of the nuclei (SW), but this one is only two times brighter than the NE nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 14348-1447 as a ULIRG whose far-infrared SED may be better modeled with amorphous ice features than with a conventional modified blackbody.

quotes.quote: FIR ice emission Turning to photometry, and returning to ULIRGs, if we accept the classification scheme then the spectrum of IRAS 14348-1447 as reported by or must be considered Class II, that is 6 m ``ice'' absorption is present and partially filled in by 6.2 m polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission. This source occupies an extreme position in the dust emissivity ( ) vs. 100 m optical depth ( _ 100 ) diagram given by with both high _ 100 (5) and (2). Since the effects of optical depth and emissivity are intertwined, a lower optical depth can imply a larger value of .

In Fig. 3 we compare the fit proposed for IRAS 14348-1447 by with an alternative model. The model shown by the dashed line is that of , while that shown by the thick solid line is a core-mantle grain model taken from but modified to have amorphous ice features in the following way: First, opacity data from for crystalline ice cooled to 70 K was matched to the curve for V _m /V _c =0.95 by inducing a ^ 1 dependence to approximate the Mie calculation results along with the addition of an underlying continuum opacity. Second, amorphous ice opacity data ( smith ; deposited at 10 K and warmed to 70 K) was substituted between 30 and 70 m based on this matching after adjustment for the differing sample thicknesses.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 14348-1447 as a Compton-thick AGN ULIRG with the fastest detected H2 outflow, likely nuclear and mostly along the line of sight.

quotes.quote: IRAS 14348-1447 The broad-component emission is seen in the SW galaxy of this galaxy-pair, which hosts an AGN that is likely to be Compton-thick iwa11 . The broad component shows the most blueshifted emission of the four galaxies in our sample, stretching out to -900 \ at zero-intensity. Previous work showed that gas outflows are on average faster in the presence of an AGN see also models by nar08 spo13,arr14,cic14,hil14 .

The fact that this is also the case when comparing IRAS 14348-1447 with the other ULIRGs in our sample strongly suggests that the outflow in IRAS 14348-1447 originates from the nuclear region. The broad component is resolved only marginally in a westerly direction, with a size of 0.9 kpc. However, the fact that the narrow-component emission is close to the systemic velocity throughout the disk suggests that the galaxy disk is aligned close to face-on.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS F14348-1447 (NE) as the northeast radio component of a merging double source, classifying it as AGN/SB and noting mid-infrared AGN and optical LINER diagnostics.

quotes.quote: IRAS F14348-1447 (NE) This is the NE radio component of a double radio source, associated with a merging galaxy-system. The radio centre at 1.49 GHz is associated with the HST bulge. The radio sources are not completely separated, so the histograms created are imperfect. We see that the NE component matches category 1 of our classification, with steepening above 1.1 but due to the uncertainties we classify it as AGN/SB. It is also a mid-IR AGN based on the PAH feature, and a LINER in the optical.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS F14348-1447 (SW) as the southwest radio component of a merging double source with a bulge-associated 1.49 GHz center and uncertain AGN/SB classification.

quotes.quote: IRAS F14348-1447 (SW) This is the SW radio component of a double radio source, associated with a merging galaxy-system. The radio centre of the south component is not matching the one at 8 m (map not shown here). The radio centre at 1.49 GHz is associated with the HST bulge. The radio sources are not completely separated, so the histograms created are imperfect. The SW radio component matches category 1, with steepening above = 1.1, but due to the uncertainties we classify it as AGN/SB.

sources.summary: The paper finds both nuclei of IRAS 14348-1447 dominated by high-equivalent-width 3.3 micron emission and flat continua, with ISO mid-infrared and X-ray spectroscopy confirming a starburst classification.

quotes.quote: IRAS 14348-1447 . The L-band spectrum of both nuclei is dominated by a high equivalent width 3.3 m emission feature (EW > 100 nm). The continuum is flat in both cases, with an uncertainty in the brightest nucleus (S) due to a possible presence of a moderate ( 0.1-0.2 ) ice absorption. If we allow for ice absorption, the best fit continuum slope is 0.2 , while without ice absorption 0.5 .

This model provides a good representation of the continuum, with a small excess in the 2.9-3.1 m, which could be due to the tail of the K-band emission coming from a broader region than the L-band emission. The starburst classification is confirmed by ISO mid-IR spectroscopy and X-ray spectroscopy.

sources.summary: IRAS 14348 1447 is an interacting-pair ULIRG; the paper finds LINER-like extranuclear emission and a clear velocity-dispersion correlation across the three line-ratio diagnostics.

quotes.quote: The sample of galaxies consists of six low- z ULIRGs (see general properties in table misulirgs ) covering a relative wide range of dynamical states of the merging process. Three of the galaxies (IRAS 08572+3915, IRAS 12112+0305, and IRAS 14348 1447) are interacting pairs separated by projected distances of up to 6 kpc, while the rest of the galaxies (IRAS 15206+3342, IRAS 15250+3609, and IRAS 17208 0014) are more evolved, single nucleus ULIRGs, some with a light profile and overall kinematics close to that of intermediate mass ellipticals (e.g. IRAS 17208 0014, Genzel et al. 2001). The two-dimensional kinematic properties (velocity field and velocity dispersion) have been studied in detail before using integral field optical spectroscopy (see Colina et al. 2005 and references therein).

According to this ratio, LINER-like emission is found in the extended extranuclear regions in three systems: IRAS 14348 1447 , IRAS 15250+3609 , and IRAS 17208 0014 . Contrary, for IRAS 08572+3915 , IRAS 15206+3342 and IRAS 12112+0305 this line ratio suggests a dominant H ii like ionization.

Individually, two systems, IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 14348 1447, show a clear correlation in the three line ratios. For three of the remaining systems, IRAS 08572+3915,IRAS 15206+3342 and IRAS 15250+3609, the range in velocity dispersion is relatively small to show the correlation.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 14348-1447 as a colliding-galaxy pair with a Seyfert 1 nucleus, mixed radio AGN and starburst emission, and a single power-law radio model.

quotes.quote: IRAS 14348-1447: classified as a pair of colliding galaxies Sanders88 with a Seyfert 1 optical nucleus Vega08 , the system shows radio emission resulting from both radio AGN and starburst Vardoulaki15 . Clemens10 fits this galaxy with a turnover at 1 GHz where our best-fit radio model turns out to be a single PL, because of having more data at a lower frequency range ( < 500 MHz). Vega08 notes an 8 AGN fraction from radio-FUV modeling, slightly higher than our estimates(=1.3 ).

gmw_00001961.name: central cluster galaxy in 2A 0335+096
gmw_00001961.overview: The central cluster galaxy in 2A 0335+096 is a D-type galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0303314] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1610.00716, arXiv:1106.0542] and a low-power radio mini-halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0303314]. It contains a massive reservoir of molecular gas [arXiv:1610.00716, arXiv:1106.0542] that is likely accreted from a stream extending toward a nearby companion galaxy [arXiv:1106.0542].

sources.summary: The paper resolves molecular gas in the 2A 0335+096 brightest cluster galaxy, finding nuclear and filamentary components, possible companion interaction, complex X-ray atmosphere, and multiphase gas tied to AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA CO(1-0) and CO(3-2) observations of the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in the 2A 0335+096 galaxy cluster ( z=0.0346 ). The total molecular gas mass of 1.13 0.15 9 is divided into two components: a nuclear region and a 7 kpc long dusty filament. The central molecular gas component accounts for 3.2 0.4 8 of the total supply of cold gas. Instead of forming a rotationally-supported ring or disk, it is composed of two distinct, blueshifted clumps south of the nucleus and a series of low-significance redshifted clumps extending toward a nearby companion galaxy. The velocity of the redshifted clouds increases with radius to a value consistent with the companion galaxy, suggesting that an interaction between these galaxies <20 ago disrupted a pre-existing molecular gas reservoir within the BCG.

Here we present ALMA Cycle 1 observations of the molecular gas in the BCG of 2A 0335+096 (also known as RXJ0338+09), traced by CO(1-0) and CO(3-2) line emission. 2A 0335+096 is one of the brightest X-ray objects on the sky edge90, reiprich02 , and has a centre with a short radiative cooling time schwartz80, singh86, singh88, white91 . Its X-ray atmosphere is complex, containing a series of cool clumps mazz03, werner06 , a cold front that was likely induced by sloshing motions mazz03, sanders09 , and several cavities corresponding to multiple generations of AGN feedback with a total enthalpy of 5 59 sanders09 .

sources.summary: The paper observes molecular gas in the central cluster galaxy of 2A 0335+096, finding a rotating H2 structure likely accreted from an emission stream and able to fuel AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: We present adaptive optics-assisted K-band integral field spectroscopy of the central cluster galaxy in 2A 0335+096 (z=0.0349). The 2 v=1--0 S(1) emission is concentrated in two peaks within 600  of the nucleus and fainter but kinematically-active emission extends towards the nucleus. The 2 is in a rotating structure which aligns with, and appears to have been accreted from, a stream of  emission extending over 14  towards a companion galaxy. The projected rotation axis aligns with the 5 GHz radio lobes.

This 2 traces the known 1.2 10^ 9  CO-emitting reservoir; limits on the  emission confirm that the 2 emission is not excited by star formation, which occurs at a rate of less than 1  in this gas. If its accretion onto the black hole can be regulated whilst star formation remains suppressed, the reservoir could last for at least 1 ; the simultaneous accretion of just 5  per cent of the gas could drive a series of AGN outbursts which offset X-ray cooling in the cluster core for the full 1 . Alternatively, if the regulation is ineffective and the bulk of the 2 accretes within a few orbital periods (25--100 ), the resulting 10^ 62  outburst would be among the most powerful cluster AGN outbursts known.

sources.summary: The paper identifies GB6 B0335+096 as a head-tail FR I radio galaxy in 2A0335+096 and uses LOFAR to reveal a long tail requiring electron re-acceleration.

quotes.quote: We also study the properties of the head-tail galaxy GB6 B0335+096 hosted in the cluster to explore the lifecycle of the relativistic electrons in its radio tails.

The new data revealed the presence of a Mpc-long radio tail associated with GB6 B0335+096. The observed projected length is a factor 3 longer than the expected cooling length, with evidence of flattening in the spectral index trend along the tail. Therefore, we suggest that the electrons toward the end of the tail are kept alive by the ICM gentle re-acceleration.

Interestingly, 2A0335+096 also hosts the head-tail radio galaxy GB6 B0335+096 ( z=0.038 ), an FR I galaxy Fanaroff-Riley_1974 with an extended radio tail Patnaik_1988,Sebastian_2017 . Tailed FR I radio galaxies are characterized by large-scales, low-brightness emission bent by the environmental pressure (thermal or ram-pressure) in the same direction, forming structures similar to tails. These radio galaxies are classified in two classes: narrow-angle tailed sources (NAT), with small angles between the tails O'Dea_1985 , and wide-angle tailed sources (WAT), with a larger angle between the tails e.g., Miley_1980,Feretti_2002 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC 013424 as the central D galaxy in 2A 0335+096, noting its offset from X-ray centroids, Seyfert/LINER history, extended emission, blue core, and radio mini-halo.

quotes.quote: The optical image shows one central D galaxy (PGC 013424, Paturel et al. 1989) with a companion smaller galaxy 7 to the North-West and another elliptical galaxy at 41 to the East. In Fig. fig:hst_image the X-ray blobs, defined previously in par:core_small (labeled 1 to 8 in Fig. fig:im_zoomed ), can be easily identified matching the small quasi-elliptical isocontour regions. Apart from one X-ray blob coincident with the cluster D galaxy none of the other blobs have optical counterparts. This strongly suggests that the X-ray blobs are produced by some kind of hydrodynamic process. For completeness in Fig. fig:hst_image we report the centroid position of the narrow and extended model components. It is worth noting that the D galaxy position does not coincide with either of these centroids but rather lies in between.

The central galaxy of 2A 0335 + 096 has been found to have a number of peculiar properties over the past 2 decades. Initially it was classified as a Seyfert 1.5 or Liner by Huchra, Wyatt Davis (1982) but more detailed optical observations by Romanishin Hintzen (1988) found a very extended optical emission line region (H + [ N ii ] ) to the North-East of the galaxy (extended over 30 kpc) and the central regions of the galaxy are anomalously blue indicating recent star-formation (see Fig. fig:halpha ).

The radio properties of 2A 0335 + 096 are also of note. Sarazin et al. (1995) find an amorphous, steep spectrum ( 1.4 ) `mini-halo' around PGC 013424 which has relatively low radio power compared to other similar systems.

gmw_00001962.name: J1307A
gmw_00001962.overview: J1307A is a quasar characterized by a high intrinsic X-ray luminosity [arXiv:1803.08508]. It exhibits moderate radio loudness and may also possess moderate obscuration [arXiv:1803.08508].

sources.summary: J1307A is modeled with a power-law X-ray spectrum, possible moderate obscuration, high intrinsic X-ray luminosity, and moderate radio loudness without clear X-ray extension.

quotes.quote: J1307A. A power law well reproduces the emission for the A component ( ^ 2 /dof, degrees of freedom = 44.3/48); a photon index of =1.53 0.10 is obtained. If we ascribe the apparently flat photon index (wrt. 1.8-2.0 typically observed in unobscured quasars; piconcelli05 ) to the presence of obscuration (i.e., fixing =1.8 and leaving the column density free to vary), we derive =1.88 ^ +1.28 _ 1.02 10^ 22  cm ^ 2 , i.e., moderate obscuration is possibly present in J1307A ( ^ 2 /dof=52.0/48). No iron emission line is required, the limit to the EW of the 6.4 keV component is 140 eV. The 2--10 keV flux is 8.6 10^ 14   , corresponding to an intrinsic (i.e., corrected for the obscuration) rest-frame 2--10 keV luminosity of 5.2 10^ 45   .

This source is the only quasar of the original H10 sample with a detection in the FIRST survey at 1.4 GHz becker95 ; the flux density is 14.3 mJy. Using the available SDSS photometry and the definition of radio loudness as reported by Kellermann et al. (1989), R = f_ 5 GHz /f_ 4400  (rest frame), The rest-frame 5 GHz flux density is computed from the observed 1.4 GHz flux density assuming a radio power-law slope of =-0.8 (i.e., S _ ^ ) while, to derive the rest-frame 4400 \ flux density, we used SDSS photometry following the procedure described in . we are able to define J1307A as moderately radio loud (R 30). We checked for the presence of \ extension in this object, possibly related to some jet emission, by comparing the source count distribution vs. the PSF; we found no clear evidence for extension.

gmw_00001963.name: J1307B
gmw_00001963.overview: J1307B is a broad-absorption line quasar that exhibits a carbon IV broad-absorption line feature in its optical spectrum [arXiv:1803.08508]. Its flat X-ray spectrum supports the presence of strong obscuration, likely originating from an outflowing wind [arXiv:1803.08508].

sources.summary: J1307B is described as a BAL quasar whose flat X-ray spectrum supports strong obscuration, with measured X-ray flux, intrinsic luminosity, and no required iron line.

quotes.quote: J1307B. This source is characterized by a \ 1549 \ BAL feature in the SDSS spectrum, indicative of extinction in an outflowing wind. BALQSOs are often characterized by obscuration also in the \ band (e.g., green96,green01,shemmer05,giustini08 ; see also luo13 for a different scenario).
