This is likely the most viable explanation for J1307B, whose spectrum is parametrized by a flat photon index ( =0.4 1.0 ).

The flat \ slope supports the presence of obscuration; if this component is included in the spectral fitting and =1.8 (fixed) is adopted, we obtain =7.2 ^ +6.1 _ 4.7 10^ 23  cm ^ 2 and a good fit (C-stat/dof=8.3/10); the spectrum is shown in Fig.  xray_spectra , top-right panel. The source 2--10 keV flux and intrinsic rest-frame 2--10 keV luminosity are 4.0 10^ 15   and 3.0 10^ 44   , respectively. The upper limit to the neutral iron K EW is 2 keV.

gmw_00001964.name: J1622A
gmw_00001964.overview: J1622A is a bright binary quasar located at a redshift of z=3.26 [arXiv:1712.01205]. It is optically classified as a broad-absorption line quasar and is heavily obscured in X-rays [arXiv:1803.08508].

sources.summary: J1622A is fitted with an absorbed X-ray power law, classified as a BAL quasar, and highlighted as heavily obscured with high X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: J1622A. Fitting the spectrum of J1622A with a power law results in =1.21 0.20 ( ^ 2 /dof=15.5/19); the addition of absorption provides an improvement in the fitting ( ^ 2 /dof=8.2/18), and a more typical photon index of =1.86^ +0.48 _ 0.44 is derived. The column density is =1.77 ^ +1.42 _ 1.13 10^ 23  cm ^ 2 .

As for J1307B, J1622A can be optically classified as a BALQSO and is heavily obscured in X-rays. No iron line is detected (EW<300 eV). The source 2--10 keV flux (luminosity, corrected for the obscuration) is 3.4 10^ 14   \ ( 2.6 10^ 45   ). The best-fitting spectrum is shown in Fig.  xray_spectra (middle-left panel).

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J1622+0702A as a bright binary quasar whose SDSS flags and colors were suitable, but whose spectroscopy was missed because of fiber collisions.

quotes.quote: (3) SDSS J1622+0702A This object is a bright ( m_ i =16.87 ) binary quasar at z=3.26 Hennawi2010 . The fatal and non-fatal flags are not raised and it actually falls into the UGR inclusion region. This quasar has the correct target flag in SDSS, but it was not observed, because of fiber collisions with a nearby galaxy.

gmw_00001965.name: J1622B
gmw_00001965.overview: J1622B is a quasar detected as the fainter component of a pair [arXiv:1803.08508]. Its X-ray emission is well-reproduced by a power law with limited obscuration and no detected iron line [arXiv:1803.08508].

sources.summary: J1622B is detected as the fainter component of the pair, with an acceptable power-law X-ray fit, limited obscuration, no iron line, and measured luminosity.

quotes.quote: J1622B. A power law with =1.5 0.6 reproduces the \ emission of J1622B reasonably well (C-stat/dof=10.7/27). The inclusion of obscuration at the source redshift provides <2.1 10^ 23  cm ^ 2 (assuming =1.8). No iron line is detected, the EW upper limit being  1.4 keV. The source 2--10 keV flux (luminosity) is 4.5 10^ 15   \ ( 1.9 10^ 44   ). The best-fitting spectrum is shown in Fig.  xray_spectra (middle-right panel).

gmw_00001966.name: J0221A
gmw_00001966.overview: J0221A is a quasar with a high X-ray luminosity [arXiv:1803.08508]. Its jointly fitted pn and MOS spectra are well described by a power law, showing limited obscuration and no strong spectral complexity [arXiv:1803.08508].

sources.summary: J0221A has jointly fitted pn and MOS spectra well described by a power law, no strong spectral complexity, limited obscuration, and high X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: J0221A. The pn and MOS1+2 spectra of J0221A were fitted simultaneously, leaving the normalizations of the pn and combined MOS cameras free to vary to account for possible (though minor) inter-calibration issues. A power law model with =2.14^ +0.26 _ 0.24 is able to reproduce the \ spectra well ( ^ 2 /dof=34.6/38), with no indication of further spectral complexities. The inclusion of obscuration at the source redshift provides a column density upper limit of 4.5 10^ 22  cm ^ 2 ; the upper limit to the 6.4 keV iron line is 730 eV. The derived 2--10 keV flux (luminosity) is 6 10^ 15   \ ( 1.9 10^ 45   ), with uncertainties of the order of 40 per cent. The best-fitting spectrum is shown in Fig.  xray_spectra (bottom-left panel).

gmw_00001967.name: J0221B
gmw_00001967.overview: J0221B is a quasar with a rest-frame X-ray luminosity of 6.9e44 erg/s in the 2 to 10 keV band [arXiv:1803.08508]. Its X-ray spectrum is well-fitted by a single power law, which indicates that any obscuration present is below 4.7e22 per square centimeter [arXiv:1803.08508].

sources.summary: J0221B is the fainter quasar component; its pn spectrum allows a basic power-law fit, limits obscuration, and yields a lower X-ray luminosity than J0221A.

quotes.quote: J0221B. The photon statistics for J0221B (pn data) allows a basic parameterization of its spectrum; a single power law provides a good fit the the data (C-stat=91/114) and returns =1.6^ +0.6 _ 0.5 . The obscuration, if present, is below 4.7 10^ 22  cm ^ 2 (with =1.8); the upper limit to the line EW is 450 eV. The derived 2--10 keV flux and rest-frame luminosity are 5.3 10^ 15   \ and 6.9 10^ 44   , respectively, with uncertainties of the order of 70 per cent. The best-fitting spectrum is shown in Fig.  xray_spectra (bottom-right panel).

gmw_00001968.name: MaNGA 1-24145
gmw_00001968.overview: MaNGA 1-24145 is an early-type, pressure-supported elliptical galaxy that contains a double nucleus and exhibits an asymmetrical optical morphology [arXiv:1702.02464, arXiv:1901.08769]. It is part of a quenched, gas-poor merger system with a companion galaxy [arXiv:1702.02464, arXiv:2101.07293].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MaNGA 1-24145/Satsuki as an early-type dry-merger galaxy with a companion, shell and tidal features, complex gas kinematics, and likely low-activity AGN ionization.

quotes.quote: Optical integral field data This system, MaNGA 1-24145 ( z = 0.0322, RA = 258.84693, DEC =57.43288, M _ * 10^ 11 based on the NASA-Sloan Atlas catalog: http://www.nsatlas.org ), was observed in the first 1392 galaxies, as part of the on-going SDSS-IV/MaNGA survey bun15,dro15,law16,yan16a,yan16b,sdss16 .

The SDSS image indicates that MaNGA 1-24145 (nicknamed `Satsuki') has a companion galaxy (nicknamed `Mei') located in the lower left to MaNGA 1-24145. The stellar absorption lines suggest that these two galaxies are at similar redshifts with the line-of-sight velocities differed by 200 . In addition, both galaxies are round and red according to the SDSS images. Therefore, these two galaxies likely form a dry (gas-poor) merger system.

While the stellar velocity field indicates that the main galaxy Satsuki is primarily pressure supported, the gas component reveals more complex structures. The central galaxy shows a weak rotation structure, while there is strong variation in the line-of-sight velocity across Totoro region, redshifted in the left tail and blueshifted in the right tail.

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J17152326+5725585 as an E0 member north of NGC 6338 with tidal-looking asymmetry, a double nucleus, and membership in the high-velocity subgroup.

quotes.quote: The brightest galaxy NGC 6338 (cD, S0, M_ B =13.6 ; z=0.02743 ) and the member galaxy 2MASX J17152326+5725585 hereafter 2MASX J1715; E0, M_ B =15.4 ; z=0.03212 ; smit2004 present some evidence of galaxy-galaxy interaction in the optical band, as shown on the SDSS image (Fig. 1a ). Both NGC 6338 and 2MASX J1715 (located north about 1.3^ from NGC 6338) are elongated in the north-south direction; In particular, the latter contains a double nucleus Fig. 1a berl2006 , and has an asymmetrical optical morphology that are possibly due to tidal forces.

The X-ray peak of the south clump (hereafter S-clump) is consistent with the optical centroid of NGC 6338 within 3^ , and the X-ray peak of the north clump (hereafter N-clump) is found at 2.8h_ 73 ^ 1 kpc ( 5^ ) north of member galaxy 2MASX J1715.

As shown in Figure 7( b ), we find that the galaxies belonging to the high-velocity sub-group are distributed mostly in the north-east part, which includes the member galaxies 2MASX J1715 and NGC 6345 (S/S0, M_ B =14.8 ; z=0.03449 ).

sources.summary: The paper places gal12 among high-mass systems whose H-alpha blobs have high metallicities and merger-associated ionization patterns, treating the system as part of a quenched interaction group.

quotes.quote: The other group includes galaxy system 2, 3, 6, 11, and 12, which present a continuous distribution from the SF region to the AGN region, with the \ blobs sitting in the lower left position of the data distribution. On the other hand, these \ blob spaxels locate to the lower right position relative to the SF sequence, indicating overall high metallicities, which is consistent with the fact that their host galaxies are all high-mass galaxies (see fig:mass_color ). Comparing this with the result we obtain in the previous section, one can see that these two groups are also show certain differences in morphology and environments, which serves as another piece of evidence that the visually found \ blobs in our sample might have different origins.

The \ blobs in the former group (consisting of galaxy system 4, 7, 9, 10, and 13) appear as low-metallicity and off-galaxy starburst or SF regions, while the \ blobs in the latter group (consisting of galaxy system 2, 3, 6, 11, and 12) seem to be associated with galaxy-galaxy interactions/mergers in which the main galaxies are already quenched.

gmw_00001969.name: Mei
gmw_00001969.overview: Mei is an elliptical companion galaxy located southeast of the early-type host galaxy Satsuki [arXiv:2009.06656, arXiv:1702.02464]. The two galaxies share a similar redshift and form a gas-poor, dry merger system [arXiv:2009.06656, arXiv:1702.02464].

sources.summary: The paper describes Satsuki as a massive early-type host galaxy in a dry merger with Mei, near the Totoro gas blob, with low molecular gas and compact nuclear H emission.

quotes.quote: The H blob (nicknamed ``Totoro'') is 3 -- 4 kpc in radius and is 8 kpc away (in projection) from the host galaxy MaNGA target 1-24145 (or MCG+10-24-117; nicknamed ``Satsuki''; 17 ^ h 15 ^ h 23.26 ^ s , +57 ^ 25 58.36 , stellar mass M_ 10 ^ 11 M _ ). The SDSS image indicates that the host galaxy Satsuki has a companion galaxy (nicknamed ``Mei'') located to the south east of Satsuki (Figure fig_cluster_zoom ). These two galaxies are at similar redshifts ( z 0.03), with their line of sight velocity differing by 200 km s ^ 1 . Both galaxies are elliptical and thus form a dry (gas-poor) merger, also known as VII Zw 700.

Moreover, as somewhat expected, an early-type galaxy like Satsuki has a low molecular gas content. While there is a strong and compact H emission at the nucleus of Satsuki, no CO detection is found at this position. Instead, two knots are moderately detected in the northeast and southwest of the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mei as Satsuki's southeastern companion in the VII Zw 700 dry merger; both galaxies are elliptical, close in redshift, and part of the group-group merger region.

quotes.quote: The SDSS image indicates that the host galaxy Satsuki has a companion galaxy (nicknamed ``Mei'') located to the south east of Satsuki (Figure fig_cluster_zoom ). The companion is also within the hexagon bundle field of view (FoV; 32.5 in diameter) of MaNGA. These two galaxies are at similar redshifts ( z 0.03), with their line of sight velocity differing by 200 km s ^ 1 . Both galaxies are elliptical and thus form a dry (gas-poor) merger, also known as VII Zw 700. On the large scale, the dry merger (Satsuki and Mei) is located at the overlap region of a group-group merger Osu19 .

Satsuki and Mei are associated with the less massive northern component, while the nearby large elliptical galaxy NGC 6338 is the brightest galaxy of the more massive group (Figure fig_cluster ). The dry merger (Satsuki and Mei) and NGC 6338 are separated by 42 kpc in projection and by 1400 km s ^ 1 in velocity.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mei as Satsuki’s nearby early-type companion at a similar redshift, forming a likely dry merger system and interacting with Satsuki in the Totoro scenario.

quotes.quote: The SDSS image indicates that MaNGA 1-24145 (nicknamed `Satsuki') has a companion galaxy (nicknamed `Mei') located in the lower left to MaNGA 1-24145. The stellar absorption lines suggest that these two galaxies are at similar redshifts with the line-of-sight velocities differed by 200 . In addition, both galaxies are round and red according to the SDSS images. Therefore, these two galaxies likely form a dry (gas-poor) merger system.
As already mentioned, it has an early-type companion (Mei) just 4 kpc away to the South, which makes them a possible dry merger candidate.
There are several possible scenarios to explain the origins of Totoro: Scenario 1: Totoro is associated with gas tidally stripped from Satsuki during the interaction between Satsuki and Mei.

gmw_00001970.name: Totoro
gmw_00001970.overview: Totoro is a giant emission-line blob that lacks an optical counterpart [arXiv:1702.02464]. It is connected to the nearby galaxy Satsuki by tail-like structures, suggesting the two systems are undergoing a galaxy interaction [arXiv:1702.02464].

sources.summary: The paper presents Totoro as a giant emission-line blob with no optical counterpart, possibly ejected gas or an extremely low-surface-brightness dark galaxy interacting with Satsuki.

quotes.quote: What is striking about this system is that in the upper-right corner of the map, there exists a giant blob (RA = 258.84314; DEC = 57.43529) with which however does not have any optical counterpart in the SDSS images. The size of the blob is 3.2 kpc in radius. This blob (nicknamed `Totoro') is 7.7 kpc away from Satsuki, and is connected to the emission of Satsuki through tail-like structures.

Using the first-year MaNGA data, we discover a giant blob, Totoro, associated with a dry merger system. This object, however, does not have any optical counterparts down to 26.9 mag arcsec ^ 2 in deep CFHT/MegaCam g, , and i images.

Scenario 4: Totoro is an UDG (or alternatively, a LSB galaxy), which falls below the detection limit (26.9 mag arcsec ^ 2 in band) of CFHT imaging data, making it a `dark' galaxy. The morphology of Totoro (Figure 1), especially the (tail) bridge-like structures that connect between Satsuki and Totoro, indicates that Satsuki is likely under interaction with the hosting galaxy of Totoro, which has a comparable physical size as Satsuki.

gmw_00001971.name: Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG)
gmw_00001971.overview: The Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG) of MACS J0417 is classified as an active galaxy [arXiv:1811.02505]. It exhibits strong optical emission lines [arXiv:1811.02505] and is aligned with the dark matter peak of its dynamically evolving host cluster [arXiv:1811.02505].

sources.summary: The paper discusses MACS J0417’s BCG as active, optically line-emitting, aligned with the main dark-matter peak, and central to interpreting offsets in the merging cluster core.

quotes.quote: System 1, nick-named The Doughnut , consists of three images of a complex ring galaxy at z = 0.8718 and a fourth, partial and radial image close to the Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG) only discernible thanks to its strong [ OII ] line emission. The best-fit mass model (rms of 0.38 ) yields a two-dimensional enclosed mass of M( R < 200 kpc ) = (1.77 0.03) 10^ 14 and almost perfect alignment between the peaks of the BCG light and the dark matter of ( 0.5 0.5 ) . We observe a significant misalignment when system 1 radial image is omitted.

Using Chandra to map the intra-cluster gas, we observe an offset between gas and dark-matter of ( 1.7 0.5 ) , and excellent alignment of the X-ray peak with the location of optical emission line associated with the BCG. We interpret all observational evidences in the framework of on-going cluster merger activity, noting specifically that the coincidence between the gas and optical line peaks may be evidence of dense, cold gas cooled directly from the intra-cluster gas.

The Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG) of MACS J0417 shows strong optical emission lines and atypical photometric colours, consistent with a classification of `active' in the framework advanced by green16 , reinforcing the status of MACS J0417 as a dynamically evolving galaxy cluster.

gmw_00001972.name: ESO 417-G06
gmw_00001972.overview: ESO 417-G06 is an active galaxy optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert [arXiv:1505.01166]. It is identified as a Compton-thin candidate and has an observed radio counterpart [arXiv:1505.01166].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 417-G06 as a type 2 Seyfert with a radio counterpart, Compton-thin classification, and spectral variability from hard-X-ray absorber changes.

quotes.quote: ESO 417-G06 This galaxy was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy maia2003 . A radio counterpart was observed with VLA data nagar1999 . It was classified as a Compton thin candidate trippe2011 . This galaxy was observed twice with XMM -Newton in 2009. Long-term variability studies were not found in the literature.

gmw_00001973.name: MARK 1066
gmw_00001973.overview: Mrk 1066 is a barred, early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1002.2534, arXiv:1505.01166] containing a double nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0503070, arXiv:1505.01166] and a heavily obscured Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1001.0130, arXiv:1505.01166]. Its central region features a rotating stellar disk [arXiv:1009.4832, arXiv:2111.10036], a compact molecular disk [arXiv:1009.4832], and a radio jet that drives biconical gas outflows [arXiv:1002.1585, arXiv:1009.4832].

sources.summary: The paper maps Mrk 1066 with near-infrared integral-field spectroscopy, finding spatially varying stellar populations and linking a low-velocity-dispersion nuclear ring to young-intermediate age stars.

quotes.quote: Pastoriza ^ 2 ^ 1 Universidade Federal de Santa Maria, Departamento de F sica, Centro de Ci encias Naturais e Exatas, 97105-900, Santa Maria, RS, Brazil ^ 2 Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul, IF, CP 15051, 91501-970, Porto Alegre, RS, Brazil rogemar@smail.ufsm.br We report the first two-dimensional stellar population synthesis in the near-infrared of the nuclear region of an active galaxy, namely Mrk 1066. We have used integral field spectroscopy with adaptative optics at the Gemini North Telescope to map the to map the age distribution of the stellar population in the inner 300 pc at a spatial resolution of 35 pc. An old stellar population component (age 5 Gyr) is dominant within the inner 160 pc, which we attribute to the galaxy bulge.

Beyond this region, up to the borders of the observation field ( 300 pc), intermediate age components (0.3--0.7 Gyr) dominate. We find a spatial correlation between this intermediate age component and a partial ring of low stellar velocity dispersions ( _* ). Low- _* nuclear rings have been observed in other active galaxies and our result for Mrk 1066 suggests that they are formed by intermediate age stars.

Mrk 1066 Mrk 1066 is an SB0 galaxy harboring a Seyfert 2 nucleus and located at a distance of 48.6 Mpc, for which 1 corresponds to 235 pc at the galaxy. Previous studies of the central region of Mrk 1066 have shown that line emission from high-excitation gas is dominated by gas in a bi-conical outflow oriented along position angle PA=135/315 ^ , which seems to be associated to the radio jet, while the low-excitation gas is more restricted to the plane of the galaxy bower95,nagar99,knop01,paper1,paper2 .

sources.summary: The paper maps Mrk 1066’s inner 350 pc, finding stellar rotation with an oval distortion, an intermediate-age low-dispersion ring, a compact molecular disc, and jet-driven ionized outflows.

quotes.quote: We present stellar and gaseous kinematics of the inner 350 pc radius of the Seyfert galaxy Mrk 1066 derived from J and K _l bands data obtained with the Gemini's Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrograph (NIFS) at a spatial resolution of 35 pc. The stellar velocity field is dominated by rotation in the galaxy plane but shows an S-shape distortion along the galaxy minor axis which seems to be due to an oval structure seen in an optical continuum image. Along this oval, between 170 and 280 pc from the nucleus we find a partial ring of low _* ( 50 ) attributed to an intermediate age stellar population. The velocity dispersion of the stellar bulge ( _* 90 ) implies a super-massive black hole mass of 5.4 10^6 M _ .

The velocity fields for all emission lines are dominated by a similar rotation pattern to that observed for the stars, but are distorted by the presence of two structures: (i) a compact rotating disc with radius 70 pc; (ii) outflows along the radio jet which is oriented approximately along the galaxy major axis. The compact rotating disc is more conspicuous in the 2 \ emitting gas, which presents the smallest values ( 70 ) and most clear rotation pattern, supporting a location in the galaxy plane. We estimate a gas mass for the disc of 10^7 M_ . The 2 \ kinematics further suggests that the nuclear disc is being fed by gas coming from the outer regions.

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 1066 has rotating stellar kinematics, an S-shaped zero-velocity line indicating a nuclear bar or spiral arms, low-sigma rings, and h3-velocity anti-correlation.

quotes.quote: A rotating disk pattern is recognized in the LOS velocity field for all galaxies, with a straight zero velocity line for most of them. For two galaxies -- Mrk 1066 m1066_kin and NGC 5899 (Fig. n5899 ) -- an S shape zero velocity line is observed, which is a signature of the presence of a nuclear bar or spiral arms in these galaxies e.g. combes95,emsellem06 . For most galaxies, the maximum amplitude of the rotation curve is expected to be observed outside of the NIFS FoV. The rotation disk signature is clearly seen in the one one-dimensional plots shown in Fig. vcuts , obtained by averaging the velocity and values within a pseudo-slit with width of 0 25 oriented along the major axis of the galaxy. In addition, distinct structures of low- values ( 50-80 ) are seen in the the maps: low rings or partial rings are seen for Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788.

The velocity dispersion maps show low- ( 50-80 ) rings for 4 galaxies (Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788) or ``patches" of low- structures (for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899) at typical distances of 200 pc, interpreted as being originated in young/intermediate age stellar populations. Centrally peaked maps are observed for three galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388). The h_ 3 moment is anti-correlated with the velocity field for 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066) -- positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where the velocity field shows blueshifts and h_ 3 <0 for locations where the velocity field shows redshifts. The presence of these wings are attributed to the contribution of stars from the galaxy bulge that present lower rotation velocities. The observed velocity fields are well reproduced by a rotating disk model, with deprojected velocity amplitudes in the range 60--340 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1066 as a FIR-luminous early-type galaxy with a double nucleus and newly detected H2O maser components bracketing its systemic velocity.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1066 is a FIR luminous ( L_ FIR 7 10 ^ 10 L _ ) SB0+ galaxy, containing a double nucleus (e.g. Gimeno et al. 2004). It is one of the few early-type galaxies that have been detected in CO (see Henkel Wiklind 1997; note that their FIR luminosity (their Table II) is too low). Its systemic velocity is 3605 km s ^ 1 (see Table 1), corresponding to a distance of 50 Mpc.

There is a strong narrow maser spike at 3636 km s ^ 1 with a full width to half maximum linewidth of less than 2 km s ^ 1 (Figs. mrk1066a and mrk1066b ) and a peak flux density of 80 mJy. The spike becomes narrower between March 7 and 10, 2002, appears to be unresolved in frequency in May and reaches only 50 mJy in September. It seems that a gradual decrease of the linewidth is finally accompanied by a decrease in peak flux density. At a distance of 50 Mpc, isotropic luminosities reach 10 L _ . A second much wider component, at 3550 km s ^ 1 , has a peak flux density of 10--20 mJy and an isotropic luminosity of 40 L _ .

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 1066 as a marginal Seyfert whose OASIS maps reveal bar-aligned emission, an off-center low-excitation reddened feature, and radio/optical alignment.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1066 While being classified as a marginal Seyfert by Oster83 , a high-excitation component of the spectral lines was found by Bower95 . Radio emission was observed at PA 135 by Haniff ; and Ulve89 , and the narrow-band images indicated an alignment of the optical [ O iii ] emission with the radio jet Bower95,MulObs . The direction of both types of emission then agrees approximately with the orientation of the galactic bar of PA 143 Mazza93,Bower95 . According to Bower95 , the velocities in H + [ N ii ] are consistent with rotation, probably in the galactic disc, while [ O iii ] shows little or no rotation, which they interpret as an outflow.

A prominent emission feature located in the east is seen in the hydrogen lines and partially in [ N ii ] and [ S ii ] , while absent in [ O iii ] , and is associated with high interstellar reddening, low excitation, and a small velocity dispersion (Figs. Mrk1066maps and Mrk1066vel ). We conclude that it is probably a region that has not been ionised by the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper maps the inner 700 pc of Seyfert galaxy Mrk 1066, identifying unresolved hot dust, extended radio and near-IR line emission, jet-related gas, extinction, and star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: We present integral field spectroscopy of the inner 700 700 pc ^2 of the Seyfert galaxy Mrk 1066 obtained with Gemini's Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrograph (NIFS) at a spatial resolution of 35 pc. This high spatial resolution allowed us to observe, for the first time in this galaxy, an unresolved dust concentration with mass 1.4 10^ 2 M_ . This unresolved concentration, with emission well reproduced by a blackbody with temperature 830 K, is possibly part of the nuclear dusty torus.

Mrk 1066 was selected for this study because it presents extended radio e.g ulvestad89,nagar99 and near-IR line emission e.g. rodriguez-ardila05a,knop01 allowing us to investigate the role of the radio jet in the emission of the NLR gas. In addition, its central kpc has a high extinction in the optical e.g. stoklasova09 and thus a near-IR study is better suited for the investigation of the gas excitation and kinematics. Mrk 1066 is an SBO+ galaxy harboring a Seyfert 2 nucleus; it is located at a distance of 48.6 Mpc Distance quoted in NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED -- http://nedwww.ipac.caltech.edu) , for which 1 ^ corresponds to 235 pc at the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports a peculiar [ FeII ] line-to-continuum distribution in Mrk 1066, co-rotating disk gas, and a centrally peaked velocity-dispersion profile across its central kiloparsec.

quotes.quote: The H _2 line-to-continuum ratio is constant or drops significantly towards the nucleus in most galaxies, except NGC 2273, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. For Br , in contrast, the ratio peaks towards the nucleus in many galaxies, consistent with stronger ionization mechanisms towards the active nucleus. These two results both explain the observed central drop of the H _2 /Br ratio. Of the three emission lines, the [ FeII ] line-to-continuum ratio shows the most distinct radial variations. Its profile is centrally peaked in Mrk 1, Mrk 3, NGC 262, NGC 1320, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. Several sources show two maxima (or a central drop, e.g. NGC 1667, NGC 3185, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 4388, and NGC 5506). Finally, Mrk 1066, NGC 931, NGC 2110, NGC 2992, and NGC 4051 show peculiar distributions, e.g. the distribution in NGC 2110 shows three separate peaks.

However, the gas velocity curves are similar to those of the stars, which define the galaxy disk. This is the case in Mrk 1, Mrk 1066, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1667, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 3185, NGC 4388, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506. In these galaxies, all three emission lines display a velocity curve that follows that of the stars and is reminiscent of rotation, thus strongly implying that the line-emitting gas is located in the disk of the galaxy and rotating with the disk. Other galaxies (Mrk 3, NGC 1320, NGC 2992, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, and NGC 7450) display velocity curves different from the stellar velocity curve in one or more emission lines, which may be a signature of non-rotational motion, either in- or outflows or other non-planar motion of the gas.

The radial dispersion profiles in Mrk 3, Mrk 1066, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506 have a central peak, which indicates a higher kinetic gas temperature in the nucleus. Either the ISM is more turbulent (possibly caused by a jet- or wind-driven outflow), and/or more intensely heated by the incident radiation field. In either case, a peaked dispersion distribution is a strong indication for the AGN governing the physical state of the gas at the nucleus. The dispersion peak is most often present in [ FeII ] (indicating dust destruction close to the nucleus), and/or in Br (indicating a strong radiation field), while the H _2 line shows the least-pronounced central rise. Examples for flat velocity dispersion profiles are NGC 3081, NGC 3185, and NGC 4388, which show no significant change over up to 1.2 kpc in diameter. The velocity dispersion in all three sources is of order 100 km/s, well within the range observed for star forming regions, which is consistent with star formation being the dominant the gas excitation mechanism. Finally, the dispersion profiles in Mrk 1, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1320, NGC 1667, NGC 2992, and NGC 7450 show marked differences between the three emission lines, with variations of 100 km/s or more at a given radius. This indicates that the ionized atomic and molecular gas is responding differently to the central AGN in these sources, perhaps due to the presences of asymmetric energy input (a jet or shocks) together with a stratified medium, e.g. a thin molecular disk in a thick ionized medium.

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 1066 has the sample's smallest Seyfert 2 opening angle, with northwest emission knots modeled as one cone obscured by the host disk.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1066 Mrk 1066 hosts the Seyfert 2 AGN with the smallest opening angle in our sample with a _ max of 25 ^ . The modeled kinematics provide a good fit to the data. The majority of the detected emission resides in three knots northwest of the nucleus, which can be modeled as a single cone inclined 10 ^ out of the plane of the sky. Bow95 creates an [ O III ] + H emission-line image that depicts a similar single cone to the northwest of the AGN nucleus with a _ max of 23 ^ . At the modeled orientation, including the disk geometry determined by Kin00 , the bicone does not intersect with the host disk, which obscures the unobserved second cone, similar to Circinus.

sources.summary: The paper describes MARK 1066 as an early-type spiral type 2 Seyfert with a double nucleus, radio jet, extended soft emission, and heavy obscuration.

quotes.quote: MARK 1066 MARK 1066 is an early-type spiral galaxy afanasev1981 showing a double nucleus gimeno2004 . It was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert by , and broad lines were not detected in polarized light gu2002 . A radio counterpart showing a jet was found by . At X-rays, extended soft emission can be observed, aligned with a nuclear spiral structure observed at optical frequencies, also aligned with the IR emission (see Appendix multiimages ). found this to be a heavily obscured AGN, with N_H > 10^ 24 cm^ 2 and an equivalent width of the Fe line 3 keV using ROSAT and ASCA data, i.e., it was classified as a Compton thick candidate.

sources.summary: Mrk 1066 is classified as SB0 and hosts a Seyfert 2 AGN, with low S/N data but a prominent nuclear featureless continuum, old stellar populations, rotating stellar kinematics, and nuclear reddening and metallicity drops.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1066 This galaxy is classified as SB0 and hosts a Seyfert 2 AGN. It is one of the galaxies with the lowest S/N of our sample. Nonetheless, it is still possible to detect a very prominent contribution of a FC at the nucleus, with mostly old stellar populations throughout the datacube. Regarding the stellar kinematics, we found that the stars are well reproduced by a disk in rotation, with a slight S-shaped assymetry, and a nearly constant stellar . Lastly, we detected a drop in both A _ V and metallicity in the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1066 as a Seyfert 2 with weak AGN signatures, no detected broad permitted-line components, and the sample's strongest H2 emission.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1066 Bower95 reported low-excitation emission line ratios similar to those of LINERs for this Seyfert 2 galaxy, from ground-based spectroscopy. Spectropolarimetric observations of Mrk 1066 have not revealed broad components in the permitted lines Miller90 .
From our near-infrared spectrum, Mrk 1066 seems to be a Seyfert 2 with relatively weak AGN signatures.
Mrk 1066 is the object in our small sample of galaxies with more intense H _ 2 emission.

sources.summary: The paper uses Gemini NIFS data for Mrk 1066 to show Gauss-Hermite fits better capture near-infrared emission-line profiles and reveal asymmetric outflowing gas driven by the radio jet.

quotes.quote: The profit routine was used to fit the [ Fe ii ] =1.257 m emission-line profiles for about 1800 spectra of the inner 350 pc of the Seyfert galaxy Mrk 1066 obtained with Gemini NIFS and shows that the line profiles are better reproduced by Gauss-Hermite series than by the commonly used Gaussian curves. The two-dimensional map of the h_3 Gauss-Hermite moment shows its highest absolute values in regions close to the edge of the radio structure. These high values may be originated in an biconical outflowing gas associated with the radio jet -- previously observed in the optical [ O iii ] emission. The analysis of this kinematic component indicates that the radio jet leaves the center of the galaxy with the north-west side slightly oriented towards us and the south-east side away from us, being partially hidden by the disc of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 1066 as a comparison active galaxy whose NIR stellar-population maps show an intermediate-age component aligned with a low-velocity-dispersion nuclear ring.

quotes.quote: In addition, we have mapped SPCs spatial distributions and performed the first two-dimensional (2D) SP synthesis in the NIR of the nuclear region of an active galaxy Mrk 1066, rogemar10b . A spatial correlation between the intermediate age SPC and a partial ring of low stellar velocity dispersions ( _* ) was found, supporting the interpretation that the low- _* structures commonly observed in the inner few hundreds parsecs of Seyfert galaxies are due to colder regions with more recent star formation than the underlying bulge barbosa06,rogemar08,rogemar09a,rogemar10d .

Interestingly, very similar results were found by rogemar10b for Mrk 1066. Thus, the results found for Mrk 1157 and Mrk 1066 support the use of low stellar velocity dispersion as a tracer of young-intermediate age stars in the galaxy centre, thus confirming the interpretation of the above studies.

sources.summary: The atlas treats Mrk 1066 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1066 . rm99 describe this Seyfert 2 galaxy as a dusty object with a single broad dust lane dominating its morphology. It is an FIR luminous galaxy containing a double nucleus gim04 . Recently, water vapor maser emission was detected hen05 , with two components bracketing the systemic velocity of its parent galaxy. The non-simultaneous J- and K band spectroscopy of vgh97 shows strong [ Fe ii ] and Pa , with weak excess of emission seeing at the sides of both lines. They put stringent constraints on the flux of broad Br and Pa .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1066 as a starburst Sy2 galaxy with star-forming knots about 400 pc from the center and AGN-dominated near-infrared lines near the nucleus.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1066 is a starburst galaxy with a Sy2 nucleus. Ramos-Almeida14 found star-forming knots at 400 pc of the galaxy center, after subtracting the AGN component. Alonso-Herrero14 suggest that close to the center ( 125 pc) the near-IR lines are dominated by the AGN processes.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1066 as a barred galaxy with soft thermal emission, an Fe line, and a completely absorbed intrinsic AGN, but no detected extended HRI emission.

quotes.quote: Only in the case of Mrk 1066 do we fail to find any evidence for extended emission.
Mrk 1066 Mrk 1066 has a bar but is not a member of a group or cluster.
In the spectrum of Mrk 1066, we observe a single power law component, soft thermal emission, and an Fe line.

sources.summary: Mrk 1066 is described as a buried-AGN composite with a small radio jet and a perpendicular X-ray halo attributed to starburst-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: We interpret the low foreground column density and lack of a second, absorbed (in the \ bandpass) power-law component in NGC 1068, Mrk 1066, Mrk 266, and NGC 7130 as evidence that the intrinsic AGN is completely buried and fully absorbed by N_H 10^ 24 cm^ 2 .
Mrk 1066 also has a small (300 pc) linear radio jet and coincident [ O 3 ] emission Bow95 .
In the more inclined examples (Mrk 1066 and Mrk 78), the X-ray halos extend most obviously perpendicular to the planes of the galaxies, as expected from starburst-driven outflows.

gmw_00001974.name: MARK 1210
gmw_00001974.overview: Mrk 1210 is a face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1610.06243, arXiv:1705.09762] classified as a Seyfert 2 [arXiv:1610.06243, arXiv:2401.06972] with a nuclear spiral structure [arXiv:1505.01166] and a circumnuclear starburst [arXiv:1610.06243, arXiv:astro-ph/0406402]. It exhibits long-term X-ray variability [arXiv:1902.05545], changing between Compton-thick and Compton-thin states [arXiv:1902.05545, arXiv:astro-ph/0608534], and hosts an H2O megamaser [arXiv:1801.06332, arXiv:astro-ph/0402142].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1210 as a Seyfert 2 Sa galaxy with circumnuclear starburst evidence, hidden BLR signatures, broad Pa emission, and possible NLR outflows.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1210 is a nearby Sa galaxy, hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus veron10 . The spectral energy distribution peaking near 60 m made it a member of the sample of the so-called 60 m peakers galaxies (60PKs, heisler99 ). In the optical, found Wolf-Rayet features in the central 200 pc of the galaxy, indicating the presence of a circumnuclear starburst. Moreover, broad H and H components (FWHM 2400 ) were detected in the polarized light ( tran92 ; tran95 ). Near infrared spectra reported by show a Pa profile characterized by a strong narrow component on top of a broad base with FWHM 1600 , suggesting the presence of a hidden broad line region.

In summary, we found evidence for a broad component in the Pa , having FWHM 1900 , that can be attributed to the BLR emission. This finding is also supported by the detection of broad H and H in polarized light, indicating the presence of a hidden broad line region. Furthermore, the detection of a blueshifted intermediate component both in the [ Fe II ] and in the Pa could be attributed to the presence of outflows in the NLR which are compatible to the observations of the [ O III ] and [ Fe II ] line profiles by .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 1210 as an outlying Seyfert 2/S1h nucleus with high diagnostic ratios, a polarized BLR, rich coronal-line emission, and hotter dust continuum.

quotes.quote: The three outliers The three Seyfert 2s labeled outliers (yellow filled dots in Fig. fig:fig4 ) correspond to the galaxies ESO 138-G01, Mrk 1210 and NGC 4507. Although the densities inferred from the \ doublet are comparable to the other sample objects, their \ values are significantly higher than the average of the other nuclei. They depart from the averaged blue dots position in Fig. fig:fig4 a by more than seven times the cluster dispersion in .

Interestingly, a polarized BLR component has been detected in Mrk 1210 Tr92,Tr95,St98 and NGC 4507 Mo00 . They were classified as S1h by VC10,VC06 . They possibly represent borderline cases between Type I and II AGNs. With respect to ESO 138-G01, the detailed analysis of ABBP reveals a strong stratification of the emitting gas clouds that correlates with the density as well as with the presence of high ionization Fe species. Mrk 1210 presents a very rich coronal line spectrum that includes [ Si X ] , which is a very high excitation line not frequently detected Ma07 .
Mrk 1210 and NGC 4507, the average index is 0.46 , indicating that the continuum emission originates from dust that is hotter than in typical Seyfert 2s.

sources.summary: The paper highlights Mrk 1210 as kinematically decoupled, with narrow H2, strong inner star formation, low rotational temperature, and likely circumnuclear-starburst excitation.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1210 is another interesting case for a kinematical decoupling of the molecular gas. The forbidden NLR lines are remarkably similar in FWHM (see Table FWHM ); however, H _ 2 (1-0)S(1) 2.121 m is significantly narrower, almost half the FWHM value of the [ Fe ii ] 1.257 m (Figure perfis b). Radio observations in the CO(1 0) 115 GHz and CO(2 1) 230 GHz carried out on this object (Raluy et al. ) show a high level of star forming activity within the inner 5 Kpc.

For this galaxy, we derived a T_ rot 900 K. We already know that UV fluorescence has at most, a 30 contribution. It means that the low T_ rot can be attributed to UV heating and this process can be the main source of 2 \ excitation. UV heating models (Black van Dishoek ), where FUV photons emitted by OB stars (photodissociation regions) heat the gas, predict excitation temperatures of 1000K for the 2 \ thermal component. Such low values are typically found in starburst galaxies (Mouri ), suggesting that the 2 \ emission observed in Mrk 1210 may primarily come from a circumnuclear starburst.

sources.summary: The paper classifies Mrk 1210 as the Phoenix Galaxy, a face-on Sc Seyfert with clockwise spiral arms, a 170 pc nuclear feature, and uncertain disk/ring morphology.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1210 We classify Mrk 1210 (the Phoenix Galaxy) as a face-on Sc galaxy. It has been discovered to be a Seyfert galaxy by 1983Balzano and its luminosity distance is 59.5 Mpc 2000Mould . A jet position angle has been measured by to be 125 at 50 pc. The spiral structure is wound clockwise with two arms clearly visible.

We identify a 0.6 arcsec ( 170 pc) nuclear feature characterized by a flattening in the position angle profile and a maximum in ellipticity. No dust features seem to be associated with it in the galaxy's structure map. However, spiral structure appears to be visible around it, so we find it likely to be either a disk or a ring.

sources.summary: The paper shows Mrk 1210's red and blue maser clusters have ambiguous geometry, considers a tilted warped disk, and estimates 0.2-2.2e7 solar masses if disk-like.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1210 The Mrk 1210 VLBI map in Figure mapa shows redshifted and blueshifted maser features in separate clusters separated by 7 mas or 2 pc , with a PA 120^ . A single maser component detected near the systemic velocity falls between the two clusters but offset from the connecting line. While the general configuration of maser features is roughly consistent with disk rotation, the exact nature of the maser system remains inconclusive. If indeed that masers arise in a disk, the misalignment of the maser features suggests the potential of warping or eccentricity in the disk, or an inclination different from edge-on.

Assuming a disk origin and approximating the radius as half the full extent of the megamaser emission, and the rotation velocity as the velocity offset of the high-velocity masers from the systemic velocity, we estimated the mass enclosed within each high-velocity maser component. These values are distributed over a wide range from 0.2 to 2.2 10^7 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper reports VLBA continuum structure in Mrk 1210, compares it with earlier 6 cm observations, and discusses its rotating maser configuration and long-term X-ray obscuration variability.

quotes.quote: Five out of 18 galaxies observed were detected at 8 or higher levels (Mrk 0001, Mrk 1210, Mrk 1419, NGC 2273 and UGC 3193). While all these sources are known to have maser disks, four of these five sources exhibit a maser disk with known orientation.

While for three sources morphologies are very similar in both observations, for Mrk 1210 the southeastern component was not detected in the data set from the first observations and the northwestern extended emission is less prominent in this data, likely due to insufficient sensitivities. A former 6 cm VLBA observation of this galaxy has shown a similar structure as our re-observations which is shown in Fig. fig:images1 , except that the northwestern component is resolved into four sub-components middelberg2004 .

Mrk 1210 shows long-term variability in the X-rays and has changed from Compton-thick to Compton-thin over a period of approximately 17 years masini2017 . This variability was interpreted as a combination of the intrinsic variability of the AGN and presence of an obscuring cloud passing through the line of sight.

sources.summary: The paper presents BeppoSAX/XMM-Newton evidence that Mrk 1210 is a variable Seyfert 2 whose hard ASCA component was reflected emission and whose torus absorption changed dramatically.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 2 galaxy Mrk 1210 was found to exhibit a flat hard X-ray component by ASCA, although ASCA could not distinguish whether it is an absorbed direct component or a reflected one. We then observed Mrk 1210 with BeppoSAX, and found that the X-ray spectral properties are quite different from those of ASCA, as have been confirmed with XMM-Newton; the flux is significantly higher than that in the ASCA observation, and a clear absorption cut-off appears below 5 keV. A bright hard X-ray emission is detected up to 100 keV.

The reflection component is necessary to describe the BeppoSAX PDS spectrum, and represents the ASCA hard component very well. Therefore, the hard component in the ASCA spectrum is a reflected one, whose intensity is almost constant over 6 years. This indicates that a dramatic spectral variability is attributed to a large change of the absorption column density by a factor of >5 , rather than the variability of the nuclear emission.

sources.summary: The paper presents optical and near-infrared spectroscopy of Mrk 1210, describing its Seyfert 2 nucleus, circumnuclear star formation, dusty obscuration, outflow signatures, and peculiar X-ray state changes.

quotes.quote: Near-infrared and optical spectroscopy of the nuclear and extended emission region of the Seyfert 2 galaxy Mrk 1210 is presented. This galaxy is outstanding because it displays signatures of recent circumnuclear star formation and a high-level of X-ray activity, in addition to the classical spectral characteristics typical of an AGN.

Mrk 1210, also known as the Phoenix Galaxy, is a nearby Sa Seyfert 2 galaxy at z=0.01350 . It has been extensively studied in the optical region, mainly because of the Wolf-Rayet features found within the central 200 pc (Storchi-Bergmann et al. 1998), indicating the presence of a circumnuclear starburst, and the detection of broad H and H components in polarized light (Tran et al. 1992; Tran 1995).

In X-rays, Mrk 1210 is peculiar. It is one of the very few cases of an AGN caught in a dramatic transition between a Compton-thick, reflection dominated state, and a Compton-thin state (Guainazzi et al. 2002).

sources.summary: MARK 1210 is a Seyfert 2 with broad emission-line components, a compact ENLR, and strong optical and infrared coronal-line emission.

quotes.quote: MARK 1210: This galaxy has been imaged with HST in the F606W filter by Malkan98 . All the emission lines in this Seyfert 2 galaxy have a broad ( 475 km s ^ 1 ) component. The ENLR is confined within the central 1.0 kpc, in agreement with the IR results of Mazzalay07 . The coronal lines are strong - see Figure fig:fig4 , and the IR coronal spectrum has been extensively studied by Mazzalay07 . Coronal lines of [ S 8 ] , [ S 9 ] , [ Si 7 ] , [ Si 10 ] , [ and [ Ca 8 ] were detected.

sources.summary: The paper describes MARK 1210 as a type 2 Seyfert with polarized broad lines, nuclear spiral structure, compact radio counterpart, and changing-look Compton-thick/thin behavior.

quotes.quote: MARK 1210 This galaxy, also called the Phoenix galaxy or UGC 4203, was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert by . Broad lines have been observed in polarized light using spectropolarimetric data tran1992, tran1995 . The HST image shows a nuclear spiral structure (see Appendix multiimages ). A very compact radio counterpart was found with VLA at 3.5 cm, with no evidence of jet structure falcke1998 . At X-rays, a point like source is observed in the 4.5-8.0 keV energy band (see Appendix multiimages ).

sources.summary: The paper attributes Mrk 1210's broad forbidden-line structure to redshifted and blueshifted outflow components and places the obscured nucleus at the kinematic center.

quotes.quote: In Mrk 1210, a redshifted component R contributes to the exceptional width of [ O i ] 6300 . An additional blueshifted component B strong in [ O iii ] allows to fit the H + [ N ii ] blend without a broad component of H . B and R are likely to be outflow components. The location of the brightness maximum BM is slightly ( 1 ) shifted relative to the kinematical center KC. Locating the true obscured nucleus in KC makes it understandable that BM displays faint broad scattered H in polarized light.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 1210 as an amorphous Seyfert 2 galaxy whose near-UV HST emission closely follows a 15 mas one-sided MERLIN radio jet.

quotes.quote: High resolution MERLIN 5 GHz observations (0 04) of 7 Seyfert galaxies, selected as the ones previously showing evidence of collimated ejection, have been compared with high resolution archive HST data. The radio maps reveal rich structures in all the galaxies. NGC 2639 and TXFS 2226-184 have multiple knot parsec-scale extended structures, Mrk 1034, Mrk 1210, NGC 4922C and NGC 5506 reveal one-sided jets, while IC 1481 exhibits a jet-like extension. The close correlation between the radio-emitting relativistic plasma and the ionized gas in the inner regions of these galaxies allows us to study in detail the physics close to the center of low luminosity AGN.

Mrk 1210: The near-UV emission revealed in a high resolution (0 027) ACS/HRC HST (F330W) image of Mrk 1210 follows closely the 15 mas one-sided jet unveiled in the MERLIN 5 GHz map (Fig. mkn1210 ) of this amorphous Seyfert 2 galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper highlights Mrk 1210 as the sample object with the largest chi-squared value, whose best fit leaves significant short-wavelength residuals but resembles the proposed composition.

quotes.quote: In addition, we show in Figure fig:best_fit_Mrk1210 the best fit of the spectrum of Mrk 1210 because it is the object with the largest ^2_ value among those of the sample we analysed, and the only one with ^2_ >2 . It shows residuals within 5-10 at >10 \ m but at <10 \ m the model does not fit the data well, which is the reason for its ^2_ value of 4.88.

For Mrk 1210, the residuals of the particular combination are not very different from the ones of the best fit. This is because both compositions are very similar, the only difference is that the best fit includes CDEOl01 instead of MieOl01, which have almost the same behavior (see Fig. fig:Qext_silicates_sizes ).

sources.summary: The Brackett profile in Mkn 1210 can be split into narrow and broad components, but matching [Si IX] wings led the authors to reject a BLR interpretation.

quotes.quote: The Brackett line of Mkn 1210, for example, can be plausibly decomposed into a broad and a narrow component. We decided against a BLR interpretation, however, because the [ Si IX ] line shows wide wings as well, the two profiles being indistinguishable within the S/N limitations. This argues against the interpretation of Veilleux et al. ( ) who considered Mkn 1210 a BLR detection (but see caveats in their appendix). Their [ Fe II ] and Paschen profiles are very similar to our [ Si IX ] and Brackett profiles, while we do not find evidence for the FWHM 3000 km/s component possibly seen in their Brackett data.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1210 as an emission-line-rich Seyfert 2 with young starburst light dominating the blue continuum, plus both a hidden broad-line region and a very young starburst.

quotes.quote: In several cases, however, the emission line spectrum is so rich that the unmasked spectrum contains relatively few stellar features apart from the continuum shape (eg, Mrk 1210, NGC 4507).
In Mrk 1210, NGC 5135 and NGC 7410 they account for more than half of the 4020 flux, and in several other Seyfert 2s their contribution exceeds 20 .

An illustrative example in this respect is Mrk 1210, with D_n(4000) = 1.3 and H _A = 1.6 . This galaxy has both a hidden BLR detected through spectropolarimetry (Tran, Miller Kay 1992) and a very young starburst, responsible for its WR bump (Storchi Bergmann, Cid Fernandes Schmitt 1998; Joguet 2001).

sources.summary: The atlas treats Mrk 1210 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1210 . Optically classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy, this object shows broad polarized lines in H and H tran92 . The NIR nuclear spectrum, studied in detailed by mza05 , is dominated by H i and He i recombination lines as well as [ S ii ] , [ S iii ] and 2 \ forbidden lines. Coronal lines of [ S viii ] , [ S ix ] , [ Si vi ] , [ Si x ] , and [ Ca viii ] in addition to molecular H _ 2 lines are also detected. The analysis of the emission line profiles, both allowed and forbidden, shows a narrow ( FWHM 500 ) line on top of a broad ( FWHM > 1000 ) component, ruling out the presence of a hidden BLR claimed to be present in earlier NIR observations vgh97 and confirming the results of lut02 . mza05 reports extended emission of [ S iii ] and He i , up to a distance of 500 pc from the center.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 1210 as an amorphous Seyfert 2 galaxy with an H2O megamaser and steep-spectrum components whose nuclear location is unclear.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1210 Mrk 1210 is an almost face-on, amorphous Seyfert 2 galaxy.
Mrk 1210 hosts an H_2O megamaser with an isotropic luminosity of 84 L_ ( 1994ApJ...437L..99B ) which might indicate the presence of an edge-on circumnuclear molecular disk or torus.
In Mrk 1210 , both the SE and the NW components have steep radio spectra and here the location of the nucleus is unclear.

gmw_00001975.name: IC 2560
gmw_00001975.overview: IC 2560 is a nearby barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0209245] containing a pseudobulge [arXiv:1012.3073], a nuclear disk [arXiv:1602.06960], and clear dust structures [arXiv:1304.4254]. It hosts a Compton-thick, Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1408.5414, arXiv:1505.01166] that powers a water megamaser disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0209245, arXiv:2101.07736].

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 2560 as the faintest target, finding a Compton-thick, reflection-dominated Seyfert nucleus with poor soft-X-ray statistics, a steep intrinsic photon index, and strong iron lines.

quotes.quote: We present X-ray spectral analyses for three Seyfert 2 active galactic nuclei, NGC 424, NGC 1320, and IC 2560, observed by in the 3--79 keV band. The high quality hard X-ray spectra allow detailed modeling of the Compton reflection component for the first time in these sources. Using quasi-simultaneous and data, as well as archival data, we find that all three nuclei are obscured by Compton-thick material with column densities in excess of 5 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , and that their X-ray spectra above 3 keV are dominated by reflection of the intrinsic continuum on Compton-thick material. Due to the very high obscuration, absorbed intrinsic continuum components are not formally required by the data in any of the sources. We constrain the intrinsic photon indices and the column density of the reflecting medium through the shape of the reflection spectra.

IC 2560 is the faintest target considered in this paper, and correspondingly has the poorest photon statistics. The quasi-simultaneous observation is too short to provide useful soft X-ray data, so for the initial modeling we use the data alone. From the best fit of the approximate model we find that the intrinsic photon index is steeper than in the other two targets ( =2.2_ 0.4 ^ +0.3 ) and that the iron lines are strong; the equivalent widths of the Fe K and K lines are 2.1_ 0.5 ^ +1.3 keV and 0.6_ 0.4 ^ +0.6 keV, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper decomposes IC2560 into a large-scale disk with spiral arms, an X-shaped bulge/bar, a nuclear disk, and an extended envelope, emphasizing how those components affect bulge fitting.

quotes.quote: IC2560 (Figures fig:IC2560-1 and fig:IC2560-2 ) contains a large-scale disk with well-defined spiral arms at intermediate radii. The disk is detectable out to 150'' ( 30 ) along the major axis (with PA = -50 ) and has an axis ratio of 0.6 ; it grows rounder at large radius. The tightly-wound spiral arms range from 6-12 kpc ( 30-60" ) and broadly resemble a ring. The central X-shaped bulge/bar dominates the light inside 10'' ( 2 ). Finally, the surface-brightness profile exhibits a ``knee'' at 1'' ( 0.2 ) that corresponds to a nuclear disk.

Our best-fit model (Figure fig:IC2560-1 ) also includes a faint nuclear disk that is exposed in the residual image and indicated by profile inflections around 1'' . Although the formal ^2 improves only marginally when the disk is added, we see spiral structure associated with the nuclear disk in the residual image, and the disk model perfectly fits the apparent knee in the profile at SMA 1'' ( 0.2 ). The bulge component changes when the nuclear disk is included, growing by 1 (a factor of two) and 0.34 , as it is no longer trying to fit the very compact nuclear disk.

sources.summary: The paper fits IC 2560 as a Compton-thick AGN whose spectrum needs no secondary power law but does require Fe-complex Gaussian components.

quotes.quote: Our sample consists of 10 local ( z<0.1 ) CT AGN observed by : NGC 424, NGC 1068, 2MFGC 2280, NGC 1320, NGC 1386, NGC 3079, IC 2560, Mrk 34, NGC 4945 and the Circinus galaxy. With the exception of 2MFGC 2280, these sources were selected to be known CT AGN and to cover a wide range in X-ray luminosity. Particular attention was paid to selecting sources in the low ( 10^ 42 ) and high ( 10^ 44 ) luminosity regimes, thus enveloping the bulk of the local CT AGN population, which is at moderate luminosities, so that our sample could be as representative as possible, despite its small size.

No secondary power-law was required in the fit for IC 2560. For NGC 1068, NGC 4945 and Circinus where only \ data were used, the apec model was not required. This is expected since this model generally describes emission outside the \ bandpass. The spectra of NGC 424 and NGC 1320 also did not require the apec model. NGC 1068 has a well known Fe complex at 6-7 keV, consisting of neutral Fe K and emission, plus ionised emission at 6.7 and 6.96 keV. While the torus model accounts for the neutral emission, we add Gaussian components fixed at the energies of the ionised emission to account for these lines, where the widths are fixed at small values (1 eV). As found by and for Circinus and for NGC 4945, we also find that the \ spectra of these AGN require an additional Gaussian component each to model lines in the Fe complex, fixed at 6.7 keV in NGC 4945 and allowed to vary around 6.6 keV for Circinus, as required by the data. Additional Gaussian components at the energies of the Fe complex were required to fit the spectra of NGC 1320, NGC 1386 and IC 2560. We summarize all the models used for each source in Table table_cnomo . We allow the cross-normalization between the two \ focal plane modules (FPMs) to vary in the fitting to account for instrumental cross-calibration. We also allow the cross-normalization between the FPMs and \ to vary to account for instrumental cross-calibration and the fact that the the \ observations are not simultaneous with . We fix the other parameters to each other since there is a known agreement between spectral parameters determined from \ and \ e.g. walton13,walton14 . We present the cross-normalizations in Table table_cnomo . The observations are mainly consistent with the cross-normalization between the FPMs being unity, with the exceptions being NGC 1068, NGC 1320 and Circinus. NGC 1068 and Circinus both show that the normalization of FPMB to be 3 greater than the normalization of the FPMA. For NGC 1320 the FPMB normalization is 9 lower. also investigate the cross normalization between the two FPMs for NGC 424, NGC 1320 and IC 2560, and our results agree. Full details of \ in-orbit calibration is presented in Madsen et al. (in preparation). Column (2) gives the cross-normalization between the two FPMs, column (3) gives the cross-normalization between FMPA and \ (MOS 1 and MOS 2 respectively for IC 2560 and pn and combined MOS for Mrk 34) and column (4) lists the models used to fit each of the spectra. Our spectral fitting with the torus model reproduces the data well in all ten sources, with reasonable reduced . We confirm the Compton-thick nature of all 10 AGN, with \ constraints in excess of 1.5 10^ 24 . The Compton-thick nature of 2MFGC 2280 is shown here for the first time. The best-fit spectral parameters are presented in Table table_specpar , along with their 90 confidence intervals calculated using a ^2=4.61 criterion on two interesting parameters in order to minimize degeneracies. The uncertainty on the luminosity is propagated from the uncertainty on the normalization of the torus model. Details of the parameters of the components used to fit the soft X-ray excesses are listed in Table table_specpar3 . The best fit \ measured in our sample spans a large range, from 26--80 , limited by the allowed range of the model. The uncertainties on \ range from 5--40 , with the best constraints coming from NGC 1068, NGC 4945 and Circinus which have the highest signal-to-noise spectra in the sample. While the fit to Mrk 34 gives a fairly large , the constraints are poor, with statistically allowed values ranging from 26--80 . The case is similar for 2MFGC 2280, albeit with slightly tighter constraints. In two sources, NGC 3079 and NGC 4945, we discover bimodality in statistic space for , with local minima at small and large opening angles. We show their \ as a function of \ in Figure fig_thetacon . For NGC 3079, the large opening angle provides a marginally better fit, whereas for NGC 4945 the small opening angle is a significantly better fit. For two sources, NGC 1386 and NGC 4945, we find that the best-fit \ reaches the lower limit on this parameter (26 ), which implies that both the sources are highly covered. We also find that the values for these fits deviate significantly from the canonical value of 1.9 (steep at 2.52 for NGC 1386 and flat for NGC 4945 at 1.58). The fact that the deviations from the canonical value occur at the edge of the \ parameter space suggests that the true \ of these sources lies outside of the range of the torus model (i.e. < 26 ). also present a model of a fully covered source (i.e. 0 \ opening angle), which includes variable iron and elemental abundances, known as sphere . We fit this model, with the iron abundance free, in place of the torus model for NGC 1386 and NGC 4945, and present the results in Table table_specpar . This is also done for NGC 3079 since the small opening angle also provides a good fit to that source. An improvement in the fit statistic is found for both NGC 3079 and NGC 4945, where the spherical model produces a significantly better ( > 90 significance) fit than the torus model for both ( ^2 =4 and 136, respectively). The iron abundance has been estimated to be slightly higher, albeit statistically consistent with solar metalicity. The inferred from the sphere model is also consistent with the canonical value for both sources. For NGC 1386, the sphere model produces extreme and iron abundances values and iherefore it is not likely to be a good description of the spectrum. Due to the range of \ allowed for the torus model, we cannot test if a very large opening angle ( 90 ) would provide as equally good a solution for NGC 3079 or NGC 4945. However for the local minimum at the largest angle, the for NGC 4945 is even flatter (1.43) than the smallest opening angle and does not suggest that a large opening angle would describe the spectrum well. For this solution, =2.64 10^ 24 \ and =2.27 10^ 42 . We conclude from this that NGC 3079 and NGC 4945 are heavily buried sources with a covering factors close to unity. For all further analysis we assign a value of =0 \ to these sources. We discuss this result later, especially for NGC 4945 where previous results have favoured a very low covering factor e.g. madejski00 . Parameters determined by the torus model are listed in the top rows. For NGC 1386, IC 3079 and NGC 4945 we also present the best-fit parameters from the sphere model as the torus model finds that these sources are highly covered (bottom rows). Column (1) lists the source name, column (2) gives the redshift of the source, column (3) shows the total number of pulse height analysis (PHA) bins used in the spectrum, column (4) gives the \ of the fit, column (5) gives the reduced , equal to \ divided by the number of degrees of freedom in the fit, column (6) gives the \ in units of 10 ^ 24 , with uncertainties, column (7) gives the photon-index, column (8) gives , determined by the torus model in degrees or the iron abundance with respect to solar hydrogen abundance from the sphere model, column (9) gives the logarithm of the observed 3-79 keV \ flux in \ and column (10) gives the logarithm of the intrinsic (deabsorbed) rest-frame 2-10 keV luminosity in . +u indicates that a parameter has reached the upper limit when estimating the uncertainty. l indicates that the lower limit has been reached. H Column (1) gives the source name, column (2) gives the power-law index of the secondary power-law, column (3) gives the normalization of the secondary power-law, column (4) gives the temperature of the apec model in keV, column (5) gives the normalization of the apec model, column (6) gives the energy of the Gaussian line in keV and column (7) gives the normalisation of the Gaussian line. The best-fit unfolded spectra fit with the torus model are presented in Figure fig_spec . This illustrates the variety of spectral shapes seen above 10 keV for these Compton-thick sources, as well as the range in signal-to-noise in the sample. The data-to-model ratios are presented in Figure fig_ratios which indicate how well the torus model fits the data. The worst reduced \ from the torus fits result from the spectrum of NGC 1068 (1.72), where there is significant curvature in the data-to-model ratio, implying that the torus model is not a good description of the data. This was also the conclusion from the detailed analysis of this source in , which utilizes data from a large array of telescopes and several model combinations. They find that the data require a complex combination of models to reduce this curvature and that a monolithic torus structure is not likely. First in our investigation into what drives the covering factor in AGN, we examine how the measurement of \ in our analysis relates to the other measurements made with the torus model, namely \ and , by plotting these quantities against each other along with their uncertainties (Figure fig_specpar ). No uncertainties can be derived for the =0 \ values assigned to NGC 3079 and NGC 4945 as these have been determined from the sphere model which has a fixed opening angle of zero. There is no clear relationship between these quantities. In our analysis, we fix the torus inclination angle to an edge-on position of 87 . This is primarily motivated by the observation that the inclination angle does not have a large effect on the observed spectrum above 10 keV and thus allows us to reduce the number of free parameters in the fit. Furthermore an edge-on inclination angle allows the full range of opening angles to be explored. Fixing the inclination angle also avoids the scenario where the inclination angle approaches the opening angle, which produces a partially covered solution due to the angular binning in the torus model. We investigate whether allowing the inclination angle to be free allows it to be constrained. This is not the case, however Circinus is an exception, as found by , thus we have allowed the inclination angle to be free for this source. The \ band is also well suited to studying the high-energy cut-off in the X-ray spectra of AGN, and has been used to constrain this in a number of bright unobscured sources e.g. SWIFT J2127.4+5654, IC 4329A, 3C 382, MCG-5-23-16, Balokovi et al. submitted, respectively marinucci14,brenneman14, ballantyne14 . However, the torus model does not include this parameter and thus we have neglected its effect in this work. The potential effect of neglecting the high-energy cut-off here is to over-estimate the Compton hump, which is produced by the down-scattering of high-energy photons. As the covering factor roughly correlates with the strength of the Compton hump, then over-estimating the Compton hump will lead to a systematic under-estimation of the covering factor. We expect this effect to be very small as the lowest high-energy cut-off energy detected so far by \ is at 108 keV in SWIFT J2127.4+5654 marinucci14 , which is far above that of the Compton hump ( 30 keV). For photons originating around a cut-off of this energy to affect the Compton hump, at least 10 scatterings would be required. To understand this effect fully, the torus model should be improved to include the high-energy cut-off. For this study, we have a heterogeneous sample in terms of X-ray spectral coverage and most of our sample have accompanying soft X-ray data to constrain the spectral components below 10 keV. However, for four sources, we use NuSTAR data alone. No soft X-ray data exist for 2MFGC 2280, meanwhile for NGC 1068, NGC 4945 and Circinus, the NuSTAR data are high signal-to-noise enough that they can constrain the torus parameters alone. We test this assumption for all three sources by adding in an \ observation to model the soft X-rays below 3 keV. We find an additional apec model is required to fit these data in addition to the torus and power-law model already in place. Fitting the data simultaneously with the soft component constrained by \ produces only small changes in , and within measurement uncertainties. We conclude that the exclusion of the soft X-ray data does not bias our results significantly in these sources. When fitting \ and \ data together, we have kept the spectral parameters fixed between the two data sets. Since the observations were not made simultaneously, spectral variability could be missed, with the exception of the intrinsic luminosities, which is accounted for by the cross-normalisation between the two observatories being a free parameter. We investigated spectral variability in the five sources where we use both \ and \ data together by allowing the \ parameter to vary between data sets. We find that the \ parameter is consistent within the uncertainties for all of these except NGC 424. For this source, \ measures =4.6^ +1.3 _ 0.7 10^ 24 \ and \ measures =2.1^ +0.6 _ 0.3 10^ 24 . Allowing for the \ to vary like this does not however change our result on . Lastly we investigate how further spectral components added to the fit may affect our results. Specifically, we investigate the addition of the semi-infinite slab reflection model pexrav in order to represent a contribution to reflection from the accretion disc. We use the pure reflection component of this model and fix to that of the torus model, set the high-energy cut-off to maximum ( 1 10^ 6 eV), the adundances to solar and the cosine of the inclination angle to 0.45. The normalization is free. In four sources, NGC 1068, 2MFGC2280, NGC 1320, NGC 1386 and IC 2560, the normalization of the pexrav component falls to zero in the fit. For NGC 3079 and NGC 4945, the addition of the pexrav component has no effect on , whereas for Circinus, the change is 5 . For NGC 424 the addition of pexrav to the fit causes a shift in \ of 25 . While the change in \ is large for NGC 424, when considering the sample as a whole we conclude that the addition of this components does not appear to introduce a systematic effect in the determination of . We note that the pexrav component added here is unabsorbed and not subjected to the same absorption as the primary power-law. To treat this correctly, the pexrav spectrum would need to be added to the intrinsic emission of the Monte-Carlo models that calculate the effect of Compton scattering within the torus model, which is not easily done. The use of the torus model here to determine the opening angle of the torus is fairly novel, as is the use of X-ray torus models to understand high-energy emission in heavily obscured AGN. Therefore, in Section sec_models we compared the spectral fit parameters determined from the classical pexrav model and the more recent mytorus model when fitted to simulated spectra from the torus model for a range of \ and . Here, we compare the fits to real \ data with the torus model presented here to those made with pexrav and mytorus from previous published works. We list the different \ and values obtained from each in Table table_speccomp . In our initial analysis, we found that pexrav systematically underestimates with respect to both torus and mytorus , most likely because it cannot reproduce the full strength of the Compton hump produced by a torus geometry. This is also found in our comparison of fits to real data, as fits with pexrav produce a lower than both the torus or mytorus model for NGC 424, NGC 1068, NGC 1320 and IC 2560.

sources.summary: IC 2560 is discussed as a confirmed disk maser whose 183 GHz emission is a systemic complex resembling the blended 22 GHz structure.

quotes.quote: IC 2560 The 22 GHz maser system in IC 2560 was discovered by Braatz_1996 , and VLBI observations presented in Nakai_1998 and Ishihara_2001 confirm that it is a disk maser system. Our 183 GHz spectrum of IC 2560 is shown in the lower righthand panel of fig:hex1 , along with a 22 GHz spectrum reproduced from Pesce_2015 (see Fig. 1 in that paper). The 22 GHz spectrum is an average of multiple epochs of spectral monitoring observations taken with the GBT.

The only component seen in the 183 GHz spectrum of IC 2560 is the central set of systemic maser features, which span a velocity range of 60 around a central velocity of 2925 . The peak flux density of the systemic features is 29.6 mJy, which is a factor of 5 fainter than the typical peak flux density of the 22 GHz systemic masers. The heavily blended structure of the systemic maser complex at 183 GHz is similar to that at 22 GHz, with no prominent narrow features present. Adopting a Hubble law distance of 42 Mpc, the isotropic luminosity of the 183 GHz maser emission from IC 2560 is L_ iso 282 L _ .

sources.summary: The paper includes IC 2560 in the megamaser AGN sample, reports no independent bolometric-luminosity measurement, and says mytorus becomes unreliable for its extreme column.

quotes.quote: These AGN include well-studied sources that have been the subject of detailed spectral analysis of \ data, such as Circinus arevalo14 , NGC 4945 puccetti14 , NGC 1068 bauer15,marinucci16 , and NGC 3393 koss15 , as well as samples of sources such as IC 2560, NGC 1368, and NGC 3079 balokovic14,brightman15 . In all of these studies, the mytorus and torus models were used to obtain the intrinsic and 2 10 keV luminosities, , correcting for columns of 10 ^ 23 10^ 26 .

Finally we investigate other sources of bolometric luminosity that are independent of the X-ray measurements. These usually come from fitting spectral energy distributions of the AGN from optical to mid-infrared wavelengths. Table tab_lbol lists these, along with the corresponding \ for the given \ of the AGN. No independent \ measurement could be found for NGC 4945 or IC 2560.

For IC 2560, mytorus reaches the upper limit in both (2.5) and \ ( 10^ 25 ). Since the torus model indicates that >10^ 26 \ in this source, beyond the range of mytorus , the result from mytorus may not be reliable and thus we also do not include this source in our analysis with mytorus . This is in agreement with where more detailed spectral modeling of this source is presented.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2560 as a nearby barred Seyfert 2 megamaser galaxy whose Chandra data reveal extended soft X-ray emission and a strong iron line from an obscured nucleus.

quotes.quote: A short Chandra ACIS-S observation of the Seyfert 2 galaxy IC 2560, which hosts a luminous nuclear water megamaser, shows: 1) the X-ray emission is extended; 2) the X-ray spectrum shows emission features in the soft ( E < 2 keV) X-ray band; this is the major component of the extended emission; and 3) a very strong (EW 3.6 keV) iron K line at 6.4 keV on a flat continuum.

Galaxies: individual: IC 2560 --- X-rays: galaxies --- masers IC 2560 is a relatively nearby ( D=26 Mpc) barred spiral galaxy, classified as a Seyfert 2 (Fairall 1986; Kewley et al 2001). Ishihara also analyzed an ASCA observation of IC 2560 and concluded that it possesses a fairly heavily obscured ( N_H 3 10^ 23 ) but low luminosity ( L_ 2-10 keV 10^ 41 ) X-ray source. In this paper we report on a short Chandra observation of IC 2560, which reveals spatially extended soft X-ray emission and a substantially different nature to the hard X-ray source than was inferred by Ishihara (2001) from the ASCA data.

Extended X-ray emission X-ray emission from IC2560 is found to be extended in the Chandra image. A simple comparison with the PSF indicates that the core region of IC2560 is extended with a significance of larger than 3 and at least by 80 pc in radius.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra observations of IC 2560, interpreting its hard X-rays as obscured Compton reflection and its soft emission as likely starburst-related plasma.

quotes.quote: Observation of the H _2 O megamaser galaxy IC 2560 with the Chandra Observatory reveals a complex spectrum composed of soft X-ray emission due to multi-temperature thermal plasma, and a hard continuum with strong emission lines.
