The continuum is most likely a Compton reflection (reprocessing) of primary emission that is completely absorbed at least up to 7 keV. The lines can be identified with fluorescence from Si, S and Fe in the lowest ionization stages.

One good candidate for detailed measurements is the Seyfert 2 galaxy IC 2560. It contains an H _2 O megamaser (Braatz, Wilson, Henkel 1996), with a peak flux density of up to 0.4 Jy. This relatively nearby (26 Mpc; v_ rec = 2876 km s ^ 1 ) source has been partially resolved with the VLBI (Ishihara et al. 2001) and a central black hole mass of 3 10^6 M _ inferred, which is relatively low but comparable to the mass of the black hole in our own Milky Way (Ghez et al. 2005; Sch odel et al. 2003) and Circinus Galaxy (Greenhill et al. 2003).

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2560 as a type 2 Seyfert with hard X-ray point source, Compton-thick classification, and no reported short- or long-term X-ray variations.

quotes.quote: IC 2560 This galaxy was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert see an optical spectrum in Appendix multiimages fairall1986 . At hard X-rays it shows a point-like source (see Appendix multiimages ). It was classified as a Compton thick object balokovic2014 . It was observed once with XMM -Newton in 2003 and once with Chandra in 2004. Variability studies were not found in the literature.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 2560 as a steep-spectrum megamaser AGN; its maser disk is mostly Keplerian with jet contribution, while recalibrated X-ray scaling departs from dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: To test this hypothesis, we have selected the two sources with the flattest spectra of our sample (NGC 4388 and NGC 4945) and the two sources with the steepest spectra (IC 2560 and Circinus), and recalculated their \ with the scaling method assuming =2.17 for the flattest sources and =1.66 for the steepest sources.

Instead, all four sources, which were originally consistent with their maser respective estimates based on the mass ratio criterion described above (see Table table:tab5 and Figure figure:fig6 ), showed a clear departure from the dynamical \ values with three sources (NGC 4388, NGC 4945, and IC 2560) that were not formally consistent with the maser values anymore (their new mass ratios were 8.0, 7.1, and 6.7, respectively) and only Circinus (ratio of 0.5) still consistent, but only by virtue of the fact that the original ratio was basically 1.

In IC 2560 the megamaser emission has been attributed to an edge-on thin disk with Keplerian rotation with some additional contribution from a jet yama12 . IC 2560: The starting spectral model for this source is again provided by the work of mas16 , who fitted the \ spectrum with the \ model, which favors a heavily absorbed primary emission characterized by a steep photon index. The results from our baseline model are broadly consistent with their results.

sources.summary: The paper finds IC 2560 has a high-ionization Seyfert-2 nucleus, blue-skewed line wings suggesting outflow, and a central velocity gradient consistent with a rotating disk.

quotes.quote: The active nucleus of IC 2560 (innermost 2 ) emits lines typical for a high-ionization Seyfert-2 spectrum albeit with comparatively narrow profiles (FWHM 200 km/s). Line wings are stronger on the blue side than on the red side suggesting outflow. The central velocity gradient fits into the general velocity curve of the galaxy. Attributing it to a rotating disk coplanar with the galaxy leads to a Keplerian mass of 10^7 M _ inside a radius of 100 pc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC2560 as one of nine disk galaxies hosting pseudobulges and places it among the pseudobulges lying below the paper's inferred scaling relations.

quotes.quote: By means of the S e rsic index, we identify nine disk galaxies as hosting pseudobulges: Circinus, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368, NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594.

Figure fg:mbh illustrates that 4 out of 9 pseudobulges (NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594) are consistent with the correlation for classical bulges, while the others (CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368) are outliers, at more than 4 below the LINMIX ERR linear regression.
Of these, CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, and NGC3398 lie below all the \ scaling relations; NGC3368 deviates from , - but not from , while the contrary happens for NGC3998.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 2560 as a megamaser disk galaxy with narrow-line dominated central blue light, an uncertain inner PA assignment, and clear dust structure.

quotes.quote: IC 2560 is a difficult case, because the central blue light is dominated by narrow-line emission.
Since the PA profile is flat for radii > 0.5 , we assign IC 2560 an inner PA of 45 , but caution that the identification in this case is uncertain.
In the case of NGC 2273, NGC 2960, and IC 2560, the dust structure is clear.

gmw_00001976.name: MARK 268
gmw_00001976.overview: MARK 268 is optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1505.01166]. It hosts a compact hard X-ray source and a radio counterpart with a weaker component located 1.1 kpc from the nucleus [arXiv:1505.01166].

sources.summary: The paper describes MARK 268 as a type 2 Seyfert with a radio counterpart, compact hard X-ray source, and no detected X-ray or UV variability.

quotes.quote: MARK 268 This galaxy was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert by . A radio counterpart was detected with VLA data at 6 cm, with a weaker component 1.1 kpc away from the nucleus ulvestadwilson1984 . XMM Newton data show a compact source at hard X-rays (see Appendix multiimages ) . It was observed twice with XMM -Newton in 2008. Variability studies were not found in the literature.

gmw_00001977.name: UGC 4056
gmw_00001977.overview: UGC 4056 is a giant barred spiral galaxy that rotates clockwise with trailing spiral arms [arXiv:1907.07998]. Although it has been studied as an active galactic nucleus host [arXiv:1907.07998], its weak X-ray emission and ambiguous emission line ratios prevent confirmation of an active nucleus [arXiv:2007.08541].

sources.summary: The paper derives gas and stellar kinematics for barred AGN host UGC 4056, attributing its central gas velocity bump and radial inflow to bar-driven motions.

quotes.quote: We derive the circular velocity curves of the gaseous and stellar discs of UGC 4056, a giant barred galaxy with an active galactic nucleus (AGN). We analyse UGC 4056 using the 2D spectroscopy obtained within the framework of the Mapping Nearby Galaxies at APO (MaNGA) survey. Using images and the colour index g- from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS), we determined the tilt of the galaxy, which allows us to conclude that the galaxy rotates clockwise with trailing spiral arms.

We found that the gas motion at the central part of the UGC 4056 shows peculiar features. The rotation velocity of the gaseous disc shows a bump within around three kiloparsecs while the rotation velocity of the stellar disc falls smoothly to zero with decreasing galactocentric distance. We demonstrate that the peculiar radial velocities in the central part of the galaxy may be caused by the inflow of the gas towards the nucleus of the galaxy.

sources.summary: UGC 4056 has an X-ray luminosity upper limit and non-star-forming line ratios, but its weak X-ray emission and ambiguous ratios prevent AGN confirmation.

quotes.quote: 1-43783 - This galaxy is UGC 4056, and has an upper limit on the x-ray luminosity of L(0.3 - 10 keV ) < 3 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , as reported in the 1SXPSC . The measured emission line ratios are consistent with non-star-forming ionization mechanisms in all three BPT diagrams, but the [ S 2 ] /H emission line ratios fall within the LINER-like region of the BPT diagram, which is not necessarily indicative of an AGN. Given the weak X-ray emission and ambiguous emission line ratios, we cannot confirm that UGC 4056 harbors an AGN.

gmw_00001978.name: 1H 1934-063
gmw_00001978.overview: 1H 1934-063 is a nearby radio-quiet narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2405.07494] that displays variable X-ray fluxes [arXiv:2405.07494]. The galaxy features a rich high-ionization coronal-line spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0208062], molecular hydrogen emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0208062], and changing ultra-fast outflow phases [arXiv:2405.07494].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 1H 1934-063 as a nearby radio-quiet NLS1 with warm absorbers and changing ultra-fast outflow phases between 2015 and 2021.

quotes.quote: 1H 1934-063 is a nearby ( z=0.0102 ) radio-quiet NLS1 galaxy, presenting variable X-ray fluxes 2012Ponti . The spin of the central SMBH was estimated at a_ <0.56 2022Xu or a_ >0.4 2019Jiangdensity depending on whether a high-density relativistic reflection model was adopted. In the 2015 observation of 1H 1934-063, a 20 s time lag was detected between the disk reprocessing component and the primary continuum, indicating a compact corona with a height of 9  4 R_ g ( R_ g  GM_ BH /^2 ) in the lamppost geometry.

In the RGS spectrum, distinct signatures of a WA and a moderately ionized UFO have been identified as well 2022Xu . Moreover, the tentative evidence for the reprocessing of the coronal photons onto the base of winds has also been observed.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1H 1934-063 as a Seyfert with strong [ S 3 ], molecular H2, unambiguous Fe 13, and a rich high-ionization coronal-line spectrum.

quotes.quote: We found that [ S 3 ] 0.953 m is, by far, the strongest of all narrow forbidden lines and one of the brightest lines in the NIR range, particularly in 1H 1934-063 and ARK 564. Molecular H _ 2 emission is also observed in these two objects as well as in the broad-line Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 863. [ Fe 13 ] 1.074 m is observed unambiguously in 1H 1934-063 (Fig. hcor ), ARK 564 (Fig. arkcor ), MRK 335 (Fig. 335cor ) and TON S 180.
In contrast, 1H 1934-063 and Ark 564 display very rich CLR spectra, with bright [ Si 6 ] 1.963 m, [ Si 10 ] 1.430 m, [ S 9 ] 1.252 m, [ S 8 ] 0.991 m and detectable [ Ca 8 ] 2.321 m.

gmw_00001979.name: Mrk 1044
gmw_00001979.overview: Mrk 1044 is a highly inclined [arXiv:2202.13839] narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1807.00839, arXiv:2202.13839] featuring a disk-like morphology [arXiv:1807.00839] with a small pseudo-bulge and spiral arms [arXiv:1710.02194]. The galaxy hosts a highly accreting active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1807.00839, arXiv:2308.11552] that drives multi-phase ionized outflows [arXiv:2211.00657, arXiv:2305.11966] and exhibits variable X-ray emission [arXiv:0709.1962, arXiv:1804.02703].

sources.summary: The paper selects HE 0227 0913/Mrk 1044 for deep Chandra observations as a high-accretion AGN host, describes it as a disk-like NLS1 with ionized-gas rotation and star-forming rings, and finds only tentative inner-kpc X-ray excess.

quotes.quote: From the CARS sample we selected two galaxies, HE 0227 0913 (also known as Mrk 1044) and HE 0351 + 0240, for deep pilot Chandra observations to detect extended hot diffuse gas. The high accretion rates of the AGN and detection of kpc-scale ionized gas filaments make both galaxies good candidates to test the presence of expanding X-ray bubbles as expected for the radiative AGN feedback mode. The two galaxies presented here are complementary in studying the SMBH-galaxy connection, as they have contrasting host galaxy properties and are in very different evolutionary stages. HE 0227 0913 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLS1) at z=0.016 with a disk-like morphology.

The host galaxy of HE 0227 0913 is a disk which is clearly supported by the ionized gas velocity field. This velocity field was modeled using the KinMS software https://github.com/TimothyADavis/KinMS Davis:2013 to obtain an inclination angle of 67.4 4.5^ . Ongoing star formation dominates the gas excitation in a circumnuclear disk, with an inner star forming ring a few arcsec ( <1 kpc) in diameter and an apparently outer star forming ring at 20 \ ( 7 kpc) distance that is likely related to the spiral arms.

We do not find any significant evidence for excess emission above the PSF, except in the first bin for HE 0227 0913; we find a 2- excess at R<1 kpc, however the systematic uncertainty due to pileup in the central region makes this detection very tentative.

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 1044 has complex BLR kinematics: virialized motion plus outflow signatures, with high accretion rate and self-shadowing suggested as possible drivers.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1044. In Figure VDM1044 , we show the H and H velocity-delay maps of Mrk 1044. The maps exhibit three connected features. The strongest one is at 15 days and v +200 km s ^ 1 . Of the two weaker ones with <5 days and v 500 km s ^ 1 , the stronger is in the blue wing, suggesting outflow. In du2016b , the velocity-resolved analysis divides the H emission line of Mrk 1044 into 4 wavelength bins and shows longer lags at smaller velocities and shorter lags at higher velocities.

In general, the BLR of Mrk 1044 tends to be virialized. However, the overall structure of the map is accompanied by an outflow signature, with shorter response towards the blue end and longer response occurring in the red end (the average delay is 8 days for < 0 , and 11 days for > 0 ).

As an AGN with extremely high accretion rate (its dimensionless accretion rate M = M _ ^2/L_ Edd 16.6 where M _ is the accretion rate and L_ Edd is the Eddington luminosity; see du2015 ), the relatively stronger radiation pressure, compared with the normal AGNs, would be a likely driver of the outflow component of the BLR.

sources.summary: The paper observes Mrk 1044 as a nearby bright Seyfert/NLSy1, models its polarized spectra, and places it at the highly inclined end of the sample viewing-angle sequence.

quotes.quote: Using the FORS2 instrument at the European Southern Observatory's (ESO) Very Large Telescope, We performed spectropolarimetric observations of three NLSy1: Mrk 1044, SDSS J080101.41+184840.7, and IRAS 04416+1215. We then modeled the region in the reduced spectra using IRAF spectral fitting procedures and estimated the Stokes parameters and the viewing angles of the three sources. The viewing angles recovered for the three sources indicate that they occupy separate locations in the viewing angle plane, from an almost face-on (IRAS 04416+1215) to an intermediate (SDSS J080101.41+184840.7), to a highly inclined (Mrk 1044) orientation.

The source Mrk 1044 ( z = 0.016451 0.000037 ; RA 02:30:05.5, DEC -08:59:53 from NED https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ) is one of the nearest and brightest Seyfert galaxies V mag = 14.5, from NED; veronCetty2006 . The line width of the Balmer lines was already measured by rafanelli1983 . The authors reported an FWHM of 3600 km s ^ 1 for the broad components (BCs) \ of both the and lines. They stressed the comparable intensity of the narrow components (NCs), with an FWHM width of 1000 km s ^ 1 , and the very low value of the [ OIII ] / ratio (below 0.6). The source was later reclassified as an NLSy1 galaxy osterbrock1985 .

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE observations of Mrk 1044 to connect its rotating host, central gas spirals, circumnuclear star formation, and ongoing AGN feeding.

quotes.quote: We present VLT MUSE NFM-AO observations of Mrk 1044, the nearest super-Eddington accreting NLS1. Together with archival MUSE WFM data we aim to understand the host galaxy processes that drive Mrk 1044's black hole accretion. We extract the faint stellar continuum emission from the AGN-deblended host and perform spatially resolved emission line diagnostics with an unprecedented resolution. Combining both MUSE WFM and NFM-AO observations, we use a kinematic model of a thin rotating disk to trace the stellar and ionized gas motion from 10 kpc down to 30 pc around the nucleus.

Mrk 1044's stellar kinematics follow circular rotation, whereas the ionized gas shows tenuous spiral features in the center. We resolve a compact star forming circumnuclear ellipse (CNE) that has a semi-minor axis of 306 pc .

With an integrated SFR of 0.19 0.05 M _ yr ^ 1 the CNE contributes 27 of the galaxy-wide star formation. We conclude that Mrk 1044's nuclear activity has not yet affected the circumnuclear star formation.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mrk 1044 as a nearby super-Eddington NLS1 with compact multi-phase ionized outflows, including a resolved component that may affect host-galaxy star formation.

quotes.quote: We aim to constrain the properties of the ionised gas outflow in Mrk 1044, a nearby super-Eddington accreting NLS1. Based on the outflow energetics and the associated timescales, we estimate the outflow's future impact on the ongoing host galaxy star formation on different spatial scales. We apply a spectroastrometric analysis to VLT MUSE NFM-AO observations of Mrk 1044's nucleus. This allows us to map two ionised gas outflows traced by [ O iii ] which have velocities of 560 20 km s ^ 1 and 144 5 km s ^ 1 . Furthermore, we use an archival HST/STIS spectrum to identify two Ly- absorbing components that escape from the centre with approximately twice the velocity of the ionised gas components.

We explore whether an expanding shell model can describe the velocity structure of Mrk 1044's multi-phase outflow. We find an additional ionised gas outflowing component that is spatially resolved. It has a velocity of 211 22 km s ^ 1 and projected size of 4.6 0.6 pc .

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 1044, a nearby high-accretion NLS1 AGN, finding no significant X-ray/UV correlation and favoring relativistic reflection for its soft excess and iron line.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1044 is a nearby highly accreting NLS1 AGN at a redshift of z 0.016 that has been known for its peculiar variability properties having been observed multiple times in different energy bands see e.g., Wang2001, Dewangan2007, Du2015, Mallick2018, Gliozzi2020 . Using a short XMM-Newton observation of Mrk 1044 , Dewangan2007 found no evidence for a lag between the 0.2-0.3 keV and the 5-10 keV . X-ray bands on the basis of which they ruled out reprocessing as the plausible origin of its prominent soft X-ray excess. From the broadband 2013 XMM-Newton and 2016 NuSTAR observations of the source, Mallick2018 found prominent soft and hard X-ray excesses as well as iron K_ line well described by the relativistic reflection model. The observations revealed that the X-ray continuum of Mrk 1044 can adequately be described by the relativistic reflection model similar to those of 1H 0707-495 and IRAS 13224-3809 . The black hole mass of Mrk 1044 has been measured through reverberation mapping campaign to be 3 10^ 6 M_ Wang2001, Du2015 .

To investigate X-ray/UV correlated variability in Mrk 1044 , we carried out cross-correlation analysis using three long XMM-Newton and numerous Swift monitoring observations with simultaneous UV and X-ray data. Our analysis revealed that there is no significant correlation between emission in the UV and the X-ray energy bands on short as well as long time scales. Although there appears to be anti-correlation features in the CCFs, they are considered insignificant.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 1044 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 comparison target, reporting weak soft-hard X-ray correlation, acceptable smeared-wind or Comptonization fits, and likely two-component corona behavior.

quotes.quote: We investigate the origin of the soft X-ray excess emission from narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxies Akn 564 and Mrk 1044 using observations. Based on a short observation of Mrk 1044, we find no correlation between the 0.2-0.3 and 5-10 bands at zero lag. The smeared wind and optically thick Comptonization models both describe the spectrum of Mrk 1044 satisfactorily, but the ionized reflection model requires extreme parameters.

X-ray emission from Mrk 1044 is variable with trough-to-peak variations of 2 ( 0.2-0.5 ) and 3 ( 4-10 ). However, the 0.2-0.5 and 4-10 light curves do not appear to be strongly correlated. Long observations of Mrk 1044 are required to confirm the time delay. The temporal and spectral characteristics of Akn 564 and Mrk 1044 are consistent with a two component corona -- a compact low temperature, optically thick corona and an extended hot corona.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mrk 1044's broadband X-ray spectrum and variability, finding high-density relativistic disc reflection, reverberation lags, and variability driven by changes in the hot corona.

quotes.quote: We present the first results from a detailed spectral-timing analysis of a long ( 130 ) observation and quasi-simultaneous and observations of the highly-accreting narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 1044. The broadband (0.3 50 ) spectrum reveals the presence of a strong soft X-ray excess emission below 1.5 , iron K _ emission complex at 6 7 and a `Compton hump' at 15 30 . We find that the relativistic reflection from a high-density accretion disc with a broken power-law emissivity profile can simultaneously explain the soft X-ray excess, highly ionized broad iron line and the Compton hump.

At low frequencies ( [ 2-6 ] 10^ 5 ), the power-law continuum dominated 1.5 5 band lags behind the reflection dominated 0.3 1 band, which is explained with a combination of propagation fluctuation and Comptonization processes, while at higher frequencies ( [ 1-2 ] 10^ 4 ), we detect a soft lag which is interpreted as a signature of X-ray reverberation from the accretion disc. The fractional root-mean-squared (rms) variability of the source decreases with energy and is well described by two variable components: a less variable relativistic disc reflection and a more variable direct coronal emission. Our combined spectral-timing analyses suggest that the observed broadband X-ray variability of Mrk 1044 is mainly driven by variations in the location or geometry of the optically thin, hot corona.

sources.summary: The paper reports a multi-peaked H I profile for Mrk 1044, requiring constrained BusyFit parameters and attributing prior width discrepancies to a noise spike.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1044: The emission profile for this galaxy contains several flux peaks between the flanks (see Fig. \ batmen2 ). This caused the initial BusyFit model to fit the flank slopes while converging with a value of 0 for the central trough amplitude. In order to fit a weak dual-horn signature, we held the trough amplitude ( ) fixed at 0.0001, the trough offset ( x_ p ) fixed at 235, and the polynomial degree ( n ) fixed at 2. For this object, employed the National Radio Astronomy Observatory (NRAO) 91 m telescope's 384 channel autocorrelation spectrometer with 11 km s ^ 1 channel spacing and velocity resolution of 22 km s ^ 1 . The spectrum reported by contains a noise spike close to the redshifted side of the HI profile.

sources.summary: J0230 is classified as a complex NLS1 with strong soft excess, Fe K features, a Compton hump, and relativistic high-density disk reflection.

quotes.quote: J0230 (Mrk 1044) The spectral fitting of J0230 between 2-12 keV reveals that a simple power-law is not a good representation for the data giving a ^2_ = 1.76. Indeed, we classify it as a C-NLS1, as it exhibits a relativistic reflection from a high-density accretion disk with the presence of a strong soft X-ray excess below 1.5 keV, Fe K emission complex at 6-7 keV, and a Compton hump at 15-30 keV in its broad-band (0.3-50.0 keV) spectrum Mallick2018 . This object had been analyzed by Mallick2018 in detail. The combined XMM-Newton EPIC-PN (0.3−10.0 keV), Swift XRT (0.3−6.0 keV), and NuSTAR FPMA+FPMB (3.0−50.0 keV) spectra of J0230 can be reproduced by a Galactic absorption, a primary power-law emission, an ionized high-density relativistic disk reflection, a neutral distant reflection, and a Gaussian absorption line. We note that J0230 was classified as a S-NLS1 in Gallo2006 , whereas we consider it as a C-NLS1. If the flux state scenario is true, this object might have undergone a flux change from normal to low state.

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 1044 hosts a rapidly responding radiatively driven UFO, with kinetic power high enough for feedback and rare blueshifted large-scale emission lines.

quotes.quote: Here we perform a time- and flux-resolved X-ray spectroscopy on four \ observations of a highly accreting narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy, Mrk 1044, to study the dependence of the outflow properties on the source luminosity. We find that the UFO in Mrk 1044 responds to the source variability quickly and its velocity increases with the X-ray flux, suggesting a high-density ( 10^ 9 - 4.5 10^ 12 cm ^ 3 ) and radiatively driven outflow, launched from the region within a distance of 98 - 6600 R_ g from the black hole. The kinetic energy of the UFO is conservatively estimated ( L_ UFO 4.4 L_ Edd ), reaching the theoretical criterion to affect the evolution of the host galaxy. We also find emission lines, from a large-scale region, have a blueshift of 2700 - 4500 km/s in the spectra of Mrk 1044, which is rarely observed in AGN. By comparing with other sources, we propose a correlation between the blueshift of emission lines and the source accretion rate, which can be verified by a future sample study.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1044's velocity-resolved lags as relatively symmetric, consistent with virialized BLR motions where higher-velocity gas lies nearer the center.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1044. The velocity-resolved lags of Mrk 1044 are relatively symmetric and show evidence of simple virialized motions, that high-velocity gas is located in the more central region, which is similar to the cases of NGC 5548 denney2009 , SBS 1116+583A bentz2009 , Mrk 1310 bentz2009 , NGC 4051 denney2009b, denney2010 and 3C 120 grier2013 in the previous works.
Mrk 1044 is consistent with the case of virialized motions. The symmetric pattern of Mrk 1044 is consistent with virialized motions.

sources.summary: The paper reports that MRK 1044 lacks significant coronal-line emission, has insufficient CLR kinematic information, and may have weak or absent low-to-intermediate forbidden optical lines.

quotes.quote: MRK 1044 and TON S 180 were not included because they lack significant coronal line emission.

This might be the case for MRK 1044 and TON S 180, which present a very weak CLR spectra. However, the hydrogen column density N_ H of these objects, measured from X-ray observations, show little or no excess of neutral H over the Galactic value as is observed in columns 6 and 7 of Table tab1 ). Another possibility is that coronal line emitting material in the line of sight is absent, or there is a high anisotropy in the continuum radiation that reaches the NLR. Interestingly, low to intermediate forbidden lines in the optical region for the above two sources are also very weak or absent com98 , contrary to what is seen in the remaining objects.

sources.summary: The paper reports soft X-ray variability in Mrk 1044, a roughly five-day reverberation signal, and the largest conservative accretion-rate estimate in the monitored sample.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1044 : ROSAT observations show evidence for soft X-ray variability of this source (Boller 1996). Our CCF analysis shows a peak at 5 days with a maximum cross-correlation coefficient of r_ max 0.55 . The measured combined with the conservative assumption on the spin give 2.73 , the largest in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper finds a small pseudo-bulge plus inner and outer spiral disks and reports broad agreement with prior bulge and B/T measurements.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1044 (Fig. 11.20) --- \ \ The mosaic image of WFPC2 is saturated in the center. The fit finds a small pseudo-bulge ( n=0.81 ), an inner disk, and an outer disk with spiral arms ( B/T 0.18 ). The results are generally in good agreement with Orbad de Xivry et al. (2011; n_ bul 1.45 and B/T 0.30 ).

gmw_00001980.name: ID=1088
gmw_00001980.overview: ID=1088 is an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of 0.577 [arXiv:astro-ph/0210146]. It acts as a gravitational lens, producing an arc system with a radius of 0.9 arcseconds around itself [arXiv:astro-ph/0210146].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID=1088 as the elliptical lensing galaxy in the HDF-S arc system, confirms its redshift at 0.577, and uses it to bound the arc redshift.

quotes.quote: An evident gravitational lensing effect is present in the HDF-S field. The system consists of an arc at a radius of 0.9'' around an elliptical galaxy ( ). We have obtained a spectrum of the lensing galaxy ( ID=1088 , Nobj=37 ), which turns out to be an elliptical at redshift 0.577 (see also ). In this way the results obtained by Barkana et al. assuming z_ phot 0.6 for the lensing galaxy are confirmed.
Since the arc is not a U-dropout, we can put an upper limit to its redshift of about 2.5, while a lower limit is provided by the redshift of the lensing galaxy, 0.577.

gmw_00001981.name: ID=373
gmw_00001981.overview: ID=373 is a radio source located in the Hubble Deep Field-South [arXiv:astro-ph/0210146]. It is classified as an extremely red object with a very high radio-to-optical ratio and an estimated photometric redshift of approximately 1.7 [arXiv:astro-ph/0210146].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID=373 as an unusual HDF-S radio source and extremely red object with a very high radio-optical ratio and likely photometric redshift near 1.7.

quotes.quote: In the HDF-S there is an interesting radio source identified and discussed by Norris et al. (1999). This object (ID=373) is unusual in that its radio-optical ratio is about 1000 times higher than that of any galaxy known in the local Universe. It has also been identified as an Extremely Red Object (ERO) with an (I-Ks)_ AB =3.45 .

In absence of strong emission lines the faintness of the object ( I_ AB =25.75 ) has not allowed us to measure a spectroscopic redshift. The absence of strong emission lines, however, suggest that the photometric redshift estimated by Fontana et al. (2002), z_ phot 1.7 , is more likely than other, higher redshift possibilities ( ).

gmw_00001982.name: FLS02-N
gmw_00001982.overview: FLS02-N is the northern component of the interacting FLS02 galaxy system [arXiv:2202.10576]. The galaxy exhibits co-spatial continuum and CO(3-2) emission, disturbed disk kinematics, and a high infrared star formation rate of 353 solar masses per year [arXiv:2202.10576].

sources.summary: FLS02-N is the northern component of the interacting FLS02 U/LIRG system, with co-spatial continuum and CO(3-2) emission, a high apportioned infrared SFR, and disturbed disk kinematics.

quotes.quote: For the northern source in FLS02 (hereafter FLS02-N) the continuum and CO(3-2) emission are approximately co-spatial and the centre of the continuum coincides with the centre of CO(3-2).

For FLS02 we measure the total continuum flux from both sources and find each galaxy's relative contribution to the total flux. This apportions the SFR _ IR = 380 M _ yr ^ 1 for the system into 353 M _ yr ^ 1 for FLS02-N and 27 M _ yr ^ 1 for FLS02-S.

In the four targets that have CO(3-2) emission line detections FLS02-N and FLS02-S show signatures of disturbed disks and were unable to be adequately fitted with a ^ 3D BAROLO model; whereas CDFS1-W and SWIRE7 show ordered rotation and were fit with a model thin disk.

gmw_00001983.name: FLS02-S
gmw_00001983.overview: FLS02-S is a galaxy that exhibits signatures of a disturbed disk [arXiv:2202.10576]. It displays weak CO(3-2) emission, marginal continuum emission, and has an apportioned infrared star formation rate of 27 solar masses per year [arXiv:2202.10576].

sources.summary: FLS02-S is the southern component of FLS02, with weaker CO(3-2), marginal continuum emission, an apportioned infrared SFR of 27 solar masses per year, and disturbed disk signatures.

quotes.quote: The southern source, hereafter FLS02-S, has weaker CO(3-2) emission with a marginal 4 detection in the continuum (see Figure fig:maps ).

For FLS02 we measure the total continuum flux from both sources and find each galaxy's relative contribution to the total flux. This apportions the SFR _ IR = 380 M _ yr ^ 1 for the system into 353 M _ yr ^ 1 for FLS02-N and 27 M _ yr ^ 1 for FLS02-S.

In the four targets that have CO(3-2) emission line detections FLS02-N and FLS02-S show signatures of disturbed disks and were unable to be adequately fitted with a ^ 3D BAROLO model; whereas CDFS1-W and SWIRE7 show ordered rotation and were fit with a model thin disk.

gmw_00001984.name: SWIRE5
gmw_00001984.overview: SWIRE5 is a rotating disk galaxy that exhibits ordered rotation [arXiv:2202.10576]. Its carbon monoxide and continuum emissions show different morphologies, featuring two star-forming peaks separated by 7 kpc and a kinematic disturbance that may indicate a disk instability or a minor merger [arXiv:2202.10576].

sources.summary: SWIRE5 has CO(3-2) and continuum emission with different morphologies, two star-forming peaks separated by 7 kpc, ordered rotation, and a disturbance possibly linked to a minor merger or disk instability.

quotes.quote: The SWIRE5 CO(3-2) and continuum emission show very different morphology with the star forming region smaller in extent than the molecular gas and appears to have two peaks which are separated by 7 kpc (see Figure fig:maps ).

All four galaxies for which we detected a CO(3-2) emission line show signatures of rotating disks. Two of these show ordered rotation (CDFS1-W and SWIRE5) that could be adequately fit with a model rotating gas disk using ^ 3D Barolo.

As both FLS02 and CDFS1 are interacting systems and SWIRE5 shows a disturbance that deviates from the disk model, which may be a hidden minor merger or a disk instability, it seems that morphology and kinematics play a significant role in elevating the SFE of a galaxy above the MS.

gmw_00001985.name: CDFS1-W
gmw_00001985.overview: CDFS1-W is a luminous infrared galaxy that exhibits a disk-like structure in its CO(3-2) emission [arXiv:2202.10576]. The galaxy features a compact continuum in its central region and displays ordered rotating disk kinematics [arXiv:2202.10576].

sources.summary: CDFS1-W is the western component of CDFS1, showing a disk-like CO(3-2) structure, compact central continuum, assumed system infrared SFR dominance, and ordered rotating disk kinematics.

quotes.quote: In CDFS1, the western source, CDFS1-W, shows a disk like structure in CO(3-2) with a more compact continuum located in the central region of the disk (see Figure fig:maps ).
Since CDFS1-E is not observed in either continuum or CO(3-2), it is not included in this analysis, and we assume that all the SFR _ IR arises from CDFS1-W.

All four galaxies for which we detected a CO(3-2) emission line show signatures of rotating disks. Two of these show ordered rotation (CDFS1-W and SWIRE5) that could be adequately fit with a model rotating gas disk using ^ 3D Barolo.

gmw_00001986.name: CDFS1-E
gmw_00001986.overview: CDFS1-E is a galaxy that has been detected in H-alpha emission [arXiv:2202.10576]. However, it remains undetected in both CO(3-2) and continuum observations, suggesting it may be a CO-dark dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2202.10576].

sources.summary: CDFS1-E is the eastern CDFS1 component, detected in H-alpha previously but not in CO(3-2) or continuum here; the authors suggest it may be a CO-dark dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: Neither CO(3-2) or continuum are observed in CDFS1-E despite this galaxy having a H detection in PS19 (see Appendix appendix:companion for its expected position with respect to CDFS1-W and Figure fig:CDFS1_optical for the optical observations of this system, noting that north points down in the optical image).
Since CDFS1-E is not observed in either continuum or CO(3-2), it is not included in this analysis, and we assume that all the SFR _ IR arises from CDFS1-W.

Although CDFS1-E is observed in H in PS19 it is not detected in either CO(3-2) or continuum. This galaxy has the lowest metallicity amongst all the galaxies in PS19, so it is possible that CDFS1-E is a CO-dark dwarf galaxy, and that the large L _ IR is arising entirely from CDFS1-W. Without further observations we cannot offer a definitive conclusion for the nature of this galaxy.

gmw_00001987.name: SWIRE7
gmw_00001987.overview: SWIRE7 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy with a likely redshift of approximately 0.414 [arXiv:2202.10576]. Although it has a strong continuum detection, it lacks CO(3-2) emission, suggesting it may contain a CO-dark interstellar medium [arXiv:2202.10576].

sources.summary: SWIRE7 lacks CO(3-2) but has a strong continuum detection, likely H redshift near 0.414, possible CO-dark ISM, and follow-up is required.

quotes.quote: SWIRE7 poses more of a challenge as it has a strong ( 20 ) detection in the continuum despite having no CO(3-2) detection. PS19 has a robust detection of an emission line at 927.8 nm with the most likely identification to be H at z = 0.414. Having re-examined the SPIRE-FTS spectrum presented in M14 there are possible 3 detections of both [ CII ] 158 m and [ OI ] 145 m lines at the expected wavelength, observed at 222 and 205 m respectively, as shown in Figure fig:spire-fts .

Alternatively, the continuum detection may be due in part to thermal free-free emission from electrons scattering in the HII regions (e.g. condon_essential_2016 ), as opposed to being the tail of the Rayleigh-Jeans continuum, which would partly explain the 20 continuum detection despite being a metal poor object. Recently, possible examples (or cases) of CO-dark gas have been observed in a small number of intermediate-z LIRGs which have been detected in [ CI ] and dust continuum but are deficient in CO ( dunne_dust_2021 ). Although we do not have a [ CI ] detection, if the marginal detection of [ CII ] is correct then the galaxy may not be carbon deficient but may have some mechanism in the ISM that is rapidly dissociating CO into [ CI ] and [ CII ] (e.g. bisbas_cosmic-ray_2017 ). If SWIRE7 is a true CO-dark ULIRG, and not an incorrect redshift, then it poses a challenge to the understanding of the ISM within dusty star forming galaxies galaxies, so followup observations are required.

gmw_00001988.name: Canes Venatici II
gmw_00001988.overview: Canes Venatici II is a distant, ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal satellite of the Milky Way [arXiv:0712.2241, arXiv:1111.6608] dominated by an ancient, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:1310.0824]. The galaxy exhibits an extended star formation history [arXiv:1510.05401] and displays tentative stream-like overdensities that may indicate stellar mass loss [arXiv:1111.6608].

sources.summary: The paper treats Canes Venatici II as a distant faint satellite, measuring its structure, old metal-poor population, and tentative stream-like overdensities that may indicate stellar mass loss.

quotes.quote: Among the new MW satellites, Canes Venatici II (CVn II), Leo V, and Pisces II are an intriguing triplet with similar properties see for their discovery papers, respectively Belokurov07,leov,Belokurov10 . They are all very distant from the MW ( D > 150 kpc), have similar half light radii ( 50 r_ h 100 pc) and are among the faintest objects (with 4.0 M_ V 5.0 ) detectable in the SDSS at that distance Koposov08,Walsh09 . Based on spectroscopic results, CVn II appears to be dark matter dominated with a M/L_ V =360^ +380 _ 180 Wolf10 and is metal poor, with [ Fe/H ] =-2.19 Kirby08 .

CVn II and Leo V show direct hints of stellar mass loss through the stream-like structures tentatively observed in our matched filter maps, and more tenuous indirect hints through their [ Fe/H ] and through Leo V's alignment with the Galactic center. Leo V has a spectroscopic [ Fe/H ] of 2.0 leovspec , nearly 0.5 dex higher than expected for its luminosity of M_ V =-4.4 mag, and CVn II has a spectroscopic [ Fe/H ] of 2.2 , 0.2 dex higher than expected based on the positive correlation between dwarf luminosity and [ Fe/H ] observed by Kirby11 . While at face value, this may indicate that at one time Leo V and CVn II were more luminous, neither are strong outliers from the [ Fe/H ] vs. luminosity relation of Kirby11 . As can be seen in Figure fig:smoothmap , Leo V's major axis is roughly aligned with the direction to the MW, which might be another hint of its disturbed structural state (see sec:align for further discussion). Wide field kinematics and deep imaging with improved star/galaxy separation (e.g. with HST or near-infrared photometry), will provide a clearer picture of Leo V's and CVn II's evolutionary states.

sources.summary: The paper presents photometry of Canes Venatici II, describing its faint dwarf spheroidal nature, coordinates, main-sequence turnoff, RR Lyrae variables, and distance.

quotes.quote: We report on the detection of variable stars in the Canes Venatici II (CVn II) dwarf spheroidal galaxy, a new satellite of the Milky Way recently discovered by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey. We also present a V, color-magnitude diagram that reaches V 25.5 mag, showing the galaxy's main sequence turn off at V 24.5 mag and revealing several candidate blue straggler stars. Two RR Lyrae stars have been identified within the half-light radius of CVn II, a fundamental-mode variable (RRab) with period P_ ab = 0.743 days, and a first-overtone (RRc) RR Lyrae star with P_ = 0.358 days.

CVn II (R.A. = 12 ^ h 57 ^ m 10 ^ s , DEC = 34 ^ 19 ^ 15 ^ , J2000.0; = 113.6^ , b= 82.7^ ) is one of the faintest of the newly discovered SDSS galaxies, with M_ V = -4.8 0.6 mag and surface brightness 29.5 mag arcsecond ^ 2 ( be07 ). It is a low-mass ( [ 2.4 1.1 ] 10^6 M_ ; sg07 ), compact dSph with a half-light radius r_h = 3.0^ 0.5 ^ ( be07 ), corresponding to 132 15 pc at a distance d = 151^ +15 _ 13 kpc. The i, g-i color-magnitude diagram (CMD) by be07 shows that CVn II has a well-defined and narrow red giant branch and a horizontal branch (HB) that extends to the blue, through the RR Lyrae instability strip. sg07 obtained spectra for 24 bright stars in CVn II, from which they derived a velocity dispersion of 4.6 1.7 km sec ^ 1 and an average metal abundance [ Fe/H ] = 2.31 0.12 dex with a dispersion of _ Fe/H =0.47 dex using the ru97 technique, which provides metal abundances consistent with the cg97 metallicity scale.

sources.summary: The paper finds Canes Venatici II difficult for the two-burst model: many observed low-alpha stars imply more second-burst stars or enrichment within the first burst.

quotes.quote: The brown14 model found that 95 of the stars in Canes Venatici II formed in the first burst. Our modelled [ /Fe ] and [ Fe/H ] for this scenario is shown along with observational data vargas13,brown14 in the second panel of Figs. f:afefeh f:fehhist . While our model of the two-burst scenario can fit most of the stars, it produces insufficient scatter to fit the stars at [ Fe/H ] 1.2 . Furthermore, four of the eight stars for which [ /Fe ] data is available show [ /Fe ] suppressed by 0.5 dex compared to the other four stars. If low [ /Fe ] reflects self-enrichment, these stars must have formed in the second burst.

sources.summary: Canes Venatici II is a distant ultra-faint dwarf in the HST sample; the paper uses deep CMD photometry to infer ancient, metal-poor and synchronized stellar populations.

quotes.quote: For the distant ( > 130 kpc) satellites in the sample (Hercules, Leo IV, and Canes Venatici II), we surveyed a narrow pencil beam (1 or 2 tiles in each camera) more deeply (10--16 orbits, faint limit of V 29 mag). The varying depth and area of each survey was intended to provide a sample of a few hundred stars in the vicinity of the MSTO with a photometric precision of 0.01 mag, thus providing tight constraints on age when coupled with independent knowledge of the metallicity distribution from Keck.

It is clear that the CMD of each UFD looks like the CMD of an ancient globular cluster, and that each is dominated by an old (age > 12 Gyr) population of metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] < -2 ) stars. A prominent blue straggler sequence (BSS) is present in each galaxy -- ubiquitous in old populations.

sources.summary: The paper treats Canes Venatici II as a new probable dwarf galaxy satellite at about 150 kpc, with round central contours, a southward extension, and an old metal-poor stellar population.

quotes.quote: The first row of Figure  fig:objsa refers to a satellite in Coma Berenices (henceforth Com), the second to a satellite in Canes Venatici (henceforth CVn II), the third to a probable globular cluster (henceforth Segue 1), the fourth to a satellite in Hercules (henceforth Her) and the fifth to a satellite in Leo (henceforth Leo IV)  There is already a dwarf galaxy known in the constellation of Canes Venatici  Zu06a , and two known in Leo. Leo III is an alternative name for the Leo dwarf irregular, also called Leo A  Be00 . .

CVn II is at a distance of 150^ +15 _ 14 kpc and has a half-light radius of 140 pc. The central density contours are round, but there is a southward extension clearly visible in the deep Subaru data. Its CMD has a clearly defined subgiant branch with a hint of a red clump, a reasonably prominent blue horizontal branch and a narrow giant branch.

sources.summary: The paper includes Canes Venatici II in its ultra-faint dwarf sample, describing its SDSS discovery and classifying it among systems with extended star formation history.

quotes.quote: This paper reports on the detailed abundance determination of stars belonging to the UfDSphs Bootes II, Canes Venatici I, Canes Venatici II, Hercules and Leo IV. Canes Venatici II The UfDSph Canes Venatici II is one of the four UfDSph discovered by belo2007 in the Sloan digital Sky Survey. Based on the results from webster2015 we classify Her and LeoIV as "fossil galaxies" and CVnII, BooI and CVnI as galaxies with extended star formation history.

gmw_00001989.name: Sgr
aliases_external: NAME SDG / Sagittarius Dwarf Spheroidal Galaxy
Sagittarius Dwarf Spheroidal Galaxy / Sgr dSph
gmw_00001989.overview: The Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal is a disrupting Local Group galaxy undergoing an ongoing merger with the Milky Way [arXiv:1611.00089, arXiv:2107.08055]. Its tidal disruption has produced a massive stellar stream wrapping around the celestial sphere [arXiv:1611.00089, arXiv:1402.4519], which includes a complex, bifurcated trailing arm [arXiv:1612.06829].

sources.summary: The paper maps the Sgr dwarf's southern trailing debris, identifying a line-of-sight fork separated by at least 5 kpc, with RR Lyrae distances suggesting the fork merges near the Sgr dwarf nucleus.

quotes.quote: We take advantage of the deep and wide coverage of the VST ATLAS survey to study the line-of-sight structure of the Sagittarius stellar stream in the Southern hemisphere, only 40^ away from the progenitor. We use photometrically selected Sub-Giant Branch (SGB) stars to reveal a complex debris morphology of the trailing arm and detect at least two clear peaks in the SGB distance modulus distribution. The separation between the two line-of-sight components is at least 5 kpc at the edge of the VST ATLAS footprint, but appears to change along the stream, which allows us to conclude that these detections correspond to two physically independent stellar structures, rather than a mix of co-distant stellar populations within a single stream. Our discovery of a fork in the Sgr trailing arm is verified using Blue Horizontal Branch stars and our distance measurements are calibrated using RR Lyrae stars from the Catalina Real-Time Transient Survey. Comparing with numerical simulations of the Sgr dwarf disruption, the more distant of the two components in the fork matches perfectly with the track of the trailing debris.

RRL stars further away from the Sgr dwarf (i.e., at higher | _ | ) show a clear bimodality, with two peaks located at 19.3 and 23.4 kpc, nicely connecting the two peaks found using SGB and BHB stars. Closer to the dwarf, the distribution peaks at 26.0 kpc, with a wider range of distances. This suggests that most probably the two peaks forming the fork get merged as they are going towards the actual position of the Sgr dwarf nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper models the Sagittarius (Sgr) dwarf spheroidal as a surviving Local Group dwarf whose Milky Way infall, progenitor mass, and tidal debris constrain the Galaxy halo and stream extent.

quotes.quote: With a Galactocentric distance of only 25 kpc, the Sagittarius (Sgr) dwarf spheroidal is one of the nearest dwarf galaxies kunder09 . First discovered by ibata94 , Sgr is in a near polar orbit around the Milky Way (MW) and has experienced multiple passages through the disk e.g. law10, purcell11 . The resulting stream of tidally stripped stars wraps a full 360 ^ around the celestial sphere.

In this paper, we set out to find a model for the full infall of the Sgr dwarf into the MW, until it reaches its current observed position and velocity. This includes the early infall phase at Galactocentric radii > 60 80 kpc not simulated by law10 , purcell11 or gomez15 . The survival of the Sgr satellite until the present day implies that it cannot have formed deep inside the MW halo, where it would have been cannibalized already. Rather, hierarchical models of galaxy formation suggest that the Sgr dwarf likely formed early on in the periphery of the assembling host halo.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sgr as a disrupting dwarf spheroidal whose central nucleus and M54 form a compact central massive object, with M54 likely decayed into Sgr's center.

quotes.quote: Within this context the system comprising Sgr Ibata:1994p417 and the GC M54 presents an excellent prototype to study these issues on different mass scales. Sgr is a disrupting dwarf spheroidal, with a constant surface brightness core of about 1.7 . At its center lies (i) a stellar nucleus (hereafter Sgr,N) made of typical Sgr metal-rich stars with a scale-length r_c = 0.4 pc, and (ii) M54, a very bright metal-poor GC ( r_c = 0.9 pc, M =2 10^6 ; Pryor:1993p11793 ).

As demonstrated in hereafter B08 Bellazzini:2008p8100 , these two sub-systems are coincident in position and velocity, but exhibit different velocity dispersion ( ) profiles, thus suggesting that M54 was born in the halo of Sgr and was driven to coincide with Sgr,N by orbital decay via dynamical friction. Sgr is an excellent candidate for a system that would appear to have a compact stellar nucleus if it were located at the distance of the Virgo cluster. Indeed, the CMO of Sgr appears to be a combination of the central cluster M54 and Sgr,N, and the F06 relation suggests a mass for Sgr of 10^9 , in good agreement with pre-disruption estimates Ibata:1998p389 .

sources.summary: The paper finds that the LMC and the Milky Way response significantly perturb Sgr orbit and debris, producing stream material misaligned with the present-day orbital plane.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, we show that the gravitational pull of the LMC and response of the Milky Way are likely to significantly affect the orbit and phase space distribution of tidal debris from the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy (Sgr). Such effects are larger than previous estimates based on the torque of the LMC alone. As a result, Sgr deposits debris in regions of the sky that are not aligned with the present-day Sgr orbital plane.

The Sgr dwarf galaxy is launched with the same initial conditions as in the ``LMC-less'' simulations. Clearly, the addition of the LMC results in significant perturbations in the phase-space distribution of Sgr debris. The top panels of Figures fig:hs and fig:ls show the simulated present-day Sgr stream and the remnant core projected in right ascension (RA) and declination.

sources.summary: The paper models the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy interaction with the Milky Way, varying Sgr mass, orbit, and halo assumptions, and concludes Sgr alone cannot drive the observed solar-neighbourhood perturbation.

quotes.quote: The ongoing merger of the Sagittarius (Sgr) dwarf galaxy with the Milky Way is believed to strongly affect the dynamics of the Milky Way's disc. We present a suite of 13 N body simulations, with 500 million to 1 billion particles, modelling the interaction between the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy (Sgr) and the Galactic disc.

We explore the effects of changing the mass model of Sgr, the orbital kinematics of Sgr, and the mass of the Milky Way halo. We find that none of the simulations match the observations of the vertical perturbation using Data Release 2. In the simulation that is the most similar, we find that the final mass of Sgr far exceeds the observed mass of the Sgr remnant, the asymmetry wavelength is too large, and the shape of the asymmetry does not match past z 0.7 kpc. We therefore conclude that our simulations support the conclusion that Sgr alone could not have caused the observed perturbation to the solar neighbourhood.

sources.summary: The paper models the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal core with high-resolution N-body simulations, reproducing its morphology and kinematics, tracing dark-matter stripping by Milky Way tides, and predicting full core disruption within 2 Gyr.

quotes.quote: The infall of the Sagittarius (Sgr) Dwarf Spheroidal Galaxy in the Milky Way halo is an unique opportunity to understand how the different components of a dwarf galaxy could be tidally removed. In this work, we reconstruct the Sgr core morphology and kinematics on the basis of a model that has already successfully reproduced the Sgr stream. Here we use a very high resolution model that almost resolves individual stars in the Sgr core.

It reproduces most of the observed morphology and kinematic properties, without specific fine-tuning. We also show that the dark matter may have been almost entirely stripped by Milky Way tides after two passages at pericenter. Finally the model predicts that the Sgr core will be fully disrupted within the next 2 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper links about 10 percent of Milky Way globular clusters to the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy and finds a 13-cluster group resembling Sagittarius, recovering seven of eight prior assignments.

quotes.quote: According to this technique, about 60 of GCs belong to the in-situ group, 20 are associated with the Gaia-Sausage/Enceladus event, and 10 are associated with the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy.

Pioneering analysis of the stellar abundances and kinematics in the outer regions of the Galactic bulge by ibata_dwarf_1994 was the first to uncover evidence of a merging event: the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy.

Using the Bayesian method with an accuracy threshold of 68 , we find that one of the groups resembles the Sagittarius dwarf. This group consists of 13 GCs, located in the high-energy and L_z 0 region in the IoM space. Remarkably, seven of the eight GCs unambiguously assigned to the Sagittarius dwarf by massari_origin_2019 fall into this group. The one exception is NGC 5824, which was also not among the six most definite Sagittarius GCs suggested by these authors. No GC from other unambiguous progenitors falls into this group.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal as a disrupting system with a globular-cluster population, bona fide body members, and stripped former members linked to tidal interactions.

quotes.quote: In this respect the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal galaxy (hereafter Sgr), due to its ongoing disruption into our Galaxy, offers different environments where the BS phenomenon can be studied. Since the discovery of Sgr ibata94 , a plethora of globular clusters have been putatively associated with it. Four of them, M54 (NGC 6715), Arp 2, Terzan 7 and Terzan 8, lying within the body of Sgr, are typically considered as bona fide members dacosta95 . Using several arguments, including dynamical modeling, the position over the stream of stars from the disrupting Sgr and abundance ratios, several other globular clusters have been considered as (former) members of the Sgr cluster system that have been stripped away due to tidal interactions with the Galaxy dinescu00,palma02,bellazzini03,cohen04,carraro07,law10,forbes10 . In the present paper we study the BS population in four of the Sgr globular clusters: the bona fide members Arp 2 and Terzan 8, and the stripped NGC 5634 and Palomar 12.

sources.summary: The paper models the Sagittarius progenitor as an accreted dwarf galaxy whose orbit and tidal debris impact the Milky Way disk, produce spiral structure, and leave broad stellar and dark-matter streams.

quotes.quote: Throughout this proceeding, we discuss the results initially presented in purcell_etal11 and purcell_etal12 , in which we perform high-resolution N body experiments involving a dwarf galaxy model with parameters similar to those indicated by observational constraints on the Sagittarius (Sgr) progenitor niederste-ostholt_etal10 , as it is accreted into a Milky Way-like system that is initially a stellar disk in equilibrium with a host halo. Following an orbital path and producing tidal debris consistent with the phase-space characteristics of the observed Sgr stream, the infalling subhalo impacts the disk twice in the recent Galactic past, with a third pericenter presently occurring as Sgr approaches the southern face of the Milky Way disk. Each of these disk crossings results in the emergence of swing-amplified global spirality modes, such that the present-day Galaxy resembles a multi-armed spiral structure with intermediate-scale arms see the discussion of spirality categorization in binney_tremaine08 . The massive Sgr progenitor unfurls into a thick polar stream wrapping around the Milky Way, not only in stellar material but also in the attendant dark matter stripped from the satellite as it is accreted into the Galactic mass. Being from a much dynamically hotter and larger distribution initially, the dark tidal debris cuts a much wider path across the Milky Way disk than the stellar Sgr stream, but is otherwise similarly distributed in phase space at the present day.

sources.summary: The paper models the Sgr dwarf spheroidal as a tidally disrupting Milky Way companion whose close passages can perturb the disk and help explain recent star-formation bursts.

quotes.quote: At least one major merger is currently taking place in the Milky Way (MW). The Sgr dwarf spheroidal galaxy is being tidally destroyed while orbiting around the MW, whose close passages perturb the MW disc externally. In this work, using a series of hydrodynamical simulations, we investigate how massive dwarf galaxies on quasi-polar Sgr-like orbits impact the star formation activity inside the MW-like discs.

Our simulations are in favour of the causality between the latest passages of the Sgr galaxy and the bursts of the SF in the SNd at 1 and 2 Gyr 2020NatAs...4..965R,2021A&A...647A..39S . However, according to our model, a potential burst at 6 Gyr 2020NatAs...4..965R requires a very close passage ( <20 kpc from the MW centre) of a massive ( >2 10^ 10 ) Sgr precursor with a very substantial mass loss allowing to prevent a rapid coalescence with the MW until the present.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Sagittarius dwarf as the satellite whose early pericentric passages and resonant interaction with the Milky Way excited the Anticenter Stream, motivating constraints on its orbital mass-loss history.

quotes.quote: Put togehter, all of this evidence support predictions from simulations of the interaction of the Sagittarius dwarf with the Milky Way, which argue that the Anticenter Stream (ACS) is the remains of a tidal tail of the Galaxy excited during Sgr's first pericentric passage after it crossed the virial radius, whereas Monoceros consists of the composite stellar populations excited during the more extended phases of the interaction.

This allowed us to explore and confirm a falsifiable prediction for their respective formation mechanisms as presented in laporte19a , namely that the ACS is the remnant tidal tail of the MW disc which formed through a resonant interaction with the Sgr dwarf galaxy. The ACS was kicked up shortly after the dwarf's crossing of the Galaxy's virial radius during one of the first pericentric passages.
Via modelling of such structures one can hope to time the impact events, putting strong constraints on the orbital mass-loss history of the Sgr dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00001990.name: NGC 0855
gmw_00001990.overview: NGC 0855 is a relatively isolated [arXiv:0808.1789, arXiv:1001.0234] blue dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1601.00344] that has also been classified as a polar ring galaxy [arXiv:0808.1789]. The system is highly irregular with dust contamination [arXiv:1406.6697], ongoing star formation [arXiv:1601.00344], and CO emission [arXiv:1601.00344].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 855 as relatively isolated, unusually blue for an elliptical with a polar ring, and dominated by a recent star-formation burst in the model.

quotes.quote: NGC 855 =HI021404.3+275302 is projected 13' to the east of the NGC 784 group, about 5.5 Mpc from the NGC 784 group and 2.2 Mpc from the NGC 672 group (assuming only Hubble expansion). Karachentsev et al. \ (2004) associated it with the NGC 925 group with a similar radial velocity but located 9 ^ 1.3 Mpc from NGC 855 itself. The large distances indicate that NGC 855 is relatively isolated from the rest of the galaxies of the sample. NGC 855 has been classified as an elliptical galaxy with a polar ring (Wallington et al. \ 1988), but with (B-V)=0.7 0.1 mag it is bluer than expected for this morphological type. Its appearance on our B-band image is that of a disk with a brighter centre, which has an off-centre extension.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 855 among the isolated Local Volume E/S0 galaxies, noting similar hydrogen masses, comparable specific star formation, and blue-sequence E/S0 status.

quotes.quote: Among 450 Local-volume (LV) galaxies with heliocentric distances D < 10 Mpc Karachentsev2004 there are only three E and S0 galaxies with absolute magnitudes [ 16.0 > M_B >-18.0 ] and negative `tidal indices' TI < 0, which allowed these galaxies --- NGC 404, NGC 855, and NGC 4460 --- to be classified as isolated objects.

The NGC 404, NGC 855, and NGC 4460 galaxies differ appreciably in luminosity, but have almost similar hydrogen masses 1 10^8M_ . Specific star-formation rate per unit luminosity differs little among these galaxies. The parameter P^*_K is only slightly smaller than zero for all three galaxies, and this indicates that the galaxies are capable of reproducing most of their stellar mass during cosmological time T_0 provided that star formation is at the current level.
All these properties are similar with the Local Volume isolated E/S0 galaxies described above, therefore NGC 4460, NGC 404, and NGC 855 seem to be belonging to the population of blue-sequence E/S0s.

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 0855 as an irregular, dusty system with bar-like star-forming clumps, likely a dE/dIrr object, and excluded from analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 0855 This object is highly irregular, with many clumps of star formation extending in a bar across the galaxy. There is also a great deal of dust contamination. This object may be considered a dE/dIrr object, as defined in Ferrarese et al. 2006. This object was excluded from analysis.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 855 as a FUV-bright, CO-emitting, blue star-forming dwarf elliptical with the weakest detected radio power in the sample.

quotes.quote: Also, NGC 855 shows CO emission nakanishi2007 , NGC 3928 has a starburst nucleus balzano1983 and IC5267 has a large number of star formation sites caldwell1991 , indicating ongoing star formation activity.
The weakest radio source detected is NGC 855, and it has a radio power of 3.86 10^ 19 WHz^ 1 and M_ K_s =-19.4 . This is a blue star forming dwarf elliptical galaxy nakanishi2007, walsh1990 .

gmw_00001991.name: NGC 1331
gmw_00001991.overview: NGC 1331 is an elliptical galaxy that hosts a nuclear star cluster [arXiv:1406.6697]. The galaxy's nucleus is slightly bluer than its outer regions, and its light profile is well fit by a double-Sersic profile [arXiv:1406.6697].

sources.summary: The appendix identifies NGC 1331 as an elliptical galaxy with a nuclear star cluster, a double-Sersic light-profile fit, and a nucleus bluer than its outskirts.

quotes.quote: NGC 1331 NGC 1331 is an elliptical galaxy with a nuclear star cluster. This object's light profile is well fit by a double-S e rsic profile. The nucleus is slightly bluer than the outer regions of the galaxy. In fitting with GALFIT, the S e rsic index of the nucleus had to be held fixed.

gmw_00001992.name: NGC 1370
gmw_00001992.overview: NGC 1370 is an early-type galaxy characterized by an extremely dusty torus [arXiv:1406.6697]. Dust dominates a large portion of the galaxy in the g band and is visible around the nucleus in the z band [arXiv:1406.6697].

sources.summary: The appendix says NGC 1370 has a very dusty torus, with dust dominating much of the galaxy in g band, so it was excluded from analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 1370 NGC 1370 has an extremely dusty torus. Dust dominates a significant portion of the galaxy in g band, and is visible around the nucleus in z band. This object was excluded from analysis.

gmw_00001993.name: NGC 2970
gmw_00001993.overview: NGC 2970 is a tidally distorted early-type dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1410.7848] that exhibits a faint, merger-like spiral structure [arXiv:1406.6697] and a nuclear star cluster [arXiv:1406.6697]. Located within a stellar stream, the galaxy shows signs of active tidal stripping [arXiv:1709.07012] and hosts a young stellar population under 0.5 Gyr old [arXiv:1410.7848].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HdE3 as a tidally distorted early-type dwarf with SDSS spectroscopy, weak H emission, a bluer center, and a young stellar component.

quotes.quote: Four (HdE2, HdE3, HdE4 and HdE5) of our galaxies are targeted for spectroscopic observation in the SDSS survey. We retrieve the optical spectra of these galaxies from the SDSS data archive and they seem to have reasonable signal-to-noise ratio. They are observed with the spectrograph fed with fibers of 3 diameter, and since the sample galaxies cover a wide range of distances the central 3 represents 200 pc to 900 pc central region of galaxies depending on the distance to these galaxies. The optical spectra of these galaxies do not show up strong emission lines and resemble a typical spectrum of a non-star-forming galaxy.

We find that only HdE3 contains a minute emission line of H while we inspect it with zooming into this region, and not surprisingly this galaxy has the center relatively bluer than the out-skirts, similar to the blue-centered dEs in the Virgo cluster Lisker06 .
We find the two galaxies, HdE3 and HdE5, possess a significant young components of age less than 0.5 Gyr, and other two, HdE2 and HdE4, contain no such young stellar population.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2970 as a compact-elliptical candidate in a stream, with signs of current stripping and uncertain evolution toward dE, cE, or UCD regimes.

quotes.quote: Finally, we include one more object that also has sufficient S/N SDSS spectrum: NGC 2970. Despite having been classified as a dE Paudel2014 , its reported size and stellar mass are very close to our limiting criteria. What is most interesting is that it is located in a stream, showing signs of being currently stripped. What it is unknown for now is whether this stripping is in its late stages (and thus NGC 2970 will remain as a dE), or whether this is only an early stage and further evolution towards the cEs or UCDs regimes is expected.

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 2970 as a blue galaxy with faint merger-like spiral structure, a nuclear star cluster, and a double-Sersic light-profile fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 2970 NGC 2970 has a faint spiral structure, perhaps from a merger. It is the bluest object in our sample with a (g-z) color of 0.96. NGC 2970 has a nuclear star cluster and is well fit by a double-S e rsic profile.

gmw_00001994.name: PGC 056821
gmw_00001994.overview: PGC 056821 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1406.6697]. Its light profile is well fit by a single Sersic profile [arXiv:1406.6697].

sources.summary: The appendix says PGC 056821 has a single-Sersic light profile but its F850LP images were affected by scattered-light streaks that were corrected with masking and modeling.

quotes.quote: PGC 056821 The light profile of PGC 056821 is well fit by a single S e rsic profile. The images of PGC 056821 taken in the F850LP filter suffer from streaks of scattered light from a nearby star. Some of these streaks cut across the galaxy. These were corrected for by masking them, creating a model using the IRAF ELLIPSE and BMODEL tasks, and filling in those regions with the model.

gmw_00001995.name: Mrk 622
gmw_00001995.overview: Mrk 622 is a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:0905.3349, arXiv:2106.13971] that contains both a star-forming region and a Seyfert 2 cloud [arXiv:astro-ph/9811342]. It is a triple-peaked narrow-emission-line system [arXiv:1711.06655] with a very large velocity dispersion [arXiv:2106.13971] and likely experienced a recent merger [arXiv:2106.13971, arXiv:1711.06655].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 622 as a Seyfert with extended radio emission, asymmetric multi-component [O III] profiles, complex ionized-gas kinematics, and ambiguous velocity fields requiring component modelling.

quotes.quote: Mrk 622 The radio emission in this galaxy at the 20 cm wavelength is extended along PA =0 Nagar99Radio . No prominent axis in either the optical emission or the excitation map was detected on the scale of 5 by MulObs , while Schm03Obs reported elongation in [ O iii ] emission at PA =55 in the inner 1 , perpendicular to the host-galaxy major axis. Shuder81 discovered multi-component [ O iii ] profiles of total FWHM = (1050 50) km s ^ 1 , in contrast to the nearly-Gaussian shapes of the H , [ N ii ] , and [ O ii ] lines of FWHM = 350 75 km s ^ 1 . They concluded that two emission-line regions of different ionisation are present, with estimated temperatures of 11 000 K in [ O iii ] and 6000 K in H , [ N ii ] , and [ O ii ] .

The [ O iii ] lines are highly asymmetric and their multiple components are detected throughout the field of view (Fig. Mrk622spec ), with a separation between the components as large as 600 km s ^ 1 , not present in the other lines, confirming the results of Shuder81 . Single-Gaussian modelling of [ O iii ] thus results in large-FWHM fits, as seen in Fig. Mrk622vel . The one-component fits have a strong impact on the interpretation of the velocity field of this object.

sources.summary: The paper highlights Mrk 622 as a Seyfert galaxy with very large central and off-central [O III] velocity dispersion, possible multiple AGNs, and merger-enhanced gas motions.

quotes.quote: The [ O iii ] velocity dispersion of one Seyfert galaxy (Mrk 622) is too large to be displayed within the displayed range of figure sigma_mass . The stellar mass of Mrk 622 is (M_*/M_ ) = 10.3 , and its velocity dispersions at central and off-central regions are _ [ O \ III ] = 455 km s ^ 1 and 411 km s ^ 1 , respectively. Figure mrk shows the MaNGA [ O \ III ] velocity dispersion map of Mrk 622. The velocity dispersion is very large (400--500 km s ^ 1 ) at almost all spaxels in the central and off-central regions, i.e., not only at the nucleus of Mrk 622.

Ben19 found that Mrk 622 has double or triple AGNs, which suggests that the galaxy has experienced a merger. Such merging events may enhance the velocity dispersion of gas clouds in the entire region of Mrk 622. Thus this object is very interesting to study the relation between the galaxy merger and AGN activity.

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies Mrk 622 from a double-peaked dual-AGN candidate to a triple-peaked narrow-emission-line system, likely a composite AGN amid a binary AGN candidate from a recent merger.

quotes.quote: Mrk 622 is a Compton Thick AGN and a double-peaked narrow emission line galaxy, thus a dual AGN candidate. In this work, new optical long-slit spectroscopic observations clearly show that this object is rather a triple peaked narrow emission line galaxy, with both blue and red shifted narrow emission lines, as well as a much narrower emission line centred at the host galaxy systemic velocity. The average velocity offset between the blue and red shifted components is 500 km s ^ 1 , which is producing the apparent double-peaked emission lines. These two components are in the loci of AGN in the Baldwin, Phillips Terlevich (BPT) diagrams and are found to be spatially separated by 76 pc. Analysis of the optical spatially resolved spectroscopic observations presented in this work favours that Mrk 622 is a system consisting of a Composite AGN amidst a binary AGN candidate, likely the result of a recent merger.

sources.summary: The paper confirms Mark 622 has two emission components, one star-forming and the other a Seyfert 2 cloud, explaining its weak line ratios.

quotes.quote: Mark 622 . The line ratios published by Shuder Osterbrock (1981) show that it is a Seyfert 2 galaxy, although the 6300 relative flux is too low for such a class ( \ = 0.06). Furthermore, these authors found that the \ lines are much broader (FWHM 1 050 ) than the , \ and \ li nes (FWHM 350 ) suggesting the presence of two emission-line regions of different ionization. Wilson Nath (1990) have shown that, in the nucleus, the \ lines are broader than \ (FWHM = 340 20 and 240 20 \ respectively); moreover, the \ image of this object is only slightly resolved, while 60 of the \ emission comes from an extended component (Mulchaey et al. 1996). Our spectra confirm these results; in fact, two components are needed in order to obtain a good fit: one is representative of a \ region, the other is typical of a Seyfert 2 cloud.

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies Mrk 622 with Seyfert 2-like mid-infrared behavior, emphasizing its shorter-wavelength continuum decline and reduced hot-dust contribution compared with Seyfert 1 spectra.

quotes.quote: Mrk 622 was considered to be a Seyfert 1.9 1995ApJ...440..141G , but its Seyfert type is listed in NED as Seyfert 2, and its mid-IR continuum is similar to those of Seyfert 2s like Mrk 3.
In the case of Mrk 622, the continuum diminishes toward shorter wavelengths.

Comparing NGC 7603 with the Seyfert 1 spectra from the archival sample, we note that hot dust contributes more in Seyfert 1s, while Seyfert 2s like Mrk 3 and Mrk 622 are dominated by a lack of contribution from hot dust.

gmw_00001996.name: NGC 6798
gmw_00001996.overview: NGC 6798 is an isolated lenticular [arXiv:1312.6572] and early-type galaxy [arXiv:2107.13630] featuring a stellar ring [arXiv:1406.7121] and a star-forming ring [arXiv:1312.6572]. It hosts an extended gaseous disk [arXiv:1312.6701] where the ionized gas counterrotates relative to the stars [arXiv:1312.6701, arXiv:1406.7121].

sources.summary: NGC 6798 has a very extended gaseous disk whose gas counterrotates relative to the stars and continues beyond the optical galaxy in H I mapping.

quotes.quote: Another galaxy with a very extended gaseous disc is NGC 6798 . However in this galaxy the gas counterrotates with respect to the stars; our measurements reach the radius up to 35 arcsec (5.2 kpc) and reveal a slightly higher amplitude of the gas rotation curve with respect to the stellar one. Such behaviour is expected due to asymmetric drift taking into account sufficiently high stellar velocity dispersion in the disc of this galaxy. The gas counterrotation continues beyond the optical borders of the galaxy as HI mapping shows Serra2012_atlas3d13_HI . NGC 6798 also was considered by atlas3d_10 where the authors calculated an angle between the kinematical axes of stars and gas _ ion-star =174.0 6.9 degree.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 6798 has counterrotating ionized gas, extended neutral hydrogen, a 15-20 arcsec stellar ring, and homogeneous half-solar stellar metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 6798 It is another galaxy which was studied earlier within the ATLAS-3D survey cappellari_atlas7_2011,davis_2011 , like NGC 3248, and where a counterrotating disc of the ionized gas was found, while the neutral hydrogen in this galaxy is extended far beyond the stellar disc. Analyzing the photometric structure of the galaxy with the white-light image obtained by using the SCORPIO-2 , we have noted the presence of a stellar ring at the radius of 15 -20 . This ring is 5 2 Gyr old that, taking into account the error bar, almost coincides with the age of the disc of 7 4 Gyr . However the ring is slightly cooler dynamically than the disc that is consistent with its perhaps younger age. At the same time the metallicity of the stars throughout the galaxy is homogeneous, being half-solar.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC6798 as an early-type galaxy whose angular-momentum profile fails convergence, though this does not alter the early-type excess-angular-momentum result.

quotes.quote: We find that all the galaxies except two early-type galaxies (NGC3626 and NGC6798) follow the convergence criterion. We also fit a function P = k. exp(-a/x), (which is zero at x 0 ^ + becomes constant as x ) to the last three points of the cumulative j profile. We then, take the ratio r_ k (k being baryons/gas) of j_ k at last measured point to the maximum value that P would have if extrapolated to infinity. This ratio, (i.e. the convergence factor), is calculated for all the j _ bar and j _ gas profiles and listed for all the galaxies in Tables Table4 , Table5 and Table6 . Only 2 of the early-type galaxies (NGC 3626 and NGC6798) and 1 of the ultra-diffuse galaxies have a convergence factor < 0.7.

Two of these early-type galaxies (NGC3626 and NGC6798, marked with a circle around the blue open triangles in Fig fig:S0 ) do not satisfy the convergence criterion. We note that they do not appear to have discrepant \ as that of the early-type galaxies with converged specific angular momentum curves. If the lack of convergence means that these two galaxies have a higher specific angular momentum than measured, it does not affect the result that the early–type galaxies in our sample have significantly higher \ than expected from the \ power law.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6798 as an isolated lenticular with decoupled counterrotating ionized gas, a star-forming ring of subsolar oxygen abundance, and young-star-dominated disk-plane gas excitation.

quotes.quote: Kinematical profiles for stars and ionized gas in a few most striking examples (NGC 2350, NGC 6798, UGC 9519) are presented in Fig.

We have found that 7 of 12 ( 58 14 ) lenticular isolated galaxies studied by us reveal a presence of extended ionized-gas discs which rotation is mostly decoupled from the stellar kinematics: in 5 of 7 ( 71 17 ) -- NGC 3248, NGC 6798, NGC 7351, UGC 4551, UGC 9519 -- the gas demonstrates visible counterrotation.
NGC 6798 has a starforming ring at = 30 arcsec where 12+ O/H = 8.4-8.5 dex.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 6798 in the H I kinematic subsample, reporting regular rotation, a clear gas-disc misalignment, and reduced dark-matter fractions when H I is included.

quotes.quote: However, only a subsample of four galaxies shows regular rotating features in their velocity fields in deeper observations with the WSRT: NGC 2685, NGC 4203, NGC 5582 and NGC 6798 (see Appendix sec:HI-fields for these velocity maps).
