There is a significant misalignment between the stellar disc (or the equatorial plane of the stellar component) and the disc for NGC 4203, and a clear misalignment for NGC 2685 and NGC 6798 (about 15 ignoring counter-rotation).

Among the four galaxies in the subsample, the dark matter fraction of NGC 2685 remains unchanged throughout all radii when include kinematics, while the dark matter fraction of NGC 4203 increases, and the dark matter fractions of NGC 5582 and NGC 6798 decrease, especially at f_ DM (5R_ e ) .

gmw_00001997.name: NGC 7351
gmw_00001997.overview: NGC 7351 is a symmetrical dwarf lenticular galaxy featuring a small bar [arXiv:1406.7121, arXiv:1509.02356]. While its central ionized gas rotates with the stars and is excited by current star formation, the outer gas is kinematically decoupled and exhibits counterrotation [arXiv:1406.7121, arXiv:1312.6572].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7351 as a dwarf lenticular with low velocity dispersion, slow rotation, subsolar metallicity, outward-increasing stellar ages, and decoupled outer gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 7351 It is a dwarf lenticular galaxy with a low stellar velocity dispersion both in the center and in the disc, and with a slow rotation velocity. Its stellar kinematics was previously investigated by simien_prugniel . As it might be expected in the low-luminosity galaxy, the metallicity of its stars is below the solar one, but while in the center it is lower than the solar only by a factor of 2.5, in the disc it is lower at least by a factor of 4. The ages of the stellar population monotonically increase from the center to the edge of the galactic disc, from 1.5 Gyr in the nucleus to 5--8 Gyr in the disc. The comparison with the characteristics of the gaseous component which we published in ilg_gas has shown that in the center the ionized gas rotates together with the stars and is excited by current star formation, while outside the central region the gas distribution deviates from the plane of the galactic disc, and its kinematics becomes strongly decoupled from the stellar kinematics.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7351 as a symmetrical S0 galaxy with a small bar, spiral-like component colors, a relatively blue nucleus, and evidence for old secondary star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 7351. This is a symmetrical S0 galaxy with a small bar. In spite of the fact that NGC 7351 is the earliest-type galaxy in our sample (Table table:tab1 ), the colors of its components are more characteristic of Sab--Sb spiral galaxies. The nucleus of NGC 7351 is slightly bluer than the bulge and the disk, it has the same colors as the bar (Figs. figure:fig8 a, figure:fig8 b). The disk of NGC 7351 is shifted to the right of the NCS on the (U-B) (B-V) and (B-V) (V-I) diagrams. Its color characteristics correspond to those of stellar systems with a secondary burst of star formation long in the past ( >1 Gyr).

sources.summary: The paper reports counterrotating ionized gas in NGC 7351, subsolar oxygen abundances at its center and outer region, and young-star-dominated gas excitation in the disk plane.

quotes.quote: We have found that 7 of 12 ( 58 14 ) lenticular isolated galaxies studied by us reveal a presence of extended ionized-gas discs which rotation is mostly decoupled from the stellar kinematics: in 5 of 7 ( 71 17 ) -- NGC 3248, NGC 6798, NGC 7351, UGC 4551, UGC 9519 -- the gas demonstrates visible counterrotation.
Oxygen abundance at the distance 7^ 8^ in the NGC 7351 has value of 8.2 dex, while at the center 8.45 dex.

Our observational data indicate that in the galaxies where the gas is probably confined to the disc planes (NGC 2350, NGC 6798, NGC 7351, the very outer part of NGC 6654) the excitation by young stars is preferable.

sources.summary: NGC 7351 is described as a dwarf S0 with weak emission lines and decoupled ionized gas beyond the center; its excitation is preferably from young stars.

quotes.quote: Almost all measurements in NGC 2350 and NGC 7351 as well as in the outer ring of NGC 6798 are located in the BPT-diagram region where star-formation excitation mechanism dominates.

NGC 7351 , a dwarf S0 galaxy, has also weak emission lines with relatively large scatter of measurements; despite that the gaseous kinematical profile in Fig. pics_kinematics clearly indicates the decoupled kinematical properties of the ionized gas in this galaxy beyond the very central part, R>5 .

Through the observational data presented here we see that in the galaxies where the gas is probably confined to the disc planes (NGC 2350, NGC 6798, NGC 7351, the very outer part of NGC 6654) the excitation by young stars is preferable.

gmw_00001998.name: HFF-DS 4877
gmw_00001998.overview: HFF-DS 4877 is a massive quiescent galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 2.650 [arXiv:2207.13625]. Its spectrum exhibits strong Balmer and 4000-Angstrom breaks with no detected emission lines, indicating a quenched stellar population [arXiv:2207.13625].

sources.summary: The paper spectroscopically confirms HFF-DS 4877 as a massive quiescent galaxy at z=2.650, with Balmer/4000 breaks, no detected emission lines, and lensing-corrected stellar mass 10.53.

quotes.quote: Although ID=4877 is the most massive quiescent candidate of the sample, with (M^ corr _ star /M_ ) =10.67 it is comparable to the lowest mass quiescent galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at z>2 to-date EstradaCarpenter2020 . On the other hand, ID=5607, with (M^ corr _ star /M_ ) =9.92 , pushes the limit well below previous works, since it has a stellar mass estimate before lensing magnification correction <10^ 10.5 M _ . Figure fig:nirissspecs shows the best-fit \ model (red curve) which places them, respectively, at redshifts of z_ spec =2.650^ +0.004 _ 0.006 and z_ spec =2.433^ +0.032 _ 0.016 (3 errors). The NIRISS spectra show strong Balmer/4000 breaks, as well as other blended stellar continuum absorption features (the locations of CaII HK, H , H , H , and NaD are highlighted in Fig. fig:nirissspecs ). Furthermore, the NIRISS spectra do not show evidence of emission lines, confirming the quiescent nature of the candidates.

The gravitational lensing magnification corrected stellar masses are therefore found to be (M^ corr _ star /M_ ) =10.53^ +0.18 _ 0.06 for ID=4877 and (M^ corr _ star /M_ ) =9.93^ +0.06 _ 0.07 for ID=5607.

gmw_00001999.name: HFF-DS 5607
gmw_00001999.overview: HFF-DS 5607 is a quiescent galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 2.433 [arXiv:2207.13625]. It has a lensing-corrected stellar mass below 10^10 solar masses, making it the lowest-mass quiescent galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at z > 2 [arXiv:2207.13625].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HFF-DS 5607 as the first spectroscopically confirmed low-mass quiescent galaxy at z>2, with z=2.433 and lensing-corrected stellar mass below 10^10 solar masses.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, ID=5607, with (M^ corr _ star /M_ ) =9.92 , pushes the limit well below previous works, since it has a stellar mass estimate before lensing magnification correction <10^ 10.5 M _ . Figure fig:nirissspecs shows the best-fit \ model (red curve) which places them, respectively, at redshifts of z_ spec =2.650^ +0.004 _ 0.006 and z_ spec =2.433^ +0.032 _ 0.016 (3 errors). The NIRISS spectra show strong Balmer/4000 breaks, as well as other blended stellar continuum absorption features (the locations of CaII HK, H , H , H , and NaD are highlighted in Fig. fig:nirissspecs ). Furthermore, the NIRISS spectra do not show evidence of emission lines, confirming the quiescent nature of the candidates.

The gravitational lensing magnification corrected stellar masses are therefore found to be (M^ corr _ star /M_ ) =10.53^ +0.18 _ 0.06 for ID=4877 and (M^ corr _ star /M_ ) =9.93^ +0.06 _ 0.07 for ID=5607. With a stellar mass below 10^ 10 M _ , HFF-DS 5607 is the lowest mass quiescent galaxy to be spectroscopically confirmed at z>2 to-date.

gmw_00002000.name: ZS7
gmw_00002000.overview: ZS7 is a high-redshift galaxy system at z=7.154 [arXiv:1606.01304, arXiv:2312.03589] that is undergoing a close merger [arXiv:2312.03589]. The system hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2301.03599, arXiv:2312.03589] and exhibits intense optical line emission [arXiv:1606.01304].

sources.summary: The paper identifies COS-zs7-1 as an extreme optical-line emitter, obtains a MOSFIRE Ly line at z=7.154, and finds the line agrees with the photometric redshift.

quotes.quote: Prior to this paper, COS-zs7-1 was the only source from lacking a near-infrared spectrum. Similar to the other galaxies from RB16, COS-zs7-1 is bright in the near-infrared (H _ 160 =25.1) and has IRAC color ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = 1.03 0.15 ) that indicates intense optical line emission. In addition to RB16, the galaxy has been reported elsehwere (e.g., Tilvi2013, Bowler2014 ). We estimate an [ OIII ] +H rest-frame equivalent width of 1854 325 \ based on the [ 4.5 ] flux excess, making COS-zs7-1 the most extreme optical line emitter in the RB16 sample. RB16 derive a reasonably well-constrained photometric redshift (z _ phot =7.14 ^ +0.12 _ 0.12 ) that places Ly in a narrow 290 \ window between 9750 and 10041 .

We identify a 6.25 emission line at 9913 \ that is coincident with the expected spatial position of COS-zs7-1 (Figure fig:cos ). The emission line is seen to have the standard negative - positive - negative pattern, indicating that it is present in both dither positions. If the feature is Ly , it would correspond to z_ Ly =7.154 , in excellent agreement with the photometric redshift derived by RB16.

sources.summary: The paper reports JWST/NIRSpec-IFU evidence that ZS7 at z=7.15 hosts an offset type-1 AGN and another likely obscured AGN in a close galaxy merger.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the detection of a broad line in the z=7.15 galaxy system ZS7, which identifies it as a (type 1) AGN. ZS7 is also known by its CANDELS ID Grogin11, Koekemoer11, Nayyeri17 COSMOS13679 Pentericci16, Pentericci18 , COSY-0237620370 RobertsBorsani16, Laporte17, Witten23 , and COS-zs7-1 Stark17 . This galaxy was already identified to host an AGN, based on the detection of and in the rest-frame UV Laporte17 . However, the peculiarity of this system is that the BLR is offset from the primary (narrow) nebular line emitter. Additionally, also the latter emitter displays nebular line signatures typical of an AGN.

This essentially confirms what was already seen in the NIRCam colors shown in Fig. f:target . This further indicates that we are likely observing two distinct objects in close proximity. The blue continuum centered on the centroid is likely tracing a young stellar population.

sources.summary: The paper describes COSY as a high-redshift source whose emission-line spectrum implies an AGN-like radiation field, high UV luminosity, an overdense environment, and a large ionized bubble.

quotes.quote: Laporte+17 detect , HeII and NV, as well as upper bounds on the flux of CIII ] and CIV in the spectrum of COSY allowing for the determination of the source of its radiation field. The most likely hypothesis is that the radiation field of COSY is inconsistent with that from star-forming galaxies, instead it likely is produced by an active galactic nucleus (AGN) Laporte+17, Costa+14 and additionally its high UV luminosity makes it likely to trace an overdense region eg. Barkana+04,Furlanetto+04 . Therefore it is likely COSY additionally resides within a large, ionized bubble.

gmw_00002001.name: QSO J2233-606
gmw_00002001.overview: QSO J2233-606 is a high-redshift quasar located at an emission redshift of 2.23 [arXiv:astro-ph/9901022]. High-resolution spectroscopy reveals a cluster of intrinsic ultraviolet absorption systems outflowing from its active galactic nucleus at radial velocities of 3800 to 5000 kilometers per second [arXiv:astro-ph/0604357].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes intrinsic UV absorption in QSO J2233-606, deriving outflow covering factors, column densities, ionization state, abundances, and velocity substructure from VLT/UVES spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of the intrinsic UV absorption in the central HDFS target QSO J2233-606, based on a high-resolution, high S/N ( 25 -- 50) spectrum obtained with VLT/UVES. This spectrum samples the cluster of intrinsic absorption systems outflowing from the AGN at radial velocities v 5000 -- 3800 km s ^ 1 in the key far-UV diagnostic lines - the lithium-like CNO doublets and \ Lyman series. We fit the absorption troughs using a global model of all detected lines to solve for the independent velocity-dependent covering factors of the continuum and emission-line sources and ionic column densities. This reveals increasing covering factors in components with greater outflow velocity.

We have presented a detailed analysis of the intrinsic UV absorption in the central HDFS target, QSO J2233-606. Our analysis is based on a high-resolution ( R 30,000 - 50,000), high S/N ( 25 -- 50) spectrum obtained with VLT/UVES. This spectrum samples the cluster of intrinsic absorption systems outflowing from the AGN at radial velocities v 5000 -- 3800 km s ^ 1 in the lithium-like CNO doublets and \ Lyman series lines, which serve as key diagnostics to determine the covering factors, column densities, ionization state, and abundances of the outflow.

sources.summary: The paper treats J2233--606 as the HDFS quasar target whose STIS/HST and UCLES/AAT spectra are used to study intermediate-redshift Lyman-forest absorbers along its line of sight.

quotes.quote: Much of the ground-based and HST HDFS campaign (Williams et al. \ 1999) has been devoted to the observations of the quasar J2233--606 ( z_ em =2.23 ) that lies in the STIS field (about 5 and 8 arcmin from the WFPC2 and NICMOS fields). The study of the quasar incorporates both spectroscopy (Sealy et al. \ 1998; Savaglio 1998; Outram et al. \ 1998; Ferguson et al. \ 1999) and imaging (Gardner et al. \ 1999). Here we present the interesting features shown by the distribution of the clouds obtained by the fitting of the absorption lines in the redshift range 1.20 < z < 2.20 in the high and medium resolution spectra taken with STIS/HST (Ferguson et al. \ 1999 See also the HDFS web site at http://www.stsci.edu/ftp/observing/hdf/hdfsouth/hdfs.html ) and UCLES/AAT (Outram et al. \ 1998).

In contrast to the empty field used for the HDF North program, the HDFS has been selected close to a high redshift quasar, J2233--606 ( z_ em =2.23 ). The QSO is centered in the STIS field, located approximately 5 and 8 arcmin from the WFPC2 and NICMOS fields respectively. The forest of J2233--606 has been observed at high and medium resolution (FWHM = 10, 50 and 8.5 in the wavelength intervals =2670-3040 , =3040-3530 and =3530-3900 ) using STIS/HST (Ferguson et al. \ 1999) and UCLES/AAT (Outram et al. \ 1998), giving the opportunity to analyze the properties of the intergalactic medium in the large redshift interval z=1.20-2.20 .

gmw_00002002.name: NGC 1521
gmw_00002002.overview: NGC 1521 is an isolated elliptical galaxy with a morphologically relaxed hot gas halo [arXiv:1204.3095]. The galaxy features a detected dark matter halo [arXiv:1204.3095] and contains multiphase gas that does not extend beyond a radius of 2 kpc [arXiv:2201.01306].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC1521 as an isolated elliptical with a relaxed hot gas halo, constrained gravitating mass and baryon fraction, a detected dark matter halo, and hot-gas-dominated baryons.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present a \ and \ study of one such candidate, , and constrain its distribution of dark and baryonic matter. We find a morphologically relaxed hot gas halo, extending almost to , that is well described by hydrostatic models similar to the benchmark, baryonically closed, Milky Way-mass elliptical galaxy NGC 720. We obtain good constraints on the enclosed gravitating mass ( M_ 500 = [ 3.8 1.0 ] 10^ 12 , slightly higher than NGC 720), and baryon fraction ( = 0.13 0.03 ). We confirm at 8.2 the presence of a dark matter (DM) halo consistent with . Assuming a Navarro-Frenk-White DM profile, our self-consistent, physical model enables meaningful constraints beyond , revealing that most of the baryons are in the hot gas.

sources.summary: NGC 1521 has low measured entropy slope and no extended multiphase gas, but the paper attributes its uncertain model comparison to poor spatial resolution.

quotes.quote: NGC 1521 NGC 1521 has _v = 233.6 8.9 , _K (1--10 kpc) = 0.38 0.39 , (t_ cool /t_ ff ) = 20.90 0.51 , and multiphase gas that does not extend beyond 2 kpc. The best-fitting value of _K is lower than the model prediction, but its uncertainty is large, most likely because of low spatial resolution. Its entropy profile has only four radial bins between 1 and 10 kpc, and one additional radial bin interior to 1 kpc. The resulting uncertainty does overlap the analytic prediction. Therefore, improved spatial resolution is necessary to determine if the galaxy does indeed conform to the model.

gmw_00002003.name: NGC 4125
gmw_00002003.overview: NGC 4125 is a peculiar elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1611.09438] containing an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2201.01306], a central nuclear disk [arXiv:0809.0698], and filamentary nuclear dust [arXiv:2201.05080]. Its fine structure [arXiv:1710.05025] and non-rotational gas motions suggest the presence of material from a past merger [arXiv:2201.05080].

sources.summary: NGC 4125 has uncertain multiphase classification, shallow outer entropy, low X-ray luminosity, an AGN component, and possible cooling-dominated outer flow.

quotes.quote: NGC 4125 NGC 4125 has _v = 239.8 6.9 , _K (1--10 kpc) = 0.13 0.45 , and (t_ cool /t_ ff ) = 28.22 12.35 . Its multiphase gas classification is unknown. Like NGC 1521 its apparently shallow entropy slope, relative to the model prediction, may also result from poor spatial resolution. However, the shape of its entropy profile and some of its other characteristics are a bit more interesting. Its X-ray luminosity (measured inside 10 kpc) is the lowest in the HQ sample ( 0.023 0.001 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 ).

Between 1 and 10 kpc, the entropy profile is almost flat, based on six radial bins. However, interior to 1 kpc, the entropy profile slope is much steeper, and Lakhchaura2018 find a power-law component in the X-ray spectrum, indicating the presence of an AGN with luminosity 0.006 0.001 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 . Wiklind1995 placed an upper limit on the molecular gas content, but the measurement is uncertain due to high systematic errors. The combination of the presence of an AGN, _v < 240 km s^ 1 , and the flattened entropy profile at larger radii indicate that this may be a galaxy in which the steady flow is cooling-dominated at larger radii and prone to developing entropy inversions Voit2019 .

sources.summary: The paper excludes bright central knots in NGC 4125, treats its GCLF turnover separately, and notes that its discrepant fit drives much of the turnover scatter.

quotes.quote: In particular, data in the vicinity of a central nuclear disc in NGC 1387 and the bright knots of emission in the centre of NGC 4125 were excluded.
We indicate by ellipses cases where the data could not be constrained (NGC 1387 and NGC 5845) or where the parameters were already derived allowing the GCLF turnover to be free (NGC 4125).

Omitting the most discrepant data-point, that of NGC 4125, significantly improved the fit ( ^2 /dof=23/15), although it remained only marginally acceptable. For this fit we constrained = 7.30 0.05 , in good agreement with previous estimates kundu01b , especially when we account for recent revisions to the Cepheid zero-point jensen03 . In general, however, the ad hoc removal of data-points should be avoided. Taking our fit to NGC 4125 at face value implies intrinsic scatter (estimated as described in   sect_mass_metallicity ) of 0.4 mag, with a 90 lower limit of 0.26 mag, and a best-fitting \ of 7.26 0.15 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4125 as SN 2016coj's early-type peculiar elliptical host, giving its redshift, stellar mass, adopted distance modulus, and low expected host-galaxy extinction.

quotes.quote: SN 2016coj is 5 0 east and 10 8 north of the nucleus of the host galaxy NGC 4125, which has redshift z=0.004523 according to the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ), an early-type peculiar elliptical morphology (E6 pec; de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991), and a stellar mass of 2.4 10^ 11 \ from its 3.6 m flux (Wilson et al. 2013).

The distance modulus of the host galaxy NGC 4125 is quite uncertain owing to different measurements given in NED. However, some of them are outdated, or adopted an inappropriate H _0 value. The one with the smallest uncertainty (and also the latest estimate) is 31.90 0.14 mag (Tully et al. 2013), which was based on H _0=74.4 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , quite close to the current widely accepted value of H _0 70 . We therefore adopt this distance for the following ananlysis.

Although this is a rather large upper limit, it is consistent with the small host-galaxy extinction constrained from our low-resolution spectra (see ss:spectra ) and also with the low extinction expected given the early type of the host, NGC 4125.

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 4125 with NGC 0474 as a bright core-less early-type galaxy whose prominent fine structure fits an in-the-making core-galaxy scenario.

quotes.quote: We therefore applied this magnitude cut to the core- less galaxies with a measured (or T_c ), resulting in the selection of NGC 0474 and NGC 4125 We excluded NGC 5982 for coherency, being one of the few objects for which richings did not report a distance, although the distance retrieved from NED technically makes this object part of the selection. . Such a small number of matching objects (2 out of the parent 41) was indeed expected since observing a galaxy soon after its formation is a statistically rare occurrence: the vast majority core- less objects are ``pure'' galaxies. These two objects appear in panel B-1, where their mass deficit has been set to an arbitrarily small value.

Remarkably, galaxies which will eventually develop a core (see NGC 4125 and NGC 0474 in panel B-1 ) consistently present indication of prominent fine structure. This strongly suggests that galaxy relaxation and core excavation are indeed happening in parallel.

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 4125 as Dusty, noting filamentary nuclear dust, extended soft X-rays, PAH-correlated ionized gas, and non-rotational gas motions linked to past merger material.

quotes.quote: NGC 4125 (see Fig. Figure_AB4 ): This elliptical galaxy contains a disc e.g. vandenBosch1994,Goudfrooij1994a and filamentary nuclear dust e.g. Goudfrooij1994b,Braun2007 , visible in our sharp-divided image, that lead us to a Dusty morphological classification. This galaxy is not detected with radio emission Filho2002 , H I or CO gas Wilson2013 . The soft-X rays are much more extended and not correlated with the ionised gas, that is however correlated with the PAH emission Kaneda2011 . obtained the long slit spectrum of the nucleus, finding evidences of rotation along both the major and minor axes of the galaxy, and young stellar populations in the centre, which could indicate the existence of material coming from a past merger. They found the ionised gas to be oriented along the major axis of the galaxy (as we see it in our H image), following non-rotational motions, no ascribed to outflows.

sources.summary: For NGC4125, the BeppoSAX power-law fit is acceptable, while thermal and Fe-line additions are only marginal and the column density remains poorly constrained.

quotes.quote: NGC4125 The power-law provides an acceptable fit with ^2=13.5/10 dof. The addition of a RS component ( kT=0.17^ +0.60 _ 0.06 keV) yields a better fit with ^2 4.5 for two additional parameters; however this is statistically significant at only the 90 per cent confidence level. Both the power-law slope and the column density are poorly constrained: =2.52^ +0.58 _ 0.55 , while the 90 per cent upper limit on N_H is 3 10^ 22 . Unfortunately, no straightforward comparison can be made with Trinchieri et al. (2000) as these authors chose not to fit a power-law model to the data. The addition of an Fe line ( E=6.75_ 0.35 ^ +0.25 keV) is significant at just below the 90 per cent confidence level.

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 4125 as a literature LINER and excludes it from pure-photoionization metallicity analysis because significant Ne III flux implies complex excitation.

quotes.quote: Four of the galaxies listed in Table Xmetal Line Ratios are reported as low ionization nuclear emission-line regions (LINERs) galaxies in the literature: NGC 2768 LINER_Heckman80 , NGC 3115 Luis_LINER_ngc3115_template , and NGC 4125 and NGC 4261 LINER_SPEC_NGC4261 .

The line ratios for the four LINER galaxies are consistent within the photoionization, but the excitation mechanism and thus the valid regions in diagnostic diagrams are still uncertain and being debated for LINERs (for a review of the physical mechanisms behind LINERs see LINER_review_96 and LINER_review_02 ). Although it is not entirely clear what to expect for LINER galaxies, the presence of strong collisionally excited lines casts a cloud of doubt of pure photoionization. The [ Ne III ] 3869 line is an indicator of the importance of collisional excitation because the collisional cross-section is over an order of magnitude larger than the photoionization transition probability for this line. NGC 2768 and NGC 4125 have significant [ Ne III ] 3869 fluxes and thus we conclude that these systems have a more complex physical structure and excitation mechanism than photoionization. These two galaxies are excluded in the subsequent sections where pure photoionization is assumed.

sources.summary: The paper reports a hard central point source in NGC 4125 surrounded by diffuse emission, possibly radio-loud and contributing most of the hard central X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4125 (T2, E6) has only one hard point source at its center surrounded by diffuse emission. This point source did not have enough counts to justify fitting various models to its spectrum, but its luminosity was estimated to be L_ 0.5-10 keV =9 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 . This source was not detected in the radio but a limit was set: 0.5 mJy at 5 GHz (Wrobel Heeschen 1991). This means that R_ X < -2.7 so this source could be radio loud.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4125 as a low-temperature elliptical and finds it still needs MOND dark matter, though less than its luminous matter.

quotes.quote: More recently the analysis of the large elliptical galaxy NGC 4125 (included here) with T=0.5keV shows more evidence for MOND DM in ellipticals. Here we find that we require NGC 4125 to have a minimum M/L_K =1.7 meaning, for the only system in our sample, there is less DM than luminous matter (note that this mass-to-light ratio in the K-band is obtained after subtracting the hot gas, and cannot be due to this component itself). The requirement of DM in MOND at 0.5 keV for NGC 4125, 0.6 keV for NGC 720 and the SDSS galaxies but no requirement for HSBs (Sanders Noordermeer 2007) or X-ray dim ellipticals and galaxy groups (Milgrom Sanders 2003, Milgrom 2002), necessitates a transition temperature below which the fraction of residual mass diminishes in importance and above which it increases in importance, peaks (between 0.5 keV and 2 keV) and then falls at higher temperatures if neutrinos are present (because the residual mass saturates at 10^ 13 but the dynamical mass continues to rise).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4125 as a luminous giant elliptical with diffuse northeast radio emission and offset inflated radio lobes relative to the galaxy core.

quotes.quote: NGC 4125 NGC 4125 is a luminous giant elliptical galaxy residing in the NGC 4125 group (alternatively called LGG 266). The radio emission (Figure fig:chandra_vla_ps_b ), detected in the archival D configuration data, has a diffuse morphology with an extension to the northeast, corresponding to a point-like radio source visible in the archival 1.4 GHz Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT) observations. More prominent structure has a form of inflated radio lobes, which are offset by 4 kpc (46 arcsec) in projection with respect to the radio, optical, and X-ray core of NGC 4125.

sources.summary: NGC 4125 is described as an elliptical with an incipient disk, merger-indicating fine structure, and a modest nuclear radio source at the stellar nucleus.

quotes.quote: This is an elliptical galaxy containing an incipient disk. According to Fabbiano Schweizer ( ) its rich fine-structure (they report two plumes or disks crossing at right angels) is indicative of a recent merger. We detect a modest nuclear source coincident with the stellar galaxy nucleus and clearly separated from what appears to be a classical double radio galaxy displaced to the South-West.

gmw_00002004.name: NGC 533
gmw_00002004.overview: NGC 533 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2201.01306] that serves as the dominant central galaxy of its surrounding group [arXiv:1001.1630, arXiv:2111.02430]. It hosts extended multiphase gas [arXiv:2201.01306], a filamentary dust distribution [arXiv:2111.02430], and a central radio source with a compact bow-tie morphology [arXiv:1001.1630, arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: NGC 533 has extended multiphase gas and a model-consistent entropy slope, but its larger-halo central status and low cooling ratio may promote precipitation.

quotes.quote: NGC 533 NGC 533 has _v = 271.9 5.6 , _K (1--10 kpc) = 0.88 0.14 , (t_ cool /t_ ff ) = 12.28 3.75 , and extended multiphase gas. Along with M87, it is one of only two galaxies in the HQ sample with _v > 245 km s^ 1 that Lakhchaura2018 classified as E. But unlike M87, its entropy slope is completely consistent with the prediction of the simple analytical model. One reason for this blend of features may be that it is the central galaxy of a larger halo, like M87.

According to ZabludoffMulchaey1998 , the velocity dispersion of the galaxies surrounding NGC 533 is 464 km s^ 1 . Furthermore, its low value of (t_ cool /t_ ff ) suggests that the atmosphere of NGC 533 is susceptible to precipitation.

sources.summary: NGC 533 is described as a group-dominant galaxy with central radio emission, possible X-ray cavities, elongated larger-scale X-ray structure, and a southwest bay needing low-frequency radio follow-up.

quotes.quote: NGC 533 is the dominant galaxy of a group of the same name. Our 4.9 VLA radio data show that the radio emission is dominated by a central point source, although some extension to the west and southwest is detected at modest significance. The X-ray peak is coincident with the central radio source (see Sun09,Gastaldello07, Piffaretti05 ). On smaller scales, two crescent shaped depressions in the X-ray emission are observed, either side of the peak, with a roughly northeast-southwest orientation (see also Fig. fig:ngc533 ). The resolution of the current radio data is insufficient to determine unambiguously whether these features are the result of AGN interaction.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 533 as a group CDG with compact bow-tie radio emission, young-radio-galaxy signatures, X-ray cavities, and optical line emission with dust.

quotes.quote: NGC 533* NGC 533 is the CDG of the NGC 533 group of galaxies. The new high resolution A configuration data observed within our project 15A-305 revealed a central bow-tie-shaped radio emission with a total extent of 5 kpc (Figure fig:chandra_vla_a e), which resembles the radio morphology of FR II radio sources (see Discussion sec:frII ). \ NGC 533 is one of the two galaxies in our sample (besides NGC 708; Section sec:ngc708 ) which shows signatures of being a young radio galaxy o'dea1998 : a compact morphology of the radio emission within the host galaxy without extended features, cavities in X-rays as a sign of small-scale interaction of the radio and X-ray plasma, a double morphology of the radio emission. In comparison with the central X-ray emission, previously studied using Chandra X-ray images by and , we suggest that the radio lobes visible in the red contours in Figure fig:chandra_vla_a e could correspond to the presence of the north-eastern and especially south-western X-ray cavity. The optical analysis presented in , , and showed co-spatial H + [ N II ] emission coincident with a filamentary dust distribution.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 533 as the coolest group-like X-ray system, with strong Fe XVII/XVIII emission, no O VII detection, fixed Galactic absorption, and line-fitting issues.

quotes.quote: 1 keV Groups : NGC 533 is the lowest temperature system and has emission from the same ions as in the 2-4 keV clusters. In this case, Fe XVII and XVIII are strong due to the lower temperature of the system. However, this spectrum is also inconsistent with the standard cooling-flow predictions, since Fe XVII is not much stronger than Fe XVIII and OVII is not detected.
In NGC 533 the absorption is set to the galactic value since it has little low energy continuum emission.
Another problem is evident in the detailed line fitting of the spectrum of NGC 533.

gmw_00002005.name: NGC 4073
gmw_00002005.overview: NGC 4073 is a giant elliptical galaxy that dominates the poor cluster MKW 4 [arXiv:2111.02430]. It features a relaxed X-ray halo [arXiv:2111.02430], hot single-phase gas [arXiv:2201.01306], and a point-like central radio source [arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: NGC 4073 is a single-phase outlier with high X-ray luminosity, hot gas, high pressure, and a large cooling-to-freefall ratio suppressing multiphase condensation.

quotes.quote: Conversely, the single phase galaxy NGC 4073 has one of the greatest X-ray luminosities in the HQ subsample, along with one of the hottest temperatures, and lies above the P_ eq locus. It has _K 0.6 and _v 268 km s^ 1 and does not conform to the analytic model within the measurement uncertainty. However, it also has (t_ cool / t_ ff ) = 32.22 2.92 , which is large enough to strongly suppress multiphase condensation and would be an unusually large value of (t_ cool / t_ ff ) for a galaxy with extended multiphase gas.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4073 as a dominant giant elliptical in MKW 4 with point-like radio morphology, weak C-band detection, and a relaxed X-ray halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 4073* The giant elliptical NGC 4073 is dominant member of the poor cluster MKW 4 and shows a point source-like radio morphology in our new high resolution VLA A configuration observation (Figure fig:chandra_vla_ps_b b). A weak detection of the central radio source has been reported by with VLA in the C-band (4--8 GHz). \ The X-ray emission was studied by who found a 1.7 keV halo within the relaxed system.

gmw_00002006.name: Mrk 1034
gmw_00002006.overview: Mrk 1034 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy that forms an interconnected pair with Ark 80 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603549]. High-resolution radio observations of the galaxy reveal an east-west elongated structure with a collimated jet extending to the east, which matches its near-infrared elongation [arXiv:astro-ph/0603549].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1034 as a Seyfert galaxy with an east-west MERLIN radio structure, an eastern collimated jet extension, and matching near-infrared elongation.

quotes.quote: High resolution MERLIN 5 GHz observations (0 04) of 7 Seyfert galaxies, selected as the ones previously showing evidence of collimated ejection, have been compared with high resolution archive HST data. The radio maps reveal rich structures in all the galaxies. NGC 2639 and TXFS 2226-184 have multiple knot parsec-scale extended structures, Mrk 1034, Mrk 1210, NGC 4922C and NGC 5506 reveal one-sided jets, while IC 1481 exhibits a jet-like extension. The close correlation between the radio-emitting relativistic plasma and the ionized gas in the inner regions of these galaxies allows us to study in detail the physics close to the center of low luminosity AGN.

Mrk 1034: The MERLIN 5 GHz map (Fig. mkn1034 ) of Ark 81 - one of the Seyfert 1 galaxies of the pair Ark 80, Ark 81 that are interconnected- shows an E-W elongated radio structure (20 mas) with a 10 mas jet extension to the E consistent with a highly collimated radio jet in that direction. Mrk 1034 has not been observed yet with HST. However, the inner contours of a 2MASS near-IR image of Mrk 1034 (Fig. mkn1034 ) follow the same elongation along the E-W direction.

gmw_00002007.name: NGC 2639
gmw_00002007.overview: NGC 2639 is an early-type SAa spiral galaxy [arXiv:2202.03449] that hosts a variable [arXiv:astro-ph/0606540, arXiv:astro-ph/0406597] Seyfert 1.9 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1503.01836, arXiv:2008.06039] and a water megamaser [arXiv:1503.01836]. The galaxy lacks a bar [arXiv:2208.14950], exhibits nearly vanished spiral arms [arXiv:2208.14950], and displays episodic radio jet activity [arXiv:2301.01610] alongside a central molecular gas deficit [arXiv:2105.02916, arXiv:2301.01610].

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 2639 as an X-ray nuclear source whose spectrum is best fit by thermal soft emission, outside the bonafide AGN set despite optical Type-II AGN classifications and possible obscuration.

quotes.quote: This is the case for most objects in the sample with the exception of six (namely NGC 0507, NGC 2639, NGC 5953, NGC 6166N01, UGC 12127 and NGC 7619) which are best fitted to pure-thermal models and could be in principle rejected as AGN candidates. However, these objects could also be low-luminosity sources dominated by the extended emission, or even highly obscured sources. In either case, longer exposure times are needed for the intrinsic continuum to be detected. Therefore, we treat these sources as AGN candidates, along with the rest of the detected sample, for which better SNR is needed to perform a spectral analysis.

NGC 2639 is the only object in common with that is not classified as bonafide AGN in our work. We find that the best-fit model only accounts for thermal soft X-ray emission. This object is classified as a Type-II AGN in their work, which might suggest that is highly obscured and cannot be detected with the Chandra capabilities.

sources.summary: The paper reports new polarized VLA observations of NGC 2639, finding orthogonal radio lobes, organized magnetic fields, and evidence for an AGN origin likely tied to a minor merger.

quotes.quote: The radio morphology of NGC 2639 resembles a Seyfert galaxy with lobes like Mrk 6 Kharb06 or NGC 6764 Kharb10 . The north-south radio lobes are highly polarized showing an average fractional polarization of 20 3 in the southern lobe and 30 7 in the northern lobe. Although the southern lobe is brighter in total intensity, the average fractional polarization is slightly higher in the northern lobe. For the optically thin radio lobes in NGC 2639, the inferred magnetic field structures are aligned with the direction of the lobes, but show a slight change in orientation (by about 30 ) at the end of the lobes, both in the north and the south.

This fact along with the collimated morphology of the lobes as well as high degree of linear polarization and an organised magnetic field structure, all point to an AGN origin for the radio lobes in NGC 2639. The discovery of an additional pair of radio lobes in our polarization-sensitive VLA observations at 5.5 GHz makes NGC 2639 only the third known Seyfert galaxy after Mrk 6 and NGC 2992, with multiple pair of lobes on kpc scales aligned at different angles to each other.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2639 as a Type II AGN host with especially depleted central molecular gas, high [OIII] luminosity, complex radio jets, and strong bulge dominance.

quotes.quote: Four galaxies are thus selected: NGC 2410, NGC 2639, NGC 6394 and IC 2247, all of which have previously been identified as Type II AGN by Lacerda et al. (2020), and are also identified as strong AGN by Kalinova et al. (2021). The fifth galaxy in the EDGE-CALIFA sample that contributes AGN spaxels to Fig. mgms_ensemble , NGC 7738, is excluded from the galaxy-by-galaxy analysis due to a lack of star-forming spaxels for comparison.

The gas fraction distribution of AGN spaxels is offset to lower values than the star-forming spaxels in all four galaxies, typically by a factor of a few, but by more than an order of magnitude in NGC 2639. Although all four of the galaxies in Fig. fgas have low molecular gas fractions in their central AGN regions compared with the extended star-forming regions, NGC 2639 is particularly poor in molecular gas. Most of the AGN spaxels in NGC 2639 have log L( [ OIII ] ) > 40 ergs s ^ 1 , whereas the AGN spaxels in the other galaxies rarely have luminosities above this value, indicating that the level of molecular gas depletion may be governed by AGN luminosity. NGC 2639 is also known to have a complex radio jet structure (e.g. Sebastian et al. 2019), and is the most bulge dominated galaxy in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2639 as a strong type-2 AGN with unusually low local and central molecular gas, explaining the low concentration by its lack of a bar and nearly vanished spiral arms.

quotes.quote: Among our 4 Seyferts, there is one type 1 AGN (UGC 3973) and three type 2 AGNs (NGC 2410, NGC 2639, NGC 6394), as identified by . NGC 2410 and NGC 2639 are strong AGNs Kalinova2021 . There are one Seyferts lie above the SF best-fit function, and three lie below it. show that NGC 2639 has significantly lower local gas fraction in the AGN region than in SF regions. Consistent with their results, this galaxy (bottom-left corner on the diagrams for Seyferts) has the lowest gas concentrations: C_ CO = 0.22 and C_ mol = 0.52 .

However, because NGC 2639 lacks a bar and its spiral arms almost vanish, the inflow of gas driven by spiral arms and bars ceases. The low molecular gas concentration observed in NGC 2639 can therefore be explained by the lack of disk instabilities that transport gas inward.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2639 as a Seyfert galaxy with four jet episodes, central molecular-gas and star-formation deficits, and possible jet-driven AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert galaxy NGC 2639 was known to exhibit three episodes of AGN jet/lobe activity. We present here the upgraded Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (uGMRT) 735 MHz image of NGC 2639 showing a fourth episode as witnessed by the discovery of 9 kpc radio lobes misaligned with the previously known 1.5 kpc, 360 parsec, and 3 parsec jet features detected through the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) and the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA), respectively. Using the spectral ageing software BRATS, we derive the ages of the 9 kpc, 1.5 kpc, and 360 parsec episodes to be, respectively, 34^ +4 _ 6 Myr, 11.8^ +1.7 _ 1.4 Myr, and 2.8^ +0.7 _ 0.5 Myr, and conclude that minor mergers occurred 9-22 Myr apart.

NGC 2639 shows a deficit of molecular gas in its central 6 kpc region. The GALEX NUV image also shows a deficiency of recent star-formation in the same region, while the star formation rate (SFR) surface density in NGC 2639 is lower by a factor of 5-18 compared to the global Schmidt law of star-forming galaxies. This makes NGC 2639 a rare case of a Seyfert galaxy showing episodic jet activity and possible signatures of jet-driven AGN feedback.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 2639, a LINER megamaser galaxy, has a bright radio core and knotty east-west wings correlated with HST ultraviolet star-formation morphology.

quotes.quote: High resolution MERLIN 5 GHz observations (0 04) of 7 Seyfert galaxies, selected as the ones previously showing evidence of collimated ejection, have been compared with high resolution archive HST data. The radio maps reveal rich structures in all the galaxies. NGC 2639 and TXFS 2226-184 have multiple knot parsec-scale extended structures, Mrk 1034, Mrk 1210, NGC 4922C and NGC 5506 reveal one-sided jets, while IC 1481 exhibits a jet-like extension. The close correlation between the radio-emitting relativistic plasma and the ionized gas in the inner regions of these galaxies allows us to study in detail the physics close to the center of low luminosity AGN.

NGC2639: The MERLIN 5 GHz map (Fig. ngc2639 ) of this well-known LINER megamaser galaxy reveals a bright core and symmetrical E-W ``wings'' with rich structure (multiple knots), that is highly correlated with the UV star formation morphology of the HST ACS/HRC image (Fig. ngc2639 ).

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 2639 the brightest sample source, with three merged radio components, diffuse outer emission, a VLBA nucleus, and strong radio variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 2639 . The brightest source in our sample shows a 2 ^ wide structure of three components that are merged at the available resolution (Fig. ngc2639 , left). Interestingly, there is some diffuse emission around the two outer components that is constricted at the position of the central source. Wilson et al. ( ) reported a VLBA nucleus and the variability of this source. In fact, their flux on arcsecond scales from an earlier VLA map (Ulvestad Wilson ) was smaller than their VLBA flux. This flux variablity is also obvious when including our data: Our peak flux is 5 times greater than the flux measured by Ulvestad Wilson ( ) and 3 times greater than that of Wilson et al. ( ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2639 as an SAa Ca-rich transient host with large bulge light, declining star formation, low sSFR, and an early major star-formation episode.

quotes.quote: NGC 1032 (SN2005E) is an S0/a galaxy that is in the transition period between E and Sa galaxies on the stage sequence, while NGC 2639 (PTF10hcw) is an SAa galaxy. These two galaxies produce large bulge light and are considered early-type systems along the Hubble sequence with strongly decreasing SFR, suggesting a dearth of new stars within the galaxies, resulting in a small sSFR Sandage1986 . also found that massive red spirals such as NGC 2639 and NGC 1032 and massive elliptical galaxies have very similar SFHs. This means that they have only experienced one major star formation episode early on (and possibly fast quenching).

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 2639 as a variable, triple radio source with a bright central component, a southeast radio peak, and weaker eastern mas-scale emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 2639 (0840+503) Y : This much studied object is a triple (P.A. of 77 ) with a bright central component UlvestadWilson89 . Our map does not resolve the triple structure and, as in the map of , the radio peak lies to the south-east of the optical nucleus ArgyleEldridge90 . This source is variable (A. Wilson, private communication). The integrated spectral index obtained using the observations of at 8.4 GHz (July 1995/November 1996 data) and of at 5 GHz (February 1985 data), is _ 1.4 GHz ^ 5 GHz = 0.5 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2639 as a Seyfert 1.9 megamaser galaxy with notable nuclear stellar kinematics and a mostly unremarkable infrared spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 2639 (S1.9) Like NGC 4258, NGC 2639 hosts a rare nuclear H _2 O megamaser, interpreted as arising in a dense accretion disk Braatz94,Wilson95 . The stellar velocity structure of the nucleus is also noteworthy, with measurements of the calcium triplet lines indicating a drop in velocity dispersion towards the nucleus that may be due to the presence of a young stellar population in a circumnuclear disk Marquez03 . The IR spectrum of NGC 2639 (Fig.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2639 as a grand-design Seyfert galaxy whose EVLA observations reveal a highly polarized, misaligned second lobe pair attributed to restarted AGN activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 2639 classified NGC 2639 as a grand-design spiral galaxy hosting a Seyfert 1.9 nucleus. Our EVLA observations revealed an extra pair of lobes in the north-south direction which appeared to be highly polarized and misaligned by almost 90 from the already identified pair of lobes sebastian2019b . Our observations favor a scenario where the secondary lobes are generated as a result of stopping and restarting of AGN activity.

gmw_00002008.name: NGC 4922C
gmw_00002008.overview: NGC 4922C is the middle radio component of the NGC 4922 interacting system, which is located in the outskirts of the Coma cluster [arXiv:0910.2351, arXiv:0603549]. High-resolution radio observations of NGC 4922C reveal a one-sided jet structure elongated in the northeast-southwest direction [arXiv:0603549].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4922 as a Coma post-merger with two nuclei, a weak AGN, and a UV-bright tidal tail containing young tidal dwarf galaxy candidates.

quotes.quote: Here, we focus on the formation of TDGs in the tidal tail of NGC 4922. NGC 4922 (M _ = -22.1 , z 0.0235 ) is a post-merger galaxy located at the outskirts of the Coma cluster. It is known as a merger between an early-type galaxy and a spiral galaxy. Two nuclei from its progenitors can be resolved, separated by 10 kpc. This galaxy is especially interesting because the northern nucleus is a weak active galactic nucleus (AGN), probably as a consequence of the merging event.
In this paper we study the tidal tail around a post-merger galaxy, NGC 4922, in the Coma cluster. Combining UV and optical photometry, we discover a few TDG candidates in the tail.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4922 as Coma’s best-known merger, a radio- and infrared-bright spiral/early-type pair with H I emission, absorption, and nearby gas lacking an optical counterpart.

quotes.quote: NGC 4922 This is the best known case of a merger in Coma, and the only pair detected in this survey; it is located in the northern most field, 1.4 from the cluster center. It is very bright in radio (27.5 mJy) and the brightest IR source in Coma (Mirabel Sanders 1988). NGC 4922 consists of two merging galaxies, one spiral in the north and one early type galaxy in the south.

We detected in emission and absorption. The shown in Fig. 24, centered in the spiral galaxy, is strongly attenuated by the absorption. An emission equivalent to 5.8 10 ^ 7 was detected south of the merging system, but no optical counterpart is evident from the DSS image.

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 4922C as the middle radio component in the NGC 4922 interacting system and describes its one-sided, northeast-southwest radio structure.

quotes.quote: High resolution MERLIN 5 GHz observations (0 04) of 7 Seyfert galaxies, selected as the ones previously showing evidence of collimated ejection, have been compared with high resolution archive HST data. The radio maps reveal rich structures in all the galaxies. NGC 2639 and TXFS 2226-184 have multiple knot parsec-scale extended structures, Mrk 1034, Mrk 1210, NGC 4922C and NGC 5506 reveal one-sided jets, while IC 1481 exhibits a jet-like extension. The close correlation between the radio-emitting relativistic plasma and the ionized gas in the inner regions of these galaxies allows us to study in detail the physics close to the center of low luminosity AGN.

NGC 4922: The MERLIN 5 GHz map (Fig. ngc4922 ) detects the middle component C, PGC 044896 (FIRST J130125.2+291849 radio source), from the three (3) galaxy system NGC 4922 (NGC 4922A/B are both Seyfert 2) as shown in HST WFPC2 (F606W) image (Fig. ngc4922 ). The radio structure is elongated in the NE-SW direction, in good agreement with the orientation of the interacting galaxies.

gmw_00002009.name: TXFS 2226-184
gmw_00002009.overview: TXFS 2226-184 is a LINER galaxy classified as an elliptical or S0 galaxy [arXiv:2003.10459]. It hosts an exceptionally luminous 22 GHz gigamaser [arXiv:2003.10459] and displays a multiple-knot parsec-scale radio jet that aligns with larger-scale ionized gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0603549].

sources.summary: The paper studies TXS 2226-184 as a LINER elliptical/S0 galaxy hosting an exceptionally luminous 22 GHz gigamaser likely linked to nuclear jet activity.

quotes.quote: TXS 2226-184 VLBI observations of the gigamaser in . Outside the Milky Way, the most luminous masers at 22 GHz, called 'megamasers' because of their extreme luminosity with respect to the Galactic and extragalactic masers associated with star formation, are mainly detected in active galactic nuclei. In the case of the maser detected in the nuclear region of the galaxy for the first time the term 'gigamaser' was used. However, the origin of this very luminous maser emission has never been investigated into details. We study the nature of the 22 GHz gigamaser in by measuring for the first time its absolute position at milliarcsecond resolution, by comparing the morphology and characteristics of the maser emission at the very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) scales after about 20 years, and by trying to detect its polarized emission.

, located at distance of 107.1 Mpc (derived assuming H_ 0 =70 , kuo18 ) and with a systemic velocity of V_ lsr, radio =7270 tay02 , has been optically classified as an elliptical/S0 galaxy koe95 , even though fal00 proposed an alternative classification as a possible later-type galaxy. Furthermore, is spectroscopically identified as a LINER ben04 , which is a signature of an AGN or a nuclear starburst. Indeed, despite the radio core is relatively weak (or heavily obscured), the symmetric and tight structure of the nuclear radio emission is seemingly produced by a jet (position angle PA =-36 ) that extends over 100 pc oriented at large angle to the line of sight tay04 . hosts one of the most luminous known extragalactic maser sources, the so called 'gigamaser', with an isotropic luminosity of 6100 koe95 .

sources.summary: The paper reports TXFS 2226-184 as having a multiple-knot parsec-scale radio structure aligned with larger-scale H emission and perpendicular to the galaxy major axis.

quotes.quote: High resolution MERLIN 5 GHz observations (0 04) of 7 Seyfert galaxies, selected as the ones previously showing evidence of collimated ejection, have been compared with high resolution archive HST data. The radio maps reveal rich structures in all the galaxies. NGC 2639 and TXFS 2226-184 have multiple knot parsec-scale extended structures, Mrk 1034, Mrk 1210, NGC 4922C and NGC 5506 reveal one-sided jets, while IC 1481 exhibits a jet-like extension. The close correlation between the radio-emitting relativistic plasma and the ionized gas in the inner regions of these galaxies allows us to study in detail the physics close to the center of low luminosity AGN.

TXFS 2226-184: The multiple knot radio structure (Fig. txfs2226 ) coincides with the nucleus of the host galaxy and then is aligned with the H emission on the larger scale. This alignment reflects an interaction between the radio jet and the interstellar medium. The H and radio structures are perpendicular to the galaxy major axis (Fig. txfs2226 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports possible wide H I absorption toward the gigamaser galaxy TXS 2226--184, interpreting its mostly blue-shifted absorption as likely gas outflow pending confirmation.

quotes.quote: HIPASS J2229--18 : weak \ absorption over a wide velocity range, from 6900 to 7650 , appears to be detected towards the gigamaser galaxy TXS 2226--184 ( \ = 7551 ). With the majority of the absorption clearly blue-shifted with respect to the systemic velocity, gas outflow is a likely explanation. VLA \ measurements reveal a much narrower (420 ) absorption feature (Taylor et al. 2002, 2004). Further \ data are needed to confirm this result.

gmw_00002010.name: IC 1481
gmw_00002010.overview: IC 1481 is a LINER [arXiv:2112.07084, arXiv:astro-ph/0603549] and Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1708.02683] that may be an ongoing merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0603549]. Its active nucleus hosts a water megamaser disk [arXiv:1104.2457, arXiv:astro-ph/0603549] and exhibits a post-starburst stellar population [arXiv:2112.07084, arXiv:1708.02683] alongside extensive, cone-like ionization structures [arXiv:2112.07084, arXiv:1708.02683].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC1481 as an AGN maser-disk example, fitting its rotation data in a position-dynamical mass diagram and finding consistent fits with disk mass exceeding black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: An example: the case of IC1481 The top panel in Fig. fig:ic1481 shows the rotational velocity of maser spots observed in the outer disk of the active nucleus in galaxy IC1481 versus the distance from the center mamyoda09 . The radius of the outermost maser spot is set to (although the gas disk can extend farther away It can be shown that Eqs. eq:o1 and eq:o2 still hold if one uses a reference value a_0 other than the outer radius since R^ s+2 . Then refers to the cumulative disk mass up to this reference radius a_0 . ). The bottom panel displays the same data once converted into a ( , )- diagram, as well as the result of the fit by Eq. eq:to2 (non-linear) and by Eq. eq:tmus (linear). For each point, the square of the radial deviation between the data and the linear fit is shown (see below).

These are in good agreement, especially because the solution of the non linear fit gives an s parameter close to 1 . In such a case, the curvature is difficult to detect by eye. As announced in In , an extreme case without black hole was considered. , the disk mass is higher than the black hole mass by a factor of 2-3 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1481 as a LINER with extraplanar diffuse ionized gas, post-starburst stellar absorption, off-nuclear higher ionization, and emission-line morphology interpretable as east-west ionization cones.

quotes.quote: IC 1481 is listed as showing extraplanar diffuse ionized gas based on the CALIFA IFU survey by . Our BTA SCORPIO spectrum (Fig. fig-IC1481NGC7679spec ) shows LINER emission at the nucleus, much like NGC 1052, with unusually strong [ N I ] 5199 emission. The stellar population shows post-starburst signatures, with strong absorption in the higher-order Balmer lines. Higher ionization levels are seen off the nucleus. While the MaNGaL emission-line image has better spatial sampling and image quality in [ O III ] , the CALIFA data are in good agreement with the overall emission-line structure, and indicate that both H and [ O III ] occur in similar regions (which may mean that the structure we see is limited by the presence of neutral gas rather than ionizing photons).

The morphology of the emission-line structure can be interpreted as a pair of ionization cones with axes east-west, crossing very close to the continuum and H peak, so IC 1481 may join NGC 1052 NGC1052 and NGC 2442 NGC2442 as LINERs with ionization cones.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1481 as a LINER water maser galaxy and possible ongoing merger with an elongated MERLIN radio structure linked to parsec-scale maser disks.

quotes.quote: High resolution MERLIN 5 GHz observations (0 04) of 7 Seyfert galaxies, selected as the ones previously showing evidence of collimated ejection, have been compared with high resolution archive HST data. The radio maps reveal rich structures in all the galaxies. NGC 2639 and TXFS 2226-184 have multiple knot parsec-scale extended structures, Mrk 1034, Mrk 1210, NGC 4922C and NGC 5506 reveal one-sided jets, while IC 1481 exhibits a jet-like extension. The close correlation between the radio-emitting relativistic plasma and the ionized gas in the inner regions of these galaxies allows us to study in detail the physics close to the center of low luminosity AGN.

IC 1481: The radio map of this LINER water maser galaxy and a possible site of ongoing merger, shows elongated structure (Fig. ic1481 ) which agrees with VLBI observations that have shown that water maser emission is associated with thin, parsec-scale molecular disks located near the spout of radio continuum jets.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe IC 1481 as a post-starburst circum-nuclear system with a reddened Seyfert 2 nucleus, extensive figure-eight narrow-line emission, and additional knots across the field.

quotes.quote: IC 1481: This extraordinary galaxy has a post-starburst circum-nuclear continuum spectrum with apparently large reddening at the Sy 2 nucleus itself. The ENLR is very extensive, its brightest portions forming a figure of eight extending 13 arcsec at PA = 50^ either side of the nucleus. Additional knots of emission are visible to the edges of the field.

gmw_00002011.name: J1000+0221S
gmw_00002011.overview: J1000+0221S is a strongly gravitationally lensed dwarf galaxy located at a redshift of 3.417 [arXiv:1505.00502, arXiv:1403.6470]. It is characterized as an extremely metal-poor star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1403.6470, arXiv:1410.5048] with high ionization conditions [arXiv:1703.00354, arXiv:1403.6470] and a low stellar mass [arXiv:1505.00502, arXiv:1403.6470].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID=59330 as a strongly lensed COSMOS galaxy at z=3.417 with very faint intrinsic luminosity, low metallicity, high ionization, and exclusion from the stacking analysis.

quotes.quote: Notes on individual objects: (a): object ID=59330 in COSMOS is the lensed galaxy studied by and by . The U- and R-band magnitudes are the observed photometry for ID=59330, while the absolute magnitude provides the intrinsic luminosity after the correction by a magnification factor of 40 1 .

To push our LyC analysis even deeper, we added the lensed galaxy by , at z=3.417 and magnified by a factor of 40 1 , with intrinsic magnitude of R_ delensed =28.31 , corresponding to L=0.036L^* ( M_ 1500 =-17.4 ). The properties of this galaxy have also been discussed by . Interestingly, this galaxy has been classified as a metal poor galaxy by , with 12+log(O/H)< 7.44 (or equivalently Z<0.05Z_ ). In addition, it shows high ionization conditions, with [ OIII ] /H ratio >5 and [ OIII ] / [ OII ] >10 .
The galaxy ID=59330 in the COSMOS field is the lensed object studied by and it will not be considered in the stacking procedure, which is restricted to the 69 galaxies of luminosity L 0.2L^* .

sources.summary: The paper models J1000+0021 as a quadruple galaxy-galaxy lens at lens redshift 1.53, noting its highly magnified low-mass source galaxy and quiescent flattened lens.

quotes.quote: J1000+0021 Using imaging data from CANDELS and the large binocular telescope, van der Wel and colleagues recently reported the quadruple galaxy-galaxy lens J100018.47+022138.74 (J1000+0221), which is the first strong galaxy lens at z_ lens > 1 vanderwel2013 . This interesting system has a z_ lens =1.53 and a z_ source =3.417. , analyzed this system in the manner described previously by , and reported an Einstein radius of R_E=0.35 '' with an enclosed mass of M_E =(7.6 0.5) with an upper limit on the dark matter fraction of 60 . The highly magnified (40 ) source galaxy has a very small stellar mass ( 10^8 ). The z=1.53 lens is a flattened, quiescent galaxy with a stellar mass of 6 .

There have been no other lens model analyses of this system using software models and therefore all models were developed for this study using data from , and is thus is a direct comparison of the four lens software models studied. There were no priors used in the lens models of J1000+0021 in this study.

sources.summary: The paper presents J1000+0221S as a strongly lensed z=3.417 extreme emission-line dwarf with high ionization, very low metallicity, low stellar mass, and active star formation.

quotes.quote: We investigate the gas-phase metallicity and Lyman Continuum (LyC) escape fraction of a strongly gravitationally lensed, extreme emission-line galaxy at z=3.417 , J1000+0221S , recently discovered by the CANDELS team. We derive ionization and metallicity sensitive emission-line ratios from H+K band LBT/LUCI medium resolution spectroscopy. J1000+0221S shows high ionization conditions, as evidenced by its enhanced [ ] / [ ] and [ ] / \ ratios. Consistently, strong-line methods based on the available line ratios suggest that J1000+0221S is an extremely metal-poor galaxy, with a metallicity of 12 + (O/H) < 7.44 ( Z < 0.05 Z_ , placing it among the most metal-poor star-forming galaxies at z 3 discovered so far.

In combination with its low stellar mass (2 10 ^ 8 M _ ) and high star formation rate (5 M _ yr ^ 1 ), the metallicity of J1000+0221S is consistent with the extrapolation to low masses of the mass-metallicity relation traced by Lyman-break galaxies at z 3, but it is 0.55 dex lower than predicted by the fundamental metallicity relation at z 2.5.

sources.summary: The paper models J1000+0221S as a strongly lensed, metal-poor star-forming galaxy with line ratios consistent with AGN and starburst grids and a Seyfert-2-like AGN flux.

quotes.quote: J1000+0221S . Amor i n et al. (2014) report the spectrum observed from this strongly gravitationally lensed galaxy. The observed line ratios appear in Table 6. They are similar to those calculated by models presented in GRID1 (table 4, mod 36) for AGNs and in GRID2 (table 1, mod 6 and table 2, mod 7) for SBs. The AGN flux F =3 10 ^ 11 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 eV ^ 1 at the Lyman limit, is suitable to Seyfert 2 AGN and is higher by 7-200 than in the other galaxies of the present sample. Amor i n et al. define this object as a metal-poor star forming galaxy based on the low O/H and low U deduced by empirical +photoionization method.

gmw_00002012.name: NGC 2350
gmw_00002012.overview: NGC 2350 is a lenticular S0/a galaxy that features a bar with bright edge structures known as ansae [arXiv:1406.7121, arXiv:1312.6701]. The galaxy hosts an extended, co-rotating ionized gas disk that is undergoing star formation across its entire structure [arXiv:1406.7121, arXiv:1312.6701].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2350 as an S0/a galaxy with ansae, an ionized gas disc, current disc-wide star formation, young ansa-region stars, and metal-poor central stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 2350 This poorly studied galaxy was unfortunately missed by the SDSS survey. According to the HyperLeda and NED, it is classified as S0/a. In the white-light image obtained with the SCORPIO-2 at the 6m Russian telescope, we see a complex distribution of surface brightness shaped as bright spots at the edges of the bar -- the so-called ``ansae'' phenomenon. In addition, from the analysis of the emission-line component of the spectrum, the galaxy has an extended disc of ionized warm gas. Plotting the emission-line ratios onto the diagnostic BPT-diagrams, we have made sure that it is excited mainly by the radiation of young stars ilg_gas ; i.e., this lenticular galaxy is undergoing current star formation over the entire disc.

sources.summary: NGC 2350 has gas emission to 25 arcsec that co-rotates with its stellar disk, two velocity-dispersion peaks, and line ratios dominated by star formation.

quotes.quote: Almost all measurements in NGC 2350 and NGC 7351 as well as in the outer ring of NGC 6798 are located in the BPT-diagram region where star-formation excitation mechanism dominates.

NGC 2350 is characterized by gas emission extending up to 25 arcsec ( 3.5 kpc); and all the gas co-rotates the main stellar disc. The gas has two peaks at velocity dispersion profile at radii =-17 arcsec and =12 arcsec, where the galaxy image reveals bar ``ansae'' features.

gmw_00002013.name: NGC 3248
gmw_00002013.overview: NGC 3248 is a galaxy surrounded by a dozen faint companions beyond a radial distance of 100 kpc [arXiv:1406.7121]. Its central region contains counterrotating ionized, neutral, and molecular gas [arXiv:1406.7121] with an asymmetric velocity profile [arXiv:1312.6701], showing shock-like excitation and no ongoing star formation [arXiv:1406.7121].

sources.summary: NGC 3248 shows an asymmetric gas velocity profile, consistent long-slit and SAURON rotational velocities, and a previously measured ionized-gas versus stellar misalignment.

quotes.quote: In NGC 3248 the gas LOS velocity profile has asymmetric shape: the North-West side has higher velocity amplitude than the South-East one. Probably this asymmetry is associated with unsettled state of the gas subsystem. This galaxy is included into the ATLAS-3D sample and is studied with the IFU spectrograph SAURON. The maximum rotational velocity reached within the SAURON field of view, k_1=87.4 atlas3d_2_krajnovic2011 , is consistent with our long-slit measurements. atlas3d_10 detected misalignment between the ionized gas and stars ( _ ion-star =179.5 9.8 degree) in NGC 3248.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 3248 has faint companions, central gas studied by SAURON, counterrotating ionized, neutral, and molecular gas, shock-like excitation, and intermediate-age stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 3248 Beyond a radial distance of 100 kpc, the galaxy is surrounded by a dozen of faint companions, the brightest of which is only 3^ m fainter than the host galaxy. The central region of the galaxy, R<15 , has been investigated in detail by means of panoramic spectroscopy with the SAURON spectrograph within the ATLAS-3D survey cappellari_atlas7_2011,davis_2011 . The galaxy is found to contain a lot of gas, ionized, neutral and molecular, and all this gas counterrotates with respect to the stellar component. We have expanded the kinematic profiles to the distance of 30 \ from the center and confirmed the counterrotation of the gas concentrated in the central region of the galaxy. The ionized-gas excitation is shock-like, and there are no signs of ongoing star formation.

gmw_00002014.name: NGC 6654
gmw_00002014.overview: NGC 6654 is a luminous barred lenticular galaxy featuring a low-surface-brightness stellar disk [arXiv:1406.7121] and a secondary nuclear bar [arXiv:1312.6701, arXiv:0811.3527]. The galaxy hosts an outer gaseous ring with ongoing star formation [arXiv:1406.7121], while its central region contains ionized gas with decoupled kinematics [arXiv:1312.6701] and shock-like excitation [arXiv:1406.7121].

sources.summary: NGC 6654 has weak emission lines, unusual ionized-gas velocity amplitudes, and inferred decoupled stellar and gaseous disks rather than bar-driven planar non-circular motion.

quotes.quote: NGC 6654 is an example of weak emission lines. This galaxy was observed under instrumental setup covering only and [ O ] lines. [ O ] doublet shows intense increase of the relative LOS velocity amplitude up to 200--250 in the central few arcsec being perhaps decoupled though not counterrotating with respect to stars, and then falls to the stellar rotation velocity level at a radial distance of 10--15 arcsec. Kinematics of the emission line follows the stellar rotation; this line is visible only in a few spatial bins at large radial distances, R>40 arcsec. NGC 6654 was studied as a double-barred candidate by Moiseev6654 who used the Multi-Pupil Fiber Spectrograph (MPFS) at the 6-m Russian telescope.

One can see that ionized gas has a similar kinematical major axis as the stars but the velocity amplitudes are quite different and both are consistent with our long-slit measurements. To explain extreme visible velocity amplitudes of the ionized gas, we have considered a possibility of planar non-circular gas motions within triaxial potential of a nuclear bar with a radius of 4 arcsec which is known in this galaxy erwinsparke . However, the orientations of both bars in NGC 6654, PA=13^ for the primary one and PA=135^ for the secondary one nirs0s , differ substantially from the disc line of nodes orientation and are not orthogonal to it, so the gas affected by these bars must demonstrate the turn of the kinematical major axis away from the disc line of nodes.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6654 as a luminous barred lenticular with a low-surface-brightness disc, outer star-forming gaseous ring, central shock-like gas, and disc rejuvenation.

quotes.quote: NGC 6654 It is a luminous galaxy with a large-scale bar and a large-scale stellar disc of low surface brightness. The NED database gives it a classification as (R ' )SB(s)0/a. We have accepted the main parameters of the photometric structure of the galaxy from laurikainen_2010 . The outer edge of the disc reveals an emission-line gaseous ring with ongoing star formation. Apart from this outer ring, the ionized gas is only present in the central region of the galaxy, where it demonstrates shock-like excitation and rotates too fast to be in the main symmetry plane of the galaxy which is inclined by some 45 to the line of sight (according to the HyperLeda).

sources.summary: The paper reports MPFS stellar kinematics for NGC 6654, finding regular circular motion across the photometric secondary bar and velocity-dispersion hollows similar to other candidate double-bar systems.

quotes.quote: Fig.  fig1 shows the MPFS maps for NGC 6654. The kinematical axis derived from the velocity field coincides with disk position angle at <4.5'' . In other words, a pure circular regular rotation is observed on the radii corresponded to the secondary bar.
Despite the smaller FOV of the MPFS, we found similar features in NGC 2950 and NGC 6654 (see Fig.  fig1 ).

gmw_00002015.name: UGC 4551
gmw_00002015.overview: UGC 4551 is a galaxy featuring a counterrotating gaseous component [arXiv:1406.7121] and complex ionized-gas kinematics [arXiv:1312.6701]. Its central region and outer disc contain old stellar populations, while a younger stellar lens is situated between the bulge and the disc [arXiv:1406.7121].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 4551 as having counterrotating gas, old central and outer disc populations, a younger stellar lens, and dynamically hot lens and disc components.

quotes.quote: UGC 4551 It is one more galaxy with a counterrotating gaseous component, which may rotate towards the stars right in the plane of the galactic disc ilg_gas . Nevertheless, both the central part of the galaxy and its external disc contain uniformly old, T>10 Gyr , stellar populations (Fig. kin_stpop_profiles:Katkov_n ). A stellar lens with a flat brightness profile is observed between the bulge and the disc. Here, in the lens, stellar population is significantly younger than in the disc and in the bulge. From the dynamical point of view both the lens and the disc look rather ``hot'' >100 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: UGC 4551 has complex ionized-gas kinematics, AGN/LINER-like line ratios over most measurements, and shock or post-AGB excitation linked to inclined gas motions.

quotes.quote: Emission-line measurements for the other galaxies -- NGC 3248, UGC 9519, and the most part of UGC 4551 -- fall into the AGN/LINER-dominated (or shock-dominated) region of the BPT-diagram.

Ionized gas kinematics in UGC 4551 has a complex character. Relative LOS velocities of the gas rise rapidly in the central region, then the amplitude falls down to null and then the North-Western side of the velocity profile rises up to 200 with following declining.

Shocks or post-AGB stars are the main agent of gas excitation in the remaining galaxies (NGC 3248, UGC 4551, UGC 9519) whose velocity profiles indicate asymmetries and complex features which are probably resulted from the gas motions in the inclined planes.

gmw_00002016.name: NGC 4264
gmw_00002016.overview: NGC 4264 is a gas-poor lenticular galaxy located in the region of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1906.09853, arXiv:2303.11441]. It is undergoing a tidal interaction with its neighbor NGC 4261, but its strong, fast-rotating internal bar was formed through self-generated internal processes rather than being triggered by this interaction [arXiv:1906.09853, arXiv:1909.13523].

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 4264's bar size, strength, pattern speed, circular velocity, and rotation rate, concluding it hosts a fast strong bar formed internally.

quotes.quote: We present surface photometry and stellar kinematics of NGC 4264, a barred lenticular galaxy in the region of the Virgo Cluster undergoing a tidal interaction with one of its neighbours, NGC 4261. We measured the bar radius ( a_ bar =3.2 0.5 kpc) and strength ( S_ bar =0.31 0.04 ) of NGC 4264 from Sloan Digital Sky Survey imaging and its bar pattern speed ( _ bar =71 4 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 ) using the Tremaine-Weinberg method with stellar-absorption integral-field spectroscopy performed with the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer at the Very Large Telescope. We derived the circular velocity ( V_ circ = 189 10 km s ^ 1 ) by correcting the stellar streaming velocity for asymmetric drift and calculated the corotation radius ( R_ cor =2.8 0.2 kpc) from the bar pattern speed. Finally, we estimated the bar rotation rate ( R_ cor /a_ bar =0.88 0.23 ). We find that NGC 4264 hosts a strong and large bar extending out to the corotation radius.

The accurate measurement of the bar rotation rate allows us to infer that the formation of the bar of NGC 4264 was due to self-generated internal processes and not triggered by the ongoing interaction.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 4264 with NGC 4277, finding NGC 4264 is a gas-poor lenticular with a fast bar and lower inner dark-matter fraction.

quotes.quote: We investigate the link between the bar rotation rate and dark matter content in barred galaxies by concentrating on the cases of the lenticular galaxies NGC 4264 and NGC 4277. These two gas-poor galaxies have similar morphologies, sizes, and luminosities. But, NGC 4264 hosts a fast bar, which extends to nearly the corotation, while the bar embedded in NGC 4277 is slow and falls short of corotation. We derive the fraction of dark matter f_ DM, bar within the bar region from Jeans axisymmetric dynamical models by matching the stellar kinematics obtained with the MUSE integral-field spectrograph and using SDSS images to recover the stellar mass distribution.

We find that the inner regions of NGC 4277 host a larger fraction of dark matter ( f_ DM, bar = 0.53 0.02 ) with respect to NGC 4264 ( f_ DM, bar = 0.33 0.04 ) in agreement with the predictions of theoretical works and the findings of numerical simulations, which have found that fast bars live in baryon-dominated discs, whereas slow bars experienced a strong drag from the dynamical friction due to a dense DM halo.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4264 as a Virgo lenticular interacting with NGC 4261, measuring its strong fast bar and concluding the recent interaction did not trigger bar formation.

quotes.quote: We present surface photometry and stellar kinematics of NGC 4264, a lenticular galaxy in the region of the Virgo Cluster undergoing a tidal interaction with its neighbour, NGC 4261. We measured the bar radius and strength from SDSS imaging and the bar pattern speed from MUSE integral-field spectroscopy. We find that NGC 4264 hosts a strong and large bar, which is rotating fast. The accurate measurement of the bar rotation rate allows us to exclude that the formation of the bar was triggered by the ongoing interaction.

gmw_00002017.name: NGC 4458
gmw_00002017.overview: NGC 4458 is a slowly rotating, misaligned elliptical galaxy in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1105.4809, arXiv:2311.13747]. It hosts a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:1105.4809], an old nuclear stellar disk [arXiv:1511.08219], and central velocity dispersion measurements that indicate the presence of a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2311.13747].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4458 as a low-luminosity core galaxy whose profile admits non-unique nucleus-plus-envelope interpretations, including a dwarf-S0 bulge/disk reading.
