B3 0309+411 and 4C 74.26 require reflection at more than 99 confidence level, while 3C 390.3 and QSO B0241+62 at a lower confidence level (98 and 92 respectively). However, in B3 0309+411 and 4C 74.26 the observed iron line EW is too small for the reflection measured: a possible explanation resides in the scatter expected in the EW values due to a variation in the iron abundance, or to a possible anisotropy of the source seed photons which might affect the observed spectrum b42,b43 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NuSTAR and Swift spectra of radio-loud quasar 4C 74.26, finding a high-energy cut-off, ionized reflection, and a mildly recessed accretion disk without significant inner-radius evolution.

quotes.quote: Here, we present the hard X-ray NuSTAR observations of the radio-loud quasar 4C 74.26 in a joint analysis with quasi-simultaneous, soft X-ray Swift observations. Our spectral analysis reveals a high-energy cut-off of 183 _ 35 ^ +51 keV and confirms the presence of ionized reflection in the source. From the average spectrum we detect that the accretion disk is mildly recessed with an inner radius of R_ in =4-180 R_ g . However, no significant evolution of the inner radius is seen during the three months covered by our NuSTAR campaign. This lack of variation could mean that the jet formation in this radio-loud quasar differs from what is observed in broad-line radio galaxies.

sources.summary: 4C +74.26 is described as a large quasar-associated giant radio galaxy with typical FR-II lobes, aligned one-sided pc-scale and kpc-scale jets, and an old lobe population.

quotes.quote: 4C +74.26: the TGSS image reported here (Fig. maps2 , bottom-left panel) shows a typical FRII morphology, with prominent emission from the lobes. It is one of the largest radio sources associated with a quasar. revealed a one-sided, pc-scale, nuclear emission through VLBI observations, well aligned with the kpc-scale jet visible with the VLA: this is suggestive of a jet orientation close to the line of sight, with an estimated angle < 49 ^ . Finally, the core is reported to show a GPS radio spectrum in , while the lobes have an age of 90-120 Myr as estimated from dynamical considerations 2011MNRAS.413.2429M .

sources.summary: The paper reports preliminary Chandra/HETGS results for 4C 74.26, describing it as a radio-loud quasar with strong X-ray flux and detected emission and absorption lines.

quotes.quote: 4C 74.26 ( z = 0.104, V 14.8 ) is a radio loud quasar with relatively strong X-ray flux compared with other quasars. It was observed with Chandra /HETGS on December 2003 for 70 ks. A preliminary analysis of the data revels emission and absorption lines from H-like and He-like ions of Mg, Al, and Si, as well as lower ionization Si absorption lines. These data, as well as the ones presented in the previous sections, indicate that highly-ionized WAs are present in quasars and can be detected with the power of high resolution X-ray spectroscopy.

sources.summary: The paper presents 4C +74 as a radio-loud quasar candidate dusty warm absorber, where dusty absorption fits the soft X-ray spectrum with a steeper intrinsic power law and reddening-consistent column density.

quotes.quote: 4C +74.26 4C +74 is a radio-loud quasar. In an analysis of and data, Brinkmann et al. (1998) find an unusually flat soft X-ray spectrum ( 1.3 to 1.6 ; as compared to 2.2 typically seen in radio-loud quasars), a steeper powerlaw (PL) spectrum, and evidence for the presence of a warm absorber. Applying the model of a dusty warm absorber to the spectrum we get a successful spectral fit, with a steeper intrinsic PL spectrum (now consistent with the value and the general expectation for radio-loud quasars), and a column density N_ w consistent with optical reddening (Tab. 1). Alternatively, excess cold absorption of large column density fits the spectrum, and better spectral resolution soft X-ray data are needed to exclude a cold absorber.

sources.summary: The review highlights 4C74.26 as a giant radio quasar whose nuclear jet aligns with the large-scale jet and whose flux ratio implies inclination below 49 degrees.

quotes.quote: Two of the GRQs with prominent jets are 4C34.47 Barthel89vlbi and 4C74.26 riley89 . 4C34.47 has a prominent large-scale jet without any counter jet with the flux density ratio > 10. VLBI observations of the core show a nuclear jet exhibiting superluminal motion indicating that this component lies within 44 ^ to the line of sight Barthel89vlbi . The nuclear jet in 4C74.26 is also aligned with the large-scale jet. Although there are no clear features to monitor for the motion of the nuclear jet, the flux density ratio suggests an angle of inclination < 49 ^ pearson92 .

sources.summary: The paper applies a dusty warm absorber model to the radio-loud quasar 4C +74.26, finding a successful fit but noting cold absorption remains viable.

quotes.quote: 4C +74.26 In an analysis of and data of the radio-loud quasar 4C +74, Brinkmann et al. (1998) find an unusually flat soft X-ray spectrum ( 1.3 to 1.6 ; as compared to 2.2 typically seen in radio-loud quasars), a steeper powerlaw (PL) spectrum, and evidence for the presence of a warm absorber. Applying the model of a dusty warm absorber to the spectrum we get a successful spectral fit, with a steeper intrinsic PL spectrum (now consistent with the value and the general expectation for radio-loud quasars), and a column density N_ w consistent with optical reddening. However, excess cold absorption provides an alternative successful spectral fit, and higher X-ray spectral resolution is required to exclude a cold absorber.

gmw_00002032.name: PKS 2356--61
gmw_00002032.overview: PKS 2356--61 is a giant radio galaxy [arXiv:2003.09183, arXiv:1903.05922] that exhibits a disturbed optical morphology with shells, fans, and features indicative of a past merger or interaction [arXiv:1008.2683]. Its active nucleus is classified as a narrow-line radio galaxy [arXiv:2211.13031, arXiv:1008.2683] and is modeled with a geometrically thick torus of smooth gas and clumpy dust [arXiv:2104.11263].

sources.summary: The paper says PKS2356-61 has longer far-infrared bands dominated by non-thermal core emission, correctable shorter-band contamination, and unusually strong core contamination tied to high radio luminosity.

quotes.quote: In addition, there are two objects -- PKS0915-11 and PKS2356-61 -- for which the longer far-IR wavelengths are clearly dominated by non-thermal core emission, but whose well-sampled radio-to-far-IR SEDs (including ALMA , APEX LABOCA and Herschel SPIRE data) show evidence for a turnover to steeper spectra at 350 - 500 . In these cases, a simple power-law extrapolation of the LABOCA to longer-wavelength SPIRE points allows us to estimate the contribution of the non-thermal contamination on the shorter-wavelength fluxes. We mark these objects with a "C" in Table tab:NTcontam , and Table tab:NTcor shows the far-IR fluxes for these two sources after this non-thermal core power-law has been subtracted.

The two NLRG/FRII objects that show clearest evidence for non-thermal core contamination at far-IR wavelengths are PKS0442-28 Note that although its ALMA and high frequency radio fluxes are high relative to the far-IR fluxes for this source, PKS0042-28 remains an uncertain case because of the lack of LABOCA and SPIRE flux measurements. and PKS2356-61. It is unlikely that the prominence of the non-thermal core components in these cases is due to beaming effects, for example related to intermediate inclinations of their jets to the line of sight, since the core-to-extended radio flux ratios of the two sources are typical of NLRG sources and somewhat below the average values for BLRG/Q objects in the 2Jy sample morganti97 . Rather, it is more plausible that the relatively high level of radio core contamination in these sources is related to their high overall radio luminosities: they represent the two objects with the highest total radio luminosities of the 20 FRII radio galaxies in the 2Jy sample at z<0.15 tadhunter98 .

sources.summary: PKS 2356--61 is discussed as a possible double-double giant radio galaxy with unresolved hot spots, symmetric diffuse regions, emission minima, and signs of past jet activity.

quotes.quote: PKS 2356--61: this source was observed with ATCA at 1.4 GHz by , who detected two unresolved hotspots at the lobes edges, connected by continuous emission peaking into two diffuse emission regions symmetrically located about the core. Moreover, those authors noted a minimum of emission along the jets axes, reminding a double-double morphology. A similar morphology is seen in the ATCA image at 20 GHz presented by , where a further minimum of emission, this time between the core and the supposed inner sets of lobes, is evident. Moreover, the bottle-necked morphology of the external sets of lobes (Fig. 8 in 1996MNRAS.279..257S ), could be a consequence of the jets emerging from a past activity emission region (see discussion in 2013MNRAS.436..690S ). The latter could be traced by the electric field vectors map as presented by (see their Fig. 8), showing a tentative circumferential magnetic field structure, which is also seen for the weaker inner pair of lobes suggestive of possible termination points associated with past activity.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2356-61 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: The optical morphology of this NLRG, as shown in our GMOS-S image, appears to be full of features which are very likely the result of a past merger/interaction (Figure pks2356_online ). At shorter scales, we detect one arc-like irregular feature of depth 24.9 mag arcsec ^ 2 in the V-band at the northern side of the galaxy center. There is also a shell towards the south of the galaxy, and a fainter one to the NE ( _V =25.1 and 25.6 mag arcsec ^ 2 respectively). Further from the brighter shell there is also a broad fan/shell in the same direction, at a 25.8 mag arcsec ^ 2 depth level.

sources.summary: The paper groups PKS 2356-61 with Sy2 sources lacking absorption variability, favoring smooth gas and clumpy dust in a geometrically thick torus viewed at large inclination.

quotes.quote: Panel 6 of Figure fig:cartoon_Sy2 (e.g. ESO 138-G1, PKS 2356-61, and NGC 788): Sy2 sources without absorption variability, large covering factors Large covering factor is found in PKS 2356-61 if it is fitted to clumpy torus at mid-IR, although the smooth model is equally good from the statistical point of view. , and intermediate or high half-opening angles of both gas and dusty structures. The gas is smoothly distributed while the dust is located within clumps. A geometrically-thick torus of dust and gas is able to explain the observed characteristics of these three objects, where the dust is located in clumps while the gas is smoothly distributed. Interestingly these objects are seen at relatively large inclination angles but, in spite of that, the large value of the half opening angle of the torus prevents a direct view of the accretion disk.

sources.summary: The paper discusses PKS 2356-61 as a giant radio galaxy core, using literature or archive radio measurements to evaluate spectral shape, variability, or restarting activity.

quotes.quote: PKS 2356-61 : this source has a too low declination for Effelsberg observations. We present the measurement at 1.4 GHz, published in , and two ATCA measurements at 2.3 GHz and 5 GHz from , of 20.5 2.0 mJy and 35.0 3.5 mJy, respectively.

gmw_00002033.name: SDSS J083823.91+490241.1
gmw_00002033.overview: SDSS J083823.91+490241.1 is a Type-2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2207.06748] located at a redshift of 0.101 [arXiv:2207.06748]. Its narrow emission line region has an estimated upper limit size of approximately 1 arcsecond, or 2250 parsecs [arXiv:2207.06748].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J0838 as a Type-2 AGN at z=0.101, measuring narrow-line spectra and deriving a 1 arcsecond upper limit on its NLR size.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage In the Letter, an interesting method is proposed to estimate size of narrow emission lines regions (NLRs) of a Type-2 AGN SDSS J083823.91+490241.1 (=SDSS J0838) at a redshift of 0.101, by comparing spectroscopic properties through the SDSS fiber (MJD=51873) (diameter of 3 arcseconds) and through the eBOSS fiber (MJD=55277) (diameter of 2 arcseconds). After subtractions of pPXF method determined host galaxy contributions, the narrow emission lines of SDSS J0838 in the SDSS spectrum and in the eBOSS spectrum can be well measured by Gaussian functions, leading more than 90 of [ O iii ] emissions to be covered by the eBOSS fiber with diameter of 2 arcseconds. Meanwhile, both none broad emission components and none-variabilities of ZTF 3years-long g/-band light curves can be applied to confirm SDSS J0838 as a Type-2 AGN, indicating few orientation effects on the projected NLRs size in SDSS J0838. Therefore, upper limit about 1arcsecond (2250pc) of the NLRs size can be reasonably accepted in SDSS J0838. Combining with the intrinsic reddening corrected [ O iii ] line luminosity, the upper limit of NLRs size in SDSS J0838 well lies within the 99.9999 confidence bands of the R-L empirical relation for NLRs in AGN. galaxies:active - galaxies:nuclei - quasars:emission lines - galaxies:Seyfert Apparent narrow emission lines from narrow emission line regions (NLRs) are fundamental characteristics of Active Galactic Nuclei (AGN).

However, among the objects discussed in detail in our manuscripts in preparation, SDSS J0838 is selected as the main target in the Letter by the following three main reasons. First, SDSS J0838 is a well classified Type-2 AGN discussed in the following sections, indicating few orientation effects on estimated NLRs sizes in SDSS 0838. Second, SDSS 0838 has its SDSS spectrum and eBOSS spectrum with almost totally the same drilled fiber positions, leading to few effects of pointing error on estimated NLRs sizes in SDSS 0838. Third, SDSS J0838 has its single-Gaussian described [ O iii ] emissions almost totally covered by the eBOSS fiber leading to clear upper limits of NLRs sizes. Before proceeding further, the spectroscopic features of the SDSS spectra and the eBOSS spectrum are carefully checked in SDSS J0838, in order to measure the emission lines after subtractions of starlight.

gmw_00002034.name: xid 54
gmw_00002034.overview: The source xid 54 is identified with a pair of interacting galaxies at a redshift of z = 0.350 [arXiv:astro-ph/0612361]. Its optical spectrum shows no signs of active galactic nucleus activity, and its X-ray emission may originate from a surrounding group of galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0612361].

sources.summary: The paper treats xid 54 as an X-ray source associated with two interacting galaxies at z=0.350, favoring thermal or group emission over a clearly detected optical AGN.

quotes.quote: Source xid 54, if fitted with an APL model, gives a large value for the spectral slope ( > 3 ) and significant residuals in the 0.3-10 keV energy range. An alternative description of its spectrum is obtained assuming we are observing thermal emission, parameterized with a Raymond-Smith model raymond77 with a temperature kT = 1.6^ +0.4 _ 0.2 keV fixing the metalicity to 0.3 solar. Source xid 54 is identified with two interacting galaxies (see Fig. acs54 ) at redshift z =0.350 with no sign of AGN activity from its optical spectrum. Its X-ray luminosity of 3 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 is larger than that expected for early-type galaxies matsushita01 and, from the optical imaging, there is a concentration of galaxies around xid 54 with the same photometric redshifts therefore supporting the idea that we are looking at the X-ray emission from a group of galaxies.

gmw_00002035.name: xid 2608
gmw_00002035.overview: The source xid 2608 is a heavily absorbed active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0612361]. Its X-ray spectrum features an Fe K fluorescent line at 6.4 keV with a high equivalent width, suggesting that the object is likely Compton-thick [arXiv:astro-ph/0612361].

sources.summary: The paper identifies xid 2608 as a heavily absorbed, likely Compton-thick AGN whose X-ray spectrum includes a significant Fe K line with high equivalent width.

quotes.quote: An additional source that requires a more complex modeling of its spectrum is xid 2608. The fit with the APL model gives an extremely flat value for ( 0.3 ) and large residuals at both low and high energies (see left panel of Fig. 2608_fit ). found that this source is located in an area populated by local Compton-thick Seyfert-2 galaxies in an X-ray color-color diagram (see Fig. 12 of guainazzi05 ). This, together with other evidences based on lines ratios from the optical spectrum, supports the hypothesis that source 2608 is a heavily absorbed AGN.

The presence of the Fe K fluorescent line at 6.4 keV is significant at 95 according to an F-test. The presence of the line is a clear sign that the source is heavily absorbed, but a useful observable to confirm its Compton-thick nature is the Equivalent Width (EW) of the same line. The nominal best fit value for the EW ( 792^ +1151 _ 493 eV) is higher than the maximum (600 eV) observed EW in Compton-thin objects turner97 . This supports the idea that source 2608 is a Compton-thick AGN, although we have to mention that with the current photons statistics, the error for the observed flux of the line ( and consequently for the EW) is still large.

gmw_00002036.name: NGC 4460
gmw_00002036.overview: NGC 4460 is an isolated lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1001.0234, arXiv:1511.07638] that hosts a central starburst [arXiv:0710.0711] and a pseudo-ring of active star formation [arXiv:1001.0234]. This activity drives a modest galactic wind [arXiv:1001.0234, arXiv:1511.07638] with outflow velocities insufficient to escape the galaxy's gravitational well [arXiv:1511.07638].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4460's isolated lenticular disk, finding central star formation, an inner exponential disk, shock-ionized extraplanar gas, and a modest galactic wind.

quotes.quote: We used available UV and optical images to explore the structure of the isolated lenticular galaxy NGC 4460. Also we obtained long-slit and 3D spectroscopic data to probe the origin of the bright nebulosity surrounding the central region of the galaxy.

All ongoing star formation with the rate about 0.3 M_ /yr resides in a pseudo-ring with radius about one kiloparsec. Together with antitruncated shape of the surface brightness profile it suggests a recent formation of the inner exponential disc, whose radial scale three times smaller than global old stellar disc. Whereas gas in the circumnuclear disc is photoionized by radiation of young stars, the external regions of nebulosity are ionized by shocks. This fact, in combination with a bi-conical shape of the ionized gas nebulosity, with the radial distributions of the emission lines velocities and width can be explained as a galactic-scale outflow (superwind) induced by a central star formation.

We estimated outflow velocity as 130 . Galactic wind parameters, such as outflow velocity, formation time and total kinetic energy are several times smaller comparing with the known galactic wind in NGC 253, which is explained by substantially different star formation rates.

sources.summary: NGC 4460 is an isolated lenticular with a galactic wind; new Fabry-Perot observations refine outflow velocities to 30-80 km/s, too slow to escape.

quotes.quote: NGC 4460 is an isolated lenticular galaxy, in which galactic wind has been earlier discovered as a gas outflow associated with circumnuclear regions of star formation. Using the results of observations in the H line with the scanning Fabry--Perot interferometer on the SAO RAS 6-m telescope, we studied the kinematics of the ionized gas in this galaxy. The parameters of gas outflow from the plane of the galactic disk were refined within a simple geometric model. We show that it is impossible to characterize the wind by a fixed velocity value. Characteristic outflow velocities are within 30--80 km s ^ 1 , and they are insufficient to make the swept-out matter ultimately leave the galaxy.

A comparison of the velocities of galactic wind and the model escape velocities for the case of pseudo-isothermal dark matter halo suggests that the wind energy is not strong enough to eject the gas beyond the galactic gravitational well. Therefore, we expect that over time, having cooled down, the matter will again infall onto the galactic disk.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4460 as an isolated lenticular with a compact central emission disk, minor-axis protuberances, short gas-exhaustion time, and possible interaction with an H I cloud.

quotes.quote: NGC 4460 . This is a lenticular galaxy without signs of spiral structure. Surprisingly, we find a compact emission disk in its core, from which diffuse emission protuberances originated along the minor axis. Using the surface brighness fluctuations, Tonry et al. (2001) derived its distance to be 9.59 Mpc.

We regard the isolated ( TI=-0.7 ) lenticular galaxy NGC 4460 to be the most intriguing object in the considered sample. A powerful star formation burst in its center exhasts all available reserve of gas just for 170 Myr. Possibly, we observe here a rare event of interaction of the S0 galaxy with an intergalactic HI cloud.

gmw_00002037.name: PKS J0116-473
gmw_00002037.overview: PKS J0116-473 is a radio galaxy [arXiv:1610.02400] that exhibits recurrent nuclear activity [arXiv:0811.1907]. It is classified as a restarting X-shaped radio galaxy with a double-double structure, featuring new inner lobes embedded within relic outer lobes [arXiv:0811.1907].

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs ATCA observations of the radio galaxy PKS J0116-473, using its studied morphology and showing PURIFY can recover fine details without uniform weighting.

quotes.quote: In particular, we consider observations of the radio galaxies 3C129, Cygnus A, PKS J0334-39, and PKS J0116-473.

PKS J0116-473 The observation of PKS J0116-473 used in this work was first presented in . The total intensity, polametric structure, and morphology of PKS J0116-473 have been studied in detail at 12 and 22 cm emission. The ATCA observations of PKS J0116-473 used in this work were extracted from the archive (PI:Shankar, project code C770), then calibrated and flagged following a standard ATCA data reduction procedure found in the MIRIAD manual http://www.atnf.csiro.au/computing/software/miriad/userguide/userhtml.html . The phase center is located at RA = 14h 59m, 15.75s DEC =-36 ^ 55 ^ 47.87 ^ (J2000), and the central observation frequency is 1.384 GHz.
However, PURIFY has the ability to reconstruct the fine details of PKS J0334-39 and PKS J0116-473 without uniform weighting.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0116 473 as a restarting X-shaped radio galaxy with double-double structure, inner lobes embedded in relic lobes, and a bar-like wing candidate.

quotes.quote: J0116 473:  sar02 presented a detailed study of this radio source, pointing out that it has a double-double structure and is a spectacular example of recurrent nuclear activity in which new edge-brightened inner lobes have been created embedded within outer edge-brightened relic lobes of past activity; the outer lobes lack hotspots. Additionally, they drew attention to a peculiar distinct bar-like feature that is associated with the inner part of the southern lobe and offset from the core, and is oriented almost orthogonal to the radio source axis. If we consider this bar as a `wing' then J0116 473 is another example of a restarting X-shaped radio galaxy that lacks hotspots at the lobe ends.

gmw_00002038.name: PKS 0346 27
gmw_00002038.overview: PKS 0346 27 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of z = 0.991 [arXiv:1906.08314, arXiv:2308.11317]. The galaxy hosts a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:2308.11317] and exhibits compact multi-wavelength variability, including gamma-ray flaring episodes [arXiv:1906.08314, arXiv:2205.04719].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0346 27 as a z=0.991 blazar and characterizes its 2018 high state, compact variability, harder spectrum, and two-order-of-magnitude SED peak shifts.

quotes.quote: PKS 0346 27 (also known as BZQ J0348 2749, 2009A&A...495..691M ), with coordinates R.A. = 57.1589354 ^ , Decl. = 27.8204344 ^ J2000, Beasley2002 is a blazar source located at a redshift z = 0.991 White1988 . It was first identified as a radio source in the Parkes catalog 1964AuJPh..17..340B , and classified it as a quasar based on its optical spectrum. It was later revealed as an X-ray source by ROSAT and references therein 1999A&A...349..389V .

PKS 0346 27 entered an elevated ray activity state starting from the beginning of 2018. The high-state continued throughout the year, displaying the highest fluxes in May 2018. We find evidence of short-time scale variability down to 1.5 hours, which constrains the ray emission region to be compact. The extended flaring period was characterized by a persistently harder spectrum with respect to the quiescent state, indicating changes in the broadband spectral properties of the source. This was confirmed by the multi-wavelength observations, which show a shift in the position of the two SED peaks by 2 orders of magnitude in energy and peak flux value.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0346-27 as an FSRQ blazar with a central SMBH and reports a 100-day gamma-ray periodicity best explained by helical motion in a curved jet.

quotes.quote: PKS 0346-27 is an FSRQ blazar at a redshift z = 0.991 1988ApJ...327..561W . The source has been detected in the different EM bands Sushanth_2023 and recently also discovered as a ray emitter and listed in the Fermi-LAT Fourth Source Catalog (4FGL) 2020ApJS..247...33A . The mass of the SMBH at the center of PKS 0346-27 is estimated by multi-wavelength SED modeling in the low flux state of the source to be 2 10 ^ 8 M _ 2019A&A...627A.140A . Multi-wavelength observation of this source has detected it in the high activity state in optical-NIR 2018aNesciATel11269....1N , as well as in UV and X-ray regions 2018bNesciATel11455....1N .

We detect a periodicity feature of 100 days duration for the entire period of observation with a statistical significance of 3 , which amounts to a 99.7 confidence level. The global significance of this feature is found to be 96.96 . Based on the Akaike Information Criteria, the most probable explanation is that the observed emission is enhanced due to the helical motion of a blob within a curved jet. The origin of this QPO is very likely a region of enhanced emission moving helically inside a curved jet.

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 0346-27 as an FSRQ blazar at z=0.991, identifying gamma-ray flares, short multi-band variability, and one-zone leptonic SEDs with synchrotron and SSC emission.

quotes.quote: PKS 0346-27 is an FSRQ class blazar with coordinates R.A. = 57.1589354^ , Decl. = 27.8204344^ (J2000, ) and a redshift, z = 0.991 ( ). It is also known as BZQ J0348-2749 ( ).

We identified multiple flaring episodes by analyzing the gamma-ray light curve generated from the Fermi-LAT data over a two-year period. The light curves of these individual gamma-ray flares with one-day binning were then modeled using a sum-of-exponentials fit. We found the minimum variability times for the gamma-ray light curve to be 1.34 0.3 days and a range of 0.1–3.2 days for the Swift wavelengths suggesting the compactness of the source. The broadband emission mechanism was studied by modeling the simultaneous multi-waveband Spectral Energy Distributions (SED) using the one-zone leptonic emission mechanism. We found that the optical-UV and X-ray data can be explained by the synchrotron and Synchrotron Self-Compton (SSC) emissions.

sources.summary: The paper describes MRC0346-279 as a low-signal-to-noise, BL-Lac-continuum quasar whose spectrum weakly favors a Lorentzian-plus-Gaussian model and lacks fitted narrow lines.

quotes.quote: MRC0346-279 : The continuum is strongly sloped, with 2.7 ; this is a BL-Lac type continuum.

MRC0346-279 : This spectrum suffers from low signal-to-noise, and so there is little evidence to discriminate between the models: all but one of the models fall within the Jeffreys' criterion of the best-fit model. Model 9, the fit with the broad Lorentzian and the broad Gaussian, was selected by the Bayesian evidence, which is the next simplest model after the single Lorentzian. The selected model has greater evidence by ( Evidence ) = 0.81 than the next best fit. The low signal-to-noise ratio is likely to be the reason for the lack of fitted narrow emission lines in this spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 0346--27 as a flaring blazar in the Fermi-LAT region of interest, replacing the catalog log-parabola with an exponential-cutoff power law and freeing its fit parameters.

quotes.quote: We also notice that the blazar PKS 0346--27 is in our ROI and has been flaring occasionally since 2018, thus including the observational window of this study . Its spectral model from the 4FG-DR2 catalogue -- a log-parabola -- does not characterise properly the flaring state, while a power law with an exponential cut-off can account for the spectrum observed by the Fermi LAT. We therefore modify accordingly the background model and free the spectral parameters of PKS 0346--27 in our fit, together with the normalization of all sources within 3^ of the ROI's centre.

gmw_00002039.name: SGAS J091541+382655
gmw_00002039.overview: SGAS J091541+382655 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha-emitting galaxy at a redshift of z=5.200 [arXiv:1003.0039]. The galaxy is strongly lensed by a foreground massive galaxy cluster and hosts a young, dust-free stellar population with active star formation [arXiv:1003.0039].

sources.summary: The paper presents SGAS J091541+382655 as a strongly lensed z=5.200 Ly-alpha-emitting galaxy, deriving its magnitudes, equivalent width, intrinsic Ly-alpha luminosity, star-formation rate, and young stellar population.

quotes.quote: We identify the two sources as SGAS J091541+382655, at z=5.200 , and SGAS J134331+415455 at z=4.994 . We measure their AB magnitudes at (i,z)=(23.34 0.09,23.29 0.13 ) mags and (i,z)=(23.78 0.18,24.24^ +0.18 _ 0.16 ) mags, and the rest-frame equivalent widths of the Lyman- emission at 25.3 4.1 and 135.6 20.3 for SGAS J091541+382655 and SGAS J134331+415455, respectively. Each source is strongly lensed by a massive galaxy cluster in the foreground, and the magnifications due to gravitational lensing are recovered from strong lens modeling of the foreground lensing potentials. We use the magnification to calculate the intrinsic, unlensed Lyman- and UV continuum luminosities for both sources, as well as the implied star formation rates (SFR). We find SGAS J091541+382655 and SGAS J134341+415455 to be galaxies with (L _ Ly- , L _ UV ) (0.6 L _ Ly- ^ * , 2 L _ UV ^ * ) and (L _ Ly- , L _ UV )=(0.5 L _ Ly- ^ * , 0.9 L _ UV ^ * ), respectively.

Since in all cases the magnification is higher than 10 , we adopt this number as our working order-of-magnitude estimate and determine an upper limit for the intrinsic isotropic Lyman- line luminosity for SGAS J091541+382655 of L _ Ly- 5.9 1.1 10^ 42 ergs s ^ 1 h_ 0.7 ^ 2 and a corresponding SFR _ Ly- 5.3 1.0 M _ yr ^ 1 h_ 0.7 ^ 1 .
The best-fit SED for SGAS J091541+382655 is shown in Figure fig:sed0915 and corresponds to a dust-free single burst with an age of 1.4 Myr.

gmw_00002040.name: SGAS J134331+415455
gmw_00002040.overview: SGAS J134331+415455 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.994 [arXiv:1003.0039]. The galaxy is strongly lensed by a foreground massive galaxy cluster and has an intrinsic star formation rate of 4.1 solar masses per year [arXiv:1003.0039].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGAS J134331+415455 as a strongly lensed z=4.994 Ly-alpha-emitting galaxy, measuring its equivalent width, lensing-corrected Ly-alpha luminosity, and star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: We identify the two sources as SGAS J091541+382655, at z=5.200 , and SGAS J134331+415455 at z=4.994 . We measure their AB magnitudes at (i,z)=(23.34 0.09,23.29 0.13 ) mags and (i,z)=(23.78 0.18,24.24^ +0.18 _ 0.16 ) mags, and the rest-frame equivalent widths of the Lyman- emission at 25.3 4.1 and 135.6 20.3 for SGAS J091541+382655 and SGAS J134331+415455, respectively. Each source is strongly lensed by a massive galaxy cluster in the foreground, and the magnifications due to gravitational lensing are recovered from strong lens modeling of the foreground lensing potentials. We use the magnification to calculate the intrinsic, unlensed Lyman- and UV continuum luminosities for both sources, as well as the implied star formation rates (SFR). We find SGAS J091541+382655 and SGAS J134341+415455 to be galaxies with (L _ Ly- , L _ UV ) (0.6 L _ Ly- ^ * , 2 L _ UV ^ * ) and (L _ Ly- , L _ UV )=(0.5 L _ Ly- ^ * , 0.9 L _ UV ^ * ), respectively.

Based on this model, the magnification at the location of the LAE is m 12 , which we use to calculate the intrinsic isotropic Lyman- line luminosity for SGAS J134331+415455 to be L _ Ly- =4.5 0.7 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 h_ 0.7 ^ 2 . Assuming Case B recombination and taking the prescription from kenn1998 we calculate the star-formation rate (SFR) for SGAS J134331+415455 to be SFR _ Ly- =4.1 0.6 M _ yr ^ 1 h_ 0.7 ^ 1 .

gmw_00002041.name: PG 1341+258
gmw_00002041.overview: PG 1341+258 is a luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2311.13135] that exhibits an excessively flat X-ray spectrum [arXiv:2311.13135]. Observations of the galaxy have also yielded a tentative, low signal-to-noise detection of CO(2-1) gas emission [arXiv:1912.00085].

sources.summary: The paper uses Ton 730 as a luminous AGN with an excessively flat X-ray spectrum, modeled with hybrid thermal and non-thermal coronal electrons.

quotes.quote: However, there were four objects (Ton 730, Mrk 290, SBS 1136 + 594, and Mrk 1310) that were not well reproduced because their X-ray spectra are excessively flat. The X-ray spectra of these objects can be fitted by a power-law with photon indices _ 2-10 keV in the range of 1.53 - 1.64 . The accretion rates of these objects are around m 0.04-0.18 . Besides, PG 1138+222 is also included due to its accretion rate m =0.07 and its X-ray spectrum fitted by a power-law with photon index _ X =1.92^ +0.07 _ 0.07 2019MNRAS.487.3884C . In this section, we apply our model to explain the observational spectra of these AGNs in the optical/UV (UVOT) and X-ray ( 2 - 10 keV) wavelengths.

The modeling result for each object is plotted by the thick black line in Figure fig:fitting . The parameters are noted as m=M_ BH /M_ and m = M / M _ Edd . For Ton 730, Mrk 290, and PG 1138+222, their observed luminosity ratios L_ 2-10 keV /L_ UVOT are 0.26, 0.45, and 0.34, respectively. The number of f_ th is around 0.5 - 0.6 . In other words, more than 40 of the magnetic energy in the corona is allocated to the non-thermal electrons.

sources.summary: The survey treats PG 1341+258 as a tentative low-S/N CO(2-1) detection, noting that repeated cleanings at different velocity-channel widths recover the source at four-sigma significance.

quotes.quote: Appendix apd:plt gives the data for the remaining 20 detected objects, all of which were successfully fit, apart from the tentative detection of PG 1341+258, which suffers from exceptionally low signal-to-noise ratio.
The signal-to-noise ratio of PG 1341+258 is too low to robustly fit its line profile.
We believe PG 1341+258 is marginally detected, because we always detect the source with 4 significance when we clean the data with different velocity channel widths.

gmw_00002042.name: quasar host galaxy
gmw_00002042.overview: The quasar host galaxy is part of a system containing a nearby early-type lensing galaxy at the same redshift [arXiv:1110.5514]. This host galaxy has a total mass of 10.27 dex within its 1.2 kiloparsec Einstein radius, which was measured using strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:2306.01042].

sources.summary: The system contains an early-type lensing galaxy and nearby QSO companion at the same redshift, with a ring-like background source forming an Einstein ring.

quotes.quote: The extended object is located at the center of a ring-like structure that makes a good candidate for a full Einstein ring. A plausible explanation is that the lensing galaxy is the red extended object, i.e., a low redshift early-type galaxy and that the point-like object is a QSO. The LRIS slit contains both objects. The integrated spectrum shows only two sets of lines at two different redshifts, not three. The QSO and the galaxy in Fig. fig:j0919_image must therefore be at the same redshift.

We conclude that we see an early-type galaxy lensing a background source as a full Einstein ring, and that the lensing galaxy has a nearby QSO companion at the same redshift. It is not clear at this points whether the QSO has its own host galaxy or whether its host galaxy is the one at the center of the Einstein ring. Although the QSO is brighter than the galaxy, we expect it to be much less massive and to contribute only as a perturber to the main lensing potential well.

sources.summary: The paper measures the quasar host galaxy total mass with strong lensing, finding an Einstein radius, a 10.27 dex enclosed mass, stellar mass estimates, and robust evidence for the host component.

quotes.quote: Most of the lensing effect is produced by the main elliptical lens galaxy (see Fig. fig:rgb ), but the quasar and its host galaxy, even hidden in the glare of the quasar light, produce detectable lensing signal. We take advantage of this fortunate lensing event and of the fact that the quasar and the main elliptical lensing galaxy do not share the same location, to measure for the first time the total (dark + luminous) mass of a quasar host galaxy with strong gravitational lensing.

As the quasar host is not centered on the main lens galaxy, our models have high sensitivity to its mass and we can measure it in a very robust way. Our best model provides its Einstein radius _ E, h = 0 355^ +0.024 _ 0.028 , which translates into a total mass of _ 10 (M_ Tot, h /M_ ) = 10.27^ +0.06 _ 0.07 within the Einstein radius of 1.2 kpc in the lens plane. Models generated with and without including the quasar host galaxy have a very different Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC), with BIC = 234 (see Table tab:BICvalue for alternative modeling assumptions). This strongly favors models with explicit modeling of the substructure marked by the quasar and shows that we have high sensitivity to it.

The stellar mass of the quasar host galaxy, M_ , h , is inferred with the SED fitting package GSF https://github.com/mtakahiro/gsf Morishita2019 , adopting a range of stellar ages up to 10.0 Gyr and solar metallicity.

gmw_00002043.name: IRAS 14378 3651
gmw_00002043.overview: IRAS 14378 3651 is a single-nucleus ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1105.1731, arXiv:1510.04453] optically classified as a LINER [arXiv:1510.04453]. This distorted, final-stage merger [arXiv:1612.08181] hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1105.1731] and a molecular outflow [arXiv:1105.1731, arXiv:1612.08181].

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 14378-3651 among strong-AGN-contribution ULIRGs, notes low signal-to-noise but confirmed high terminal velocity, and computes its AGN fraction from mid-infrared diagnostics.

quotes.quote: For this first study of outflow signatures in our data we use a sub-sample that is mainly constrained by the observing schedule of Herschel, but that covers a broad range of AGN and starburst activity, including a starburst template (NGC 253), a cold, starburst dominated ULIRG (IRAS 17208-0014,) warm ULIRGs (S25/S60 > 0.1) and/or ULIRGs with strong AGN contributions (Mrk 231, IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 14378-3651), and a heavily obscured ULIRG (IRAS 08572+3915), which hosts a powerful AGN (e.g. Veilleux et al. 2009, V09).

The S/N in the IRAS 14378-3651 spectrum is relatively low, but the high terminal velocity is confirmed by the OH 119 m transition (see Figure Fig1 ). These high OH outflow velocities may be the long-sought conclusive evidence of powerful mechanical feedback from vigorous star formation and/or accreting central black holes.

For IRAS 13120-5453 and IRAS 14378-3651 (which are not part of V09) we calculated AGN fractions using method 1 from V09, based on the observed [ O IV ] / [ Ne II ] ratio (and the observed upper limit for IRAS 14378-3651) from Farrah et al. (2007). AGN luminosities are then calculated as L _ AGN = L _ bol .

sources.summary: IRAS 14378--3651 is described as a single-nucleus LINER ULIRG detected by Chandra, with low X-ray flux and a hardness ratio indicating strong absorption.

quotes.quote: IRAS 14378--3651 shows a single nucleus bushouse2002 in optical and near-infrared HST images. It is optically classified as a LINER kim98 . This source was detected by Chandra with 40 counts in 14 ks iwasawa11 . The observed 2--7 keV band flux is 2.1 10^ 14 \ and the 2--10 keV X-ray luminosity assuming no extinction other than Galactic is 3.4 10^ 41 . Its hardness ratio implies a highly absorbed spectrum with 0.35 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 14378 3651 as a distorted final-stage merger whose OH outflow has removed much nuclear molecular gas and requires at most a moderate momentum boost.

quotes.quote: IRAS 14378 3651 : found no obvious optical companion to this distorted galaxy, which the authors suggest is a final stage merger. This is consistent with the OH observations: the prominent outflow probed by the OH119, OH79, and OH84 doublets appears to have expelled a significant fraction of the nuclear molecular gas, leaving a relatively weak OH65 absorption (GA15, Fig. fitsoh65 ). A moderate (250 ) velocity component, mostly responsible for the broad and blueshifted OH84 absorption, and a high velocity component give a total momentum flux of 5 10^ 35 dyn, which is of the same order as the momentum rate supplied by the (dominant) starburst. This suggests at most moderate momentum boost ( 3 ).

sources.summary: The survey detects both CO and [C I] in IRAS F14378-3651, but noisy continuum maps require manual aperture photometry for the continuum measurement.

quotes.quote: IRAS F14378-3651 Both CO (4--3) and [ C 1 ] (1--0) lines were detected. Because the continuum maps associated with CO (4--3) emission are noisy, the `` imfit " task does not work correctly. We manually chose a flux aperture of 6 6 for the photometry.

gmw_00002044.name: IRAS 10565+2448
gmw_00002044.overview: IRAS 10565+2448 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2302.00943] undergoing a merger involving three galaxies with highly disturbed stellar morphology [arXiv:2302.00943, arXiv:2402.10786]. Its emission is dominated by starburst activity [arXiv:1612.08181, arXiv:2402.10786], which drives a multiphase outflow containing cold neutral, molecular, and ionized gas [arXiv:1009.6020, arXiv:2302.00943].

sources.summary: The paper maps neutral and ionized outflows in the ULIRG F10565+2448, finding a dusty nuclear outflow, asymmetric neutral-gas velocities, and ionized outflow decoupled from the neutral component.

quotes.quote: For the first time, we have mapped and analyzed the 2-D kinematics of a cool neutral outflow in a ULIRG, F10565+2448, using the integral field unit (IFU) on Gemini North to observe the Na I D feature. At the same time we have mapped the ionized outflow with the [ N 2 ] and H emission lines. We find a systemic rotation curve that is consistent with the rotation of the molecular disk determined from previous CO observations. The absorption lines show evidence of a nuclear outflow with a radial extent of at least 3 kpc, consistent with previous observations.

The strength of the Na I D lines have a strong, spatially resolved correlation with reddening, suggesting that dust is present in the outflow. Surprisingly, the outflow velocities of the neutral gas show a strong asymmetry in the form of a major-axis gradient that is opposite in sign to disk rotation. This is inconsistent with entrained material rotating along with the galaxy or with a tilted minor-axis outflow. We hypothesize that this unusual behavior is due to an asymmetry in the distribution of the ambient gas. We also see evidence of asymmetric ionized outflow in the emission-line velocity map, which appear to be decoupled from the neutral outflow.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 10565+2448 as a z=0.0431 ULIRG merger with disturbed morphology. uGMRT data localize a broad blueshifted H I outflow, connect it to molecular outflows, estimate a large multiphase mass rate, and implicate a radio jet.

quotes.quote: IRAS 10565+2448 is a z =0.0431 ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) with an infrared luminosity of 1.2 10^ 12 L_ and a star formation rate of 131.8 M_ yr ^ 1 rupke2013,u2012 . It is a merging system involving three galaxies with highly disturbed stellar morphology; see the gri bands composite image from Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) in Fig. fig:opt_image on which the three galaxies have been marked as A, B, and C. An Arecibo observation with a velocity resolution of 8.3 km s ^ 1 and a spatial resolution of 3.3 arcmin revealed both H i emission and absorption towards the source mirabel1988 . An intriguing H i spectral property of IRAS 10565+2448 is a broad, shallow, and blueshifted H i absorption wing indicating an outflow.

Our higher spatial resolution observations have localised this blueshifted outflow, which is 1.36 kpc southwest of the radio centre and has a blueshifted velocity of 148 km s^ 1 and a full width at half maximum (FWHM) of 581 km s^ 1 . The spatial extent and kinematic properties of the H i outflow are consistent with the previously detected cold molecular outflows in IRAS 10565+2448, suggesting that they likely have the same driving mechanism and are tracing the same outflow. By combining the multi-phase gas observations, we estimate a total outflowing mass rate of at least 140 M_ yr^ 1 and a total energy loss rate of at least 8.9 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 , where the contribution from the ionised outflow is negligible, emphasising the importance of including both cold neutral and molecular gas when quantifying the impact of outflows. We present evidence of the presence of a radio jet and argue that this may play a role in driving the observed outflows.

sources.summary: The survey describes IRAS 10565+2448 as a poorly studied HII-spectrum LIRG with extended near-IR emission and millimeter continuum mostly consistent with dust.

quotes.quote: IRAS 10565+2448 This galaxy has been relatively poorly studied. v95 classify the optical spectrum of IRAS 10565+2448 as HII. c91 do not find any compact radio continuum peak at 1.49 GHz. Near-infrared images show extended emission with a brighter nucleus sco00 .

The [ NeV ] line, which is an indicator of an AGN, is not detected in this galaxy f07 . The millimeter continuum data are consistent with pure dust emission, although the 1.3 mm continuum point is on the large side of what is possible, leaving open the possibility of a small non-thermal contribution at 880 m.

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 10565+2448 as a triple system dominated by a western nucleus, with prominent OH P-Cygni profiles and two modeled outflow components consistent with momentum conservation.

quotes.quote: IRAS 10565+2448 : In the optical ( band) this galaxy appears to be a triple system mur96 , but CO is only detected in the western nucleus dow98 which dominates the luminosity of the system shi10,rup13a . The AGN luminosity may be significantly lower than that of the starburst vei09,vei13,rup13a since the value of _ AGN =0.47 is higher than that obtained from other diagnostics in . The source shows prominent OH119 and OH79 P-Cygni profiles with blue absorption trough and red emission strength similar to or stronger than AGN-dominated sources, although with lower velocity extent (Figs. fitsoh119 and fitsoh79 ). The ground-state OH lines thus indicate outflow parameters for the extended component of the outflow that are similar to AGN-dominated ULIRGs.

Our model fits two outflow components with N_ H (1-2) 10^ 22 \ and 400-500 pc each, yielding P _ tot 5.5 10^ 35 dyn. This is 1.2 times the momentum supplied by the starburst and AGN combined, suggesting a momentum-conserving molecular outflow.

sources.summary: The paper classifies IRAS F10565+2448 as a radio starburst, noting compact 1.49 GHz emission, more structured 8.44 GHz emission, and rejecting edge inversions as uncertain or obscuration-related.

quotes.quote: IRAS F10565+2448 This is a compact radio source at 1.49 GHz, but at 8.44 GHz it appears more structured. Based on the distribution of values of the radio spectral index, and the histogram, this is a radio-SB. The inverted values of located at the edges of the map suggest optically thick region at a distance of 2 arcsec from the centre of the radio source at 1.49 GHz. Having an optically thick region so far from the nucleus is rare, but has been observed before . After checking the HST image of this galaxy we do not see any feature or excess emission at the position where the radio spectral index in inverted.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 10565+2448 as interacting spiral galaxies whose infrared luminosity is star-formation dominated, with prior Chandra and XMM-Newton X-ray detections.

quotes.quote: IRAS 10565+2448 is a pair of interacting spiral galaxies, with two distinct nuclei in the optical and near-infrared scoville00 . The western nucleus is much brighter. The optical spectrum is that of an H II region or a LINER vei95, vei99a . Continuum and emission-line diagnostics from Spitzer spectra indicate that the infrared luminosity of this source is dominated by star formation, not an AGN vei09a . This source was detected by both Chandra and XMM-Newton .

sources.summary: The survey describes IRAS F10565+2448 as a two-galaxy collision where the main galaxy shows CO, [C I], and dust continuum emission while the smaller companion is undetected.

quotes.quote: IRAS F10565+2448 IRAS F10565+2448 consists of two colliding galaxies. In the main large galaxy, CO (4--3), [ C 1 ] (1--0), and dust continuum were detected. In the smaller pair galaxy (IRAS F10565+2448-2), emission were not detected. [ C 1 ] (1--0) emission was observed at the edge of the spectral window.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 10565 + 2448 as one of three ULIRGs with an anomalously high Br/Br line ratio, above case B and not explainable by dust extinction.

quotes.quote: For the remaining three galaxies for which the spectra are shown in Figure fig:anospe , a change in the wavelength range does not affect the line fluxes by more than 5 . We conclude that the three galaxies (shown as blue circles in Figure fig:brflux ), IRAS 10494 + 4424, IRAS 10565 + 2448, and Mrk 273, show Br /Br line ratios of 0.96 0.12, 0.883 0.085, and 1.086 0.053, respectively, all significantly higher than the case B ratio (0.565). These line-ratio anomalies are not explainable with case B theory and dust extinction, which could reduce but not increase the Br /Br line ratio. We show the spectra around the Br and Br lines for these three galaxies in Figure fig:anobr .

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 10565+2448 as an H II/minor-merger galaxy with multiple nuclei or companions, starburst-dominated emission, low AGN fraction, and radio SED turnover.

quotes.quote: IRAS 10565+2448: classified as H _ II galaxy by spectral type Veilleux95 undergoing minor merger Wu98 with a second nucleus at the northeast of the bright nucleus and a third galaxy at the tidal tail Murphy96 . Yuan10 classified it as a starburst-AGN composite galaxy while Vega08 and Vardoulaki15 assert it to be a starburst galaxy based on spectral-index map. The best-fit radio SED model turns out to be the same as that of Clemens10 , though with a steeper =1.1 and a higher turnover frequency, _t =0.5 GHz as compared to our analysis. The low AGN fraction= 5 and R _ AGN =1 obtained from our analysis confirms that this galaxy is indeed starburst dominated, supporting the claim by Vardoulaki15 .

gmw_00002045.name: IRAS 19297 0406
gmw_00002045.overview: IRAS 19297 0406 is a multiple colliding galaxy system dominated by star formation [arXiv:1612.08181]. The system exhibits a gas outflow that is likely associated with only a single component of the collision [arXiv:1612.08181].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes IRAS 19297 0406 as a star-formation-dominated multiple collision system with uncertain but modeled OH outflow energetics, likely involving only one component.

quotes.quote: IRAS 19297 0406 : This is a multiple colliding system dominated by star formation. Though the source is undetected in OH79, it still shows a prominent absorption in OH65 at systemic velocities. The energetics of the outflow are very uncertain, but the model is consistent with an outflowing column of N_ H 10^ 22 , M_ out 4 10^7 , M 100 /yr, and P 3 10^ 35 dyn. This is significantly lower than the momentum rate that a galaxy with L_ IR 2.5 10^ 12 \ can provide, suggesting that only a single component of the multiple system is involved in the outflow.

gmw_00002046.name: IRAS 09022 3615
gmw_00002046.overview: IRAS 09022 3615 is an interacting, ultra-luminous infrared galaxy merger [arXiv:1612.08181, arXiv:2311.02230]. It is optically classified as a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1612.08181], though near-infrared data suggest the presence of a buried active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1612.08181].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 09022 3615 as a star-forming ULIRG with near-infrared evidence for a buried AGN, weak blueshifted OH65 absorption, and uncertain outflow energetics.

quotes.quote: IRAS 09022 3615 : Optically classified as a star-forming galaxy lee11 , the red near-IR continuum in the L band suggests a buried AGN in this ULIRG lee12 . The OH65 absorption in this source is weak but detected, and shows a slight blueshift relative to the systemic velocity. This is the only source in our sample where the emission feature in OH119 is stronger than the absorption feature, thus involving important geometrical effects or significant collisional excitation. Our model is thus relatively uncertain, though it involves M 130 /yr and P 1 10^ 35 dyn.

As in the case of IRAS 19297 0406, this is significantly lower than the momentum rate that the galaxy could supply based on its luminosity. However, the energetics could be significantly underestimated if the weak absorption is due to low far-IR continuum brightness, rather than to low column densities.

sources.summary: The paper detects and resolves the water line in IRAS 09022-3615, notes a velocity offset from ALMA CO emission, and describes complex merger morphology.

quotes.quote: We report the first ground-based detection of the water line p- 211202 at 752.033 GHz in three z<0.08 ultra-luminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs): IRAS 06035-7102, IRAS 17207-0014 and IRAS 09022-3615. Using the Atacama Pathfinder EXperiment (APEX), with its Swedish-ESO PI Instrument for APEX (SEPIA) band-9 receiver, we detect this \ line with overall signal-to-noise ratios of 8--10 in all three galaxies.
In the complex interacting system IRAS 09022-3615, black the profile of the water emission line is offset in velocity with respect to the ALMA CO( J=4-3 ) emission.

We have identified a velocity shift of 100 km/s in the case of IRAS 09022-3615, which will be discussed in detail in Section 3.4. We confirm the line detection at a higher significance (at least a factor of two) and higher spectral resolution. The spectra show that p- 211202 is detected with a peak flux density of 0.45 0.09 Jy beam ^ 1 in IRAS 09022-3615, 1.28 0.34 Jy beam ^ 1 in IRAS 17208-0014 and 0.33 0.08 Jy beam ^ 1 in IRAS 06035-7102.

sources.summary: The paper classifies IRAS 09022-3615 as lower-excitation in its data but notes high-J studies require multiple excitation components, with its single-component fit giving modest density and hot gas.

quotes.quote: We allowed the fit to use up to a maximum of three Gaussian functions to reproduce the observed global line profiles, so that line asymmetries and broad wings are properly captured with separate spectral components when the S/N is high enough (see e.g., source IRAS 09022-3615, Figure fig:spec09022 ).

In particular, the sources mentioned above with poor sampling of the posterior probability distribution, as well as IRAS 06206-6315, IRAS 09022-3615, IRAS F22491-1808 and IRAS F23060+0505, are some of the galaxies that likely require a second excitation component.

And indeed, some of the sources belonging to the `lower-excitation' group under our criteria, have been observed to have high-excitation CO SLEDs when probing CO( J>7 ) lines (e.g., IRAS 09022-3615, IRAS F05189-2524, IRAS 17208-0014) . showed that, while there are sources for which a single-component CO SLED fit is sufficient, the CO ladder of IRAS 09022-3615 (classified as a warm ULIRG in their study, and included also in our work) requires three excitation components in order to reproduce the observed CO fluxes up to J_ up =13 : (i) a cold (47 K) component peaking at J_ up 3 , (ii) a warm (205 K) component peaking at J_ up 6 , and (iii) a third hot (415 K) component that dominates at high- J transitions, peaking around J_ up 11 . They propose that the hottest component traces the inner PDRs, shocks and/or AGN-heated gas (XDRs) . In our analysis, the best-fit parameters derived for IRAS 09022-3615 from the single LVG component model are n_ H_2 10^ 2.6 cm ^ 3 and T_ kin 250 K.

gmw_00002047.name: IRAS 03158 + 4227
gmw_00002047.overview: IRAS 03158+4227 is a local ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1804.03147, arXiv:1710.02356] that hosts an obscured Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0002460] and a companion galaxy with a curved tail [arXiv:1612.08181]. The system exhibits a massive, high-velocity molecular outflow [arXiv:1612.08181, arXiv:1804.03147] detected in both CO and OH line profiles [arXiv:1804.03147, arXiv:1710.02356].

sources.summary: The paper portrays IRAS 03158 + 4227 as an extreme source with a distant companion, deep OH absorption, high-velocity outflow, and a compact massive component demanding high momentum boost.

quotes.quote: IRAS 03158 + 4227 : This galaxy has a companion (projected separation of 50 kpc) with no apparent sign of interaction in the band mur96 , but found a curved tail in the companion that matches the expected result of a binary interaction. The optical spectrum shows no indication of an AGN meu01 , and find no evidence for an AGN from hard X-rays activity. However, this is (together with IRAS 20100 4156) the most extreme source in our sample.

On the other hand, the outflow is observed out to 1500 \ in OH119 as emphasized by . In OH79 the observed P-Cygni profile has a blueshifted wing apparently extending up to 1700 , though it may be contaminated by H _2 O primarily at velocities more blueshifted than 1300 . The OH84 absorption trough is deep, 10 at 800 , and a very prominent OH65 absorption is seen up to 1000 .

Nevertheless, the model captures the presence of a compact, massive ( 4 10^8 ), and excited component flowing at high velocities ( 300-1300 ), with an estimated mass outflow rate of 1300 /yr and a momentum flux of 7 10^ 36 dyn. These astonishing values cannot be understood in terms of the momentum rate supplied by a galaxy with even such high luminosity as IRAS 03158 + 4227, and a very high momentum boost is required.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 03158 + 4227 as a local ULIRG with an extreme, spatially resolved molecular outflow, short gas-depletion times, and mixed starburst/AGN driving.

quotes.quote: We report molecular gas observations of IRAS 20100 4156 and IRAS 03158 + 4227, two local ultraluminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs) hosting some of the fastest and most massive molecular outflows known. Using ALMA and PdBI observations, we spatially resolve the CO( 1-0 ) emission from the outflowing molecular gas in both and find maximum outflow velocities of 1600 and 1700 for IRAS 20100 4156 and IRAS 03158 + 4227, respectively. We find total gas mass outflow rates of M_ OF 670 and 350 , respectively, corresponding to molecular gas depletion timescales ^ dep _ OF 11 and 16 Myr. This is nearly 3 times shorter than the depletion timescales implied by star formation, ^ dep _ SFR 33 and 46 Myr, respectively.

We find AGN fractions of f_ AGN = 0.14 0.47 and f_ AGN = 0.43 0.18 for IRAS 20100 4156 and IRAS 03158+4227, respectively. These are consistent within the uncertainties with f_ AGN found using SED fitting ( sssec:sedfitting ), and also consistent with the AGN fractions found previously for these objects; nardini2009 , and those typically found in ULIRGs farrah2012 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a weak ASCA GIS detection of IRAS 03158+4227, finding a steep X-ray spectrum, no iron line, and evidence that its Seyfert 2 nucleus is Compton-thick obscured.

quotes.quote: IRAS 03158+4227: The GIS data of IRAS 03158+4227 give a detection at a low signal--to--noise level ( 6 ). The spectrum is well fitted by a powerlaw with 2.8 . The 2-10 keV flux is F=5.4 1 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

There is no evidence of the iron line, and the steepness of the spectrum suggests a thermal origin of the observed emission. IRAS 03158+4227 is classified optically as Seyfert 2, therefore it should host an AGN. From the low X-ray luminosity, relative to the infrared emission, and from the absence of AGN indicators in the X rays we deduce that the AGN is obscured by a column density N_H > 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper uses IRAS 03158+4227 as a local ULIRG template for SPICA predictions, emphasizing far-infrared OH outflow diagnostics, high-velocity absorption wings, and detectability to high redshift.

quotes.quote: We adopt 3 local template ULIRGs to make predictions on the detectability of molecular outflows at high- z , by scaling the far-IR spectroscopic observations obtained with Herschel /PACS: IRAS 03158+4227, Mrk 231, and IRAS 23365+3604 (GA17). The IR luminosities ( 8-1000 m, L_ IR ) are listed in Table tab1 . All three sources, as well as many other local ULIRGs, show clear evidence for massive molecular outflows from P-Cygni line profiles in the ground-state OH119 and OH79 doublets e.g. fis10 . In the cases of IRAS 03158+4227 and Mrk 231, high-velocity ( 1000 ) absorption wings are also detected in the excited OH84 and OH65 doublets, indicating compact outflowing gas with high column densities (GA17).

Strong outflow sources as IRAS 03158+4227 and Mrk 231 would be detected up to the maximum observable redshift (set by the instrumental limit), and more moderate outflow sources like IRAS 23365+3604 would be detectable up to at least z 1.3 .

gmw_00002048.name: IRAS 20100 4156
gmw_00002048.overview: IRAS 20100 4156 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1804.03147] consisting of a close interacting pair of disk galaxies [arXiv:1612.08181] with a double nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0210690]. The system hosts a starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0510282], a possible buried active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1612.08181], and extreme molecular outflows [arXiv:1804.03147].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 20100 4156 as a close interacting disk pair with possible third component, OH megamaser emission, buried-AGN evidence, and extreme high-velocity OH outflows.

quotes.quote: IRAS 20100 4156 : From optical and near-IR images, classify this galaxy as a close interacting pair of disk galaxies (projected separation of 5.4 kpc), with a starburst or LINER-type spectrum. A possible third component of the merger was identified by with NICMOS. The galaxy shows strong megamaser emission in OH at centimeter wavelengths har16 , with a plateau of OH emission whose blue wing traces the velocities of the absorption found in the far-IR OH doublets. Searches in X-rays have identified the possible presence of a buried AGN fra03 .

The OH doublets indicate a galaxy with extreme properties. The OH65 absorption is very deep and broad, with little decrease of the absorption at wavelengths between the two doublet components. Absorption in OH119 is observed out to 1300 , and to 1000 \ in the other 3 doublets. Similar to IRAS 03158+4227, our model involves a compact, massive ( 5 10^8 ), and excited component flowing at velocities up to 800 \ with a momentum flux of 3 10^ 36 dyn.

sources.summary: The paper finds the brightest nucleus of IRAS 20100-4156 starburst-dominated in L-band spectra but notes uncertain continuum fitting and possible minor AGN contribution from X-ray diagnostics.

quotes.quote: IRAS 20100-4156 . Only the brightest nucleus of this source is detected. The spectrum (the lowest S/N among the primary nuclei in our sources) is dominated by the 3.3 m emission line, suggesting a starburst origin of the L-band emission. The continuum slope is quite uncertain, due to the low S/N. An argument similar to that for IRAS 12112-0305NE can be applied: the continuum can be fitted (a) by a flat power law, with a residual excess at 2.9-3 m, or (b) by a steeper ( 0.5 ) continuum plus an ice absorption feature with _ 3.1 0.5 .

In both interpretations, no clear indication of the presence of an AGN is found, even if we cannot rule out a possible minor AGN contribution, due to the low statistics available. The mid-IR spectrum of this source suggests a starburst classification. In the X-rays, no clear indication of an AGN is found, however the location of this source in the X-ray diagnostic diagrams of franc03 is between the typical starbursts and AGNs positions, suggesting a possible contribution of an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 20100 4156 as a local ULIRG with an extreme, spatially resolved molecular outflow, short gas-depletion times, and mixed starburst/AGN driving.

quotes.quote: We report molecular gas observations of IRAS 20100 4156 and IRAS 03158 + 4227, two local ultraluminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs) hosting some of the fastest and most massive molecular outflows known. Using ALMA and PdBI observations, we spatially resolve the CO( 1-0 ) emission from the outflowing molecular gas in both and find maximum outflow velocities of 1600 and 1700 for IRAS 20100 4156 and IRAS 03158 + 4227, respectively. We find total gas mass outflow rates of M_ OF 670 and 350 , respectively, corresponding to molecular gas depletion timescales ^ dep _ OF 11 and 16 Myr. This is nearly 3 times shorter than the depletion timescales implied by star formation, ^ dep _ SFR 33 and 46 Myr, respectively.

We find AGN fractions of f_ AGN = 0.14 0.47 and f_ AGN = 0.43 0.18 for IRAS 20100 4156 and IRAS 03158+4227, respectively. These are consistent within the uncertainties with f_ AGN found using SED fitting ( sssec:sedfitting ), and also consistent with the AGN fractions found previously for these objects; nardini2009 , and those typically found in ULIRGs farrah2012 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 20100--4156 as an extremely bright double-nucleus ULIRG where neither nucleus shows detected polarized H emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS 20100--4156 . Lying at z=0.129583 , this object is extremely bright ( L_ IR [ 8 1000 m ] 5 10^ 12 L_ , adopting _m=0.3 , _ =0.7 , H _0 =65 km/s/Mpc). The top left panel of Figure 2 shows the spectrum of the southern nucleus: only an upper limit of 2 10^ 17 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 to the polarized H flux can be set. The northern nucleus, of comparable luminosity, does not show any polarization either.

gmw_00002049.name: B3 1504+377
gmw_00002049.overview: B3 1504+377 is a self-absorbing molecular system located at a redshift of z=0.672 [arXiv:0910.0800, arXiv:astro-ph/0701894]. It exhibits seven detected molecular absorption lines [arXiv:0910.0800, arXiv:astro-ph/0701894], with velocity separations that suggest non-circular nuclear motions and a regular outer spiral arm [arXiv:astro-ph/0701894].

sources.summary: The paper describes B3 1504+377 as a self-absorbing molecular system with narrow and broad components, unusual diffuse-gas chemistry, and velocity offsets that limit constant-variation tests.

quotes.quote: B3 1504+377 z=0.672 This is the second self-absorbing system, with a narrow component (may be corresponding to the outer disk), and a broad component, at 330km/s (may be widened by non-circular motions in the nucleus). Up to 7 different molecular lines have been detected, and the chemistry of the absorbing diffuse gas is unexpected; with high HCO ^+ abundance. The comparison of the HI- position with the molecular lines reveals strong differences in velocity, which prevent to use this system for the constant variation study.

sources.summary: The paper reports seven molecular absorption lines toward B3 1504+377, interpreting the velocity separation as non-circular nuclear motion plus a more regular spiral-arm component.

quotes.quote: Towards B3 1504+377 at z=0.672, 7 different molecular absorption lines are detected, with a large separation 330km/s, which could be explained by a highly non-circular motions in the center, with a more regular spiral arm in the outer parts. The observed HNC/HCN absorption ratio implies thermalization of the gas, with the excitation temperature equal to the kinetic one. As is frequently observed in molecular absorptions, the HCO ^+ is enhanced by 10-100, which can only be explained by a combination of a diffuse and a clumpy medium (e.g. Lucas Liszt 1994, 98).

gmw_00002050.name: PMN J0134-0931
gmw_00002050.overview: PMN J0134-0931 is a highly reddened, radio-loud gravitationally lensed quasar at a redshift of z=2.216 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510465, arXiv:1601.00544]. A foreground lensing galaxy at z=0.7645 splits the background quasar into six apparent images [arXiv:0910.0800, arXiv:astro-ph/0701894] and exhibits both HI and OH absorption lines [arXiv:0910.0800, arXiv:astro-ph/0701894].

sources.summary: The paper describes PMN J0134-0931 as a z=0.7645 lens producing six quasar images, with detected HI and OH lines useful for avoiding velocity-offset systematics.

quotes.quote: PMN J0134-0931 z=0.7645 The z=0.7645 lens produces 6 images of the background quasar at z=2.22. HI- and OH- lines have been detected at GBT (Kanekar et al 2005). The main OH lines at 1665 and 1667 MHz are in absorption, while the satellite line at 1720 MHz is in emission.

The other satellite line at 1612 MHz is in absorption, such that the sum of the two satellite lines is compatible with noise. The two satellite lines are therefore conjugate, which comes from the peculiar excitation of the upper levels through far-infrared radiation. This conjugate character ensures that the gas comes from the same regions, and therefore intrinsic velocity offsets will not perturb the constant variation study.

sources.summary: The paper describes PMNJ0134 0931 as a highly reddened lensed quasar with complex radio morphology and confirms the lensing-galaxy redshift near z=0.766.

quotes.quote: PMNJ0134 0931 : A highly reddened lensed quasar with a complex radio morphology, discovered by Winn et al. (2002a). The quasar is at redshift z=2.216 . The two brightest images are separated by 0 15 (see Fig. AllFindingCharts ). The lensing galaxy redshift was measured by Hall et al. (2002) and by Kanekar Briggs (2003) and found to be z=0.7645 . This is consistent with our result, z=0.766 0.001 , based on the spectrum shown in Fig. PMNJ0134 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PMN J0134-0931 as a lensed quasar absorber detected in H I and OH, split into six apparent images, and useful for fundamental-constant tests.

quotes.quote: PMN J0134-0931 This recent absorber has been detected in HI and OH lines at GBT (Kanekar et al 2005), from the z=0.7645 lens in front of the background quasar at z=2.22. The latter is split in 6 apparent images. Surprisingly, only upper limits of HCO ^+ or H _2 CO lines were obtained on this source, probably due to small-scale structure of the ISM, and very different continuum source extent across the radio spectrum. The absorption system provides a good probe of the fundamental constant variation.

sources.summary: The paper calls J013435.66-093103.0 a peculiar, very red, radio-loud gravitationally lensed quasar with complex radio morphology and absorber-driven lensing/reddening.

quotes.quote: (22) \ J013435.66-093103.0 Peculiar in various respects: very red (Urrutia et al. ), highest radio-loudness in our sample ( R 65 ; Sect. sec:RL ), and a gravitationally lensed quasar with complex radio morphology (Winn et al. , ). Explained by a model wherein lensing and reddening is caused by the absorber system at z 0.76 (Hall et al. ).

gmw_00002051.name: 3C 294
gmw_00002051.overview: 3C 294 is a Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy [arXiv:1907.03293, arXiv:astro-ph/0301468] at a redshift of approximately 1.78 [arXiv:1907.03293, arXiv:astro-ph/0301468] that features a central obscured radio-loud quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0301468]. The system is resolved into multiple merging subclumps [arXiv:astro-ph/0303663] surrounded by a 100-kiloparsec region of diffuse X-ray emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0301468, arXiv:astro-ph/0412026].

sources.summary: The paper revisits 3C 294 with LBT adaptive-optics imaging and spectroscopy, resolving components, associating the radio source, measuring z=1.784, and leaving dual-AGN versus projected-pair interpretations unresolved.

quotes.quote: 3C 294 is a powerful FR II type radio galaxy at z = 1.786. Past studies have identified a clumpy structure, possibly indicative of a merging system, as well as tentative evidence that 3C 294 hosts a dual active galactic nucleus (AGN). Due to its proximity to a bright star, it has been subject to various adaptive optics imaging studies. In order to distinguish between the various scenarios for 3C 294, we performed deep, high-resolution adaptive optics near-infrared imaging and optical spectroscopy of 3C 294 with the Large Binocular Telescope.

We resolve the 3C 294 system into three distinct components separated by a few tenths of an arcsecond on our images. One is compact, the other two are extended, and all appear to be non-stellar. The nature of each component is unclear. The two extended components could be a galaxy with an internal absorption feature, a galaxy merger, or two galaxies at different redshifts. We can now uniquely associate the radio source of 3C 294 with one of the extended components. Based on our spectroscopy, we determined a redshift of z = 1.784 0.001, which is similar to the one previously cited. In addition we found a previously unreported emission line at 6749.4 \ in our spectra. It is not clear that it originates from 3C 294 . It could be the Ne [ IV ] doublet 2424/2426 \ at z = 1.783, or belong to the compact component at a redshift of z 4.56. We thus cannot unambiguously determine whether 3C 294 hosts a dual AGN or a projected pair of AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper presents a deep Chandra study of 3C294, describing extended hourglass X-ray emission, a hard obscured radio-loud quasar nucleus, and an FR II radio-galaxy environment at z=1.786.

quotes.quote: Munchen, Germany We report the results from a 200 ks Chandra observation of the z=1.786 radio galaxy 3C294 and its cluster environment, increasing by tenfold our earlier observation. The diffuse emission, extending about 100 kpc around the nucleus, has a roughly hourglass shape in the N-S direction with surprisingly sharp edges to the N and S. The spectrum of the diffuse emission is well fitted by either a thermal model of temperature 3.5 keV and abundance < 0.9 ( 2 ), or a power-law with photon index 2.3.

Hot gas would still be required to confine the relativistic plasma; the situation could parallel that of the radio bubbles seen as holes in nearby clusters, except that in 3C294 the bubbles are bright in X-rays owing to the extreme power in the source and the sixty fold increase in the energy density of the CMB. The X-ray spectrum of the radio nucleus is hard, showing a reflection spectrum and iron line. The source is therefore an obscured radio-loud quasar. X-rays: galaxies -- galaxies: active: clusters: individual (3C294) -- intergalactic medium 3C294 is a powerful Fanaroff-Riley II radio source (FR II) at a redshift of 1.786.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C294 as a high-redshift radio-galaxy example, estimating scattered AGN radiation from its Chandra-detected intracluster medium and central X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: Consider for example the radio galaxy 3C294 located at z=1.786 , for which a surrounding intracluster medium has recently been revealed with Chandra (Fabian et al. ). Since the total number of counts registered from the source in the 20 ksec Chandra observation was small, 100 , of which only 30 are from the central AGN, it is only possible to make rough estimates in this case. Fabian et al. find that the gas temperature is 5 keV, the 2--10 keV rest-frame luminosity of the hot gas is 2.5 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , the corresponding (isotropic) luminosity of the AGN is 1.1 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , the photon index for the AGN emission 2 and the radial Thomson depth of the cluster is 0.004 .

We thus see that it is desirable to go to energies above 10 keV in searching for scattered AGN radiation around the 3C294 galaxy. However, photon statistics then becomes a limiting factor. We can roughly estimate that the XEUS observatory with its effective area of 2 m ^2 at 10 keV and angular resolution of a few arcsec should be able to detect only a total of 10 scattered AGN photons above 10 keV in a 100 ks observation of 3C294.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C294 as a powerful radio galaxy whose extended X-ray emission, offset from the radio axis, supports precessing and intermittent jet activity.

quotes.quote: erlund2006 found that there is considerable extended X-ray emission associated with the powerful radio galaxy 3C294; this is reproduced in Figure fig:3c294 . The bulk of the X-ray emission shows extension along an axis differently oriented from the radio axis by 50 degrees, and slightly longer than the length of the radio axis. Erlund et al suggest that the offset between these two axes arises because of the precession of the axis along which jet plasma is ejected. The fact that the superposed radio and X-ray observations reveal intermittent jet activity comes from the fact that for the two discrete directions indicated, one of them is traced by freshly accelerated high-energy radio-synchrotron emitting particles while the other direction appears to be consistent with inverse-Compton scattered CMB (ICCMB) photons (having a power-law = 2.1 0.1 ).

Consistent with the notion that the jet axis is precessing is the observation revealed in the zoom-in to Figure fig:3c294 , that at high resolution the radio axis is offset from the nearby fine scale X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper applies the 3C 356 merger-trigger model to 3C 294, describing two close optical components, compact radio-jet source identification, young jet age, and analogous optical properties.

quotes.quote: The radio galaxy 3C 294 has recently been observed at high resolution in X-rays with Chandra (Fabian et al. \ 2001) and at near-infrared wavelengths with Adaptive Optics on the Canada--France--Hawaii and Keck Telescopes (Stockton, Canalizo Ridgway 1999; Quirrenbach et al. \ 2001).
